VLD_Ascentia_SupportBundleProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_ToolDescriptors
VLD_Ascentia_ToolEvidence
VLD_Ascentia_ToolRegistration
VLD_Ascentia_ToolRepairPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_BuildDoctorReport
VLD_Ascentia_BlueprintCompileState
VLD_Ascentia_PhysicsAssetBodies
VLD_Ascentia_GameplayCueReferences
VLD_Ascentia_PluginState
VLD_Ascentia_LogFailurePatterns
VLD_Ascentia_MissingPackages
VLD_Ascentia_BrokenSoftReferences
VLD_Ascentia_BrokenHardReferences
VLD_Ascentia_PluginContentReferences
VLD_Ascentia_MotionScenarioProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DodgeDirectionSlots
VLD_Ascentia_RootMotionPolicyCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_StairFixtureResults
VLD_Ascentia_LedgeFixtureResults
VLD_Ascentia_MontageNotifyWindows
VLD_Ascentia_AudioNotifyCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_NiagaraNotifyCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_WeaponSocketProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_TrailSocketCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_ToolEvidenceRetention
VLD_Ascentia_MCPAutomationOwnership
VLD_Ascentia_RawProviderUsage
VLD_Ascentia_AutomationSessionPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_BridgeProviders
VLD_Ascentia_ContentProvenance
VLD_Ascentia_ThirdPartyPluginRisks
VLD_Ascentia_EditorScenarioProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_NetworkAuthority
VLD_Ascentia_NetworkScenarios
VLD_Ascentia_AbilityNetworkPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_MotionNetworkPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_MultiplayerLootPolicies
VLD_Ascentia_ExploitSensitivePaths

The validator should tell a Designer the fix, not merely the failure.

#12.11 Blueprint obscurity rule

When a feature ships, ask:

  • Can a Designer find the asset that owns it?
  • Can a Designer tune the values without compiling?
  • Can a Designer preview the result?
  • Can a Designer see why validation failed?
  • Can a Designer tell whether a value came from fallback, row, asset, or runtime

instance?

  • Can a Designer bind presentation without changing authority?
  • Can a playtest prove the Blueprint surface still works?

If the answer is no, the feature is not finished. It may be a good native slice, but it is not production-ready Ascentia.

#12.12 Designer surface governance starter package

This chapter should not remain only a catalog. Ascentia needs a repeatable way to prove that every native system has the right Designer surface, validation, preview path, report output, and Blueprint boundary. Treat this as a cross-phase governance package named SURF, because it applies to P11 through P23 and every bespoke tool.

Reference posture:

  • Use Editor Utility Widgets for dockable, report-driven workflows where

Designers browse, compare, validate, preview, and open related assets.

  • Use Scriptable Tools for interactive viewport placement, painting, measuring,

road/POI layout, anchor placement, and any workflow where hit-testing and gizmos matter more than a docked form.

  • Use Details panel metadata and customizations when a property needs better

ordering, edit conditions, units, buttons, inline warnings, or structured Slate UI.

  • Use Data Validation for asset correctness gates and commandlet/package

checks. Validators should emit Designer-readable repair guidance and machine-readable report rows.

  • Keep unsafe mutation behind dry-run, source-control checks, transactions, and

evidence. A one-click repair without a preview report is a production risk.

Runtime/editor ownership split:

Source/Ascentia/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceRegistry.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceReportTypes.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDetailsPanelPolicy.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceValidation.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/DesignerSurface/AscentiaDetailsPanelPolicy.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAuditCommandlet.cpp

Core assets:

PDA_DesignerSurfaceRegistry_MVP
PDA_DesignerSurfacePolicy_Default
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P11
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P12
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P13
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P14
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P15
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P16
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P17
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P18
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P19
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P20
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P21
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P22
PDA_DesignerSurfacePhaseGate_P23

Surface registry row:

FAscentiaDesignerSurfaceEntry
  SurfaceTag
  PhaseTag
  OwningSystemTag
  SurfaceKind
  AssetPath
  NativeClass
  BlueprintBase
  DataAssetType
  DataTableType
  EditorWidget
  ScriptableTool
  ValidatorName
  EvidencePath
  DashboardCardTag
  RequiredForPhaseGate
  MutatesAssets
  SupportsDryRun
  SupportsUndoTransaction
  SupportsSourceControlCheck
  DesignerOwner
  EngineeringOwner
  StatusTag

Surface kinds:

Ascentia.Surface.DataAsset
Ascentia.Surface.DataTable
Ascentia.Surface.BlueprintBase
Ascentia.Surface.EditorUtilityWidget
Ascentia.Surface.ScriptableTool
Ascentia.Surface.DetailsPanel
Ascentia.Surface.RuntimeWidget
Ascentia.Surface.Validator
Ascentia.Surface.Commandlet
Ascentia.Surface.Report
Ascentia.Surface.DashboardCard

Coverage report:

FAscentiaDesignerSurfaceCoverageReport
  RunId
  PhaseTag
  SurfacesExpected
  SurfacesFound
  MissingAssets
  MissingValidators
  MissingEvidenceReports
  MissingPreviewTools
  MissingBlueprintBases
  NativeOnlyFeatures
  UnsafeMutationTools
  BadDetailsMetadata
  StaleCatalogEntries
  NewlyDiscoveredUnregisteredAssets
  BlockingFailures
  Warnings

Every phase package should have a coverage row. Example:

P11 Combat Motion
  Required surfaces:
    PDA_CombatMotionProfile_*
    PDA_DodgeSet_*
    EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector
    EUW_AscentiaRootMotionPolicyLab
    VLD_Ascentia_CombatMotion
    Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P11/CombatMotion/{RunId}/report.json
  Blocking native-only features:
    dodge direction mapping hidden only in C++
    root-motion fallback hidden only in logs

Details panel policy:

FAscentiaDetailsPanelFieldPolicy
  FieldName
  Category
  TooltipRequired
  Units
  ClampMin
  ClampMax
  EditCondition
  AllowedClasses
  AssetBundles
  DesignerFacingName
  MutabilityTag
  ValidationRuleTag

Rules:

  • Designer-facing numeric fields need units or range hints.
  • Motion, damage, economy, and performance values need clamps.
  • Soft object/class references need allowed-class metadata where possible.
  • Fields with side effects need clear mutability tags and validation warnings.
  • Prototype-only fields must say so in category or tooltip.
  • Runtime telemetry should be BlueprintReadOnly unless mutation is explicitly

part of the surface.

  • Editor-only repair actions must use transactions and source-control checks.

EUW governance:

EUW_AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit
  Loads the surface registry, scans assets/classes, compares expected vs found
  surfaces, opens missing asset paths, and writes a coverage report.

EUW_AscentiaDesignerTuningSurfaceAudit
  Finds reflected properties in native classes/Blueprints that are likely
  Designer-facing but lack category, tooltip, units, clamp metadata, validation,
  or DataAsset surfacing.

EUW_AscentiaSurfacePhaseGate
  Shows phase readiness by surface: native class, DataAsset/DataTable,
  Blueprint base, EUW, validator, report, dashboard card, and latest evidence.

EUW_AscentiaDetailsPanelPolicyPreview
  Opens selected classes/assets and displays Details metadata policy failures
  without requiring Designers to read C++ headers.

Commandlet:

AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit
  -Registry=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Tools/PDA_DesignerSurfaceRegistry_MVP
  -Phase=P11
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces
  -FailOnNativeOnlyPhaseGate

Validation:

VLD_Ascentia_DesignerSurfaceRegistry
VLD_Ascentia_DesignerSurfaceCoverage
VLD_Ascentia_DetailsMetadata
VLD_Ascentia_EditorUtilityWidgets
VLD_Ascentia_ScriptableTools
VLD_Ascentia_UnsafeEditorMutation
VLD_Ascentia_NativeOnlyPhaseFeatures

Machine report path:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces/{RunId}/coverage_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces/{RunId}/details_metadata.csv
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces/{RunId}/native_only_features.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/DesignerSurfaces/{RunId}/unsafe_mutation_tools.json

Proof matrix:

DesignerSurface_P11_CombatMotion_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P14_WeaponBreadth_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P17_WorldZone_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P18_AI_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P19_SaveIdentity_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P20_Presentation_Coverage
DesignerSurface_P23_ContentData_Coverage
DetailsMetadata_NoUncategorizedDesignerFields
EditorMutation_AllRepairToolsHaveDryRun
NativeOnly_NoPhaseGateBlockingFeatures

SURF starter tickets:

  • SURF-001: Add PDA_DesignerSurfaceRegistry_MVP, surface entry structs, and

first registry rows for P11, P14, P17, P18, P19, P20, and P23.

  • SURF-002: Add AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit commandlet and coverage report

writer.

  • SURF-003: Add EUW_AscentiaDesignerSurfaceAudit and phase-gate view.
  • SURF-004: Add Details metadata policy scan for native classes and Blueprint

Class Defaults that are marked Designer-facing.

  • SURF-005: Add unsafe mutation audit for EUWs, Scriptable Tools, Python tools,

and repair buttons.

  • SURF-006: Add validators for registry coverage, missing assets, missing

validators, missing reports, native-only features, and stale catalog entries.

  • SURF-007: Add dashboard card output showing Designer-surface coverage by

phase.

  • SURF-008: Add CI/package gate mode that fails only on phase-gate surfaces,

while reporting future-phase gaps as warnings.

Recommended implementation order:

  1. SURF-001 and SURF-002 first. The registry and report make the catalog

actionable.

  1. SURF-003 then gives Designers and Technical Designers a navigable view.
  2. SURF-004 catches the "buried in C++ obscurity" failure mode directly.
  3. SURF-005 protects the growing tool ecosystem from unsafe repair buttons.
  4. SURF-006 through SURF-008 connect the surface report to validation,

dashboard, and package gates.

#13. Progression, Save, Death Loop, and Character Identity

#13.1 Player-facing goal

P19 is not "add a save file" or "make death respawn." It is the phase where the player's character becomes a long-term identity that can survive:

  • character creation;
  • sessions and patch versions;
  • death and recovery;
  • respec;
  • world cycle changes;
  • future class/origin changes;
  • eventual platform/user save rules;
  • eventual multiplayer discipline.

The player promise is simple:

My character is mine.
The game remembers what should matter.
Death has drama without corrupting that memory.
Respec and NG+ are explicit choices, not accidental side effects.

Everything in this chapter exists to protect that promise.

#13.2 Current Ascentia state

The MVP already has a useful native progression/save slice:

  • UAscentiaProgressionComponent tracks:
  • CharacterLevel;
  • Stones;
  • UnspentStatPoints;
  • per-primary-stat investment counts;
  • stat investment/refund telemetry;
  • threshold passive tags and tier telemetry.
  • UAscentiaSaveGame currently stores:
  • SaveVersion;
  • PlayerDisplayName;
  • SavedAtUtc;
  • level, stones, and unspent points;
  • inventory item stacks;
  • equipment entries;
  • world tier state;
  • map discovery state;
  • completed POI tags and resettable loot claims.
  • UAscentiaSaveSubsystem can:
  • build a save object from a character;
  • apply a save object to a character;
  • expose three MVP slot summaries;
  • save and load through UGameplayStatics::SaveGameToSlot and

LoadGameFromSlot.

  • AAscentiaCampsiteActor currently:
  • restores health, Breath, Eth, and potion charges;
  • saves the active slot;
  • asks the game mode to reset the MVP encounter.
  • AAscentiaGameMode handles delayed player respawn and re-applies active save

data when the active slot exists.

The current gaps are important:

  • per-stat investment counts are native but not yet persisted;
  • active threshold passive state is derivable but the source facts that produce

it must be saved;

  • there is no stable character GUID;
  • there is no explicit character creation identity;
  • there is no appearance/custom-character save payload;
  • there is no origin/class/archetype data contract beyond a placeholder

ArchetypeTag;

  • there is no death penalty state;
  • there is no corpse-run or recoverable-currency state;
  • there is no last-safe respawn anchor contract;
  • there is no save migration path;
  • there is no per-map/player transform header;
  • there is no async save/load pipeline;
  • there is no save compatibility validation report;
  • placed-world actor state is not yet saved through the stable world-actor

identity planned in section 8.8.

That means the current save is an MVP checkpoint, not a production character identity system.

#13.3 Production save principles

P19 must adopt these rules before content volume grows:

  1. Save source facts, not display output.
  2. Save stable identifiers, not actor pointers.
  3. Save versioned structs, not loose fields spread across code.
  4. Save local-player identity and slot identity together.
  5. Save enough header data to render a slot without loading the whole world.
  6. Derive transient state on load.
  7. Migrate old saves deliberately.
  8. Treat save/load as asynchronous at production scale.
  9. Treat death as a state transition, not a save corruption event.
  10. Treat character creation as identity data plus presentation data, not as

"the pawn class."

The distinction between source facts and derived state matters. For example, active threshold passive tags can be recomputed from invested stat counts and passive rules. The save should persist invested stat counts and the passive-rule version; load should recompute the active tags and report migration differences.

#13.4 Save schema target

P19 should replace the flat MVP save with explicit nested structs:

UAscentiaSaveGame
  Header: FAscentiaSaveHeader
  CharacterIdentity: FAscentiaCharacterIdentityState
  CharacterProgression: FAscentiaCharacterProgressionState
  CharacterAppearance: FAscentiaCharacterAppearanceState
  Inventory: FAscentiaInventorySaveState
  Equipment: FAscentiaEquipmentSaveState
  Resources: FAscentiaResourceSaveState
  World: FAscentiaWorldSaveState
  Death: FAscentiaDeathLoopState
  Cycle: FAscentiaWorldCycleState
  SettingsSnapshot: optional compatibility metadata only

Header:

FAscentiaSaveHeader
  SaveVersion
  BuildVersion
  CreatedAtUtc
  SavedAtUtc
  SlotName
  SlotIndex
  LocalUserIndex
  CharacterGuid
  CharacterDisplayName
  CharacterLevel
  PlayTimeSeconds
  MapAssetTag
  WorldZoneTag
  LastSafeAnchorTag
  LastKnownTransform
  HighestUnlockedWorldTier
  bHasRecoverableDeathState
  SummaryRightHandItemTag
  SummaryIconOrThumbnail

Character identity:

FAscentiaCharacterIdentityState
  CharacterGuid
  DisplayName
  OriginTag
  ClassTag
  ArchetypeTag
  BodyPresetTag
  VoiceTag
  PronounTag
  CreationVersion
  CreatedAtUtc

Appearance:

FAscentiaCharacterAppearanceState
  BodyPresetTag
  HeadPresetTag
  HairPresetTag
  SkinToneId
  HairColorId
  EyeColorId
  MorphSliderValues
  CosmeticEquipmentOverrides

This is where the placeholder AssassinGirl decision connects to the future. AssassinGirl should remain an avatar preset or temporary visual override. The save contract should never assume that a production character is "the AssassinGirl pawn." The pawn is a runtime vehicle; the character identity is data.

Progression:

FAscentiaCharacterProgressionState
  CharacterLevel
  Stones
  UnspentStatPoints
  InvestedConstitution
  InvestedEndurance
  InvestedFocus
  InvestedStrength
  InvestedAgility
  InvestedIntelligence
  PassiveRuleVersion
  ExplicitUnlockedPassiveTags
  KnownAbilitySetIds
  KnownSpellIds
  RespecCount
  LastRespecAtUtc

ExplicitUnlockedPassiveTags should be used only for passives that are not derivable from stat thresholds. Threshold passives should be recomputed from invested stats on load.

World:

FAscentiaWorldSaveState
  WorldTierState
  MapDiscoveryState
  CompletedPOIs
  ResettableLootClaims
  WorldActorStates
  ObjectiveState
  FastTravelState
  CampsiteState

WorldActorStates should use the stable identity pattern from section 8.8:

WorldActorId
ActorClassTag
WorldZoneTag
DataLayerTag
SavePolicy
PayloadVersion
PayloadBytesOrStruct

No save payload should contain raw object pointers, display names as identity, or location-only identity for placed actors.

Save schema profile:

PDA_SaveSchemaProfile
  CurrentSaveVersion
  MinimumReadableVersion
  SlotHeaderPolicy
  MigrationChain
  SaveFragmentPolicies
  DeprecatedFieldPolicies
  ContentRedirectManifest
  ValidationProfile

Save fragment header:

FAscentiaSaveFragmentHeader
  FragmentTag
  FragmentVersion
  OwnerSystemTag
  ContentSchemaVersion
  CreatedByBuildVersion
  PayloadHash

Save validation result:

FAscentiaSaveValidationResult
  SlotName
  SaveVersion
  CharacterGuid
  HeaderValid
  FragmentResults
  MissingContentTags
  DeprecatedContentTags
  DefaultedFields
  BlockingErrors
  RecoverableWarnings

Slot summary:

FAscentiaSaveSlotSummary
  SlotName
  SlotIndex
  LocalUserIndex
  CharacterGuid
  CharacterDisplayName
  OriginTag
  ClassTag
  CharacterLevel
  PlayTimeSeconds
  SavedAtUtc
  MapAssetTag
  WorldZoneTag
  SummaryEquipmentTags
  bHasRecoverableDeathState
  bRequiresMigration
  bIsCorruptOrUnreadable

Slot summaries are UI-facing and cheap to load. The full save payload is gameplay-facing and may require migration, validation, content resolution, and world load readiness.

#13.5 Save ownership and lifecycle

Production save ownership should be:

UAscentiaSaveSubsystem
  owns save slot discovery, async IO, schema migration, save validation

UAscentiaCharacterSaveFacade
  gathers character-owned save fragments from PlayerState/components

UAscentiaWorldSaveFacade
  gathers world-owned save fragments from subsystems and saveable actors

UAscentiaSaveGame
  stores data only; no gameplay logic

At save time:

  1. Pause save-sensitive mutation or take a snapshot lock.
  2. Gather character identity/progression/inventory/equipment/resource state.
  3. Gather world state from world subsystems and saveable actor components.
  4. Validate the in-memory save object.
  5. Write through async save for production paths.
  6. Emit slot header cache and telemetry.
  7. Notify UI only after success/failure is known.

At load time:

  1. Load the slot by explicit slot and local user.
  2. Validate SaveVersion.
  3. Migrate through every intermediate version.
  4. Validate migrated data.
  5. Spawn or possess the correct runtime pawn class for the current build.
  6. Apply identity/appearance before presentation begins.
  7. Apply progression/inventory/equipment.
  8. Recompute derived attributes, passives, abilities, and HUD view models.
  9. Apply world state after the target map/zone is loaded.
  10. Restore resources according to load reason.

Load reason matters:

EAscentiaLoadReason
  MainMenuLoad
  CampsiteRest
  PlayerDeathRespawn
  SeamlessTravel
  DebugCommand
  Automation

A death respawn may restore resources and place the player at an anchor while preserving a recoverable death state. A main-menu load should restore the exact last persistent state. A debug load may skip presentation.

Async request/result:

FAscentiaSaveRequest
  RequestId
  SlotName
  LocalUserIndex
  SaveReason
  SavePolicyTag
  bWriteSlotSummary
  bValidateBeforeWrite
  bBlockSaveSensitiveMutation

FAscentiaSaveResult
  RequestId
  SlotName
  bSucceeded
  FailureReasonTag
  ValidationResult
  WroteSlotSummary
  DurationMs
  EvidenceId

FAscentiaLoadRequest
  RequestId
  SlotName
  LocalUserIndex
  LoadReason
  TargetMapPolicy
  bAllowMigration
  bValidateAfterMigration

FAscentiaLoadResult
  RequestId
  SlotName
  bSucceeded
  FailureReasonTag
  LoadedSaveVersion
  MigratedToVersion
  ValidationResult
  AppliedFragments
  DurationMs
  EvidenceId

Dirty-state routing:

FAscentiaSaveDirtyEvent
  DirtyTag
  SourceSystemTag
  SourceObjectGuidOrActorId
  SaveReasonHint
  RequiresImmediateWrite
  CoalescingWindowSeconds

Rules:

  • inventory, equipment, death state, world-state changes, and character creation

all emit dirty events instead of calling save directly;

  • campsites and shell UI request saves through UAscentiaSaveSubsystem;
  • autosave policy should be data-authored and visible in EUW_AscentiaSaveLab;
  • save-sensitive transactions should either finish before snapshot or be

rejected while a snapshot lock is active;

  • load failure needs a user-facing error path owned by P21 shell, but P19 owns

the failure tags and validation details.

#13.6 Death loop contract

Ascentia should make the P19 death-loop choice explicit. The recommended default for the current Soulslike/ARPG direction is:

One recoverable dropped-currency state at a time.
Death drops recoverable Stones at the death location or a nearby safe proxy.
A second unrecovered death consumes the previous recoverable state.
Permanent progression, inventory, map discovery, and world unlocks remain.
Respawn returns to the last safe anchor.

Death state:

FAscentiaDeathLoopState
  bHasRecoverableDeathState
  LostCurrencyAmount
  CurrencyTag
  DeathWorldActorId
  DeathMapAssetTag
  DeathWorldZoneTag
  DeathTransform
  RecoveryProxyTransform
  LastSafeAnchorTag
  KillerTag
  DeathTimeUtc
  RecoveryPolicyTag
  DeathSequenceNumber

Runtime actors:

BP_AscentiaDeathEcho
  visual marker / interactable pickup / minimap ping source

UAscentiaDeathLoopSubsystem
  creates, saves, restores, replaces, and consumes death echo state

PDA_DeathPenaltyProfile_*
  authoring asset for dropped resource, percentage, caps, boss-room behavior,
  unreachable-location repair, and UI/audio presentation

Death lifecycle:

  1. Combat determines death through health/attribute authority.
  2. Pawn latches death once.
  3. Gameplay input is blocked.
  4. Death presentation begins.
  5. Death loop subsystem calculates penalty.
  6. Existing unrecovered death echo is resolved according to policy.
  7. New death state is saved before respawn if the design requires persistence

through quit.

  1. Player respawns at the last safe anchor.
  2. Campsite/anchor restore policy restores health/resources.
  3. World encounter reset policy runs.
  4. UI tells the player what was lost and where recovery is.

Recovery lifecycle:

  1. Player reaches the death echo.
  2. Interact or proximity policy validates ownership.
  3. Stones are restored.
  4. Death state is cleared.
  5. Save is updated.
  6. Presentation confirms recovery.

Unreachable death protection:

  • if death happened in a kill volume, deep fall, boss transition, streamed-out

proxy, or nav-invalid area, place the echo at a recovery proxy:

  • last valid floor;
  • boss fog entrance;
  • last safe anchor;
  • nearest authored BP_AscentiaDeathEchoProxy.

This must be deterministic and logged. "The player lost currency because the marker spawned under the world" is a production failure, not a tuning issue.

Death echo placement:

FAscentiaDeathEchoPlacementRequest
  CharacterGuid
  DeathTransform
  DeathMapAssetTag
  DeathWorldZoneTag
  LastSafeAnchorTag
  KillerTag
  DeathCauseTag
  BossArenaTag
  NavProjectionPolicy
  ProxyPolicy

FAscentiaDeathEchoPlacementResult
  PlacementTransform
  PlacementPolicyTag
  UsedProxyActorId
  UsedLastSafeAnchor
  NavProjectionSucceeded
  StreamingSafe
  FailureReasonTag
  DebugTrace

Death-loop report:

FAscentiaDeathLoopReport
  RunId
  ScenarioTag
  DeathStateBefore
  PlacementResult
  RespawnAnchor
  EncounterResetResults
  RecoveryResult
  SecondDeathResult
  SaveLoadResult
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

EUW_AscentiaDeathLoopSimulator should let Designers drop sample death points in a map and preview where the echo will land. This catches ledge, stair, boss fog, streamed-out, and kill-volume problems before a playtest loses currency in an unfair place.

#13.7 Respec contract

Respec is a progression system, not a debug refund button.

Data:

PDA_RespecProfile_*
  CurrencyTag
  BaseCost
  CostCurveByLevel
  CostCurveByRespecCount
  MinimumAnchorTag
  AllowedStats
  bRefundThresholdPassives
  bRefundSkillUnlocks
  bRequireConfirmation

UI:

WBP_StatAllocationScreen
  current values
  preview values
  derived stat deltas
  passive breakpoints gained/lost
  cost
  confirmation

Native flow:

  1. Build a preview transaction.
  2. Validate currency and rules.
  3. Show derived deltas and passive changes.
  4. Confirm.
  5. Spend currency.
  6. Apply stat changes.
  7. Recompute attributes/passives/ability grants.
  8. Save source facts.
  9. Emit telemetry and presentation.

Do not let UI call six independent stat mutations and hope the result is valid. Use a transaction object:

FAscentiaStatRespecTransaction
  RequestedRefunds
  RequestedInvestments
  Cost
  ResultingInvestedStats
  ResultingUnspentPoints
  GainedPassiveTags
  LostPassiveTags
  ValidationErrors

Respec preview result:

FAscentiaRespecPreviewResult
  ProfileTag
  RequestedTransaction
  CurrentStats
  PreviewStats
  CurrentDerivedAttributes
  PreviewDerivedAttributes
  GainedPassiveTags
  LostPassiveTags
  RemovedAbilityTags
  GrantedAbilityTags
  CostBreakdown
  FailureReasons
  ConfirmationPolicy

Respec report:

FAscentiaRespecReport
  RunId
  ProfileTag
  ScenarioTag
  PreviewResult
  CommitResult
  SaveResult
  ReloadVerification
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

EUW_AscentiaRespecPlanner should share math with the P13 build planner. Respec is where build math, item requirements, stat thresholds, ability grants, and save facts meet; if it is only a stat refund screen, it will break the first time a passive or item requirement has consequences.

#13.8 Character creation contract

Character creation must create data before it creates a pawn.

Data:

PDA_Origin_*
  OriginTag
  DisplayName
  DescriptionTextKey
  StartingStatAdjustments
  StartingItemSetId
  StartingAbilitySetId
  IntroSpawnTag
  TutorialFlags

PDA_ClassArchetype_*
  ClassTag
  DisplayName
  StartingAttributeSpread
  StartingAbilitySetId
  RecommendedWeaponTags
  PresentationPoseSet

PDA_AvatarPreset_*
  BodyPresetTag
  SkeletalMesh
  AnimRetargetProfile
  GroomOptions
  DefaultMaterials
  PhysicsAsset

Creation flow:

  1. Player selects origin/class/appearance.
  2. UI creates a preview-only FAscentiaCharacterCreationDraft.
  3. Validation checks required tags, assets, and starting inventory.
  4. Confirm creates a CharacterGuid.
  5. Save slot is initialized with identity, appearance, progression, inventory,

and starting world state.

  1. The runtime pawn is spawned from current platform/game-mode rules.
  2. Appearance is applied from saved identity.

The custom character creator should not author gameplay by swapping pawn classes. It should author a character identity that the current player pawn can render and that future multiplayer can replicate safely.

Creation validation:

PDA_CharacterCreationRules
  AllowedOriginTags
  AllowedClassTags
  AllowedAvatarPresetTags
  NameValidationPolicy
  StartingWorldStateProfile
  StartingInventoryPolicy
  StartingAbilityPolicy
  SaveSlotPolicy
  PreviewMap

Starting loadout:

PDA_StartingLoadoutProfile
  LoadoutTag
  OriginTags
  ClassTags
  StartingItems
  StartingEquipment
  StartingAbilities
  StartingSpells
  StartingCurrencies
  TutorialFlags

Character creation validation result:

FAscentiaCharacterCreationValidationResult
  Draft
  NameResult
  OriginResult
  ClassResult
  AvatarResult
  StartingLoadoutResult
  SaveSlotResult
  BlockingErrors
  Warnings

Creator report:

FAscentiaCharacterCreationReport
  RunId
  DraftsTested
  SuccessfulCommits
  FailedCommits
  OriginCoverage
  ClassCoverage
  AvatarCoverage
  StartingLoadoutCoverage
  SaveReloadResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

The creator should be able to create a valid character while the current runtime pawn class remains unchanged. That is the practical test that the system has escaped the placeholder pawn trap.

#13.9 NG+ and world cycle contract

NG+ is a world-cycle transformation over a stable character.

Data:

FAscentiaWorldCycleState
  CycleIndex
  CycleProfileTag
  StartedAtUtc
  RetainedProgressionTags
  ResetWorldStateTags
  ScalingProfileTag

PDA_WorldCycleProfile_*
  EnemyScalingCurve
  LootScalingCurve
  RetainedStateRules
  ResetStateRules
  NewRewards
  NewEnemyRules

Rules:

  • character identity persists;
  • level/stats/inventory policy is explicit;
  • map discovery policy is explicit;
  • boss/POI reset policy is explicit;
  • unlocks that affect build identity should be retained unless design says

otherwise;

  • world-state resets should be tag-driven and auditable.

Verification needs a cycle transition test, not just a level-scaling unit test.

Cycle transition:

FAscentiaWorldCycleTransitionRequest
  CharacterGuid
  CurrentCycleIndex
  TargetCycleProfileTag
  ConfirmedByPlayer
  RetainOverrideTags
  ResetOverrideTags

FAscentiaWorldCycleTransitionResult
  NewCycleState
  RetainedStateTags
  ResetStateTags
  ResetBossTags
  ResetPOITags
  NewRewardsGranted
  SaveResult
  FailureReasonTag

Cycle report:

FAscentiaWorldCycleReport
  RunId
  CycleProfileTag
  RetainResetMatrix
  BossResetResults
  POIResetResults
  LootScalingResults
  EnemyScalingResults
  SaveReloadResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

Do not implement NG+ until the first zone, boss roster, and reward economy are stable enough to make reset/retain rules meaningful. The data contract can exist early; the feature should not quietly become a debug map reset button.

#13.10 Designer surfaces

P19 needs these Editor surfaces:

PDA_DeathPenaltyProfile_*
PDA_DeathEchoPlacementProfile_*
PDA_RespecProfile_*
PDA_Origin_*
PDA_ClassArchetype_*
PDA_AvatarPreset_*
PDA_CharacterCreationRules_*
PDA_StartingLoadoutProfile_*
PDA_SaveSchemaProfile_*
PDA_SaveValidationProfile_*
PDA_SaveMigrationProfile_*
PDA_WorldCycleProfile_*
BP_AscentiaCampsite
BP_AscentiaDeathEcho
BP_AscentiaDeathEchoProxy
WBP_CharacterCreation
WBP_StatAllocation
WBP_DeathRecoveryNotice
WBP_SaveSlotScreen
WBP_NGPlusPrompt
EUW_AscentiaSaveInspector
EUW_AscentiaSaveLab
EUW_AscentiaSaveFixtureReport
EUW_AscentiaProgressionBalancePreview
EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreationValidator
EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab
EUW_AscentiaAvatarAssemblyPreview
EUW_AscentiaDeathEchoPlacementPreview
EUW_AscentiaDeathLoopSimulator
EUW_AscentiaRespecPlanner
EUW_AscentiaWorldCyclePreview

Designer-facing validation examples:

  • "Origin.GutterSaint grants Item.AshDagger, but that item is missing from the

item registry."

  • "AvatarPreset.AssassinGirlPlaceholder has no compatible physics asset for hit

reactions."

  • "RespecProfile.CampsiteRespec references Currency.Stones but has no maximum

cost cap."

  • "SaveVersion 3 migration does not populate InvestedStrength."
  • "DeathPenaltyProfile.Soulslike requires a death echo class but none is set."
  • "StartingLoadout.Rogue grants Item.AshDagger but the item is not allowed for

the selected class."

  • "SaveSchemaProfile.Current has no migration path from the earliest supported

version."

#13.11 Modular character and avatar architecture

The production character creator should not grow out of the current VisualOverride costume arrangement. That setup is acceptable as a migration bridge, but the production model needs a character identity layer, an avatar assembly layer, and a runtime pawn layer.

Character identity
  -> saved origin/class/appearance/build identity

Avatar assembly
  -> modular body parts, materials, grooms, physics, animation compatibility

Runtime pawn
  -> movement, GAS, combat, camera, inventory, interaction, replication

Unreal's modular-character options imply three Ascentia tiers:

  • Leader Pose Component: quickest path for interchangeable body/clothing pieces,

good for the first creator because the main body drives animation;

  • Copy Pose from Mesh: useful when a part needs its own physics or animation

behavior, but heavier;

  • Skeletal Mesh Merge: useful for optimized final assembled characters, but

costs setup complexity and limits morph-target flexibility.

Ascentia recommendation:

  1. Use a leader-pose modular Blueprint for creator iteration and armor/body part

swaps.

  1. Keep Copy Pose available for hair, cloth, physics-heavy parts, or exceptional

armor.

  1. Add a mesh-merge bake path later for performance-critical crowds, companions,

or final player-body optimization.

Data:

PDA_AvatarPreset
  AvatarPresetTag
  DisplayName
  BaseSkeleton
  LeaderMesh
  DefaultBodyPartSet
  DefaultMaterialPalette
  AnimRetargetProfile
  PhysicsProfile
  CollisionProfile
  CameraProbePolicy
  PreviewPoseSet
  ValidationNotes

PDA_ModularBodyPartSet
  BodySetTag
  RequiredSkeleton
  HeadMeshes
  TorsoMeshes
  ArmMeshes
  LegMeshes
  HandMeshes
  FootMeshes
  HairGrooms
  ClothPieces
  CompatibleMaterialPalettes
  ExclusionRules

PDA_AppearanceMaterialPalette
  PaletteTag
  SkinToneOptions
  HairColorOptions
  EyeColorOptions
  TattooOptions
  ScarOptions
  MaterialParameterSets

Runtime state:

FAscentiaAvatarAssemblyState
  AvatarPresetTag
  BodyPartSelections
  MaterialPaletteTag
  ScalarMorphValues
  ColorSelections
  CosmeticOverrides
  EquipmentVisualPolicy

Assembly request/result:

FAscentiaAvatarAssemblyRequest
  CharacterGuid
  AvatarPresetTag
  BodyPartSelections
  MaterialPaletteTag
  MorphValues
  EquipmentVisuals
  PreviewOrRuntime
  PerformancePolicy

FAscentiaAvatarAssemblyResult
  AssembledComponents
  LeaderPoseAssignments
  CopyPoseAssignments
  MeshMergeCandidate
  AppliedMaterials
  AppliedMorphs
  PhysicsAssets
  SocketCompatibility
  CameraProbeResult
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

Compatibility report:

FAscentiaAvatarCompatibilityReport
  RunId
  AvatarPresetTag
  BodyPartSetTag
  SkeletonMatch
  RetargetProfileMatch
  PhysicsCompatibility
  SocketCompatibility
  EquipmentOverlayResults
  AnimationPreviewResults
  CameraProbeFindings
  PerformanceEstimate

Designer rules:

  • AssassinGirl becomes PDA_AvatarPreset_AssassinGirlPlaceholder, not a hidden

hard dependency.

  • Every body part declares skeleton, physics, socket, material, and animation

compatibility.

  • Character creator preview uses the same assembly path as runtime.
  • Save data stores tags and parameter values, not direct mesh pointers.
  • Equipment visuals and body customization must share the same socket/profile

vocabulary used by P14 weapon and armor presentation.

  • EUW_AscentiaAvatarAssemblyPreview should test the same avatar against idle,

locomotion, dodge, light attack, spell cast, death, revive, inventory preview, and dialogue pose sets.

  • Avatar assembly reports should flag invalid physics bodies, missing sockets,

camera intersection risk, retarget mismatch, and body-part material parameter drift.

#13.12 Character creation authoring flow

Character creation should be authored as a transaction with validation.

Draft state:

FAscentiaCharacterCreationDraft
  DisplayName
  OriginTag
  ClassTag
  AvatarAssemblyState
  StartingStatPreview
  StartingInventoryPreview
  StartingAbilityPreview
  ValidationErrors

Confirm transaction:

FAscentiaCharacterCreationCommit
  NewCharacterGuid
  SlotName
  IdentityState
  AppearanceState
  ProgressionState
  InventoryState
  EquipmentState
  WorldStartState
  CreatedAtUtc

Creation UI:

WBP_AscentiaCharacterCreation
  -> WBP_AscentiaOriginSelect
  -> WBP_AscentiaClassSelect
  -> WBP_AscentiaAppearanceEditor
  -> WBP_AscentiaStartingLoadoutPreview
  -> WBP_AscentiaCreationConfirm

Editor tooling:

  • EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab previews origins, classes, avatar presets,

starting loadouts, and save payload.

  • EUW_AscentiaAvatarAssemblyPreview tests body-part combinations and equipment

overlays.

  • EUW_AscentiaAppearancePresetValidator catches missing skeleton, invalid

material parameter, missing groom binding, physics asset mismatch, and camera probe occlusion risk.

Proof must include both Designer-preview and runtime-pawn paths. A creator that only renders in UI but spawns the wrong pawn is not done.

Authoring report:

FAscentiaCharacterCreatorAuthoringReport
  RunId
  OriginRowsChecked
  ClassRowsChecked
  AvatarPresetsChecked
  StartingLoadoutsChecked
  AppearanceOptionsChecked
  DraftValidationResults
  CommitResults
  SaveReloadResults
  RuntimeSpawnResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

Evidence path:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/CharacterCreation/{RunId}/report.json

#13.13 Save migration and old-save fixtures

Production save work is incomplete without migration fixtures.

Migration data:

FAscentiaSaveMigrationStep
  FromVersion
  ToVersion
  MigrationTag
  Description
  RequiredBeforeTags
  DeprecatedFields
  AddedFields
  DefaultingPolicy
  ValidationPolicy

Fixture folder:

Tools/save_fixtures/
  v1_mvp_basic.sav.json
  v2_stats_invested.sav.json
  v3_character_identity.sav.json
  v4_death_echo.sav.json

Report:

Saved/Automation/Evidence/save_migration/<timestamp>/report.json
  FixturesLoaded
  FixturesMigrated
  VersionPath
  DefaultedFields
  DeprecatedFieldsRemoved
  ValidationErrors
  RoundTripHash

Rules:

  • every save version change adds at least one fixture;
  • migration must be stepwise, not a pile of "if old version then patch many

things";

  • missing fields get explicit defaulting policy;
  • removed content tags use the content migration manifest from section 23;
  • slot headers are regenerated after migration;
  • migration failures show a user-facing recovery/error path, not only a log.

Migration commandlet/report:

UAscentiaSaveMigrationCommandlet
  InputFixturePath
  OutputReportPath
  TargetVersion
  bRoundTrip
  bWriteMigratedFixture

FAscentiaSaveMigrationReport
  RunId
  TargetVersion
  FixturesLoaded
  FixturesMigrated
  FailedFixtures
  MigrationSteps
  DefaultedFields
  DeprecatedFieldsRemoved
  ContentRedirectsApplied
  RoundTripHashes
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

EUW_AscentiaSaveMigrationLab should call the same migration path as runtime load. A beautiful editor-only migration preview is not proof unless it exercises the real subsystem.

#13.14 Death-loop UX and recovery readability

Death penalty mechanics are only good if the player understands them.

Presentation data:

PDA_DeathPenaltyProfile
  LostCurrencyTag
  LostCurrencyPercent
  MaxLostCurrency
  DropPolicy
  EchoActorClass
  EchoMapMarkerPolicy
  RecoveryInteractionPolicy
  SecondDeathPolicy
  BossArenaProxyPolicy
  DeathMessageText
  RecoveryMessageText
  UIIcon
  CueProfile

UX surfaces:

WBP_AscentiaDeathRecoveryNotice
WBP_AscentiaDeathEchoMarker
WBP_AscentiaCompassDeathEchoIndicator
WBP_AscentiaMapDeathEchoMarker

Rules:

  • after death, the player sees what was lost and where recovery begins;
  • the death echo should appear in map/compass only when that fits Ascentia's

intended discovery language;

  • if the echo is above/below the player, verticality must be readable;
  • in boss arenas, the echo should not force the player through active fog-wall

state in a way that soft-locks recovery;

  • recovery is a short, satisfying confirmation, not an inventory transaction.

The FromSoft rune/souls pattern is useful because it creates tension without destroying permanent character identity: carried currency is at risk, while level, equipment, world progression, and learned knowledge remain. Ascentia can adapt the same emotional shape while making proxy placement and UI readability more explicit.

Death-loop UX report:

FAscentiaDeathLoopUXReport
  RunId
  Scenarios
  LostCurrencyNoticeShown
  MapMarkerPolicyResults
  CompassMarkerPolicyResults
  VerticalityReadabilityFindings
  BossFogRecoveryFindings
  RecoveryCueResults
  TextKeyFindings

This report belongs with P20 presentation eventually, but P19 owns whether the death-loop facts are exposed. Presentation cannot fix missing facts.

#13.15 P19 tooling and validation matrix

Editor tools:

EUW_AscentiaSaveInspector
  inspect slot headers, nested structs, version, identity, death state,
  world state, inventory, and migration status

EUW_AscentiaSaveLab
  run save/load scenarios, inspect dirty events, compare slot summaries, and
  open evidence from the latest proof

EUW_AscentiaSaveMigrationLab
  run fixtures, show defaulted fields, compare pre/post save summaries

EUW_AscentiaSaveFixtureReport
  list fixtures by version, expected migration path, round-trip hash, and
  blocking validation failures

EUW_AscentiaDeathLoopSimulator
  simulate death location, proxy placement, second-death policy, recovery

EUW_AscentiaDeathEchoPlacementPreview
  preview proxy placement in the selected map for falls, boss arenas, kill
  volumes, and streamed-out areas

EUW_AscentiaRespecPlanner
  preview stat transactions, passive deltas, costs, and save result

EUW_AscentiaWorldCyclePreview
  preview NG+ retained/reset tags, scaling, rewards, and boss/POI resets

EUW_AscentiaCharacterCreatorLab
  preview origin/class/avatar/loadout combinations and commit draft save payloads

EUW_AscentiaAvatarAssemblyPreview
  test body parts, materials, grooms, physics assets, retarget profile,
  equipment overlays, camera probes, and animation pose sets

Validators:

VLD_Ascentia_SaveSchema
VLD_Ascentia_SaveMigrations
VLD_Ascentia_SaveValidationProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DeathPenaltyProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DeathEchoPlacementProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_RespecProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_Origins
VLD_Ascentia_ClassArchetypes
VLD_Ascentia_AvatarPresets
VLD_Ascentia_AvatarAssemblyProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_CharacterCreationRules
VLD_Ascentia_StartingLoadouts
VLD_Ascentia_ModularCharacters
VLD_Ascentia_WorldCycleProfiles

Machine proof:

  • every save fixture migrates;
  • character creation creates a slot;
  • appearance survives save/load;
  • stat investment and threshold passives survive save/load;
  • respec applies as one transaction;
  • death echo persists through quit/reload;
  • second death resolves old echo by policy;
  • NG+ resets and retains the correct tags.

Evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/SaveSchema/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/SaveMigration/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/CharacterCreation/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/AvatarAssembly/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/DeathLoop/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/Respec/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/WorldCycle/{RunId}/report.json

Required report structs:

FAscentiaSaveValidationReport
FAscentiaSaveMigrationReport
FAscentiaCharacterCreationReport
FAscentiaAvatarCompatibilityReport
FAscentiaDeathLoopReport
FAscentiaDeathLoopUXReport
FAscentiaRespecReport
FAscentiaWorldCycleReport

#13.16 P19 proof gates

Minimum proof:

Tools/remote_playtest.py character_creation
  creates a new slot
  applies origin/class/appearance
  reloads and proves identity persisted
  proves runtime pawn uses the saved avatar assembly path

Tools/remote_playtest.py save_schema_v2
  invests stats
  unlocks threshold passives
  saves
  reloads
  proves invested stats and derived passives match

Tools/remote_playtest.py death_loop
  dies with Stones
  respawns at anchor
  proves recoverable echo exists
  proves proxy placement is valid
  recovers Stones
  saves cleared death state

Tools/remote_playtest.py death_loop_replace
  dies twice without recovery
  proves first echo is consumed/replaced by policy

Tools/remote_playtest.py respec
  previews stat refund/investment
  confirms transaction
  proves attributes/passives/save state update

Tools/remote_playtest.py ng_plus_cycle
  completes cycle gate
  starts next cycle
  proves retained and reset state policies

Editor validation:

VLD_Ascentia_SaveSchema
VLD_Ascentia_SaveMigrations
VLD_Ascentia_SaveValidationProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_Origins
VLD_Ascentia_ClassArchetypes
VLD_Ascentia_AvatarPresets
VLD_Ascentia_AvatarAssemblyProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_CharacterCreationRules
VLD_Ascentia_StartingLoadouts
VLD_Ascentia_ModularCharacters
VLD_Ascentia_DeathEchoPlacementProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_DeathPenaltyProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_RespecProfiles
VLD_Ascentia_WorldCycleProfiles

The P19 "done" claim requires at least one old save fixture migrated through the current migration path. A save system without old-save fixtures is unproven.

Remote proof IDs:

save_slot_summary
save_async_roundtrip
save_migration_fixture
character_creation_commit
avatar_assembly_runtime
death_echo_proxy
death_loop_recovery
death_loop_replace
respec_transaction
world_cycle_transition

#13.17 P19 starter implementation package

P19 should begin by turning the current MVP save checkpoint into a versioned character-identity system. The first slice should not try to build the whole character creator or NG+ loop. It should prove that Ascentia can create one stable character identity, write and load it asynchronously, migrate at least one old fixture, assemble the runtime avatar through data, and preserve death-loop facts through save/load.

Reference posture:

  • Use USaveGame as the serialized payload, but keep gameplay logic in

subsystems/facades. A save object should be data, not a gameplay service.

  • Prefer async save/load requests for production paths. Blocking save/load is

acceptable for narrow debug tools and tiny MVP tests, but not as the long-term player-facing path.

  • Always pair slot name with local user identity. Even if the first SKU is

single-player PC, the schema should not assume that "slot 1" means one global human forever.

  • Save stable tags, GUIDs, versions, and source facts. Recompute attributes,

passives, UI view models, and presentation state after load.

  • Treat modular avatar assembly as data-driven presentation over a saved

character identity. AssassinGirl is a preset/placeholder, not the save schema.

  • Use Leader Pose or Copy Pose for early modular proofs, but record when a preset

should become a merged skeletal mesh path for performance, morph, physics, or equipment-overlay reasons.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Save/AscentiaSaveSubsystem.h

Tests and proof helpers:

Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Save/AscentiaSaveSchemaTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Save/AscentiaSaveMigrationTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Save/AscentiaAsyncSaveRoundtripTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Progression/AscentiaCharacterCreationTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Progression/AscentiaRespecTransactionTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Death/AscentiaDeathLoopTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Characters/AscentiaAvatarAssemblyTests.cpp
Tools/save_fixtures/v1_mvp_basic.sav.json
Tools/save_fixtures/v2_progression_stats.sav.json
Tools/save_fixtures/v3_character_identity.sav.json
Tools/save_fixtures/v4_death_echo.sav.json
Tools/remote_playtest.py scenarios: save_async_roundtrip,
  save_migration_fixture, character_creation_commit, avatar_assembly_runtime,
  death_loop_recovery, death_loop_replace, respec_transaction,
  world_cycle_transition

The first native ownership split:

UAscentiaSaveSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Owns slot discovery, async request queue, snapshot locks, migration,
  validation, slot summaries, evidence, and failure tags.

UAscentiaCharacterSaveFacade : UObject or UGameInstanceSubsystem helper
  Gathers identity, appearance, progression, inventory, equipment, resources,
  spellbook, and build-state fragments from the possessed character path.

UAscentiaWorldSaveFacade : UWorldSubsystem helper
  Gathers world-state fragments, stable world-actor records, objectives,
  discovery, fast travel, encounters, and resettable rewards.

UAscentiaDeathLoopSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  Owns death echo creation, proxy placement, recovery, second-death policy,
  save fragment writes, and death-loop evidence.

UAscentiaAvatarAssemblySubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Resolves saved appearance/identity plus equipment into a runtime avatar
  assembly request. It selects Leader Pose, Copy Pose, or merged mesh policy
  from data rather than hardcoding the current pawn costume setup.

  CosmeticEquipmentOverrides

FAscentiaAvatarAssemblyState
  AvatarPresetTag
  SkeletonTag
  RetargetProfileTag
  AssemblyPolicyTag
  BodyPartTags
  MaterialVariantTags
  GroomTags
  EquipmentOverlayTags
  CompatibilityVersion

The current GASP VisualOverride/costume path should become one assembly policy:

Ascentia.Avatar.AssemblyPolicy.GASPVisualOverride
Ascentia.Avatar.AssemblyPolicy.LeaderPose
Ascentia.Avatar.AssemblyPolicy.CopyPose
Ascentia.Avatar.AssemblyPolicy.MergedSkeletalMesh

This lets the prototype keep working while making it visible in editor data. Designers should be able to see why AssassinGirl is selected, which skeleton and retarget profile it uses, and what policy would be required for a future custom body/outfit set.

Async request queue:

FAscentiaSaveRequest

  bSnapshotLocked
  bAsyncInFlight

UAscentiaSaveSubsystem should serialize requests. Do not allow overlapping load/save operations unless the platform policy says it is safe. The subsystem should emit a clear failure tag if a request is rejected because another operation is active.

The first death-loop proof should include three map points:

  • ordinary floor death;
  • ledge/fall or kill-volume death that requires a proxy;
  • boss-arena death that uses entrance or safe-anchor policy.

P19 starter tickets:

  • P19-001: Add versioned save schema structs, slot header, slot summary, and

PDA_SaveSchemaProfile_MVP.

  • P19-002: Convert UAscentiaSaveSubsystem to a request/result facade with

async save/load support, local user fields, snapshot lock state, and dirty event routing while preserving current campsite save/load behavior.

  • P19-003: Add migration steps, fixture folder, AscentiaSaveMigration

commandlet, and one MVP v1 fixture migration proof.

  • P19-004: Add character identity, appearance, avatar assembly state, first

origin/class/avatar/loadout assets, and CharacterCreation_Commit_NewSlot proof.

  • P19-005: Add UAscentiaAvatarAssemblySubsystem, prototype AssassinGirl avatar

preset, GASP VisualOverride assembly policy report, and runtime pawn proof.

  • P19-006: Add death-loop subsystem, dropped-stones profile, death echo actor,

proxy placement request/result, ordinary death recovery proof, and save/reload death-state proof.

  • P19-007: Add second-death policy, kill-volume/boss-arena proxy proofs, and

EUW_AscentiaDeathLoopSimulator.

  • P19-008: Add respec request/preview/commit transaction, campsite profile, and

passive recompute proof.

  • P19-009: Add world-cycle profile preview, retained/reset state report, and

first NG+ policy proof.

  • P19-010: Wire SaveLab, migration lab, avatar preview, death-loop simulator,

reports, evidence folders, dashboard cards, and package gates.

Recommended implementation order:

  1. P19-001 through P19-003 first. Schema, async request routing, and migration

fixtures are the backbone; character creator work should not pile onto the flat MVP save.

  1. P19-004 and P19-005 next. These separate saved character identity from the

current GASP/AssassinGirl presentation path without breaking the prototype.

  1. P19-006 and P19-007 then make death meaningful and persistent, with proxy

placement proof before tuning punishment severity.

  1. P19-008 should happen after source stat facts are safely saved and migrated.
  2. P19-009 is last because NG+ is a policy layer over world, content, death,

rewards, and progression.

  1. P19-010 closes the loop with Designer tools and evidence.

#13.18 Character creator cosmetics, grooms, cloth, physics, and portrait package

This package is the production acceptance layer for creator cosmetics. Section 13.17 owns save identity and avatar assembly; this section owns whether body presets, morphs, modular armor, hair, cloth, physics assets, portraits, and cosmetic overlays are shippable content that Designers can inspect and fix.

The goal is not to rebuild character creation twice. It is to prevent the future custom creator from becoming the same kind of hidden dependency problem that already hit animation compression settings, attack notifies, weapon sockets, and the GASP costume path.

Engine-facing posture:

  • Modular characters should keep the section 13.17 assembly tiers: Leader Pose

for the first low-risk creator proof, Copy Pose where a child mesh needs additional bones or physics behavior, and merged skeletal meshes for later performance or morph-heavy outfits. The selected tier must be authored data, not an invisible Blueprint convention.

  • Hair should use explicit Groom profiles. A hair option is not accepted until

it has a binding policy, material/color policy, LOD/scalability policy, simulation toggle, close-camera preview, and a fallback decision for platforms or scalability levels where strands are too expensive.

  • Simulated garments should use explicit cloth profiles. A cloth item is not

accepted until its Chaos Cloth source, collision body coverage, budget, scalability behavior, and combat-readability verdict are visible.

  • PhysicsAsset coverage is part of creator acceptance, not a late combat bug.

Missing root/body coverage, invalid bodies, broken constraints, or collision disabled on playable avatar bodies should block promotion.

  • Portraits and icons must be generated from the same resolved avatar cosmetic

state used at runtime. A portrait built from a hand-assembled preview pawn is evidence debt.

Save-state extension:

FAscentiaAvatarCosmeticState
  BodyPresetTag
  MorphValues
  MaterialPaletteTag
  CosmeticPartTags
  GroomTags
  ClothTags
  TattooScarMakeupTags
  PortraitId
  Version

The save stores stable tags and slider values. It should not store direct mesh, Groom, cloth, material, or portrait asset pointers. Runtime assembly resolves the current content from those tags, then writes evidence if the content no longer matches the saved state.

Acceptance rules:

  • Every creator part has skeleton, retarget, physics, socket, material, and

save-tag compatibility.

  • Every body preset has a morph profile with ranges, defaults, clamp policy,

and at least one animation/locomotion preview.

  • Every groom has binding, LOD/scalability, color/material parameters,

simulation policy, close-camera preview, and fallback policy.

  • Every cloth item has a source asset, simulation policy, PhysicsAsset collision

compatibility, budget, scalability behavior, and combat-readability verdict.

  • Every playable avatar PhysicsAsset has root/body coverage for collisions,

hit reactions, cloth collision, and root-motion floor checks. Invalid bodies block promotion.

  • Tattoos, scars, makeup, skin details, and body paint use parameterized

material layers or approved overlays. Do not create one-off material duplicates for every appearance choice.

  • Equipment overlays resolve clipping through compatibility rules, hide masks,

or part replacement policy. Clipping should not be a "Designer will remember" problem.

  • Portrait capture uses the same avatar cosmetic state and assembly subsystem

as runtime. A portrait proof should include save/load roundtrip, not just a render target screenshot.

  • Cosmetic display names, slot names, and unlock text use stable localization

keys. Creator data should not bake player-facing text into asset names.

Designer tools:

EUW_AscentiaAvatarCosmeticBoard
EUW_AscentiaBodyMorphLab
EUW_AscentiaGroomFitLab
EUW_AscentiaClothFitLab
EUW_AscentiaPhysicsAssetBodyDoctor
EUW_AscentiaPortraitCaptureLab
EUW_AscentiaCosmeticCompatibilityMatrix

EUW_AscentiaAvatarCosmeticBoard is the owner-facing production board. It lists every creator part, current promotion state, missing dependencies, save-tag stability, assembly policy, budget cost, and the last proof run.

EUW_AscentiaBodyMorphLab previews body presets, morph sliders, armor overlays, weapon sockets, locomotion, attacks, dodge, stairs, and ledge cases. It should show clipping verdicts and morph channels that exceed accepted ranges.

EUW_AscentiaGroomFitLab previews each hair option on every accepted head/body profile, toggles simulation/scalability, captures close-camera screenshots, and reports missing bindings or fallback debt.

EUW_AscentiaClothFitLab previews garment simulation against locomotion, attacks, dodge, stairs, ledges, hit reactions, and camera distances. It should show cloth budget and collision body dependencies.

EUW_AscentiaPhysicsAssetBodyDoctor scans PhysicsAssets for missing root/body coverage, disabled collision, invalid bodies, suspect constraints, and cloth or hit-reaction body gaps. This is the direct prevention tool for the "Invalid Bodies" warning class already seen in PIE.

EUW_AscentiaPortraitCaptureLab assembles a runtime avatar from a saved cosmetic state, captures portraits/icons through approved profiles, writes output assets, and records the resolved avatar hash.

Commandlets:

AscentiaAvatarCosmeticAudit
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Avatar/Cosmetics/PDA_AvatarCosmeticProfile_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/AvatarCosmetics

AscentiaGroomAudit
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Avatar/Grooms/PDA_GroomProfile_ShortHair_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/Grooms

  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/Cloth

  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/PhysicsAssets

AscentiaPortraitCaptureAudit

  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P19/Portraits

P19C starter tickets:

  • P19C-001: Add cosmetic state/profile structs, profile assets, and save-state

extension using stable tags and versioned slider values.

  • P19C-002: Add body preset and morph-channel validation, EUW_AscentiaBodyMorphLab,

and BodyMorph_MinMax_NoClipping proof.

  • P19C-003: Add Groom profile validation, EUW_AscentiaGroomFitLab, binding

audit, scalability/fallback audit, and default hair proof.

  • P19C-004: Add cloth simulation profile validation, EUW_AscentiaClothFitLab,

collision/budget audit, and combat-readability proof.

  • P19C-005: Add PhysicsAsset root/body/constraint validation,

EUW_AscentiaPhysicsAssetBodyDoctor, and package gate for invalid playable avatar bodies.

  • P19C-006: Add cosmetic compatibility matrix, hide-mask/replacement policy,

and armor overlay proof across the first body preset.

  • P19C-007: Add portrait capture request/result path,

EUW_AscentiaPortraitCaptureLab, and runtime-avatar portrait proof.

  • P19C-008: Add Avatar Cosmetic Board, commandlets, evidence folders, dashboard

cards, and package gates.

Recommended implementation order:

  1. P19C-001 and P19C-002 first. The creator needs a stable cosmetic payload and

body/morph rules before content volume grows.

  1. P19C-005 should happen early because invalid PhysicsAssets have already

appeared in PIE, and grooms/cloth/combat all depend on trustworthy bodies.

  1. P19C-003 and P19C-004 then prove hair and cloth as explicit production

assets rather than one-off visuals.

  1. P19C-006 connects armor, body, hide masks, sockets, and clipping policy.
  2. P19C-007 and P19C-008 close the loop with portraits, boards, commandlets,

package gates, and dashboard evidence.

#13.19 Campsites, checkpoints, rest/reset, respawn anchors, and world recovery package

The current MVP campsite already saves, restores resources, and resets the arena. Production needs that loop to become a named system. In a Soulslike ARPG, resting is not only a heal button. It is a world transaction: save, refill, reset, respawn, travel, death echo, boss persistence, NPC schedule, music state, map state, and streaming readiness all agree or the world feels arbitrary.

Primary references:

  • Unreal SaveGame documentation recommends asynchronous saving for meaningful

game data because synchronous disk work can hitch and create platform risk: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/saving-and-loading-your-game-in-unreal-engine>

  • The Blueprint async save node documents AsyncSaveGameToSlot and its

completion result: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/BlueprintAPI/SaveGame/AsyncSaveGametoSlot>

  • World Partition streams grid cells from streaming sources such as the player,

which makes respawn, fast travel, Below return, and boss-arena recovery a streaming contract: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/world-partition-in-unreal-engine>

  • Data Layers can organize actors at editor and runtime, so rest/reset policy

must say which layers, actors, and world-state rows change: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/world-partition---data-layers-in-unreal-engine>

  • GAS Gameplay Effects are the right path for resource restoration, flask

refill, temporary buff clearing, rest debuffs, and death penalties: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/gameplay-effects-for-the-gameplay-ability-system-in-unreal-engine>

  • Gameplay Tags remain the shared identity language for checkpoints, rest

policies, respawn anchors, reset groups, and death echo state: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-gameplay-tags-in-unreal-engine>

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Progression/AscentiaRestTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Progression/AscentiaCheckpointTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Progression/AscentiaRespawnTypes.h
