Runtime boundary:

UAscentiaPostLaunchContentSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  QueryInstalledContent()
  ResolveContentReleaseState()
  IsFeatureAvailable(FeatureTag)
  RequestRuntimeContentMount(Request)
  ValidateMountedContent(ReleaseTag)
  QueryPatchCompatibility(SaveHeader, ContentRelease)
  EmitPostLaunchEvidence(Result)

Rules:

  • Core systems should depend on stable Ascentia interfaces, not directly on a

DLC plugin. Optional content plugs into registries, ability sets, item catalogs, encounter definitions, world zones, dialogue tables, and UI routes.

  • Patches cannot mutate released save schema casually. Any save-affecting patch

requires a P19 migration fixture, old-save load proof, new-save proof, and a rollback stance.

  • A patch note claim is a product contract. "Fixed dodge direction" must map to

a regression scenario and evidence path.

  • Balance-only patches should keep payload size small. If a numeric change

drags a huge map or mesh package into the patch, the content layout needs restructuring before release.

  • DLC content uses explicit chunk ownership and feature policy. A new zone,

boss, item set, spell school, or arena should have a release tag, chunk rule, entitlement stance, localization targets, map markers, save fragments, and unload policy.

  • Game Feature plugins may add components and data registries, but core

authority remains in Ascentia native subsystems. Features extend; they do not become a second combat/save/world engine.

  • Runtime mounted content must be hash/version checked before it is trusted.

Failed mounts should produce support evidence and a recoverable UI state.

  • Rollback is designed before the patch ships. If rollback is impossible after

save migration, the patch gate must say so and require backup/support policy.

  • Demo/full compatibility is a post-launch concern too. Demo saves, demo chunks,

and full-game unlock state must agree with P19 save migration and P21 platform policy.

  • Mods/UGC are out of scope until deliberately approved. Do not accidentally

expose mutable runtime loading paths, user script execution, or untrusted pak mounting as a "tooling shortcut."

Post-launch content categories:

Hotfix.CodeOnly
Hotfix.DataOnly
Patch.Balance
Patch.Localization
Patch.Accessibility
Patch.SaveMigration
Patch.Performance
Patch.PlatformServices
ContentUpdate.FreeZone
ContentUpdate.BossPack
ContentUpdate.WeaponPack
DLC.PaidExpansion
DLC.CosmeticPack
DLC.SoundtrackOrExtras
Demo.Update
Rollback.Emergency

Designer tools:

EUW_AscentiaPatchManifestBoard
EUW_AscentiaChunkOwnershipMap
EUW_AscentiaGameFeaturePolicyBoard
EUW_AscentiaPatchCompatibilityGate
EUW_AscentiaRuntimeContentMountLab
EUW_AscentiaPatchNotesClaimVerifier
EUW_AscentiaPatchRollbackSimulator
EUW_AscentiaDLCReadinessChecklist

EUW_AscentiaPatchManifestBoard shows base release, target release, changed packages, changed bytes, change reason tags, patch profile, release channel, save-schema touches, localization touches, platform claim touches, and evidence.

EUW_AscentiaChunkOwnershipMap maps Primary Asset Labels, chunk IDs, asset roots, plugin roots, expected bytes, optional/required state, entitlement tags, and unexpected cross-chunk dependencies.

EUW_AscentiaGameFeaturePolicyBoard reviews each Game Feature plugin for actions, component receivers, data registries, primary assets, save fragments, localization targets, Designer surfaces, and shipping allowance.

EUW_AscentiaPatchCompatibilityGate runs old saves, current saves, cloud-save fixtures, settings profiles, achievement/stat facts, and demo/full fixture states against the new content release profile.

EUW_AscentiaRuntimeContentMountLab simulates mounted content success, missing entitlement, missing chunk, hash mismatch, version mismatch, slow download, cancel, retry, and offline mode.

EUW_AscentiaPatchNotesClaimVerifier maps release notes to evidence rows. It should make vague patch notes uncomfortable.

EUW_AscentiaPatchRollbackSimulator verifies backup presence, save migration stance, content rollback possibility, player-facing messaging, and support bundle policy.

Commandlets:

AscentiaPatchManifestAudit
  -PatchProfile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/PostLaunch/Patches/PDA_PatchProfile_Hotfix_MVP
  -BaseRelease=1.0.0
  -TargetRelease=1.0.1
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/PatchManifests

AscentiaChunkOwnershipAudit
  -ChunkPlan=/Game/Ascentia/Design/PostLaunch/Chunks/PDA_PrimaryAssetChunkPlan_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/ChunkOwnership

AscentiaGameFeatureAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/PostLaunch/GameFeatures/PDA_GameFeaturePolicy_MVP
  -PluginRoot=Plugins/GameFeatures
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/GameFeatures

AscentiaPatchCompatibilityGate
  -Release=/Game/Ascentia/Design/PostLaunch/Releases/PDA_ContentReleaseProfile_MVP
  -SaveFixtures=Tools/save_fixtures
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/PatchCompatibility

AscentiaRuntimeContentMountAudit
  -MountPolicy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/PostLaunch/PDA_RuntimeContentMountPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/RuntimeContentMount

AscentiaPatchNotesClaimAudit
  -ClaimProfile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/PostLaunch/PDA_PatchNotesClaimProfile_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/PatchNotesClaims

AscentiaPatchRollbackAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/PostLaunch/Rollback/PDA_PatchRollbackPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/PatchRollback

Evidence:

P21L starter tickets:

  • P21L-001: Add content release, patch profile, payload record, chunk ownership,

and rollback policy structs plus EUW_AscentiaPatchManifestBoard.

  • P21L-002: Add Primary Asset Label/chunk ownership audit and

EUW_AscentiaChunkOwnershipMap.

  • P21L-003: Add Game Feature policy audit for plugin actions, component

receivers, data registries, primary assets, save fragments, and Designer surfaces.

  • P21L-004: Add patch compatibility gate that reuses P19 save fixtures and P21Q

release evidence.

  • P21L-005: Add runtime content mount lab with ChunkDownloader-compatible

request/result structs but no production dependency on remote hosting yet.

  • P21L-006: Add patch notes claim verifier tied to regression/evidence rows.
  • P21L-007: Add rollback simulator and backup/support policy checks.
  • P21L-008: Add DLC readiness checklist for release tag, chunk plan, save/loc

compatibility, entitlement stance, platform claims, and dashboard blockers.

Recommended implementation order:

  1. P21L-001 first. A content release profile gives every patch and DLC pack a

stable identity.

  1. P21L-002 next, before optional content exists. Chunk mistakes are cheapest

before the first expansion is authored.

  1. P21L-003 should land before Game Feature plugins become a content habit.
  2. P21L-004 protects player trust by proving old saves survive content updates.
  3. P21L-005 can remain a lab until real remote delivery exists.
  4. P21L-006 and P21L-007 make patch notes and rollback honest.
  5. P21L-008 turns DLC readiness into a dashboard gate.

#15.20 Source control, workspace, OFPA, asset locking, review, and team operations package

Ascentia can remain nimble as a solo project for a while, but the architecture needs a production-grade path before the content count and collaborator count rise. Unreal projects fail in very ordinary ways: a designer edits a binary asset without a lock, an OFPA external actor file is not submitted with the level change, a plugin upgrade lands without regression evidence, a Blueprint fix lives only in one person's local workspace, or a branch is merged because the code compiles while cooked content is broken. This package makes those risks visible in the Editor and in dashboard evidence.

The current local OneDrive workspace is acceptable for personal iteration, but it should not become the team authority. Team production needs an explicit source-control provider profile, workspace policy, binary asset lock policy, OFPA changelist validation, review gates, and verified-sync stance.

Reference posture:

  • Epic's team-scaling guidance treats version control as the first production

step before people work on code or assets. Unreal supports Perforce, Git with Git LFS, Subversion, and Diversion; Epic primarily uses Perforce, and its collaboration guidance emphasizes workspaces, review/commit, workstreams, locking, rollback, UGS, build automation, and test automation. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/resources-for-scaling-your-unreal-engine-team

  • Unreal source control exists to coordinate game development and track code and

data changes over time. The Editor exposes checkout, submit, add, and status operations in Content Browser and Editor settings, so Ascentia's team rules should be visible to Designers instead of hidden in scripts only. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/source-control-in-unreal-engine https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-source-control-in-the-unreal-editor

  • Unreal assets and maps are binary files. Epic's Perforce documentation calls

out that .uasset and .umap files cannot be text-merged normally; the Editor locks checked-out files to prevent concurrent edits. Ascentia should treat binary merge attempts as production hazards, not as normal review work. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-perforce-as-source-control-for-unreal-engine

  • One File Per Actor reduces level-file contention by saving actor instances as

external files. Epic specifically warns that partial submits can leave dangling references, recommends submitting OFPA content and actor files from within the Editor, and notes changelist support is Perforce-only. Ascentia's World Partition and Data Layer workflows must validate OFPA submissions. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/one-file-per-actor-in-unreal-engine

  • Unreal Game Sync lets teams pick a project, sync/build working revisions, and

consume verified builds from Perforce-backed workspaces. Ascentia does not need UGS on day one, but the plan should keep a verified-sync lane ready for a team transition. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/unreal-game-sync-client-setup-for-unreal-engine

  • Epic's C++ coding standard is relevant because Unreal code is long-lived,

maintained by people other than its original author, and needs to remain readable across modules and compilers. Ascentia's review gates should include module-boundary, Blueprint exposure, reflection, lifetime, save, replication, and gameplay-authority checks, not just style comments. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/epic-cplusplus-coding-standard-for-unreal-engine

  • Unreal's SourceControl developer APIs expose workspace, changelist, shelve,

status, sync preview, file-status monitor, and move-to-changelist concepts. AscentiaEditor tools can use those APIs to build Designer-visible status boards and commandlet gates. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/API/Developer/SourceControl

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/TeamOps/AscentiaTeamOpsTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/TeamOps/AscentiaSourceRevisionTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/TeamOps/AscentiaBuildIdentityTypes.h

This package is mostly Editor/build-pipeline work. The runtime types exist only for build identity, save compatibility, crash/support bundles, and dashboard records that need to ship with builds.

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/TeamOps/AscentiaWorkspaceAudit.h

/Game/Ascentia/Design/TeamOps/BranchPolicies/

/Game/Ascentia/Editor/TeamOps/

PDA_TeamOpsPolicy_MVP
PDA_SourceControlProviderPolicy_SoloGitLFS_MVP
PDA_SourceControlProviderPolicy_PerforceTeam_MVP
PDA_BranchStreamPolicy_MVP
PDA_ChangelistPolicy_MVP
PDA_OFPAChangelistPolicy_MVP
PDA_AssetLockPolicy_MVP
PDA_CodeReviewPolicy_MVP
PDA_AssetReviewPolicy_MVP
PDA_VerifiedSyncPolicy_MVP

Core data:

FAscentiaSourceControlProviderProfile
  ProviderTag
  ProviderKind: GitLFS, Perforce, SVN, Diversion, None
  IsTeamAuthority
  LockingSupport
  ChangelistSupport
  ShelveSupport
  WorkstreamPolicy
  BinaryAssetPolicy
  RequiredIgnorePatterns
  LfsPatterns
  PerforceTypemap
  RiskNotes

FAscentiaWorkspacePolicy
  WorkspaceId
  LocalRoot
  bAllowsCloudSyncFolders
  MaxPathLengthWarning
  RequiredEngineVersion
  RequiredProjectRoot
  RequiredPlugins
  RequiredEnvVars
  bRequiresShortPath
  bRequiresEditorSourceControlConnected

FAscentiaBranchStreamPolicy
  BranchTag
  Purpose
  ParentBranch
  AllowedContentRoots
  AllowedCodeModules
  RequiredGatesBeforeMerge
  RequiredReviewers
  FreezePolicy
  ReleaseChannel

FAscentiaChangelistPolicy
  ChangelistTag
  RequiredDescriptionTemplate
  AllowedFileKinds
  MaxBinaryAssets
  bRequiresEvidenceLinks
  bRequiresOFPAValidation
  bRequiresReview
  bRequiresPackageGate
  bRequiresDesignerVerdict

FAscentiaOFPAChangelistReport
  Map
  ExternalActorFiles
  LevelFile
  MissingExternalActors
  DanglingExternalActorRefs
  DataLayerRefs
  WorldPartitionCellRefs
  PartialSubmitRisk
  BlockingFindings

FAscentiaAssetLockReport
  AssetPath
  Owner
  LockProvider
  CheckedOutBy
  NotAtHead
  WritableWithoutCheckout
  BinaryMergeRisk
  StaleLockAgeHours
  SuggestedAction

FAscentiaReviewGateResult
  ChangeId
  Author
  ReviewerRows
  CodeReviewState
  AssetReviewState
  BuildGateState
  PackageGateState
  EvidenceLinks
  Waivers
  FinalVerdict

Rules:

  • Solo Git LFS can remain the near-term authority for Ascentia, but the moment

multiple designers or heavy binary collaboration enter the project, the team should deliberately evaluate Perforce plus Editor changelists and verified sync instead of drifting into unlocked binary-file chaos.

  • Binary assets are locked and reviewed as binary assets. A Blueprint, map,

skeleton, animation database, PhysicsAsset, material instance, or DataAsset change needs an owner, evidence, and latest-revision status.

  • OFPA maps must submit external actor files and related level/Data Layer files

together. Partial submits are blockers because they can create dangling world references that do not show up as friendly text conflicts.

  • Cloud-synced local folders can be personal convenience, not production truth.

Workspace Doctor should warn when a team-authority workspace lives under OneDrive, Dropbox, Google Drive, or another sync folder.

  • Changelists and PRs must declare phase/system tags, asset roots touched,

migration/save impact, cook/package impact, Designer-facing changes, evidence paths, reviewer expectations, and rollback risk.

  • Redirectors, stale locks, unchecked-out writable packages, not-at-head binary

assets, missing source-control status, and generated content without source records block merge or require an explicit waiver.

  • Code review checks Epic-style readability, module dependencies, public/private

boundary drift, reflected API surface, Blueprint exposure, UObject lifetime, GC references, async/thread use, save compatibility, replication authority, security-sensitive input, and tests/evidence.

  • Asset review checks provenance, license, folder/naming policy, skeleton and

retarget profile, PhysicsAsset/body coverage, collision, sockets, LOD/Nanite, materials, physical surfaces, Data Layers, HLOD, package/cook warnings, and Designer acceptance.

  • Verified sync records should identify the revision/changelist, build artifact,

smoke-test matrix, known blockers, waivers, and compatible engine/plugin versions. Designers should know which revision is safe without reading CI logs.

Designer tools:

EUW_AscentiaWorkspaceDoctor
EUW_AscentiaChangelistComposer
EUW_AscentiaOFPAChangelistValidator
EUW_AscentiaAssetLockBoard
EUW_AscentiaReviewGateBoard
EUW_AscentiaVerifiedSyncBoard
EUW_AscentiaBranchStreamMap
EUW_AscentiaRedirectorAndMovePlanner

EUW_AscentiaWorkspaceDoctor shows project root, cloud-sync risk, provider connection, engine version, plugin state, path length risk, source-control status, local dirty packages, active editor sessions, and recommended next action.

EUW_AscentiaChangelistComposer helps authors build a clean change: system tags, phase tags, files, binary count, evidence links, review requests, Designer verdict, package impact, save impact, and rollback notes.

EUW_AscentiaOFPAChangelistValidator decodes external actor submissions into human-readable level, actor, Data Layer, World Partition, and dangling-reference rows.

EUW_AscentiaAssetLockBoard exposes checked-out assets, owners, stale locks, not-at-head packages, writable-without-checkout packages, and risky binary merge state.

EUW_AscentiaReviewGateBoard gathers code review, asset review, automation, package, Designer, and release evidence into a single verdict for a change.

EUW_AscentiaVerifiedSyncBoard lists green revisions/build artifacts, smoke status, compatible engine/plugin versions, required DDC notes, and open blockers.

AscentiaWorkspaceAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/TeamOps/WorkspacePolicies/PDA_TeamOpsPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/TeamOps/Workspace

AscentiaChangelistPolicyAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/TeamOps/ChangelistPolicies/PDA_ChangelistPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/TeamOps/Changelists

AscentiaOFPAChangelistAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/TeamOps/ChangelistPolicies/PDA_OFPAChangelistPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/TeamOps/OFPA

AscentiaAssetLockAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/TeamOps/ProviderPolicies/PDA_SourceControlProviderPolicy_PerforceTeam_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/TeamOps/AssetLocks

AscentiaReviewGateAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/TeamOps/ReviewPolicies/PDA_CodeReviewPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/TeamOps/ReviewGates

AscentiaVerifiedSyncAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/TeamOps/PDA_VerifiedSyncPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/TeamOps/VerifiedSync

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/TeamOps/OFPA/{RunId}/ofpa_report.json

TeamOps_SourceControlProvider_ProfileValid

P21T starter tickets:

  • P21T-001: Add team-ops provider, workspace, branch, changelist, OFPA, asset

lock, and review-gate structs plus EUW_AscentiaWorkspaceDoctor.

  • P21T-002: Add EUW_AscentiaChangelistComposer and changelist policy audit

with phase tags, evidence links, binary counts, package impact, save impact, and Designer verdict rows.

  • P21T-003: Add EUW_AscentiaOFPAChangelistValidator with decoded external

actor, level, Data Layer, World Partition, dangling-reference, and partial submit checks.

  • P21T-004: Add EUW_AscentiaAssetLockBoard using source-control status APIs

for checkout owner, stale lock, not-at-head, writable-without-checkout, and binary merge risk.

  • P21T-005: Add code/asset review gate profiles and EUW_AscentiaReviewGateBoard.
  • P21T-006: Add EUW_AscentiaVerifiedSyncBoard and BuildGraph/UGS-readiness

snapshot support.

  • P21T-007: Add EUW_AscentiaBranchStreamMap and branch/stream policy audit.
  • P21T-008: Add EUW_AscentiaRedirectorAndMovePlanner for approved dry-run

asset moves, redirector fixes, and source-control-safe promotions.

Recommended implementation order:

  1. P21T-001 first. The workspace/provider policy makes current solo mode honest

and defines the team transition point.

  1. P21T-002 and P21T-003 next. Changelist hygiene and OFPA validation are the

highest-risk daily collaboration gaps for Unreal content.

  1. P21T-004 before multiple designers share maps, Blueprints, animations,

materials, or DataAssets.

  1. P21T-005 before review volume grows beyond direct conversation.
  2. P21T-006 before a second regular developer needs a known-good sync.
  3. P21T-007 and P21T-008 once content promotion and branch/release lanes become

more frequent than one-off local experiments.

#15.21 Ratings, privacy, legal text, credits, storefront claims, and player trust package

This package is production governance, not legal advice. Final binding decisions still belong to counsel, rating authorities, storefronts, platform holders, and regional law. The engineering point is that Ascentia should not discover its rating, privacy, credits, data-request, regional availability, or public-claim evidence gaps during store submission. Player trust is a build artifact.

Ascentia's MVP may begin as a mostly offline single-player game, but even a single-player PC title can collect support bundles, crash reports, telemetry, cloud-save metadata, achievement state, screenshots, store-page claims, public trailers, soundtrack credits, third-party asset attributions, and age-rating content disclosures. Those surfaces need owners, evidence, freshness, and Designer-visible review before public release.

Reference posture:

  • ESRB describes its system as rating categories, content descriptors, and

interactive elements. ESRB also notes that DLC usually inherits the base product rating, but DLC or updates that exceed the original rating must be submitted and may receive a different rating. Incomplete or inaccurate disclosure can result in corrected ratings and, for boxed products, sanctions. https://www.esrb.org/faqs/

  • IARC lets developers obtain ratings for multiple territories through a

participating digital storefront's ingestion portal. The questionnaire is available only through participating storefronts, ratings are currently free to developers at the point of use, and ratings are issued immediately after the questionnaire is completed. https://globalratings.com/faq/

  • Steam's German rating documentation states that, starting November 15, 2024,

Steam no longer displays games to customers in Germany when a valid age rating is missing. Valve's rating path depends on the developer truthfully completing Steam's built-in content questionnaire. https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/gettingstarted/contentsurvey/germany

  • Steam age gates can be enabled when entering regional ratings such as ESRB or

PEGI, so regional age-gate behavior is part of release configuration, not just copywriting. https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/store/age_gate

  • PEGI ratings are age-suitability guidance rather than difficulty guidance.

The PEGI descriptor space includes violence, fear, bad language, sex, drugs, discrimination, gambling, in-game purchases, paid random items, online interaction/communication, pressure to play, time-limited offers, and related player-protection descriptors. https://pegi.info/page/pegi-age-ratings

  • Steam's store description guidance says the short description should focus on

the product's value proposition, avoid formatting, avoid time-based text, and keep longer feature detail in the About This Game area. Text embedded in images must also be localized when the store page is localized. https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/store/page/description

  • California's CCPA gives California residents rights to know, delete, opt out

of sale/sharing, correct inaccurate information, limit certain sensitive data use, receive notice at collection, and avoid discrimination for exercising rights. A privacy policy and notice-at-collection posture therefore need a data map rather than a last-minute paragraph. https://oag.ca.gov/privacy/ccpa

  • EU data protection rules apply to EU and non-EU organizations that offer goods

or services in the EU and request or reuse personal data from individuals in the EU. Direct or indirect personal identification triggers data-protection rights such as access, correction, deletion, portability, and information about retention and recipients. https://europa.eu/youreurope/citizens/consumers/internet-telecoms/data-protection-online-privacy/index_en.htm

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaPlayerTrustTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaPrivacyDataMapTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaRatingContentTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaStoreClaimTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Compliance/AscentiaPlayerDataRequestTypes.h

The runtime layer should stay thin. It owns build identity, privacy-visible settings, consent/preferences, telemetry opt-out state, support-bundle export metadata, and player-facing policy surfaces. Rating submission packs, storefront claims, legal text, credits, regional visibility, and evidence generation live in Editor/build tooling unless a shipped screen needs to display them.

/Game/Ascentia/Editor/Compliance/

PDA_PlayerTrustPolicy_MVP
PDA_RatingEvidenceProfile_MVP
PDA_ESRBContentDisclosure_MVP
PDA_IARCQuestionnaireEvidence_MVP
PDA_PEGIDescriptorEvidence_MVP
PDA_PrivacyDataMap_MVP
PDA_TelemetryConsentPolicy_MVP
PDA_StoreClaimProfile_Steam_MVP
PDA_StoreClaimProfile_EGS_MVP
PDA_LegalTextRegistry_MVP
PDA_CreditsPolicy_MVP
PDA_RegionalAgeGatePolicy_MVP
PDA_PlayerDataRequestPolicy_MVP

Core data:

FAscentiaRatingContentSignal
  SignalTag
  SourceAsset
  SourceSystem
  RatingConcernKind:
    Violence, Blood, Gore, Fear, Horror, Language, Sex, Nudity, Drugs,
    Gambling, InGamePurchases, PaidRandomItems, UserInteraction,
    UnrestrictedCommunication, UGC, CryptoNFT, Other
  SeverityBand
  ContextDescription
  EvidenceClips
  Screenshots
  BuildVersion
  DLCOrPatchTag
  ReviewerNotes

FAscentiaRatingSubmissionProfile
  TerritorySystemTag
  StorefrontTag
  RatingAuthority
  SubmissionKind: IARC, StoreContentSurvey, Traditional, UpdateCheck
  RequiredEvidence
  ContentWarnings
  InteractiveElements
  DescriptorRows
  RegionAgeGatePolicy
  LastSubmissionId
  CertificationCode
  ChangeReviewRequired

FAscentiaPrivacyDataElement
  DataElementTag
  DisplayName
  CollectedBySystem
  RuntimeSource
  PersonalDataRisk: None, Pseudonymous, Personal, Sensitive
  CollectionTrigger
  Purpose
  LegalBasisCandidate
  RetentionPolicy
  SharedWithProviders
  UserVisibleSetting
  ExportPath
  DeleteBehavior
  OptOutBehavior
  Owner

FAscentiaStoreClaim
  ClaimId
  StorefrontTag
  Surface:
    ShortDescription, About, Screenshot, Trailer, PatchNotes, FeatureList,
    AccessibilityClaim, RatingClaim
  ClaimText
  SupportedByEvidence
  ScreenshotOrClip
  LocalizationKeys
  DateSensitive
  RegionSensitive
  RequiredFreshness
  ApprovalState

FAscentiaLegalTextEntry
  TextId
  TextKind:
    EULA, PrivacyPolicy, Credits, Attribution, ThirdPartyNotice,
    HealthWarning, AccessibilityStatement, SupportNotice
  Owner
  SourceAuthority
  Version
  LocaleCoverage
  InGameSurface
  StoreSurface
  LastCounselReview
  EvidencePath

FAscentiaPlayerDataRequestScenario
  ScenarioTag
  RequestKind: Access, Delete, Correct, OptOut, LimitSensitive, ExportSupportBundle
  DataElementsTouched
  ProviderDependencies
  ExpectedResponseArtifact
  DeleteOrAnonymizePolicy
  Exceptions
  ManualOwner
  EvidencePath

Rules:

  • Every public claim needs an evidence path, owner, approval state, locale

coverage, and freshness policy.

  • Ratings evidence is a content/system inventory, not optimism. Violent boss

finishers, blood, horror, gambling-like mechanics, paid/random items, online interaction, unrestricted communication, UGC, chat, crypto/NFT references, DLC changes, and patch changes all produce rating signals.

  • If DLC or a patch exceeds the base rating disclosure, the release gate blocks

until the rating-review path is resolved.

  • If the Steam Germany target is enabled, rating/content survey status is a

release gate.

  • Privacy data map rows own telemetry, support bundles, crash reporting,

cloud-save metadata, platform identity, achievement services, analytics, newsletter/community handoff, and any external provider export.

  • No new player data collection is allowed without a FAscentiaPrivacyDataElement

row, owner, purpose, retention policy, opt-out/delete behavior, and player visibility decision.

  • Store text must avoid unsupported claims, time-based short-description text,

unlocalized text inside images, and screenshots or trailers that imply mechanics not present in the target build.

  • Credits and attribution are generated from the P23 external content registry,

audio/music records, VO/performance records, vendor records, and generated content policy.

  • Player data request simulation verifies access/export/delete/correct/opt-out

behavior where applicable. The simulator produces engineering evidence for counsel and support operations; it does not replace legal review.

  • For the default offline single-player posture, data minimization wins: local

only unless a platform feature, support feature, telemetry setting, or explicit development option says otherwise.

Designer tools:

EUW_AscentiaRatingsEvidenceBoard
EUW_AscentiaPrivacyDataMap
EUW_AscentiaStoreClaimComplianceBoard
EUW_AscentiaLegalTextAndCreditsBoard
EUW_AscentiaAgeGateAndRegionalAvailabilityLab
EUW_AscentiaPlayerDataRequestSimulator
EUW_AscentiaMarketingAssetTruthAudit
EUW_AscentiaComplianceReleaseGate

EUW_AscentiaRatingsEvidenceBoard shows content signals, source assets, evidence clips/screenshots, DLC/update deltas, territory rows, interactive elements, descriptor concerns, missing disclosure notes, and rating-submission readiness.

EUW_AscentiaPrivacyDataMap shows collected data elements, runtime source, provider sharing, purpose, retention, opt-out/delete/export behavior, player visibility, settings surface, and owner.

EUW_AscentiaStoreClaimComplianceBoard shows every public claim by surface, evidence, freshness, region, localization key, screenshot/trailer support, approval state, and blocker status.

EUW_AscentiaLegalTextAndCreditsBoard shows EULA/privacy/support/legal notices, third-party attributions, soundtrack/VO/vendor credits, locale coverage, in-game/store surfaces, counsel-review status, and generated credits output.

EUW_AscentiaAgeGateAndRegionalAvailabilityLab shows storefront, territory, rating authority, age-gate state, valid rating presence, Steam Germany state, regional visibility, and publish blockers.

EUW_AscentiaPlayerDataRequestSimulator runs access, export, delete, correct, opt-out, limit-sensitive, and support-bundle scenarios against the privacy data map and provider dependency rows.

EUW_AscentiaMarketingAssetTruthAudit cross-checks screenshots, trailers, GIFs, capsules, press-kit copy, and store text against shipped features, rating signals, accessibility claims, and localization obligations.

Commandlets:

AscentiaRatingEvidenceAudit
AscentiaPrivacyDataMapAudit
AscentiaStoreClaimAudit
AscentiaLegalCreditsAudit
AscentiaRegionalAgeGateAudit
AscentiaPlayerDataRequestAudit
AscentiaComplianceReleaseGate

Evidence:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/Ratings/{RunId}/rating_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/PrivacyDataMap/{RunId}/privacy_data_map_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/StoreClaims/{RunId}/store_claim_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/LegalText/{RunId}/legal_text_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/Credits/{RunId}/credits_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/RegionalAgeGates/{RunId}/age_gate_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/PlayerDataRequests/{RunId}/data_request_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P21/Compliance/ComplianceRelease/{RunId}/compliance_release_report.json

Proof matrix:

Compliance_RatingEvidence_ContentSignalsComplete
Compliance_IARC_EvidencePackReady
Compliance_DLC_RatingCompatibilityChecked
Compliance_SteamGermany_RatingStatusPresent
Compliance_PEGI_DescriptorRisksReviewed
Compliance_PrivacyDataMap_AllTelemetryRowsOwned
Compliance_PrivacyPolicy_DataElementsMapped
Compliance_PlayerDataRequest_AccessDeleteExportProof
Compliance_StoreClaims_EvidenceFreshNoTimeSensitiveShortText
Compliance_Credits_AllAttributionRowsGenerated
Compliance_RegionalAgeGate_MatrixValid
Compliance_ReleaseGate_NoUnreviewedPublicClaims

P21G starter tickets:

  • P21G-001: Add compliance/player-trust structs, policy assets, and

EUW_AscentiaRatingsEvidenceBoard.

  • P21G-002: Add privacy data map, telemetry/support/cloud/provider rows, and

EUW_AscentiaPrivacyDataMap.

  • P21G-003: Add store claim compliance board and marketing asset truth audit.
  • P21G-004: Add legal text, credits, attribution, soundtrack, VO, vendor, and

generated-content registry bridge.

  • P21G-005: Add regional age-gate and rating matrix, including Steam Germany

valid-rating gate.

  • P21G-006: Add player data request simulator for access, export, delete,

correct, opt-out, limit-sensitive, and support-bundle scenarios.

  • P21G-007: Add DLC/patch rating compatibility bridge to P21L release manifests.
  • P21G-008: Add compliance release gate and dashboard blockers for unreviewed

public claims, missing rating evidence, missing privacy data rows, incomplete credits, and regional availability risks.

Recommended implementation order:

  1. P21G-001 through P21G-003 before any public store page, demo page, trailer,

press kit, or public accessibility claim goes live.

  1. P21G-004 before external content, audio, VO, generated assets, contractors,

or credits broaden.

  1. P21G-005 before regional store visibility is treated as a publishable target.
  2. P21G-006 before telemetry, support bundles, cloud saves, newsletters, account

linking, or analytics become player-facing features.

  1. P21G-007 before patch/DLC production.
  2. P21G-008 before any release candidate can call itself candidate-complete.

#16. Multiplayer, Online, Co-op, and Invasion Future-Proofing

#16.1 Player-facing goal

P22 should allow Ascentia to grow toward co-op, invasion-like pressure, messages/ghosts, or asynchronous traces without rewriting the single-player game. The player should feel that online features extend the same world rules, not that a separate multiplayer mode has replaced the game.

#16.2 Current Ascentia state

Existing future-proofing:

  • GAS is already present and supports replication/prediction patterns.
  • Many gameplay components already replicate state or telemetry.
  • AAscentiaPlayerState owns an ability system component with mixed

replication.

  • pawn/character fallbacks use minimal replication in places.
  • Mover and NetworkPrediction are enabled, and GASP/Mover integration already

treats movement as a system boundary.

  • UAscentiaCombatComponent owns replicated combat state and centralizes many

combat requests.

  • inventory/equipment has native components and data structures rather than

pure widget state.

  • world state has a subsystem, which can become an authority/sync boundary.

Current gaps:

  • no Online Subsystem/session module enabled;
  • no host/join/session UI;
  • no network test maps;
  • no listen-server smoke;
  • no dedicated-server stance;
  • no replication audit for inventory, save, world state, abilities, UI events,

or boss encounters;

  • no latency/prediction proof for dodge, attacks, montages, projectiles, loot, or

spell casts.

#16.3 Multiplayer design options

Do not implement all of these by default. P22 should pick deliberately:

Solo online
  messages, ghosts, bloodstains, asynchronous world traces

Summoned co-op
  small party, host world authority, guest restrictions

Invasions
  hostile guest rules, matchmaking constraints, anti-grief boundaries

Shared world pockets
  limited social hubs or dungeon instances

Dedicated co-op
  server-authoritative session without host migration expectations

Each mode needs:

  • session policy;
  • save progression policy;
  • loot ownership policy;
  • death/respawn policy;
  • matchmaking restrictions;
  • world-state authority;
  • disconnect recovery;
  • UI and messaging;
  • anti-cheat-sensitive path review.

#16.4 Authority model

Default rule:

Server owns gameplay truth.
Owning client predicts allowed local feel.
Non-owning clients receive replicated results and cosmetic cues.
Blueprint presentation reacts; it does not decide authority.

Authority boundaries:

  • Health, Breath, Eth, status, death, loot drops, save-relevant world state, boss

phase, enemy AI decisions, encounter completion, and item grants are server authoritative.

  • Local camera, HUD, screen effects, input buffering display, local audio mix,

and accessibility presentation are client local.

  • GameplayCue presentation can replicate or run locally depending on cue type,

but it must not be used as the source of gameplay truth.

  • Save writes are host/server mediated for multiplayer sessions. Guests should

receive explicit progress grants, not arbitrary host save writes.

Authority policy:

PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy
  PolicyTag
  SystemTag
  AuthorityOwner
  ClientPredictionPolicy
  RPCPolicy
  ReplicationPolicy
  SavePersistencePolicy
  AntiCheatSensitivity
  DesignerNotes

Authority audit row:

FAscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditRow
  SystemTag
  GameplayPathTag
  OwningClassOrAsset
  AuthorityOwner
  ClientCanRequest
  ClientCanPredict
  ServerValidates
  ReplicatesToOwner
  ReplicatesToOthers
  SaveRelevant
  ExploitSensitivity
  Unknowns[]

Authority audit report:

FAscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditReport
  RunId
  PathsChecked
  UnknownAuthorityPaths
  ClientAuthoritativeFindings
  MissingServerValidation
  SaveRelevantReplicationFindings
  ExploitSensitiveFindings
  RecommendedRepairs[]

EUW_AscentiaReplicationAudit should let a Designer or engineer filter by system, authority owner, save relevance, and exploit sensitivity. The first network milestone is not "host a lobby"; it is "no important gameplay path has unknown authority."

#16.5 GAS, prediction, and ability policy

Ability classes need a network policy:

LocalPredicted
  dodge, light attack startup, block raise, camera-local responsiveness

ServerInitiated
  enemy abilities, boss phases, world-state triggered effects

ServerOnly
  loot grants, save mutations, final reward claims, anti-cheat-sensitive actions

NonReplicated/LocalOnly
  purely local menu, camera, audio, accessibility effects

Ability proof must cover:

  • client activation accepted;
  • client activation rejected;
  • cost predicted then confirmed;
  • cooldown predicted then confirmed;
  • montage starts and cancels consistently;
  • target/hit authority matches server trace;
  • GameplayCues appear on relevant machines;
  • rollback/reconciliation does not feel worse than no prediction.

Ability network policy:

PDA_AbilityNetworkPolicy
  AbilityTag
  NetExecutionPolicy
  PredictionPolicy
  CostPredictionPolicy
  CooldownPredictionPolicy
  TargetDataAuthority
  MontageReplicationPolicy
  GameplayCueVisibilityPolicy
  ReconciliationPolicy

Ability proof report:

FAscentiaAbilityNetworkProofReport
  RunId
  AbilityTag
  ScenarioTag
  ClientActivationResult
  ServerValidationResult
  CostPredictionResult
  CooldownPredictionResult
  MontageSyncResult
  TargetDataResult
  GameplayCueResult
  ReconciliationFindings

The policy asset should be referenced by P13/P14/P15 ability reports. A weapon skill, spell, dodge, block, or boss move should not invent its own networking story later.

#16.6 Movement and combat motion

The combat motion solver from section 6.9 is the right abstraction for multiplayer because it separates player intent, authored motion, and authority. P22 should not let raw montage root motion become the network contract.

Network motion data should include:

MotionRequestId
MotionType
IntentVector
TargetActor/TargetLocation if any
Montage/Section
StartTime
PredictionKey
MovementModeAtStart
Floor/Ledge classification
CancelPolicy

Proof maps:

  • flat arena;
  • stair descent/ascent;
  • ledge fall;
  • narrow corridor wall slide;
  • enemy hit reaction;
  • boss attack root motion;
  • high-latency dodge/attack cancel.

Mover/NetworkPrediction should be evaluated as the long-term path, but the game contract must remain FAscentiaCombatMotionRequest and related interfaces so the project can swap movement implementation if needed.

Network motion report:

FAscentiaNetworkMotionReport
  RunId
  ScenarioTag
  MotionRequestId
  IntentVector
  PredictedPath
  ServerPath
  CorrectionEvents
  RootMotionDelta
  FloorLedgeClassification
  CancelWindowResult
  LatencyMs
  PacketLossPercent
  ReadabilityNotes

Motion replication policy:

PDA_MotionNetworkPolicy
  MotionTypeTag
  PredictionMode
  ServerCorrectionPolicy
  RootMotionPolicy
  FloorLedgeAuthorityPolicy
  CancelReplicationPolicy
  MaxCorrectionDistance
  DebugDrawPolicy

This report should reuse the P10/P14 motion inspector data. Network proof should not be a separate universe from root-motion, ledge, dodge-intent, and cancel proof.

#16.7 Inventory, loot, and save policy

Inventory rules:

  • server validates grants, consumes, equips, trades, drops, and crafts;
  • clients request actions by item instance ID and action tag;
  • server replies with accepted/rejected result and replicated state delta;
  • UI never assumes an item exists because it was clicked locally;
  • item instance IDs must be stable enough for replication and save.

Loot ownership options:

Host-owned world loot
  simplest, guest rewards explicit

Personal loot
  preferred for co-op fairness, more data and replication

Shared loot
  friction-heavy, needs pickup locks and grief rules

Save progression:

  • host world state saves to host slot;
  • guests save character progress they are allowed to keep;
  • world-state unlocks need explicit grant rules;
  • deaths and corpse runs need multiplayer ownership rules;
  • disconnects should not duplicate loot or lose paid costs.

Multiplayer loot policy:

PDA_MultiplayerLootPolicy
  PolicyTag
  LootOwnershipMode
  PickupLockPolicy
  VisibilityPolicy
  GuestRewardPolicy
  DisconnectPolicy
  DuplicateProtectionPolicy
  AntiGriefPolicy

Network item transaction:

FAscentiaNetworkItemTransactionRequest
  RequestId
  PlayerStateId
  ItemInstanceId
  ActionTag
  ClientContainerHash
  ClientTime
  PredictionKey

FAscentiaNetworkItemTransactionResult
  RequestId
  bAccepted
  FailureReasonTag
  ServerContainerHash
  ReplicatedDelta
  SaveGrantResult
  AntiDuplicationResult

Loot/save report:

FAscentiaNetworkLootSaveReport
  RunId
  ScenarioTag
  LootPolicy
  PickupResults
  EquipResults
  ConsumeResults
  CraftResults
  DisconnectReconnectResults
  GuestProgressGrants
  DuplicateFindings
  SaveFindings

EUW_AscentiaMultiplayerLootPolicyPreview should simulate host-owned, personal, and shared loot for the same encounter and show who sees, can pick up, can keep, and can save each reward.

#16.8 World state, AI, bosses, and encounters

Server owns:

  • enemy spawning;
  • AI decisions;
  • boss phase state;
  • encounter completion;
  • world gate state;
  • loot claim state;
  • campsite rest side effects;
  • Data Layer runtime activation that affects gameplay.

Clients own:

  • local discovery presentation;
  • map reveal animation;
  • subtitles and UI hints;
  • local-only atmosphere/accessibility presentation.

Bosses need special proof:

  • phase transitions replicate once;
  • boss health bar appears/disappears on all relevant clients;
  • fog wall state is consistent;
  • death rewards do not duplicate;
  • late joiners see correct phase/world state;
  • disconnect/reconnect does not leave boss encounter corrupted.

Network world-state delta:

FAscentiaNetworkWorldStateDelta
  DeltaId
  SourceSystemTag
  ChangedTags
  ClearedTags
  CounterChanges
  SaveRelevant
  ReplicationAudience
  LateJoinReplayPolicy

Boss network state:

FAscentiaBossNetworkState
  BossTag
  EncounterTag
  PhaseTag
  HealthFraction
  FogWallState
  AddsAlive
  ArenaStateTags
  RewardClaimState
  LastReplicatedMoveTag

Boss/world network report:

FAscentiaBossNetworkProofReport
  RunId
  BossTag
  ScenarioTag
  PhaseReplicationResult
  HealthBarReplicationResult
  FogWallReplicationResult
  LateJoinResult
  DisconnectReconnectResult
  RewardDuplicationResult
  WorldStateDeltaResults
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

Encounter network report:

FAscentiaEncounterNetworkReport
  RunId
  EncounterTag
  SpawnReplicationResults
  AIStateVisibilityResults
  CompletionReplicationResults
  LeashResetReplicationResults
  DataLayerReplicationFindings
  LateJoinFindings

#16.9 Session and online layer

Ascentia should hide platform APIs behind:

UAscentiaOnlineSubsystem
  CreateSession
  FindSessions
  JoinSession
  LeaveSession
  InviteFriend
  ResolveTravel
  GetOnlineCapabilities

UAscentiaSessionSettings
  MaxPlayers
  JoinPolicy
  Password/InvitePolicy
  PvPPolicy
  Region
  BuildCompatibility
  Mod/ContentCompatibility

The Online Subsystem/session provider should be a platform adapter, not the gameplay contract. The UI should speak in Ascentia terms: Host, Join, Summon, Invade, Password, Friends, Recent, Leave World.

Session profile:

PDA_SessionProfile
  ProfileTag
  SessionMode
  ProviderPolicy
  MaxPlayers
  JoinPolicy
  InvitePolicy
  PasswordPolicy
  BuildCompatibilityPolicy
  ContentCompatibilityPolicy
  TravelPolicy
  FailureTextPolicy

Session request/result:

FAscentiaSessionRequest
  RequestId
  RequestType
  ProfileTag
  LocalUserIndex
  SearchFilters
  InviteTarget
  PasswordOrCode

FAscentiaSessionResult
  RequestId
  RequestType
  bSucceeded
  FailureReasonTag
  ProviderError
  SessionId
  ResolvedTravelURL
  PlayerFacingMessageKey

Session report:

FAscentiaSessionProofReport
  RunId
  ProfileTag
  HostResult
  FindResult
  JoinResult
  LeaveResult
  InviteResult
  TravelResult
  FailureStateCoverage
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

Session UI should be designed even before a provider is chosen. It needs failure copy, retry paths, password/invite surfaces if used, build mismatch text, disconnect recovery, and host/guest save policy explanations.

#16.10 Anti-cheat and exploit-sensitive paths

Even if no anti-cheat provider is selected, P22 should mark sensitive actions:

  • item grants;
  • currency grants/spends;
  • stat investment/refund;
  • save migration;
  • boss rewards;
  • quest/world-state unlocks;
  • teleport/fast travel;
  • damage application;
  • status application;
  • cooldown/resource bypass;
  • movement bursts and root-motion skills.

Rule:

If cheating it would damage another player or persistent economy, the server
must own it.

Exploit review row:

FAscentiaExploitSensitivePath
  PathTag
  SystemTag
  ClientInputs
  ServerValidation
  PersistentStateTouched
  EconomyTouched
  OtherPlayersAffected
  DetectionTelemetry
  BlockingRisk

Exploit review report:

FAscentiaNetworkExploitReviewReport
  RunId
  PathsReviewed
  MissingServerValidation
  ClientTrustFindings
  PersistenceRiskFindings
  EconomyRiskFindings
  RecommendedRepairs[]

This is not an anti-cheat vendor plan. It is a trust-boundary plan. Vendor choice can wait; server ownership and validation cannot.

#16.11 Designer surfaces

FAscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditReport
FAscentiaAbilityNetworkProofReport
