FAscentiaBossNetworkProofReport
FAscentiaEncounterNetworkReport
FAscentiaSessionProofReport
FAscentiaNetworkExploitReviewReport

#16.12 P22 proof gates

P22 is not complete until:

  1. Listen-server smoke passes for move, dodge, attack, block, spell, loot, save

grant, death, respawn, and boss kill.

  1. Dedicated-server smoke either passes or the project explicitly chooses not to

support it.

  1. Packet lag/loss scenarios cover dodge, root-motion attacks, projectiles,

pickups, and boss phase transitions.

  1. Inventory/equipment replication survives equip, unequip, pickup, consume,

death, disconnect, and reconnect scenarios.

  1. World-state sync survives late join and travel.
  2. Session host/join/leave UI handles failure states.
  3. GameplayCue and presentation events are visible to the correct clients.
  4. Save progression rules are written, tested, and visible in UI.
  5. Replication audit has no "unknown authority" gameplay paths.
  6. Dashboard shows network proof evidence beside P22 status.

Remote proof IDs:

network_authority_audit
listen_server_combat_motion
listen_server_inventory_loot
listen_server_spell_projectile
boss_phase_late_join
world_state_late_join
session_host_join_leave
network_disconnect_reconnect
network_prediction_latency
network_exploit_review

#16.13 P22 starter implementation package

P22 should begin with an audit/proof spine, not with a platform provider. The first goal is to make current single-player systems name their authority, replication, save, and exploit assumptions before any Online Subsystem provider or session UI becomes production-critical.

Runtime files:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Network/AscentiaNetworkTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Network/AscentiaNetworkAuthorityTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Network/AscentiaNetworkProofTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Online/AscentiaOnlineTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Online/AscentiaOnlineSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Online/AscentiaSessionTypes.h

Source/Ascentia/Private/Online/AscentiaOnlineSubsystem.cpp

Editor/proof files:

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Network/AscentiaNetworkAuditTypes.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Network/AscentiaNetworkAuditSubsystem.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Network/AscentiaNetworkScenarioTypes.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Network/AscentiaExploitReviewTypes.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Network/AscentiaNetworkAuditSubsystem.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Network/AscentiaNetworkScenarioRunner.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Network/AscentiaExploitReview.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaNetworkAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaSessionProfileTest.cpp

First runtime stance:

Single-player runtime remains authoritative locally.
Network-ready data names future authority.
No online provider is required for P22 audit work.
No client-authored persistent economy/save path is allowed.

Authority data should be registered before multiplayer is enabled:

PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_Combat
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_CombatMotion
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_Inventory
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_Loot
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_SaveProgression
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_WorldState
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_BossEncounter
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_Services
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_FastTravel
PDA_NetworkAuthorityPolicy_SettingsLocalOnly

Starter authority paths:

Combat.LightAttack
Combat.DamageApplication
Combat.Dodge
Combat.Block
Combat.PotionUse
Magic.SpellCast
Magic.ProjectileSpawn
Inventory.Pickup
Inventory.Equip
Inventory.Consume
Inventory.Craft
Progression.SpendStones
Progression.Respec
WorldState.SetTag
WorldState.ClearTag
Encounter.Spawn
Encounter.Complete
Boss.PhaseTransition
Boss.RewardGrant
Save.WriteProgression
Session.Host
Session.Join
Session.Leave

UAscentiaOnlineSubsystem should be a facade:

UCLASS()
class UAscentiaOnlineSubsystem : public UGameInstanceSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaSessionResult CreateSession(
        const FAscentiaSessionRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaSessionSearchResult FindSessions(
        const FAscentiaSessionSearchRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaSessionResult JoinSession(
        const FAscentiaSessionRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaSessionResult LeaveSession(
        const FAscentiaSessionRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintPure, Category="Ascentia|Online")
    FAscentiaOnlineCapabilities GetOnlineCapabilities() const;
};

Facade rules:

  • returns Ascentia request/result structs, not raw provider structs;
  • maps Online Subsystem errors to DT_NetworkErrorText;
  • exposes provider capability gaps in UI;
  • supports Null/LAN/dev provider before platform provider;
  • keeps session UI independent from Steam/EOS/console specifics.

Session/profile starter structs:

FAscentiaOnlineCapabilities
  ProviderTag
  SupportsSessions
  SupportsPresence
  SupportsInvites
  SupportsFriends
  SupportsJoinInProgress
  SupportsDedicatedServer
  SupportsCrossplay
  UnavailableReasons

FAscentiaSessionSearchRequest
  ProfileTag
  LocalUserIndex
  MaxResults
  SearchFilters
  bLanOnly

FAscentiaSessionSearchResult
  bSucceeded
  FailureReasonTag
  ProviderError
  Results
  PlayerFacingMessageKey

Network audit subsystem:

UCLASS()
class UAscentiaNetworkAuditSubsystem : public UEditorSubsystem
{
    GENERATED_BODY()

public:
    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Network")
    FAscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditReport RunAuthorityAudit(
        const FAscentiaNetworkAuditRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Network")
    FAscentiaNetworkExploitReviewReport RunExploitReview(
        const FAscentiaExploitReviewRequest& Request);

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Network")
    FAscentiaSessionProofReport ValidateSessionProfiles();

    UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable, Category="Ascentia|Network")
    FAscentiaNetworkScenarioReport RunScenarioProfile(
        const FAscentiaNetworkScenarioProfile& Scenario);
};

Audit request:

FAscentiaNetworkAuditRequest
  RunId
  AssetRoots
  GameplayPathTags
  IncludeBlueprints
  IncludeNativeComponents
  IncludeDataAssets
  IncludeSaveRelevantOnly
  IncludeExploitSensitiveOnly
  ReportRoot

Network scenario profile:

PDA_NetworkScenarioProfile_ListenServerCombat
  ScenarioTag
  NetMode: ListenServer
  Map
  Clients: 2
  SimulatedLatencyMs
  SimulatedPacketLossPercent
  RequiredActions
  ExpectedReplicatedFacts
  EvidenceProfile

PDA_NetworkScenarioProfile_LateJoinWorldState
PDA_NetworkScenarioProfile_DisconnectReconnectLoot
PDA_NetworkScenarioProfile_BossPhaseLateJoin
PDA_NetworkScenarioProfile_SessionHostJoinLeave

First scenario facts:

listen_server_combat_motion
  host and client possess players
  client dodges
  client light attacks
  server validates motion/damage
  both clients receive presentation cue

listen_server_inventory_loot
  host spawns reward
  client requests pickup
  server grants or rejects
  inventory/equipment delta replicates
  save grant policy records result

world_state_late_join
  host sets gate/discovery/objective state
  late client joins
  late client receives correct visible world state

session_host_join_leave
  host session
  find session
  join session
  leave session
  failure copy resolves from `DT_NetworkErrorText`

Replication scaling stance:

MVP / P22 starter
  standard Unreal replication, explicit relevancy, small scenarios

Open-world actor density milestone
  evaluate Replication Graph for large actor sets and zone-based relevancy

Iris pilot
  evaluate only after replicated object schemas are stable
  do not mix RepGraph and Iris as simultaneous production answers

The compendium should keep Replication Graph and Iris as named choices, not vague "optimize networking later." Epic documents Replication Graph as a persistent-node system for building per-client actor replication lists, while Iris is an opt-in replication system and is not the same network driver path as Replication Graph. Ascentia should pick one route per milestone.

Exploit-sensitive starter paths:

Exploit.InventoryGrant
Exploit.CurrencySpend
Exploit.BossRewardClaim
Exploit.SaveMigration
Exploit.TeleportFastTravel
Exploit.DamageApplication
Exploit.StatusApplication
Exploit.ResourceCostBypass
Exploit.RootMotionBurst
Exploit.WorldStateUnlock

First P22 implementation tickets:

P22-001 Add network authority data structs and policy assets
  acceptance: core gameplay paths can be listed with authority owner,
  prediction policy, replication policy, save relevance, and exploit sensitivity

P22-002 Add `UAscentiaNetworkAuditSubsystem`
  acceptance: dry-run audit emits `FAscentiaNetworkAuthorityAuditReport`
  with no mutation and links every unknown authority path

P22-003 Add exploit-sensitive path registry
  acceptance: inventory grants, currency spends, boss rewards, fast travel,
  damage, status, save migration, and world-state unlocks are reviewed

P22-004 Add `UAscentiaOnlineSubsystem` facade stub
  acceptance: Null/providerless mode reports capabilities and friendly failure
  copy without requiring Steam/EOS

P22-005 Add session profile validation
  acceptance: `PDA_SessionProfile` rows validate max players, join policy,
  provider policy, travel policy, and error text

P22-006 Add listen-server combat/motion scenario profile
  acceptance: two-client proof plan covers move, dodge, attack, damage,
  presentation cue, and correction logging

P22-007 Add network loot/save scenario profile
  acceptance: pickup/equip/consume/disconnect/reconnect paths produce a
  `FAscentiaNetworkLootSaveReport`

P22-008 Add boss/world late-join scenario profile
  acceptance: boss phase, fog wall, reward claim, and world-state deltas define
  late-join expected facts

P22-009 Add network dashboard card writer
  acceptance: P22 dashboard shows authority audit, exploit review, session proof,
  combat/motion proof, loot/save proof, and world/boss proof

Commandlet/script contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaNetworkAudit
  -Authority
  -ExploitReview
  -AssetRoot=/Game/Ascentia
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/Authority

Tools/run_network_scenario.ps1
  -Scenario ListenServerCombat
  -Clients 2
  -LatencyMs 100
  -PacketLossPercent 2
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/Motion

P22 starter proof target:

network_authority_audit
  every named starter path has authority owner, validation policy, replication
  policy, save relevance, and exploit sensitivity

network_exploit_review
  every exploit-sensitive starter path has server-validation notes

session_profile_validation
  session profiles are valid even before provider implementation

The first P22 milestone is complete when the project can say, with evidence, "we know who owns every important gameplay truth if multiplayer arrives." Host, join, lag, and late-join simulations come next; provider integration comes after the contracts stop moving.

#16.14 Network scenario, replication proof, latency, and multiplayer lab package

The P22 starter package names authority and risk. This companion package makes those claims executable. It defines the repeatable network scenario lab that can launch server/client fixtures, apply latency and packet-loss profiles, watch replicated facts, collect traces, and present the results to Designers without requiring them to read raw network logs.

Reference posture:

  • Use PIE multiplayer options only for fast local iteration and debugging.
  • Use Gauntlet/UAT-style multi-session launches for real server/client proof.
  • Treat Functional Tests as useful for single-instance logic, but do not rely on

them alone to prove cross-instance replicated property behavior.

  • Prefer Networking Insights for detailed network-traffic investigation; keep

the legacy Network Profiler only as a high-level compatibility report.

  • Start with standard Unreal replication and explicit relevancy. Evaluate

Replication Graph only when actor density or connection count proves it is needed.

  • Treat Iris as an opt-in, experimental replication route until the project has

stable replicated object schemas and a deliberate migration plan.

Runtime/editor layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Network/
  AscentiaNetworkScenarioTypes.h
  AscentiaReplicationWatchTypes.h
  AscentiaNetEmulationTypes.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Network/
  AscentiaNetworkScenarioLabTypes.h
  AscentiaNetworkScenarioRunner.h
  AscentiaReplicationWatchValidator.h
  AscentiaNetworkTraceArtifactTypes.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Network/
  AscentiaNetworkScenarioRunner.cpp
  AscentiaReplicationWatchValidator.cpp
  AscentiaNetworkTraceArtifactCollector.cpp

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/
  AscentiaRunNetworkScenarioCommandlet.cpp
  AscentiaReplicationWatchAuditCommandlet.cpp

Starter assets:

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Network/Scenarios/
  PDA_NetworkScenario_ListenServerCombat_MVP
  PDA_NetworkScenario_ClientLootAndEquip_MVP
  PDA_NetworkScenario_WorldStateLateJoin_MVP
  PDA_NetworkScenario_BossPhaseLateJoin_MVP
  PDA_NetworkScenario_DisconnectReconnect_MVP
  PDA_NetworkScenario_SessionHostJoinLeave_MVP

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Network/Latency/
  PDA_NetEmulation_LocalClean
  PDA_NetEmulation_MildLatency
  PDA_NetEmulation_BadWifi
  PDA_NetEmulation_HighLossDebug

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Network/Watchlists/
  PDA_ReplicationWatch_CombatMotion_MVP
  PDA_ReplicationWatch_LootInventory_MVP
  PDA_ReplicationWatch_WorldState_MVP
  PDA_ReplicationWatch_BossEncounter_MVP
  PDA_ReplicationWatch_PresentationCues_MVP

Core structs:

struct FAscentiaNetworkClientSpec
{
    FName ClientId;
    EAscentiaNetworkRole ExpectedRole;
    FString PlayerProfileTag;
    FString SaveSlotPolicyTag;
    bool bRecordsVideo;
    bool bRecordsInsightsTrace;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioStep
{
    FGameplayTag StepTag;
    FText DesignerLabel;
    FGameplayTag RequiredClientTag;
    FGameplayTag ActionTag;
    TMap<FName, FString> ActionParameters;
    float TimeoutSeconds;
    FGameplayTagContainer ExpectedFactTags;
};

struct FAscentiaReplicationExpectedFact
{
    FGameplayTag FactTag;
    FString SourcePath;
    FString ExpectedAuthorityValue;
    FString ExpectedClientValue;
    float MaxPropagationSeconds;
    bool bRequiredOnLateJoin;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioProfile
{
    FGameplayTag ScenarioTag;
    TSoftObjectPtr<UWorld> Map;
    EAscentiaNetworkLaunchMode LaunchMode;
    int32 ClientCount;
    FGameplayTag NetEmulationProfileTag;
    TArray<FAscentiaNetworkClientSpec> Clients;
    TArray<FAscentiaNetworkScenarioStep> Steps;
    TArray<FAscentiaReplicationExpectedFact> ExpectedFacts;
    FGameplayTag EvidenceProfileTag;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioRunRequest
{
    FGuid RunId;
    FGameplayTag ScenarioTag;
    FGameplayTag NetEmulationProfileTag;
    FString BuildPath;
    FString ReportRoot;
    bool bUseEditorPIE;
    bool bUseGauntlet;
    bool bCaptureNetworkingInsights;
    bool bFailOnWarnings;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioObservation
{
    FGameplayTag StepTag;
    FGameplayTag FactTag;
    FName ObservingClientId;
    FString AuthorityValue;
    FString ClientValue;
    float PropagationSeconds;
    bool bMatched;
    FString Notes;
};

struct FAscentiaNetworkScenarioRunResult
{
    FGuid RunId;
    FGameplayTag ScenarioTag;
    bool bSucceeded;
    TArray<FAscentiaNetworkScenarioObservation> Observations;
    TArray<FString> LogPaths;
    TArray<FString> TracePaths;
    TArray<FString> ScreenshotPaths;
    TArray<FText> Errors;
    TArray<FText> Warnings;
};

Designer-facing tools:

EUW_AscentiaNetworkScenarioLab
  Selects scenario profile, launch mode, client count, latency preset, map,
  expected facts, and evidence profile; runs dry-run validation before launch.

EUW_AscentiaReplicationWatchBoard
  Shows watched replicated facts by system, owner, authority source, expected
  client visibility, late-join policy, and last proof result.

EUW_AscentiaNetEmulationPresetLab
  Lets Designers tune named latency/loss/jitter presets with warnings about
  which scenarios must pass under each preset.

EUW_AscentiaNetworkTraceBrowser
  Opens latest report, logs, screenshots, Networking Insights trace paths,
  actor/RPC/property summaries, and comparison against prior runs.

EUW_AscentiaMultiplayerUXFailureLab
  Previews host/join/leave/disconnect/build-mismatch failure text and validates
  that session UI can explain every scenario failure.

Commandlet and script contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaRunNetworkScenario
  -Scenario=ListenServerCombat
  -LaunchMode=ListenServer
  -Clients=2
  -NetEmulation=MildLatency
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios
  -CaptureInsights
  -FailOnWarnings

Tools/run_network_scenario.ps1
  -Scenario ListenServerCombat
  -Clients 2
  -LatencyPreset MildLatency
  -UseGauntlet
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios

Evidence:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios/{RunId}/summary.md
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios/{RunId}/observations.csv
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios/{RunId}/replication_watch.csv
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios/{RunId}/logs/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios/{RunId}/traces/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P22/NetworkScenarios/{RunId}/screenshots/

Proof matrix:

NetScenario_ListenServerCombat_MildLatency
NetScenario_ClientLootEquip_DisconnectReconnect
NetScenario_WorldStateLateJoin
NetScenario_BossPhaseLateJoin
NetScenario_SessionHostJoinLeave_NullProvider
NetScenario_PresentationCueAudience
ReplicationWatch_NoUnknownAuthorityFacts
ReplicationWatch_NoClientTrustedPersistentEconomy
NetEmulation_BadWifi_CombatMotionReadable
NetworkTrace_ReportHasLogsAndInsightsPath

Implementation tickets:

P22N-001 Network scenario profile and run-result structs
  Add profile/request/result/observation structs, latency preset assets, and the
  first five MVP scenario profiles.

P22N-002 Replication watchlist assets and validator
  Add watchlist assets for combat motion, loot/inventory, world state, boss
  encounter, and presentation cues.

P22N-003 Scenario lab dry-run validation
  Add an Editor subsystem path that validates map, client count, launch mode,
  expected facts, report root, and trace/log capture paths without launching.

P22N-004 Multi-session runner wrapper
  Add the PowerShell/UAT/Gauntlet wrapper contract for launching server/client
  fixtures and collecting logs/traces/screenshots.

P22N-005 Network trace artifact collector
  Add report fields for Networking Insights traces, logs, screenshots,
  observations, and actor/RPC/property summary CSVs.

P22N-006 Designer Network Scenario Lab EUW
  Add scenario selection, dry-run validation, run launch, latest-result table,
  and evidence browser links.

P22N-007 Multiplayer UX failure preview
  Add failure text/profile validation for host, find, join, leave, disconnect,
  build mismatch, provider unavailable, timeout, and version mismatch.

P22N-008 Dashboard gate integration
  Make P22 dashboard status read scenario reports, replication watch reports,
  net emulation coverage, and latest human verdict.

First next package:

  1. Implement P22N-001 through P22N-003 before launching any real multi-process

network scenario.

  1. Use PIE only for quick debugging; require Gauntlet/UAT-style multi-session

evidence before claiming a P22 scenario is proven.

  1. Do not choose Replication Graph or Iris until standard replication scenario

reports show a scaling or architecture need.

#17. World Scripting, NPCs, Objectives, Vendors, Tutorials, and Interaction

#17.1 Player-facing goal

Ascentia should not feel like a game with a quest checklist pasted onto a combat system. The player should read the world through NPCs, POIs, discoveries, memories, vendors, tutorials, map hints, gates, rewards, and consequences.

The project direction is explicit:

No traditional quest log by default.
World state is the story and objective substrate.

P12 is therefore not "build a quest system." It is "build the world-state authoring layer that lets Designers create guidance, consequence, commerce, tutorialization, and NPC life."

#17.2 Current Ascentia state

Existing useful seams:

  • IAscentiaInteractableInterface defines CanInteract, GetInteractionText,

and Interact.

  • AAscentiaCharacterBase::FindInteractTarget performs an aim trace plus a

proximity search for corpse-like interactables.

  • AAscentiaGaspCharacter::Interact has the correct high-level behavior for the

current UI: if a loot window is open, E loots; otherwise it finds an interactable target.

  • UAscentiaWorldStateSubsystem stores active FName state flags, broadcasts

state changes, tracks telemetry, and evaluates runtime objective steps from required/completion state tags.

  • AAscentiaPlayerController bridges world-state objective steps into existing

HUD objective getters.

  • AAscentiaNPC is a replicated interactable friendly NPC base with speaker

text, interaction text, dialogue rows, required state tags, state tags to set or clear, interaction telemetry, and OnDialogueLineSpoken.

  • AAscentiaVendorNPC extends the NPC base with stock rows, Stone prices,

repeatable/sold-out policy, purchase telemetry, inventory grants, Stone spend, and a world-state purchase flag.

  • UAscentiaTutorialPromptSubsystem registers world-state-gated prompts, exposes

active title/body, dismisses prompts, writes completion tags, and tracks telemetry.

  • UAscentiaCraftingSubsystem exists as a world subsystem with recipe

validation/build functions, but is not yet wired into a station/UI flow.

  • Campsite, fast travel, discovery, world gate, The Below entry/exit, corpse

loot, and map sensing already prove that the world can mutate state and present feedback.

Current limitations:

  • interaction server RPCs accept an actor reference and call Interact if it

implements the interface; production needs server-side range/LOS/eligibility revalidation before interactables multiply;

  • world-state flags are FName and transient; production needs GameplayTag or

validated tag-like identity, persistence, and migration;

  • objective steps are runtime-registered, not designer-authored assets yet;
  • dialogue rows are inline arrays, not a scalable dialogue asset pipeline;
  • vendor stock is inline and lacks sell/buyback/restock/economy curves;
  • tutorial prompts are runtime-registered, not content-authored assets;
  • no production dialogue/shop/tutorial/map UI;
  • no NPC schedule/fate/state graph;
  • no localization, VO, subtitle, or accessibility policy for NPC/tutorial text.

#17.3 Core world-state model

World state should be the shared condition language for P12.

Native authority:

UAscentiaWorldStateSubsystem
  SetStateTag
  ClearStateTag
  HasStateTag
  QueryStateTagContainer
  RegisterObjectiveSet
  EvaluateObjectiveSet
  BroadcastStateChanged
  BuildSaveFragment
  ApplySaveFragment

UAscentiaWorldScriptSubsystem
  executes authored state transitions
  validates condition/effect payloads
  routes events to UI, audio, map, NPCs, and analytics

Data should move toward:

FAscentiaWorldCondition
  RequiredTags
  BlockedTags
  RequiredAnyTags
  RequiredTagQuery
  RequiredWorldTier
  RequiredPOIState
  RequiredNPCState
  RequiredItemTags
  RequiredStatMinimums
  RequiredEncounterState
  RequiredTimeOrWeather

FAscentiaWorldEffect
  TagsToSet
  TagsToClear
  TagsToIncrement
  ObjectiveProgress
  RewardGrant
  DialogueUnlock
  VendorStockUnlock
  TutorialUnlock
  MapReveal
  DataLayerRequest
  AudioCue
  PresentationCue
  SaveDirtyReason

PDA_WorldScriptNode
  NodeId
  Conditions
  Effects
  RepeatPolicy
  SavePolicy
  AuthorityPolicy
  VisibilityPolicy
  DesignerNotes

Rules:

  • state identity must be stable, searchable, and validated;
  • state transitions must be inspectable in the Editor;
  • state writes must produce telemetry;
  • save/load must restore state before NPC/objective/tutorial evaluation;
  • UI should display consequences only when design marks them visible;
  • runtime fallback state may exist for tests, but shipped content needs assets.

World-state tag taxonomy should be owned like combat tags:

World.Region.*
World.POI.*
World.Boss.*
World.NPC.*
World.Dialogue.*
World.Objective.*
World.Tutorial.*
World.Discovery.*
World.Vendor.*
World.Service.*
World.Campsite.*
World.Gate.*
World.Memory.*
World.Event.*

Registry assets:

PDA_WorldStateRegistry
  RegisteredTags
  OwnerSystem
  SavePolicy
  ReplicationPolicy
  VisibilityPolicy
  DesignerDescription

PDA_WorldStateMigration
  FromVersion
  ToVersion
  TagRenames
  TagDeletions
  TagSplits
  DefaultTags
  FixtureSavePaths

Save fragment:

FAscentiaWorldStateSaveFragment
  SaveVersion
  ActiveTags
  TagCounters
  CompletedObjectiveSteps
  DismissedTutorialTags
  DiscoveredTags
  PurchasedLimitedStock
  NPCStateSnapshots
  LastMigrationVersion

Ordering matters. Load should restore world state before NPCs choose dialogue, vendors resolve stock, objective tracker evaluates steps, map hints reveal, or tutorials decide whether they have already been completed.

#17.3.1 World script event pipeline

World scripts should be authored as small, inspectable condition/effect nodes instead of arbitrary Blueprint event graphs.

Pipeline:

Trigger source
  -> build world event context
  -> evaluate conditions
  -> apply effects transactionally
  -> mark save fragment dirty
  -> broadcast state change
  -> update objective/dialogue/vendor/tutorial/map views
  -> write telemetry/evidence

Event context:

FAscentiaWorldEventContext
  EventTag
  InstigatorActor
  TargetActor
  SourceAssetId
  SourceMap
  SourcePOITag
  ActiveSaveSlot
  ServerTime
  ContextTags
  Payload

Effects should be transactional. If a node grants an item, sets a completion tag, opens a gate, and reveals a map hint, the report should show each effect and whether it succeeded. Failed partial state writes are how quest systems rot.

#17.4 Interaction contract

The production interaction path should be:

Client input
  -> local target preview
  -> local prompt display
  -> server interaction request
  -> server revalidates target/range/LOS/state
  -> server executes interaction
  -> replicated or local presentation response

Interaction request:

FAscentiaInteractionRequest
  RequestId
  InteractingActor
  TargetActor
  InteractionTag
  PredictedInteractionDefinition
  ClientViewLocation
  ClientViewDirection
  ClientTargetLocation
  ClientTime
  InputDeviceTag

Interaction definition:

PDA_InteractionDefinition
  InteractionTag
  DisplayText
  HoldDuration
  Range
  TracePolicy
  ReservationPolicy
  RequiredState
  BlockedState
  RequiredItemTags
  RequiredAbilityTags
  InputActionTag
  Priority
  UIStyle
  ServerAuthorityPolicy
  FailureTextPolicy
  CompletionEffects

Server revalidation:

  • target still exists;
  • target implements the interaction interface or component;
  • distance is within interaction range;
  • line of sight or accepted proximity rule passes;
  • CanInteract passes on the server;
  • interaction is not blocked by combat/death/menu state;
  • target is not already reserved if reservation matters;
  • request is rate-limited;
  • gameplay-cost or inventory requirements are rechecked.

Interaction result:

FAscentiaInteractionResult
  RequestId
  Result
  FailureReasonTag
  PlayerTextKey
  AppliedEffects
  OpenedScreenTag
  GrantedItems
  ConsumedItems
  EvidenceId

Smart Objects should be considered for multi-user or AI/player-shared interactions such as seats, shrines, crafting stations, camp tasks, doors, ritual sites, NPC work points, and world activities. Simple one-shot pickups can stay lighter.

Use Smart Objects when an interaction needs:

  • reserved slots;
  • AI and player access to the same authored activity;
  • spatial queries by tag;
  • "claim, approach, use, release" lifecycle;
  • abort handling when the target changes state;
  • streamed-world awareness.

Use a lightweight interactable when it is:

  • one player only;
  • instant pickup or prompt;
  • no shared slot;
  • no AI usage;
  • no long-lived reservation.

PDA_InteractionDefinition remains the Ascentia-facing contract either way. Smart Object definitions are provider data; Ascentia interaction definitions own UI, authority, requirements, effects, and proof.

#17.5 Objectives without a quest log

Objectives should be authored as world-state lenses, not as isolated quest objects.

Data:

PDA_ObjectiveSet
  ObjectiveSetId
  RegionTag
  Priority
  VisibilityPolicy
  Steps
  MapHintPolicy
  RewardPreviewPolicy
  CompletionEffects

FAscentiaObjectiveStep
  StepId
  LabelTextKey
  RequiredState
  CompletionState
  OptionalFailureState
  MapHint
  NPCClueTags
  TutorialHintTags
  VisibilityPolicy
  RewardPreview
  CompletionEffects
  AbandonPolicy

Visibility policies:

  • hidden until discovered;
  • vague clue;
  • explicit objective;
  • map-only;
  • NPC-only;
  • completed-history-only;
  • never shown, state only.

The HUD tracker should show only the active, player-legible layer. The dashboard and Editor tools should show the full state graph.

Proof:

  • step appears when required tags become true;
  • step completes when completion tag becomes true;
  • step hides when blocked/failure tag becomes true;
  • save/load restores the same objective state;
  • map/NPC/tutorial hints agree with the active step;
  • localization and text-size stress pass.

Objective authoring rule: no objective should be a private UI-only checklist. Every step must resolve from world state, and every visible hint should point back to an asset Designers can preview. If the game intentionally hides a step, that hidden policy should be authored, not accidental.

Objective evidence:

FAscentiaObjectiveEvidence
  ObjectiveSetId
  ActiveStepId
  RequiredTags
  CompletionTags
  VisibleTextKeys
  MapHintIds
  NPCClueIds
  TutorialHintIds
  SaveFragmentTags
  Result

#17.6 NPC and dialogue architecture

NPCs should be authored as characters with state, not as text dispensers.

Data:

PDA_NPCDefinition
  NPCId
  DisplayName
  RoleTags
  FactionTags
  Mesh/Anim/Voice placeholders
  DefaultDialogueSet
  VendorProfile
  ServiceProfiles
  ScheduleProfile
  WorldStateBindings
  FateProfile
  LocalizationNamespace

PDA_DialogueSet
  DialogueSetId
  SpeakerPolicy
  Lines
  BarkRules
  SubtitleStyle
  VoicePolicy
  LocalizationNamespace
  FallbackLineId

FAscentiaDialogueLine
  LineId
  Speaker
  TextKey
  RequiredState
  BlockedState
  Effects
  Priority
  RepeatPolicy
  Cooldown
  CameraPolicy
  AudioEvent
  SubtitleTiming
  ChoiceSet

Dialogue modes:

  • interact line: player presses E and receives a line or opens a dialogue box;
  • bark: NPC speaks due to proximity, combat, weather, POI state, or time;
  • vendor greeting: shop state plus dialogue;
  • tutorial mentor: line unlocks or explains a world system;
  • memory echo: non-character world text or spectral line;
  • branching conversation: only if the game truly needs choice UI.

Validation:

  • no dialogue line has missing localized text;
  • no line has impossible required/blocked state;
  • no line sets and clears the same tag;
  • every NPC has a fallback line;
  • every dialogue set has a previewable first interaction;
  • branching conversations have at least one exit path;
  • VO/subtitle duration matches display/readability policy when VO exists.

Dialogue should produce world events, not arbitrary side effects hidden in a widget. If a line unlocks a vendor stock row, teaches a tutorial, sets an objective tag, or changes NPC fate, the effect belongs in the line data and should be visible in EUW_AscentiaNPCStoryGraph.

Dialogue proof should include:

dialogue_state_filter
  sample required/blocked world-state combinations
  verify selected line and fallback line

dialogue_effects
  speak line
  verify world tags, objective hints, vendor unlocks, and tutorial unlocks

dialogue_localization
  pseudo-localize line, subtitle, choice text, and vendor greeting

#17.7 Vendors, services, crafting, and economy interaction

Vendors are world-state actors plus economy interfaces.

Vendor data:

PDA_VendorProfile
  VendorId
  StockTables
  BuyPolicy
  SellPolicy
  BuybackPolicy
  RestockPolicy
  PriceCurve
  DiscountRules
  WorldStateUnlocks
  DialogueSet
  ServiceProfiles
  LimitedStockSavePolicy

PDA_ServiceProfile
  ServiceTag
  DisplayTextKey
  CostPolicy
  RequiredState
  BlockedState
  RequiredItems
  Effects
  ConfirmationPolicy
  AuthorityPolicy
  ResultScreenPolicy

Vendor services can include:

  • buy;
  • sell;
  • buyback;
  • repair if durability ever exists;
  • identify/appraise if item mystery exists;
  • respec;
  • upgrade;
  • craft;
  • socket/rune/affix services;
  • lore exchange;
  • faction services.

The current AAscentiaVendorNPC::TryPurchaseStockIndex is a good first slice: server authority, Stone spend, inventory grant, sold-out state, and telemetry. Production should add:

  • stock DataAssets;
  • UI shop rows;
  • item comparison;
  • affordability styling;
  • confirm/hold-to-buy setting;
  • sell/buyback flow;
  • restock timing;
  • save persistence for sold-out limited items;
  • economy simulation through EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab.

Service execution:

FAscentiaServiceRequest
  RequestId
  ServiceTag
  ProviderActor
  RequestingActor
  TargetItemInstanceId
  OfferedCurrency
  ContextTags

FAscentiaServiceResult
  RequestId
  Result
  FailureReasonTag
  SpentCurrency
  ConsumedItems
  GrantedItems
  ModifiedItems
  AppliedWorldEffects
  PlayerTextKey

Services should be server-authoritative and data-authored. A respec shrine, crafting bench, upgrade blacksmith, flask allocator, and vendor are all service providers with different profiles. This makes P16 economy and P19 respec/death systems build on P12 instead of bypassing it.

Crafting should not stay as a dead subsystem. It should become either:

  • a vendor/service action;
  • a station interaction;
  • a menu tab unlocked by world state;
  • a future endgame system gated by P16 economy decisions.

#17.8 Tutorials and onboarding

Tutorials should teach by context, not by interrupting.

Prompt data:

PDA_TutorialPrompt
  PromptId
  TitleTextKey
  BodyTextKey
  RequiredState
  BlockedState
  CompletionState
  InputActionTags
  DisplaySeconds
  Priority
  ReplayPolicy
  CombatSafePolicy
  AccessibilityGroup
  ScreenshotProofContext
  CompletionTelemetryTag

Rules:

  • prompts are short;
  • prompts use input glyphs from the input system;
  • prompts should not block combat unless explicitly modal;
  • prompts should complete through world state, not through UI timers alone;
  • dismissed/completed prompts save;
  • settings control tutorial verbosity, duration, replay, and text size;
  • tutorial proof should verify gameplay telemetry remains unchanged when

tutorials are hidden.

Trigger types:

  • volume trigger;
  • first input/action trigger;
  • world-state trigger;
  • death/failure trigger;
  • item pickup/equip trigger;
  • boss/encounter trigger;
  • NPC/tutorial mentor trigger.

Tutorial prompt categories:

Tutorial.Combat
Tutorial.Dodge
Tutorial.Interact
Tutorial.Inventory
Tutorial.Campsite
Tutorial.Death
Tutorial.Spell
Tutorial.WorldState
Tutorial.Map
Tutorial.Vendor

Tutorials should use CommonUI/Enhanced Input glyph data and localization targets from P21. If a prompt says "Press E," that is a bug unless the glyph text comes from the input profile.

#17.9 Map, discovery, memories, and world clues

Map/discovery content is part of P12 because it is how a questless game guides the player.

Data:

PDA_Discovery
  DiscoveryId
  RegionTag
  POITag
  MemoryTag
  RevealState
  MapIcon
  LoreText
  RewardPolicy
  RepeatPolicy

PDA_MapHint
  HintId
  ObjectiveStep
  RegionBounds
  IconPolicy
  FogPolicy
  TextClue
  RequiredState
  BlockedState
  RevealEffects

Rules:

  • discoveries set world-state tags;
  • map hints reveal only as much as design intends;
  • memory/lore text is localized and save-persistent;
  • repeatable POIs separate "discovered once" from "loot reset available";
  • map UI should not become a quest log unless design chooses that path.

Memory echoes are useful because they can carry story without requiring a traditional quest journal. They still need the same rules as NPC dialogue: localized text, world-state requirements, save identity, subtitle/accessibility policy, and proof that they do not replay as "new" after save/load unless the profile says so.

#17.10 Schedules, fates, and living NPCs

Do not build a full life simulator early. Build a state-driven schedule/fate model that can grow.

Data:

PDA_NPCScheduleProfile
  NPCId
  ScheduleEntries
  FallbackLocation
  WorldStateOverrides
  WeatherOverrides
  TimeOverrides
  DeathOrDeparturePolicy
  StreamingPolicy
  SaveSnapshotPolicy

FAscentiaScheduleEntry
  RequiredState
  TimeWindow
  WeatherTags
  DataLayer
  TargetLocation
  SmartObjectQuery
  DialogueSetOverride
  VendorProfileOverride
  ServiceProfileOverrides
  Priority

Use schedule profiles for:

  • campsite NPCs;
  • wandering merchants;
  • rescued prisoners;
  • questless memory echoes;
  • region-state NPC movement;
  • post-boss world changes.

Validation:

  • no NPC has two active schedule entries of equal priority;
  • every schedule has a fallback;
  • streamed-out NPCs save only stable identity/state, not transient actor refs;
  • Data Layer requirements are valid;
  • dialogue/vendor overrides exist.

Schedule state should save stable facts only:

FAscentiaNPCStateSnapshot
  NPCId
  CurrentFateTag
  ActiveScheduleEntryId
  LastKnownWorldStateTags
  DialogueMemory
  VendorLimitedStockState
  bIsDeadOrDeparted

Do not save transient actor pointers, current path segment, animation state, or Smart Object claim handles. Those are reconstructed from stable state after the map and relevant Data Layers load.

#17.11 P12 evidence, tooling, and reports

P12 needs evidence because world-state bugs are often silent. The player simply finds an NPC gone, a vendor item missing, an objective clue stale, or a tutorial repeating forever.

Report schema:

FAscentiaWorldScriptingReport
  RunId
  MapOrRegion
  WorldStateRegistryVersion
  ObjectiveSetsTested
  DialogueSetsTested
  VendorProfilesTested
  TutorialPromptsTested
  InteractionDefinitionsTested
  SaveFixturesTested
  UnreachableLines
  ImpossibleObjectiveSteps
  MissingLocalizationKeys
  MissingInputGlyphs
  InvalidServiceActions
  UnsafeInteractionRequests
  SaveLoadMismatches
  SuggestedRepairs

First P12 proof scenarios:

world_state_objective
  set/clear tags
  verify objective HUD, map hint, NPC clue, save fragment

npc_dialogue_state
  interact across state combinations
  verify selected line, effects, localization, subtitle

vendor_service_trade
  buy, sold-out, insufficient currency, save/load

tutorial_prompt_state
  trigger, dismiss, complete, hide via settings, save/load

interaction_security
  stale actor, out-of-range, blocked state, wrong item, rate limit

schedule_state
  load world state
  resolve NPC schedule entry, dialogue/vendor overrides, fallback location

Primary tools:

EUW_AscentiaWorldStateGraph
EUW_AscentiaWorldScriptNodeEditor
EUW_AscentiaObjectiveAuthor
EUW_AscentiaNPCStoryGraph
EUW_AscentiaDialoguePreview
EUW_AscentiaVendorStockPreview
EUW_AscentiaServiceProfilePreview
EUW_AscentiaTutorialPromptPreview
EUW_AscentiaInteractionAudit
EUW_AscentiaInteractionSecurityAudit
EUW_AscentiaMapHintPreview
EUW_AscentiaNPCScheduleSimulator

EUW_AscentiaWorldStateGraph should be the center of this phase. It needs to show not just tags, but who sets them, who reads them, whether they save, whether they replicate later, and which content becomes unreachable if a tag path is wrong.

#17.12 Designer surfaces

Runtime:

WBP_AscentiaDialogueBox
WBP_AscentiaSubtitleLine
WBP_AscentiaObjectiveTracker
WBP_AscentiaShopScreen
WBP_AscentiaVendorRow
WBP_AscentiaTutorialPrompt
WBP_AscentiaInteractionPrompt
WBP_AscentiaMapHint
WBP_AscentiaMemoryEcho

Editor:

EUW_AscentiaWorldStateGraph
EUW_AscentiaWorldScriptNodeEditor
EUW_AscentiaObjectiveAuthor
EUW_AscentiaDialoguePreview
EUW_AscentiaNPCStoryGraph
EUW_AscentiaVendorStockPreview
EUW_AscentiaServiceProfilePreview
EUW_AscentiaTutorialPromptPreview
EUW_AscentiaInteractionAudit
EUW_AscentiaInteractionSecurityAudit
EUW_AscentiaMapHintPreview
EUW_AscentiaNPCScheduleSimulator

Data:

PDA_ObjectiveSet_*
PDA_WorldScriptNode_*
PDA_WorldStateRegistry_*
PDA_WorldStateMigration_*
PDA_InteractionDefinition_*
PDA_NPCDefinition_*
PDA_DialogueSet_*
PDA_VendorProfile_*
PDA_ServiceProfile_*
PDA_TutorialPrompt_*
PDA_Discovery_*
PDA_MapHint_*
PDA_NPCScheduleProfile_*
DT_DialogueLines_*
DT_TutorialPrompts
DT_VendorStock
DT_ObjectiveText
DT_WorldStateTags
DT_Interactions
DT_ServiceProfiles
DT_NPCSchedules
DT_Discoveries
DT_MapHints

#17.13 P12 proof gates

P12 is not complete until:

  1. Server interaction requests revalidate range, LOS, target identity, and

CanInteract before executing.

  1. World-state tags persist through save/load and migration.
  2. Designer-authored objective assets drive the HUD tracker.
  3. NPC dialogue lines can be authored outside C++, previewed, localized, and

validated for unreachable states.

  1. Vendor stock comes from assets/rows, supports at least buy and sold-out

persistence, and has a real shop UI.

  1. Tutorial prompts come from authored assets, use input glyphs, persist

dismissed state, and respect tutorial settings.

  1. Discovery/map/memory clues set and read world state consistently.
  2. A campsite NPC, a vendor, a memory echo, an objective chain, and a tutorial

prompt work in PIE and packaged Development.

  1. Dashboard evidence includes world-state objective, NPC dialogue, vendor

trade, tutorial prompt, save/load persistence, and interaction-security reports.

  1. Designers can answer "which asset owns this line/objective/vendor item" from

the Editor without reading native code.

#17.14 P12 starter implementation package

The first shippable P12 package should convert the current working runtime pieces into an authored, inspectable, evidence-producing world-scripting layer. The goal is not to invent a heavyweight quest system. The goal is to make world state, interaction, objective guidance, NPC dialogue, vendor services, tutorials, discoveries, and schedules share one Designer-visible contract.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldStateTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldScriptTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/World/AscentiaWorldScriptSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Interaction/AscentiaInteractionTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Interaction/AscentiaInteractionSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Objectives/AscentiaObjectiveTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Objectives/AscentiaObjectiveSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Dialogue/AscentiaDialogueTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Dialogue/AscentiaDialogueSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Services/AscentiaServiceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Services/AscentiaServiceSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/NPC/AscentiaNPCScheduleTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/NPC/AscentiaNPCScheduleSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/World/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Interaction/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Objectives/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Dialogue/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Services/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/NPC/...

Editor module layout:

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/World/AscentiaWorldStateValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Interaction/AscentiaInteractionSecurityValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Dialogue/AscentiaDialogueValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Services/AscentiaServiceValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/NPC/AscentiaNPCScheduleValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaWorldScriptAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaWorldStateSaveFragmentTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaInteractionSecurityTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaObjectiveResolutionTest.cpp

Subsystem boundaries:

UAscentiaWorldStateSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  owns save-wide state tags, counters, migration, and objective memories
  BuildSaveFragment()
  ApplySaveFragment()
  QueryWorldState()
  SetWorldTag()
  ClearWorldTag()

UAscentiaWorldScriptSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  executes map-local world script nodes against world-state authority
  EvaluateWorldScriptNode(Context, Node)
  ApplyWorldScriptNode(Context, Node)
  WriteWorldScriptReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaInteractionSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  owns server-side interaction request validation and interaction evidence
  BuildInteractionPreview(Player)
  ServerValidateInteraction(Request)
  ExecuteInteraction(Request)

UAscentiaObjectiveSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  resolves objective sets from world state and exposes HUD/map clue views
  RegisterObjectiveSet(ObjectiveSet)
  ResolveActiveObjectiveView(Context)
  WriteObjectiveEvidence(OutputPath)

UAscentiaDialogueSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  resolves dialogue lines from NPC definition, dialogue set, and world state
  ResolveDialogueLine(NPCDefinition, DialogueSet, Context)
  ApplyDialogueEffects(Line, Context)
  WriteDialogueEvidence(OutputPath)

UAscentiaServiceSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  executes vendor, crafting, respec, rest, and other NPC/world services
  PreviewService(Request)
  ExecuteService(Request)
  WriteServiceEvidence(OutputPath)

UAscentiaNPCScheduleSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  resolves schedule/fate state for spawned or streamed NPCs
  ResolveScheduleEntry(Profile, Context)
  ApplyScheduleState(NPCActor, Entry)
  WriteScheduleEvidence(OutputPath)

Authority rule:

WorldState is save-wide truth.
WorldScript executes transitions.
Interaction is the secure entry point.
Objective, Dialogue, Service, Tutorial, Map, and Schedule systems read the same
state and may request authored effects through WorldScript.

Starter content folders:

/Game/Ascentia/Design/WorldState/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/WorldScripts/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Interactions/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Objectives/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/NPCs/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Dialogue/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Vendors/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Services/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Tutorials/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Discoveries/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/MapHints/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Schedules/

Starter assets:

PDA_WorldStateRegistry_MVP
PDA_WorldStateMigration_MVP_000
PDA_WorldScriptNode_BelowEntryUnlocked
PDA_InteractionDefinition_LootCorpse
PDA_InteractionDefinition_CampsiteRest
PDA_ObjectiveSet_MVPArenaIntro
PDA_NPCDefinition_CampsiteGuide
PDA_DialogueSet_CampsiteGuide_Intro
PDA_VendorProfile_CampsiteGuide_Basic
PDA_ServiceProfile_BuyItem
PDA_ServiceProfile_CraftAtCampsite
PDA_TutorialPrompt_Interact
PDA_TutorialPrompt_Dodge
PDA_Discovery_MVPArenaGate
PDA_MapHint_FirstGate
PDA_NPCScheduleProfile_CampsiteGuide_MVP

Asset authoring rules:

  • world state identity should use Gameplay Tags or a validated tag-like wrapper

with a clear migration path to Gameplay Tags;

  • every user-facing line uses FText and a string table or localization target;
  • every prompt that names an input reads from Enhanced Input/CommonUI glyph data;
  • every interaction asset states whether it is lightweight, Smart Object-backed,

or service-backed;

  • every service profile states its authority policy and transaction owner;
  • every objective step must resolve from world state, not widget-local booleans;
  • every dialogue line, tutorial prompt, service row, and map hint carries an

asset ID so a Designer can find ownership from an in-game proof report.

Starter evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/WorldState/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Interaction/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Objectives/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Dialogue/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Services/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Tutorials/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Schedules/{RunId}/report.json

Commandlet and script contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaWorldScriptAudit
  -Scenario=MVPArenaIntro
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12
  -Map=/Game/Ascentia/Maps/MVP_Arena

Tools/run_p12_world_scenario.ps1
  -Scenario WorldStateObjective
  -Map /Game/Ascentia/Maps/MVP_Arena
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12

First packaged proof target:

world_state_objective
  Apply `World.Objective.MVP.Intro.Started`
  verify HUD objective, map hint, NPC clue, save fragment, and report row

interaction_security
  attempt valid loot, out-of-range loot, blocked LOS, stale target, spam request
  verify only the valid request applies effects

npc_dialogue_state
  interact before/after objective and vendor states
  verify selected dialogue line, fallback line, effects, and localization keys

vendor_service_trade
  buy one limited stock item, fail insufficient currency, save/load sold-out
  verify item transaction evidence and world-state sold-out tag

tutorial_prompt_state
  trigger, dismiss, complete, hide by setting, save/load
  verify input glyph source and completion state

Editor surfaces:

EUW_AscentiaWorldStateGraph
  search tags, see owners, show effects, validate migrations

EUW_AscentiaWorldScriptNodeEditor
  preview condition/effect evaluation against fixture states

EUW_AscentiaObjectiveAuthor
  preview active step by world-state fixture, map hint, and HUD text

EUW_AscentiaNPCStoryGraph
  preview dialogue, vendor unlocks, tutorial unlocks, and fate changes

EUW_AscentiaServiceProfilePreview
  preview buy/sell/craft/respec/service requests with failure reasons

EUW_AscentiaInteractionSecurityAudit
  run range/LOS/reservation/rate-limit cases against interaction definitions

EUW_AscentiaMapHintPreview
  preview objective and discovery hints by region/state

EUW_AscentiaNPCScheduleSimulator
  scrub time/weather/world-state fixtures and verify active schedule entry

Implementation tickets:

P12-001 World-state registry and Gameplay Tag migration bridge
  Add `PDA_WorldStateRegistry_MVP`, validate duplicate/unowned tags, and expose
  Blueprint-readable tag descriptions.

P12-002 Save fragment and migration proof
  Implement versioned `FAscentiaWorldStateSaveFragment`, fixture saves, and
  automated save/load comparison.

P12-003 World script node execution report
  Implement `PDA_WorldScriptNode`, transactional effects, failure rows, and
  `AscentiaWorldScriptAudit`.

P12-004 Server-side interaction validation
  Move current direct interact execution behind `UAscentiaInteractionSubsystem`
  and prove range, LOS, target identity, `CanInteract`, and rate limiting.

P12-005 Objective asset resolution and HUD bridge
  Move runtime objective registrations into `PDA_ObjectiveSet_*` assets and
  keep the current HUD getters as the presentation bridge.

P12-006 Dialogue asset resolver and localization audit
  Move NPC inline dialogue rows into `PDA_DialogueSet_*`, validate fallback
  lines, unreachable state, and missing string-table keys.

P12-007 Vendor/service profile execution
  Move vendor stock/service behavior into `PDA_VendorProfile_*` and
  `PDA_ServiceProfile_*`, keeping item transactions server-authoritative.

P12-008 Tutorial prompt authored asset path
  Move prompt definitions into `PDA_TutorialPrompt_*`, resolve input glyphs from
  the input profile, and persist dismissed/completed prompt state.

P12-009 Discovery, map hint, and schedule stub
  Add `PDA_Discovery_*`, `PDA_MapHint_*`, and the first
  `PDA_NPCScheduleProfile_*` with Data Layer and fallback validation.

P12-010 P12 packaged proof suite
  Run the five starter scenarios in PIE and packaged Development, publish JSON
  reports, and add dashboard cards for pass/fail, missing assets, and
  impossible states.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build PDA_WorldStateRegistry_MVP and save fragment migration first.
  2. Put interactions behind UAscentiaInteractionSubsystem before more

interactables are added.

  1. Convert the current loot corpse, campsite, NPC, vendor, and tutorial examples

into authored P12 assets.

  1. Add editor validation and evidence reports before expanding content volume.
  2. Only then add larger dialogue graphs, multiple vendors, Smart Object-backed

stations, or schedule/fate complexity.

#17.15 Narrative, cinematics, VO, and localization production package

The P12 world-scripting package owns the state truth. This companion package owns the production layer that lets narrative content ship without becoming a pile of hard-wired Blueprint scenes: conversations, barks, VO, subtitles, cutscenes, camera locks, localization, and the handoff between authored presentation and gameplay authority.

The engine-facing foundation is straightforward:

  • Sequencer and Level Sequence assets are the authored timeline layer for

cinematics, staging, camera beats, animation tracks, and one-off scene timing.

  • Dialogue Voice and Dialogue Wave assets are the final VO/subtitle binding

layer when speaker/listener context matters.

  • Sound Classes, Sound Mixes, Sound Attenuation, and audio components define

dialogue ducking, playback behavior, priority, distance, and completion events.

  • FText, String Tables, and Localization Targets define the text pipeline.
  • World-state tags, objective state, NPC definitions, and interaction requests

remain the gameplay truth that narrative content reads from and writes to.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Narrative/AscentiaNarrativeTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Narrative/AscentiaConversationTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Narrative/AscentiaBarkTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Narrative/AscentiaCinematicTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Narrative/AscentiaSubtitleTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Narrative/AscentiaNarrativeSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Narrative/AscentiaCinematicSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Narrative/...

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Narrative/AscentiaNarrativeValidation.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaBarkPriorityTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaCinematicRestoreTest.cpp

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Narrative/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Conversations/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Barks/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Cinematics/

/Game/Ascentia/Cinematics/ShotProof/
/Game/Ascentia/Audio/Dialogue/

Starter assets:

PDA_NarrativeScene_MVPCampsiteIntro
PDA_ConversationGraph_CampsiteGuide_Intro
PDA_BarkSet_MVPArenaCombat
PDA_CinematicScene_BelowEntry_MVP
PDA_CinematicBindingPolicy_PlayerAndGuide
PDA_VOProfile_CampsiteGuide
PDA_SubtitleStyle_DefaultReadable
PDA_LocalizationTargetProfile_Narrative
DT_NarrativeLines_MVP
DT_BarkRules_MVP
ST_Ascentia_Dialogue
ST_Ascentia_Barks
ST_Ascentia_Cinematics
LS_BelowEntry_MVP
DV_CampsiteGuide
DV_Player_Default
DW_CampsiteGuide_Intro_001

Core data structures:

FAscentiaNarrativeLineId
  StableKey
  Namespace
  SourceAsset

FAscentiaNarrativeLine
  LineId
  SpeakerTag
  ListenerPolicy
  Text
  StringTableKey
  DialogueWave
  VoiceActorDirection
  SubtitlePolicy
  WorldStateConditions
  WorldScriptEffects

FAscentiaConversationNode
  NodeId
  CandidateLines
  PlayerResponses
  EntryConditions
  ExitEffects

FAscentiaBarkRule
  BarkId
  Priority
  CooldownSeconds
  MaxRepeatsPerWindow
  InterruptPolicy
  RequiredGameplayTags
  SuppressedGameplayTags

FAscentiaCinematicRequest
  SceneAsset
  Instigator
  Participants
  ControlPolicy
  CameraPolicy
  CombatMotionPolicy
  RestorePolicy

FAscentiaCinematicBindingPolicy
  Sequence
  BindingName
  ActorSource
  RequiredTags
  MissingActorPolicy

FAscentiaSubtitleCue
  LineId
  Text
  MinSeconds
  MaxCharactersPerSecond
  SpeakerLabelPolicy
  AccessibilityProfile

FAscentiaNarrativeAuditReport
  MissingLines
  MissingDialogueWaves
  MissingSoundWaves
  MissingStringTableKeys
  MissingSequenceBindings
  SubtitleReadabilityFailures
  GameplayRestoreFailures

Subsystem boundaries:

UAscentiaNarrativeSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  ResolveConversationNode(Context, Graph)
  RequestBark(Context, BarkSet)
  ResolveNarrativeLine(LineId, Context)
  ResolveSubtitleCue(LineId, Context)
  ApplyNarrativeEffects(Line, Context)
  WriteNarrativeEvidence(OutputPath)

UAscentiaCinematicSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  RequestCinematic(Request)
  BindSequenceParticipants(Request)
  ApplyCinematicControlPolicy(Request)
  RestoreGameplayControl(Result)
  WriteCinematicEvidence(OutputPath)

Authority and ownership rules:

  • Conversations and barks may ask for world-state changes, rewards, flags, and

objective transitions only through P12 world scripts.

  • Cinematics may not hide persistent gameplay changes inside Level Sequence

tracks. Persistent effects must be explicit world-script requests with evidence rows.

  • Combat grabs, forced movement, stagger locks, and cinematic attacks route

through the P11 combat motion authority so authored scenes do not bypass movement, collision, ledge, or root-motion rules.

  • Player input, camera mode, HUD visibility, time dilation, target lock, and

movement control must be restored after every cinematic, including skips, aborts, death, level travel, and PIE stop.

  • Dialogue Waves are final playback bindings, not the source of narrative truth.

The stable line ID and authored line row remain the source of ownership, localization, subtitle policy, and validation.

  • Every user-facing line must have FText, a stable localization key, source

asset ownership, subtitle policy, speaker label policy, and a proof path.

  • Barks are rate-limited, priority-scored, interrupt-aware, and suppressed by

relevant combat/dialogue/cinematic tags so the game does not talk over itself.

  • Sequencer bindings are explicit. Placed actors, spawned cinematic actors, and

player/avatar participants must be declared in the cinematic scene asset.

  • A placeholder character, costume, or prototype pawn cannot be hard-referenced

by narrative production assets unless that dependency is marked as temporary and reported as fallback debt.

Designer-facing tools:

EUW_AscentiaNarrativeGraph
  browse conversation nodes, conditions, effects, speaker/listener context, and
  owning assets

EUW_AscentiaDialoguePreview
  preview a line with fixture world state, selected player identity, subtitles,
  optional Dialogue Wave playback, and missing-key warnings

EUW_AscentiaBarkMixer
  simulate combat/exploration bark requests, priority, cooldown, suppression,
  and interrupt behavior

EUW_AscentiaCinematicBinder
  inspect Level Sequence bindings, participant source policy, missing actors,
  skip/restore policy, camera lock, and gameplay handoff points

EUW_AscentiaVOTracker
  list recorded/missing lines, Dialogue Voice/Wave bindings, Sound Wave assets,
  actor direction, and export status

EUW_AscentiaSubtitleAccessibilityPreview
  preview subtitle scale, contrast, speaker labels, line breaks, reading speed,
  and overlapping dialogue warnings

EUW_AscentiaLocalizationMatrix
  show line ID, namespace, string-table key, gathered target, culture status,
  stale source text, pseudo-loc result, and packaged LocRes coverage

Evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Narrative/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Cinematics/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/VO/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Subtitles/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Localization/{RunId}/report.json

Commandlet and script contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaNarrativeAudit
  -Root=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Narrative
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Narrative

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaCinematicAudit
  -SequenceRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Cinematics/Sequences
  -Map=/Game/Ascentia/MVP/Maps/MVP_Arena
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Cinematics

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaVOAudit
  -LineTable=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Narrative/DT_NarrativeLines_MVP
  -VoiceRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Audio/Dialogue
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/VO

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaSubtitleAudit
  -Culture=en
  -TextScale=1.0
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Subtitles

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaLocalizationAudit
  -Target=AscentiaNarrative
  -Cultures=en,pseudo
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Localization

Starter proof matrix:

conversation_state_selection
  before/after objective state chooses correct line, fallback line, subtitle,
  optional Dialogue Wave, and world-script effect evidence

bark_priority_suppression
  combat, low health, loot, and discovery requests compete
  verify priority, cooldown, suppression, and no repeated spam

cinematic_restore_control
  play, skip, abort, death-abort, and map-travel-abort a Level Sequence
  verify input/camera/HUD/time/target-lock/movement restoration

cinematic_binding_audit
  load MVP sequences, resolve participant bindings, and report missing actors,
  hard prototype references, and fallback debt

subtitle_accessibility
  verify max characters per second, line wrapping, speaker labels, text scale,
  contrast, and no overlap with critical HUD panels

localization_matrix
  verify every narrative line has a stable key, string table entry, gathered
  target, pseudo-loc result, and packaged culture coverage

save_load_after_cinematic
  complete a cinematic-triggered world-state effect, save, load, and verify the
  scene does not replay incorrectly unless the replay policy allows it

Implementation tickets:

P12N-001 Narrative line ID and table model
  Add `FAscentiaNarrativeLineId`, `FAscentiaNarrativeLine`,
  `DT_NarrativeLines_MVP`, `ST_Ascentia_Dialogue`, and ownership validation.

P12N-002 Conversation graph resolver
  Add `PDA_ConversationGraph_*`, fixture-based state resolution, fallback line
  handling, world-script effect requests, and dialogue evidence rows.

P12N-003 Bark request and rate limiter
  Add `PDA_BarkSet_*`, priority/cooldown/suppression rules, and a bark mixer
  preview that proves no spam during combat.

P12N-004 VO and Dialogue Wave binding audit
  Add Dialogue Voice/Wave binding validation, Sound Wave presence checks,
  voice actor direction fields, and missing-recording reports.

P12N-005 Subtitle accessibility preview
  Add subtitle cue policy, CommonUI preview widget, reading-speed audit,
  text-scale/contrast checks, and overlap warnings.

P12N-006 Cinematic scene request and binding policy
  Add `PDA_CinematicScene_*`, participant binding declarations, skip/restore
  policy, and Level Sequence missing-binding reports.

P12N-007 Sequencer and WorldScript handoff proof
  Prove that a cinematic can request persistent state changes only through P12
  world scripts and that save/load handles replay policy correctly.

P12N-008 Localization matrix and pseudo-loc audit
  Add localization target profile validation, String Table key coverage,
  pseudo-loc proof, and packaged LocRes coverage checks.

P12N-009 Narrative dashboard cards
  Add dashboard cards for missing line ownership, missing VO, missing sequence
  bindings, subtitle failures, stale localization, and cinematic restore
  failures.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build P12N-001 through P12N-003 before recording or buying VO.
  2. Add P12N-006 and P12N-007 before production cutscenes are authored at scale.
  3. Make subtitle and localization audits mandatory before any narrative content

is considered shippable.

  1. Keep the first proof tiny: campsite guide conversation, one combat bark set,

one Below-entry sequence, and one pseudo-localized culture.

#17.16 Character performance, facial animation, gestures, and scene takes package

Narrative text, VO binding, and Sequencer bindings are not enough for AAA character work. As soon as Ascentia has custom player avatars, important NPCs, boss intros, vendors, and voiced scenes, the pipeline also needs a repeatable way to answer: does this line have a believable face, body gesture, camera take, runtime avatar binding, subtitles, skip/restore behavior, and accessibility proof?

Primary references:

  • MetaHuman Animator generates facial animation from video and/or audio sources:

<https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/metahuman/metahuman-animator>

  • Epic's facial performance capture guidelines describe capture device,

condition, and performer requirements: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/metahuman/facial-performance-capture-guidelines>

  • MetaHuman Animator's audio-driven animation can process audio into facial

animation in real time or offline: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/metahuman/audio-driven-animation>

  • Control Rig is Unreal's in-engine rigging and animation correction surface:

<https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/control-rig-in-unreal-engine>

  • Take Recorder records gameplay, Live Link actors, and performances into

Sequencer for iterative review: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/take-recorder-in-unreal-engine>

  • Sequencer is the Level Sequence, track, camera, keyframe, and shot authoring

layer: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/unreal-engine-sequencer-movie-tool-overview>

This package deliberately stays engine-native first. Ascentia can adopt MetaHuman Animator where it fits, but the contract should not assume every shipping character is a MetaHuman. The stable Ascentia layer is performance data, line IDs, avatar binding profiles, face/gesture tracks, solve quality reports, and runtime proof.

Core structs:

FAscentiaPerformanceLineBinding
  LineId
  SpeakerTag
  ListenerPolicy
  DialogueWave
  SoundWave
  FacialAnimation
  GestureClip
  LookAtPolicy
  SubtitleCue
  PerformanceProfile

FAscentiaFacialSolveProfile
  SolveProfileTag
  SourceKind
  TargetRigTag
  RequiredCurves
  OptionalCurves
  EmotionMoodTag
  BlinkPolicy
  HeadMotionPolicy
  RetargetPolicy
  QualityThresholds

FAscentiaFacialSolveReport
  LinesExpected
  LinesWithSolvedFace
  MissingCurves
  MissingDialogueWave
  AudioDurationMismatches
  LipSyncDriftFrames
  SolveQualityFailures
  RetargetFailures
  BlockingFindings

FAscentiaGestureProfile
  GestureTag
  BodyRigTag
  ClipAsset
  AdditiveLayerPolicy
  HandOccupancyPolicy
  WeaponVisibilityPolicy
  RootMotionPolicy
  LookAtPolicy
  BlendIn
  BlendOut

FAscentiaSceneTakeProfile
  SceneTag
  LevelSequence
  TakeNumber
  Participants
  CameraCuts
  DialogueLines
  PerformanceBindings
  SkipPolicy
  RestorePolicy
  SaveReplayPolicy
  AccessibilityPolicy

FAscentiaAvatarSceneBindingReport
  SceneTag
  AvatarProfile
  SkeletalMesh
  FaceRig
  BodyRig
  GroomProfile
  ClothProfile
  MissingBindings
  ClippingFrames
  EyeLineFailures
  SubtitleOverlapFailures

Runtime policy:

  • The narrative line remains the source of truth. Facial solves, gesture clips,

and takes attach to FAscentiaNarrativeLineId; they do not invent parallel line identity.

  • The custom character creator cannot be treated as a later exception. Player

avatar scene bindings must prove body preset, face rig, groom, cloth, armor, and camera framing compatibility through P19 avatar assembly profiles.

  • Audio-driven facial animation is acceptable for first proofs and minor NPCs.

Important scenes should carry capture provenance, solve quality, and human review state.

  • Control Rig corrections are authored assets with owner, source, review state,

and retarget policy. They are not loose edits inside one-off sequences.

  • Take Recorder output enters staging first. Approved takes promote only through

a take policy that records source, actor, scene, line IDs, timecode/duration, and replacement risk.

  • Gesture layers must declare hand occupancy and weapon visibility. A merchant

gesture, boss taunt, or player silent reaction should not clip through sword, shield, cape, hair, or armor by accident.

  • Look-at and eye-line rules are data, not per-sequence improvisation. Dialogue

scenes should know who the speaker is addressing, whether the player avatar can be framed, and what fallback to use if the target is absent.

  • Runtime playback still routes control, skip, restore, save/load, subtitle,

camera, audio, and motion policies through the section 17.15 narrative and cinematic subsystems.

Designer surfaces:

EUW_AscentiaPerformanceTakeBoard
  Shows scene, take, line IDs, actors, source capture, review status, sequence
  bindings, duration drift, and promotion blockers.

EUW_AscentiaFacialSolveReview
  Plays line audio against facial curves, viseme/curve coverage, emotion mood,
  blink/head motion policy, solve quality, and lip-sync drift frames.

EUW_AscentiaGestureAndLookAtLab
  Previews gesture clips with selected body rig, weapon visibility, hands busy,
  look-at target, blend windows, and subtitle timing.

EUW_AscentiaAvatarSceneBindingLab
  Runs cinematic/dialogue shots against player avatar presets, NPC variants,
  grooms, cloth, armor, camera framing, subtitles, and accessibility profiles.

Evidence:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Performance/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/FacialSolves/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Gestures/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/SceneTakes/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/AvatarSceneBindings/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Performance/{RunId}/screenshots/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Performance/{RunId}/clips/

Proof gates:

Performance_LineBinding_Coverage
FacialSolve_AudioDriven_CampsiteGuide
FacialSolve_CurveCoverage_NoMissingRequiredCurves
Gesture_WeaponVisibility_NoHandClip
LookAt_PlayerNPC_EyeLine
SceneTake_BelowEntry_TakePromotion
AvatarSceneBinding_PlayerPreset_MaleFemaleBodyRanges
SubtitleCamera_NoOverlapAt200Percent
SkipRestore_SaveLoad_AfterPerformanceScene

Implementation tickets:

P12P-001 Performance line binding structs and profiles
  Add `FAscentiaPerformanceLineBinding`, performance profile assets, line-to-face
  and line-to-gesture ownership validation.

P12P-002 Facial solve profile and audit
  Add solve profile assets, required curve coverage, audio duration matching,
  lip-sync drift reporting, and Facial Solve Review.

P12P-003 Gesture and look-at profile slice
  Add gesture profiles, hand occupancy, weapon visibility, root/additive policy,
  look-at target rules, and Gesture And LookAt Lab.

P12P-004 Scene take staging and promotion policy
  Add scene take profiles, Take Recorder staging metadata, human review state,
  promotion blockers, and take replacement warnings.

P12P-005 Avatar scene binding proof
  Run scenes against player avatar presets, NPC variants, grooms, cloth, armor,
  camera framing, subtitle scale, and accessibility profiles.

P12P-006 Control Rig correction ownership
  Add correction asset provenance, owning scene/line references, retarget policy,
  and stale correction warnings.

P12P-007 Dashboard and package gates
  Add dashboard cards and package blockers for missing facial solves, missing
  gestures, unresolved scene/avatar bindings, drift, clipping, and restore
  failures.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build P12P-001 before buying/recording a large amount of VO or performance.
  2. Build P12P-002 for the campsite guide and one important NPC before adding

more voiced characters.

  1. Build P12P-003 and P12P-005 before the custom creator supplies the default

player avatar for cinematics.

  1. Build P12P-004 before teams start iterating on many takes in Sequencer.
  2. Build P12P-007 before any scene is called milestone-ready.

#17.17 Player guidance, onboarding, memories, and questless objective readability package

P12 also needs a guidance companion because Ascentia is intentionally not a traditional quest-log game. The player still needs readable intent, reminders, tutorials, map clues, NPC hints, and memory echoes. The rule is simple: guidance is a view over world-state truth, not a second objective system hidden inside UI widgets or one-off Blueprints.

Accessibility and platform guidance should shape the first version, not arrive as late polish. Xbox Accessibility Guideline 116 and WCAG timing guidance both push timed content toward enough reading/interaction time, extension, disable, or replay options. Xbox Accessibility Guideline 114 pushes long UI guidance into clear sections with labels near related controls. Xbox Accessibility Guideline 115 warns against destructive or irreversible actions that rely only on button holds or unclear consequences. Xbox Accessibility Guideline 112 supports clear, consistent UI navigation for new, neurodiverse, and assistive-tech players. Guidance text also inherits the text-scale and readability requirements already captured in the accessibility package.

Source/Ascentia/Public/Guidance/AscentiaGuidanceHistoryTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Guidance/...
