/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_GuidancePolicy_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_ObjectiveHintSet_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_TutorialPromptSet_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_DiscoveryMemorySet_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_MapClueProfile_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_GuidanceAccessibilityProfile_MVP
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_FirstRunGuidanceProfile_MVP

Core data structures:

FAscentiaGuidancePolicy
  PolicyTag
  VerbositySettingTag
  MaxActiveHints
  ReminderCooldownSeconds
  bAllowReplay
  bAllowDisableTimedHints
  bRespectSpoilerTags
  bRequireWorldStateSource
  AccessibilityProfileTag

FAscentiaObjectiveHint
  HintTag
  ObjectiveTag
  WorldStateRequirements
  DisplayTextKey
  MapClueTag
  NPCClueLineId
  MemoryClueTag
  Priority
  CooldownSeconds
  bDismissible
  bReplayable
  bBlocksIfStale
  AccessibilityTags

FAscentiaTutorialPrompt
  PromptTag
  TriggerWorldState
  CompletedWorldState
  InputActionTags
  DisplayTextKey
  MinimumDisplaySeconds
  MaxRepeats
  RepeatCooldownSeconds
  bCanDisable
  bCanReplay
  bRequiresPracticeAction
  AlternativeInputNoteKey

FAscentiaDiscoveryMemory
  MemoryTag
  RegionTag
  WorldStateRequirements
  TextKey
  SpeakerOrSourceTag
  MapPinPolicyTag
  RewardPreviewTag
  SpoilerBandTag
  MemoryArchivePolicyTag

FAscentiaGuidanceEvent
  EventTag
  SourceSystemTag
  WorldStateTag
  HintTag
  LocalUserIndex
  TimestampUtc
  ActionTaken
  bDisplayed
  bDismissed
  bCompleted
  FailureReasonTag

FAscentiaGuidanceView
  ActiveHints
  TutorialRows
  MapClues
  MemoryRows
  ReplayRows
  AccessibilityState
  DebugReasons

Subsystem boundary:

UAscentiaGuidanceSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  EvaluateGuidance(Context)
  NotifyWorldStateChanged(Tag)
  NotifyInputActionObserved(ActionTag)
  MarkHintDismissed(HintTag, LocalUserIndex)
  MarkTutorialCompleted(PromptTag, LocalUserIndex)
  BuildGuidanceView(LocalUserIndex)
  WriteGuidanceEvidence(OutputPath)

Ownership rules:

  • No UI widget, map marker, tutorial popup, NPC Blueprint, or memory archive may

own private objective truth.

  • Every hint resolves from world-state tags, stable text keys, authored clue

assets, and the guidance policy.

  • Hiding or reducing guidance changes presentation only. Gameplay telemetry,

objective state, save state, and reward state remain unchanged.

  • Timed tutorial prompts must have minimum display time, settings scaling, and a

replay/disable path unless the prompt is safety-critical.

  • Tutorial completion should be based on observed player action when possible:

dodge, lock-on, light attack, interact, loot, equip, cast, block, parry, or open inventory.

  • The guidance layer may present HUD hints, map clues, NPC clue lines, and

memory echoes for the same world-state fact, but it may not turn the game into a checklist unless design explicitly reverses the questless pillar.

  • Stale hints block the package gate. If the world state changes, an obsolete

hint must become unavailable or explain the new state.

  • Spoiler bands gate future-zone, boss, reward, and NPC-fate clues.
  • Destructive or irreversible actions must explain consequence and have an

accessible confirmation route that does not rely only on a button hold.

Designer-facing tools:

EUW_AscentiaGuidanceBoard
  inspect active hints, source world-state tags, owning assets, surface coverage,
  replay/dismiss state, and latest evidence

EUW_AscentiaObjectiveHintRouteLab
  preview one objective through HUD hint, map clue, NPC clue, memory clue, and
  accessibility variants

EUW_AscentiaTutorialPromptSimulator
  simulate trigger/completion action, input glyphs, text scale, repeat cooldown,
  hide/replay settings, and save/load persistence

EUW_AscentiaMapClueAndMemoryPreview
  inspect region discovery, map clue placement policy, memory archive rows, and
  spoiler gates

EUW_AscentiaGuidanceStalenessAudit
  find hints whose world-state requirements no longer match current objective,
  dialogue, discovery, or map data

EUW_AscentiaFirstRunGuidanceLab
  play the first 10 minutes as a guidance route, showing prompt timing,
  readability, dismissal, replay, and completion evidence

Evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Guidance/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/ObjectiveHints/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/TutorialPrompts/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/MapClues/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/MemoryClues/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/FirstRunGuidance/{RunId}/report.json

Commandlet contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaGuidanceAudit
  -Root=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Guidance

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaObjectiveRouteClarity
  -ObjectiveSet=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_ObjectiveHintSet_MVP
  -Map=/Game/Ascentia/Maps/MVP_Arena
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/ObjectiveHints

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaTutorialPromptAudit
  -PromptSet=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Guidance/PDA_TutorialPromptSet_MVP
  -TextScale=1.0,1.5,2.0
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/TutorialPrompts

Starter proof matrix:

guidance_first_run_intro_prompts_readable
  verify first-run prompts have source world-state, minimum display time,
  replay/disable policy, input glyphs, and no clipping at 200 percent text scale

guidance_tutorial_dodge_completes_on_observed_action
  trigger dodge prompt, perform dodge, verify completion world-state and save
  persistence

guidance_tutorial_disabled_does_not_change_gameplay
  disable non-critical prompts, complete the same gameplay route, and verify
  objective/reward telemetry is unchanged

guidance_objective_hint_world_state_source_only
  build active objective view and verify every hint, map clue, NPC clue, and
  memory clue resolves from P12 world-state sources

guidance_no_stale_hint_after_gate_open
  change a gate/boss/objective state, verify obsolete hints disappear or explain
  the updated state

guidance_map_clue_matches_npc_clue_and_memory
  compare map clue, NPC line, memory text, and HUD hint for the same objective
  without conflicting directions

guidance_no_quest_log_checklist_leak
  verify the memory/archive surface does not expose hidden checklist rows,
  future objectives, or reward spoilers

guidance_time_limits_extend_or_disable
  verify timed prompts can be extended, replayed, or disabled according to
  guidance policy and accessibility profile

guidance_spoiler_band_blocks_future_hint
  load early-world state and verify future-zone, boss, reward, and fate hints do
  not appear

guidance_save_load_dismissed_and_completed_persistence
  dismiss one hint, complete one tutorial, save/load, and verify presentation
  state returns correctly without mutating objective state

Implementation tickets:

P12G-001 Guidance policy, event, view, and subsystem spine
  Add `FAscentiaGuidancePolicy`, `FAscentiaGuidanceEvent`,
  `FAscentiaGuidanceView`, and `UAscentiaGuidanceSubsystem` with world-state
  notifications and evidence output.

P12G-002 Objective hint sets and world-state resolver
  Add `PDA_ObjectiveHintSet_MVP`, resolve HUD/map/NPC/memory clues from P12
  world-state tags, and report private UI-owned objective state as a blocker.

P12G-003 Tutorial prompt assets and observed-action completion
  Add `PDA_TutorialPromptSet_MVP`, observed Enhanced Input action completion,
  replay/disable settings, and save/load proof for dismissed/completed prompts.

P12G-004 Map clue and discovery memory bridge
  Add map clue profiles, discovery memories, spoiler bands, and consistency
  checks between HUD, NPC, map, and memory surfaces.

P12G-005 First-run accessibility and timing proof
  Add first-run guidance route fixtures, text-scale checks, minimum display time,
  replay/disable verification, and destructive-action confirmation checks.

P12G-006 Designer Guidance Board and route labs
  Add `EUW_AscentiaGuidanceBoard`, `EUW_AscentiaObjectiveHintRouteLab`,
  `EUW_AscentiaTutorialPromptSimulator`, and staleness audit panels.

P12G-007 Dashboard/package gates
  Add guidance evidence cards and block P12 when stale hints, private objective
  state, missing text keys, spoiler leaks, or unreadable prompts remain.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build P12G-001 through P12G-003 before expanding tutorials or objective

hints beyond the MVP examples.

  1. Add map and memory clue consistency after the first objective route works.
  2. Make text-scale, timing, and replay/disable proof mandatory for first-run

guidance before P21 shell/settings polish.

  1. Keep the first proof tiny: one objective, one map clue, one NPC clue, one

tutorial prompt, one memory row, and one save/load fixture.

#17.18 Factions, reputation, crime, NPC memory, and social consequence package

P12 also needs a social consequence layer. Combat factions decide who can target whom in a fight, but open-world factions decide who remembers, forgives, trades, helps, warns, hunts, hides, or closes a door later. This package keeps that state out of one-off NPC Blueprints and vendor widgets.

Primary references:

  • Gameplay Tags provide the stable hierarchical identity language for faction,

crime, reputation, memory, service, and witness states: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-gameplay-tags-in-unreal-engine>

  • StateTree is Unreal's hierarchical state machine for organized, performant NPC

and world-actor logic: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/state-tree-in-unreal-engine>

  • Smart Objects are player/AI interaction points with a global database,

Gameplay Tag queries, spatial queries, and reservation behavior: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/smart-objects-in-unreal-engine---overview>

  • AI Perception owns sensory awareness and stimulus memory for AI listeners:

<https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/ai-perception-in-unreal-engine>

  • Data Registries support ordered data sources and structured lookup for

faction/reputation tables when content volume grows: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/data-registries-in-unreal-engine>

  • Asynchronous SaveGame writes are the preferred path for meaningful persistent

state because they avoid frame hitches and platform risk: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/saving-and-loading-your-game-in-unreal-engine>

  • CommonUI input routing and UMG optimization rules apply to reputation,

dialogue/service, crime warning, and consequence review screens: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/common-ui-quickstart-guide-for-unreal-engine> and <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/optimization-guidelines-for-umg-in-unreal-engine>

/Game/Ascentia/Tools/EUW_AscentiaFactionReputationBoard
/Game/Ascentia/Tools/EUW_AscentiaCrimeWitnessLab
/Game/Ascentia/Tools/EUW_AscentiaNPCMemoryAndDispositionLab
/Game/Ascentia/Tools/EUW_AscentiaServiceTrustEconomyLab

Core data structures:

FAscentiaFactionDefinition
  FactionTag
  DisplayName
  ParentFactionTag
  AlignmentTags
  DefaultDisposition
  HostilityRules
  ProtectedNPCQuery
  ServiceTags
  CrimePolicyTag
  ReputationProfileTag

FAscentiaFactionRelationship
  SourceFactionTag
  TargetFactionTag
  Disposition
  bHostileByDefault
  bCanBecomeHostile
  bCanRecover
  ReputationThresholds
  WorldStateOverrides

FAscentiaReputationState
  FactionTag
  Score
  RankTag
  VisibleRankTextKey
  HiddenFlags
  LastChangedWorldStateTag
  SaveVersion

FAscentiaReputationEvent
  EventTag
  SourceSystemTag
  Instigator
  TargetActor
  TargetFactionTag
  Magnitude
  VisibilityPolicy
  WitnessPolicy
  DecayPolicy
  ReasonTextKey

FAscentiaCrimeEvent
  CrimeTag
  Instigator
  Victim
  Location
  RegionTag
  Severity
  WitnessPolicy
  ForgivenessPolicy
  BountyDelta
  ReputationDelta
  WorldStateEffects

FAscentiaWitnessRecord
  WitnessActor
  WitnessFactionTag
  PerceptionSource
  CrimeOrEventTag
  Confidence
  LastKnownLocation
  bReported
  bPlayerNotified
  ExpireTime

FAscentiaNPCMemoryRecord
  NPCId
  MemoryTag
  SourceEventTag
  SubjectActorId
  DispositionDelta
  DialogueUnlockTags
  ServiceModifierTags
  ExpirationPolicy
  SavePolicy

FAscentiaServiceTrustModifier
  ServiceTag
  FactionTag
  ReputationRankTag
  PriceMultiplier
  StockUnlockTags
  RepairOrCraftingUnlockTags
  DialogueToneTag
  bServiceBlocked

Runtime policy:

  • Faction and reputation state belongs to

UAscentiaSocialConsequenceSubsystem. Individual NPCs may expose faction, witness, and memory components, but they do not own global truth.

  • Combat teams, AI threat, dialogue, vendors, Smart Object eligibility, map

guidance, and save data all read social state through the same facade.

  • Crime/witness events require server authority, range/LOS/perception checks,

source actor identity, target actor identity, and stable region/world-state context before they write persistent state.

  • AI Perception can create witness candidates, but the social subsystem decides

whether a witnessed event becomes reputation, bounty, hostility, dialogue, or memory.

  • Reputation changes should be sparse, meaningful, and explainable. The player

should not receive invisible score churn for every incidental collision, summon hit, physics nudge, or friendly AI accident.

  • Hidden social state is allowed, but Designer tools must show why an NPC,

faction, vendor, or service changed behavior.

  • Service trust is data-driven. Vendors, crafting stations, healers, ferrymen,

trainers, and faction doors resolve stock, price, refusal, and tone through service trust profiles.

  • NPC memory is not a dialogue database. It is a small set of durable facts that

can unlock dialogue, barks, service changes, schedule changes, hostility, or forgiveness windows.

  • Forgiveness, absolution, bribes, disguise, time decay, and faction resets are

authored policies. They are never ad hoc Blueprint cleanup nodes.

  • Multiplayer future-proofing: social writes are server-authoritative and must

state whether consequences are personal, host-world, party-shared, or local only before P22 can reuse the system.

Designer-facing tools:

EUW_AscentiaFactionReputationBoard
  inspect factions, relationship matrix, reputation ranks, hostility rules,
  protected NPC queries, service gates, world-state overrides, and save state

EUW_AscentiaCrimeWitnessLab
  simulate theft, assault, trespass, murder, summon damage, disguise, bounty,
  witness reporting, forgiveness, and no-witness cases in an arena fixture

EUW_AscentiaNPCMemoryAndDispositionLab
  preview one NPC's memories, dialogue unlocks, bark tone, schedule changes,
  service modifiers, hostility, forgiveness, and save/load persistence

EUW_AscentiaServiceTrustEconomyLab
  compare vendor/service stock, prices, refusal reasons, crafting/repair unlocks,
  faction discounts, and economy impact across reputation ranks

Evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Factions/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Reputation/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/CrimeWitness/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/NPCMemory/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/ServiceTrust/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/SocialConsequence/{RunId}/report.json

Commandlet contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaFactionReputationAudit
  -Root=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Social
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/Factions

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaCrimeWitnessScenario
  -Scenario=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Social/PDA_SocialConsequenceScenario_MVP
  -Map=/Game/Ascentia/Maps/MVP_Arena
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/CrimeWitness

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaNPCMemoryScenario
  -NPC=/Game/Ascentia/Design/NPC/PDA_NPC_CampsiteGuide_MVP
  -SocialScenario=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Social/PDA_SocialConsequenceScenario_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P12/NPCMemory

Starter proof matrix:

social_faction_registry_has_stable_tags
  verify every faction, service, crime, reputation rank, and memory row uses
  registered Gameplay Tags and localized display keys

social_relationship_matrix_has_no_unknown_faction
  verify faction relationship rows resolve both sides, hostility defaults, and
  recovery policies without orphan tags

social_crime_no_witness_no_global_bounty
  simulate a private crime and verify no global bounty/reputation write occurs
  unless the policy explicitly says it should

social_crime_witness_reports_reputation_and_memory
  simulate a witnessed protected-NPC assault and verify witness record,
  reputation event, NPC memory, dialogue/service change, and save dirty reason

social_vendor_service_trust_changes_price_and_stock
  apply one reputation rank change and verify stock, price, refusal, and text
  change through `PDA_ServiceTrustProfile_MVP`

social_forgiveness_policy_restores_service_without_deleting_history
  apply a forgiveness action and verify service access recovers while memory
  history remains visible to tools

social_ai_hostility_matches_reputation_and_faction
  verify AI threat/faction state reads the social facade and does not duplicate
  private hostility flags

social_save_load_reputation_memory_persistence
  write reputation and memory state, save/load, and verify dialogue, service,
  hostility, and dashboard evidence agree

social_no_private_blueprint_reputation_state
  scan NPC, vendor, interaction, and world-script Blueprints for private score,
  crime, bounty, or hostility variables outside approved components/assets

Implementation tickets:

P12F-001 Faction registry and relationship matrix
  Add `FAscentiaFactionDefinition`, `FAscentiaFactionRelationship`,
  `PDA_FactionRegistry_MVP`, `PDA_FactionRelationshipMatrix_MVP`, and registry
  validation against Gameplay Tags.

P12F-002 Reputation profile and subsystem facade
  Add `FAscentiaReputationState`, `FAscentiaReputationEvent`,
  `UAscentiaSocialConsequenceSubsystem`, rank thresholds, save fragment, and
  evidence output.

P12F-003 Crime, witness, and protected-NPC scenario
  Add `FAscentiaCrimeEvent`, `FAscentiaWitnessRecord`, witness components,
  AI Perception bridge, no-witness/witnessed fixtures, and server-authority
  validation.

P12F-004 NPC memory and disposition bridge
  Add `FAscentiaNPCMemoryRecord`, memory profiles, dialogue/bark/service unlock
  hooks, save/load proof, and NPC memory Designer lab.

P12F-005 Service trust and economy bridge
  Add `FAscentiaServiceTrustModifier`, service trust profiles, vendor/crafting/
  repair/trainer stock and price proof, and economy impact report.

P12F-006 Faction/reputation Designer boards
  Add `EUW_AscentiaFactionReputationBoard`, `EUW_AscentiaCrimeWitnessLab`,
  `EUW_AscentiaNPCMemoryAndDispositionLab`, and
  `EUW_AscentiaServiceTrustEconomyLab`.

P12F-007 Dashboard/package gates
  Add blockers for orphan faction tags, private Blueprint reputation variables,
  unexplained hostility, witness events without authority proof, service trust
  rows without localization, and social state that fails save/load.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build P12F-001 and P12F-002 after P12 world-state tags stabilize but before

multiple vendors, factions, or protected NPCs ship.

  1. Add P12F-003 before attacks, theft, or trespass can affect friendly NPCs.
  2. Add P12F-004 before writing large amounts of reactive dialogue.
  3. Add P12F-005 before vendors/crafting/services become important economy

levers.

  1. Keep first proof tiny: one friendly faction, one protected NPC, one vendor,

one witnessed assault, one forgiveness action, and one save/load fixture.

#18. Itemization Depth, Loot Economy, Crafting, Vendors, and Rewards

#18.1 Player-facing goal

Items should make the player ask interesting questions:

  • Does this weapon change how I fight?
  • Does this affix support my stat/passive direction?
  • Is this armor worth the equip-load burden?
  • Is this unique effect worth losing a better rare roll?
  • Should I sell, salvage, upgrade, craft, or keep this?
  • Is this boss reward memorable enough to shape a build?

P16 is where Ascentia turns item data into the long-term ARPG engine.

#18.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

  • EAscentiaItemQuality has Common, Magic, Rare, Legendary, Mythic, Unique.
  • EAscentiaItemCategory separates Weapon, Armor, Consumable, CraftingMaterial,

Currency, and KeyItem.

  • FAscentiaItemStack saves item tag, quantity, seed, rolled quality, rolled

affixes, and grid slot.

  • FAscentiaEquipmentEntry binds item stacks to equipment slots.
  • UAscentiaItemDefinition is a PrimaryDataAsset with item tag, display name,

icon, category, quality, slot, meshes, grip transform, trail system, stat requirements, light attack bonuses, weight, stack limit, vendor value, and equip GameplayEffects.

  • UAscentiaAffixDefinition is a PrimaryDataAsset with prefix/suffix text,

minimum quality, allowed item tags, legacy stat field, magnitude range, and GrantedModifierTags.

  • UAscentiaLootTableAsset owns deterministic and weighted rewards.
  • UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem registers native fallbacks and lets designer

assets override by GameplayTag.

  • UAscentiaLootSubsystem rolls deterministic plus one weighted entry, builds

item stacks from seeds, rolls prefix/suffix affixes by quality, and builds generated item names.

  • UAscentiaInventoryComponent owns replicated inventory/equipment, grid slots,

equip/unequip, stack consumption, display names, and equip-effect refresh.

  • UAscentiaCraftingSubsystem can check ingredient/cairn-tag requirements and

build a crafted item stack.

  • AAscentiaVendorNPC can sell stock for Stones, mark limited items sold out,

and record trade telemetry.

  • UAscentiaProgressionComponent owns Stones as the current primary currency

for leveling and vendor purchases.

Current limitations:

  • affix count is fixed at prefix for Magic and prefix+suffix for Rare+;
  • affix pools filter mostly by item tag, not by family/category/slot/build role;
  • legacy EAscentiaAffixStat still narrows power to light attack damage/poise;
  • no unique/legendary power architecture;
  • no upgrade, tempering, socket, rune, aspect, imprint, or sanctify-style

deterministic/chaotic modification loop;

  • crafting has no inventory-consuming transaction, UI, station, or economy sink;
  • vendors do not yet support selling, buyback, restock, discounts, or service

profiles;

  • item lore/flavor text is absent;
  • no item compare/sort/filter/salvage;
  • no economy simulator or faucet/sink reports.

#18.3 Item design pillars

Ascentia should mix two item traditions:

Soulslike authored rewards
  memorable placements, boss drops, lore identity, build-defining uniques

ARPG generated rewards
  affix-driven comparison, loot-table variance, crafting/upgrading, economy

Principles:

  • Items are build language, not just reward confetti.
  • Every item has a lifecycle: find, inspect, equip, compare, upgrade, sell,

salvage, craft with, store, or discard.

  • Random drops provide texture; authored rewards provide memory.
  • Affixes should create compatibility questions, not only higher DPS.
  • Bad or off-build rolls are allowed, but useless clutter is not.
  • Economy sinks must scale with faucets.
  • Item identity must survive save/load, migration, multiplayer, and content

refactors.

#18.4 Item instance and identity contract

Production item instances need stable identity beyond a stack row.

FAscentiaItemInstance
  InstanceGuid
  ItemTag
  Quantity
  ItemSeed
  RolledQuality
  ItemLevel
  WorldTierBorn
  SourceTag
  SourceActorGuid
  SourceWorldStateTag
  RolledAffixes
  CraftedAffixes
  UniquePowerTags
  UpgradeState
  SocketState
  DurabilityState if ever used
  LockFavoriteState
  DiscoveredLoreState
  MutationHistory
  BoundState
  GridSlot

Rules:

  • ItemTag identifies the base definition.
  • InstanceGuid identifies this rolled/crafted/saved item.
  • ItemSeed gives deterministic rolls for affix magnitudes and generated

presentation.

  • item instances must be serializable without hard actor references;
  • stackable materials/currencies can use compressed stack records;
  • equipped items, stash items, vendor buyback items, and dropped corpse loot all

use the same instance model.

Mutation history:

FAscentiaItemMutationRecord
  MutationId
  MutationType
  SourceSystemTag
  SourceActorOrServiceTag
  PreviousHash
  ResultHash
  ConsumedCurrencies
  ConsumedItemInstanceIds
  AppliedAffixTags
  AppliedPowerTags
  CreatedUtcOrServerTime

Do not store huge item history forever for every item. Store enough to support debugging, save migration, economy telemetry, and player trust for meaningful mutations: crafted, upgraded, socketed, imprinted, salvaged, traded, or boss reward grants.

#18.4.1 Item transaction authority

Every item mutation should use one transaction path:

FAscentiaItemTransactionRequest
  RequestId
  RequestType
  Instigator
  SourceServiceTag
  TargetItemInstanceIds
  OfferedCurrencies
  OfferedMaterials
  ContextTags
  PreviewOnly

FAscentiaItemTransactionResult
  RequestId
  Result
  FailureReasonTag
  SpentCurrencies
  ConsumedItemInstanceIds
  CreatedItemInstances
  ModifiedItemInstances
  RemovedItemInstanceIds
  AppliedWorldEffects
  PlayerTextKey
  EvidenceId

Transaction rules:

  • server-authoritative where persistence or economy is affected;
  • preview path uses the same validation as commit path;
  • failure reasons are tagged and localizable;
  • partial commits are not allowed unless the transaction type explicitly

supports multiple independent results;

  • every successful transaction marks save data dirty;
  • every economy-affecting transaction writes telemetry/evidence.

#18.4.2 Item resolver, containers, and save records

The item registry should not hand gameplay code raw DataAssets plus loose stack state. It should resolve stable item views that all systems can inspect.

UAscentiaItemResolverSubsystem
  ResolveItemDefinition(ItemTag, ContextTags)
  ResolveItemInstance(ItemInstance, ResolveContext)
  BuildTooltipView(ItemInstance, TooltipContext)
  BuildComparisonView(LeftInstance, RightInstance, CompareContext)
  WriteItemizationReport(OutputPath)

Resolved definition:

FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition
  ItemTag
  DisplayNameKey
  LoreTextKey
  Category
  EquipmentSlot
  WeaponFamilyTags
  ArmorRoleTags
  BaseQuality
  BaseItemLevel
  BaseStats
  Requirements
  Weight
  StackPolicy
  VendorValuePolicy
  MeshRefs
  IconRef
  EquipGameplayEffects
  GrantedAbilitySets
  SourceTrace[]
  ValidationSeverity

Resolved instance:

FAscentiaResolvedItemInstance
  InstanceGuid
  Definition
  Quantity
  RolledQuality
  ItemLevel
  WorldTierBorn
  ResolvedAffixes
  ResolvedPowers
  ResolvedUpgradeState
  ResolvedSocketState
  ResolvedRequirements
  ResolvedVendorValue
  ResolvedWeight
  TooltipView
  ComparisonGroups
  SaveHash

Container snapshot:

FAscentiaItemContainerSnapshot
  ContainerId
  ContainerTypeTag
  OwnerActorOrSaveId
  Items
  GridState
  StackState
  CapacityPolicy
  SortFilterState
  DirtyReasonTag

Save record:

FAscentiaItemInstanceSaveRecord
  Version
  InstanceGuid
  ItemTag
  Quantity
  ItemSeed
  RolledQuality
  ItemLevel
  WorldTierBorn
  RolledAffixTags
  RolledAffixMagnitudes
  CraftedAffixTags
  PowerTags
  UpgradeState
  SocketState
  LockFavoriteState
  BoundState
  SourceTags
  MutationSummaryHash

Rules:

  • only save stable IDs, compact values, and versioned structs;
  • runtime actors, DataAsset pointers, widget state, and temporary tooltip strings

never belong in item save records;

  • containers own placement and capacity, while item instances own identity and

mutation state;

  • every resolver output should include source trace so an item browser can show

whether a value came from a DataTable row, PrimaryDataAsset, affix, power, upgrade, socket, or fallback.

#18.5 Quality, affix count, and loot grammar

Suggested quality grammar:

Common
  base item only

Magic
  1-2 affixes, narrow but clean

Rare
  3-5 affixes, main random-build workhorse

Legendary
  rare-like affixes plus one authored legendary power or aspect

Mythic
  high-end limited legendary/unique-adjacent chase item

Unique
  authored stat package and unique behavior, not fully random

The current system's Magic prefix and Rare prefix+suffix is an MVP seed. P16 should replace it with data:

DT_ItemQualityRules
  Quality
  MinAffixes
  MaxAffixes
  PrefixLimit
  SuffixLimit
  AllowsLegendaryPower
  AllowsUniquePower
  AllowsCraftedAffixes
  AllowsSockets
  AllowsImprint
  SalvageTier
  BeamProfile
  TooltipStyle

Affix selection should filter by:

  • item category;
  • equipment slot;
  • weapon family;
  • damage school;
  • armor role;
  • world tier;
  • item level;
  • quality;
  • source type;
  • biome/region;
  • exclusion group;
  • build/stat tag;
  • unique/legendary compatibility.

Affix pool profile:

PDA_AffixPoolProfile
  PoolTag
  SourceTypes
  AllowedItemCategories
  AllowedEquipmentSlots
  AllowedWeaponFamilies
  RequiredQuality
  RequiredWorldTier
  RequiredBiomeTags
  RequiredSourceTags
  AffixWeights
  ExclusionGroups
  BadRollProtectionPolicy

Loot grammar should be previewable by source:

FAscentiaLootRollPlan
  LootContext
  CandidateBaseItems
  CandidateAffixPools
  QualityRoll
  AffixCountRoll
  GuaranteedRewards
  WeightedRewards
  DeterministicSeed

Quality roll:

FAscentiaQualityRollRequest
  LootContext
  SourceProfile
  BaseItemTag
  PlayerLevel
  WorldTier
  LuckModifiers
  DeterministicSeed

FAscentiaQualityRollResult
  SelectedQuality
  AffixCount
  QualityWeights
  AppliedLuckModifiers
  BeamProfile
  TooltipStyle
  SalvageTier

Affix roll:

FAscentiaAffixRollRequest
  BaseItemTag
  ItemCategory
  EquipmentSlot
  WeaponFamilyTags
  Quality
  ItemLevel
  WorldTier
  SourceTags
  ExistingAffixTags
  BlockedExclusionGroups
  DeterministicSeed

FAscentiaAffixRollResult
  SelectedAffixes
  RejectedAffixes
  MagnitudeRolls
  ExclusionGroupsApplied
  PoolWeights
  RollTierResults
  Warnings[]

Loot roll result:

FAscentiaLootRollResult
  LootContext
  Seed
  CreatedItemInstances
  CreatedCurrencies
  CreatedMaterials
  GuaranteedRewardsGranted
  WeightedRewardsGranted
  DuplicateProtectionApplied
  AntiFarmPolicyApplied
  PresentationEvents
  EvidenceId

This is where Blizzard's "easy to learn, difficult to master" itemization lesson fits Ascentia: base damage/armor/quality give an obvious first read, but affix tags, requirements, powers, and upgrade state carry the real build questions.

#18.6 Modifier tag vocabulary

P16 should graduate fully from EAscentiaAffixStat to modifier tags and GameplayEffects.

Modifier classes:

Ascentia.Mod.Attribute.*
Ascentia.Mod.Resource.*
Ascentia.Mod.Damage.*
Ascentia.Mod.Status.*
Ascentia.Mod.Resistance.*
Ascentia.Mod.Skill.*
Ascentia.Mod.WeaponFamily.*
Ascentia.Mod.Crit.*
Ascentia.Mod.Block.*
Ascentia.Mod.Dodge.*
Ascentia.Mod.Loot.*
Ascentia.Mod.Crafting.*
Ascentia.Mod.OnHit.*
Ascentia.Mod.OnKill.*
Ascentia.Mod.Conditional.*

Affix data:

PDA_AffixDefinition
  AffixTag
  PrefixSuffixType
  DisplayPattern
  DisplayTextKey
  ModifierTags
  GameplayEffectClass
  SetByCallerMagnitudeKeys
  MagnitudeRangeByItemLevel
  AllowedItemCategories
  AllowedEquipmentSlots
  AllowedWeaponFamilies
  RequiredQuality
  RequiredWorldTier
  ExclusionGroups
  WeightBySource
  RollTier
  TooltipPriority
  ComparisonGroup

Rules:

  • each affix has at least one modifier tag;
  • every modifier tag maps to gameplay math, UI text, and validation;
  • conditional modifiers must state the condition in data;
  • affixes that affect a specific skill reference skill tags;
  • no affix should require bespoke UI parsing.

Resolved modifier:

FAscentiaResolvedItemModifier
  ModifierTag
  SourceAffixTag
  SourcePowerTag
  Magnitude
  MagnitudeType
  AppliesToTags
  ConditionTags
  GameplayEffectClass
  SetByCallerData
  TooltipLineKey
  ComparisonGroup

Modifier application report:

FAscentiaItemModifierApplicationReport
  ItemInstanceId
  ModifierCount
  GameplayEffectsGranted
  SetByCallerMagnitudes
  AttributePreviewDelta
  AbilityTagsAffected
  TooltipLines
  MissingMappings[]
  Conflicts[]

The inventory, damage model, ability system, and tooltip should all read the resolved modifier list. UI should not parse strings like "+12 Fire Damage" to infer gameplay meaning. Gameplay should not parse tooltip text to infer math.

#18.7 Unique, legendary, and boss reward powers

Unique and legendary effects should be authored powers, not oversized numeric affixes.

PDA_ItemPower
  PowerTag
  DisplayName
  DescriptionText
  GameplayEffectClass
  GrantedAbilitySet
  TriggerRules
  PresentationCue
  AllowedItemTags
  AllowedSlots
  RequiredState
  BlockedState
  CompatibilityTags
  BalanceBudget
  StackPolicy
  SavePolicy

Power types:

  • passive attribute change;
  • skill mutation;
  • status conversion;
  • on-hit/on-kill/on-dodge trigger;
  • resource loop change;
  • defensive rule change;
  • movement/motion rule change;
  • summon/companion trigger;
  • world-state or discovery utility;
  • boss remembrance service.

Boss reward policy:

PDA_BossRewardProfile
  BossTag
  GuaranteedRewards
  WeightedRewards
  RemembranceOptions
  VendorUnlocks
  CraftingUnlocks
  WorldStateEffects
  DuplicateProtectionPolicy
  NewGamePlusPolicy

For Ascentia, boss drops should often be set authored rewards with lore and build identity, while ordinary enemies and chests can carry the random item pressure.

Legendary/aspect profile:

PDA_ItemAspectProfile
  AspectTag
  SourcePower
  ExtractionPolicy
  ImprintPolicy
  AllowedCategories
  AllowedSlots
  CostPolicy
  ConsumesSourceItem
  BindOnImprintPolicy

Rules:

  • uniques should not be fully random rares with a special color;
  • legendary/aspect powers should have compatibility data and validation;
  • boss rewards must be duplicate-safe across save/load, rest, death, and NG+;
  • remembrance-style choices must persist the chosen branch and expose alternate

branches through a vendor/service profile if design permits;

  • power text, tooltip, cue, and actual GameplayEffect/ability behavior must be

validated together.

#18.8 Crafting, upgrades, sockets, salvage, and item sinks

Crafting should be a controlled sink and agency layer, not a bypass around loot.

Crafting actions:

Craft
  consume ingredients -> produce item

Upgrade
  consume Stones/materials -> raise item level or upgrade tier

Temper/Imbue
  add or reroll one crafted affix from a narrow pool

Socket
  add socket if item supports it

Set Gem/Rune
  insert removable or destructive modifier object

Salvage
  destroy item -> materials

Transmute
  exchange low-value materials/items into higher-value target

Imprint/Aspect
  apply authored power under strict compatibility rules

Sanctify/Corrupt optional later
  high-risk final modification, locks further editing

Data:

PDA_CraftingRecipe
  RecipeTag
  StationTag
  Ingredients
  CurrencyCosts
  RequiredWorldState
  RequiredUnlocks
  ResultPolicy
  PreviewPolicy
  FailurePolicy
  PresentationCue
  ServiceProfile

PDA_UpgradeTrack
  TrackTag
  AppliesToCategories
  MaxTier
  CostsByTier
  StatScaleByTier
  UnlockRequirement
  VisualTierPolicy
  ComparisonDeltaPolicy

PDA_SalvageProfile
  Quality
  Category
  MaterialReturns
  ChanceReturns
  ProtectedItemPolicy
  PreviewPolicy

Rules:

  • crafting consumes item instances atomically;
  • preview shows the result range before confirmation;
  • irreversible actions require a confirmation policy;
  • crafted affixes are distinguishable from dropped affixes;
  • salvage destroys items and must respect locked/favorite state;
  • upgrades must not invalidate comparison clarity;
  • materials should have named faucets and sinks.

Crafting preview:

FAscentiaCraftingPreview
  RecipeTag
  TargetItemInstance
  RequiredCosts
  MissingCosts
  PossibleResults
  ResultStatRange
  ResultAffixRange
  FailureReasons
  ConfirmationPolicy

Salvage preview:

FAscentiaSalvagePreview
  TargetItemInstances
  GuaranteedMaterials
  ChanceMaterials
  BlockedItems
  LockedOrFavoriteItems
  LostPowers
  ConfirmationPolicy

Crafting, upgrading, tempering, socketing, imprinting, and salvage should call the item transaction path from section 18.4.1. P12 service profiles decide where the player can request the action. P16 item/economy rules decide whether the action is legal and what it costs.

Transaction subsystem:

UAscentiaItemTransactionSubsystem
  PreviewTransaction(Request)
  CommitTransaction(Request)
  RollLoot(LootContext)
  ApplyItemMutation(MutationRequest)
  WriteTransactionLedger(OutputPath)

Mutation preview:

FAscentiaItemMutationPreview
  RequestType
  TargetItem
  BeforeResolvedInstance
  AfterResolvedInstance
  RequiredCosts
  MissingCosts
  RiskPolicy
  Irreversible
  ConfirmationTextKey
  FailureReasons[]

Ledger entry:

FAscentiaItemTransactionLedgerEntry
  RequestId
  TransactionType
  InstigatorId
  SourceServiceTag
  BeforeContainerHashes
  AfterContainerHashes
  SpentCurrencies
  ConsumedItems
  CreatedItems
  ModifiedItems
  FailureReasonTag
  SaveDirtyReason
  EvidenceId

EUW_AscentiaItemTransactionReplay should load ledger entries and rebuild the before/after item views. This is useful for debugging "my loot vanished" class bugs because the answer should be visible as data: consumed, moved, stacked, sold, salvaged, saved, or failed.

#18.9 Economy model: faucets, sinks, caps, and conversion

Every resource needs an economy card.

FAscentiaEconomyResource
  ResourceTag
  DisplayName
  Type
  StackPolicy
  Faucets
  Sinks
  Caps
  ConversionRules
  ExpectedPerHourByTier
  TargetSpendPerHourByTier
  HoardingRisk

Economy profile:

PDA_EconomyProfile
  ProfileTag
  WorldTierRange
  ResourceDefinitions
  FaucetProfiles
  SinkProfiles
  ConversionProfiles
  VendorPriceProfiles
  CraftingCostProfiles
  UpgradeCostProfiles
  TargetCurves
  HoardingThresholds

Faucet and sink rows:

FAscentiaEconomyFaucetRow
  FaucetTag
  ResourceTag
  SourceType
  ExpectedPerUse
  ExpectedUsesPerHour
  WorldTierScalar
  RegionScalar
  AntiFarmPolicy

FAscentiaEconomySinkRow
  SinkTag
  ResourceTag
  SinkType
  ExpectedCostPerUse
  ExpectedUsesPerHour
  WorldTierScalar
  ProgressionGate
  OptionalOrRequired

Initial resources:

  • Stones;
  • crafting materials;
  • boss/remembrance tokens if chosen;
  • upgrade materials;
  • socket/gem/rune materials if chosen;
  • key items;
  • region currencies only if truly needed.

Faucets:

  • enemy kills;
  • boss kills;
  • chests;
  • POI completion;
  • The Below completion;
  • resource nodes;
  • salvage;
  • questless world-state rewards;
  • vendor buyback only if buyback returns value;
  • NG+ scaling.

Sinks:

  • leveling;
  • vendor purchases;
  • crafting;
  • upgrades;
  • respec;
  • fast-travel tolls only if design wants friction;
  • repair only if durability becomes a real mechanic;
  • stash expansion if storage friction is desired;
  • cosmetic/transmog if later appropriate.

Proof requires balance curves, not intuition. EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab should simulate kills, chests, vendors, crafting, upgrades, and salvage over expected playtime.

Economy simulation profile:

PDA_EconomySimulationProfile
  ProfileTag
  WorldTierRange
  PlayerLevelRange
  EncounterMix
  ChestOpenRate
  BossKillRate
  VendorVisitRate
  CraftingUseRate
  UpgradeUseRate
  SalvageUseRate
  RunCount
  RandomSeeds
  TargetMetrics

Economy report:

FAscentiaEconomySimulationReport
  ProfileTag
  Runs
  ItemsPerHourByQuality
  AffixFrequency
  UniquePowerFrequency
  StonesPerHour
  MaterialsPerHour
  SpendPerHour
  HoardingRiskByResource
  DeadDropRate
  VendorValueDistribution
  SalvageReturnDistribution
  UpgradeAffordabilityCurve
  CraftingAffordabilityCurve
  SuggestedTuningDeltas

Economy report acceptance should include percentile bands, not only averages. The average player and the unlucky player experience different economies. A P16 report should therefore show p10/p50/p90 for item quality, Stones, upgrade material pressure, and crafting affordability.

Value chain:

Drop -> Inspect -> Equip or Keep -> Upgrade/Craft or Sell/Salvage -> Spend

Each resource should have a visible path through that chain. If a material drops but has no meaningful sink in the next several hours of play, it is clutter. If a sink exists but the faucet cannot support it, the system feels punitive.

#18.10 Vendors, shops, stash, and buyback

P12 defines vendors as NPC interactions. P16 defines the economy rules.

Vendor data:

PDA_VendorProfile
  VendorId
  StockPools
  StaticStock
  RestockPolicy
  BuyPolicy
  SellPolicy
  BuybackPolicy
  PriceCurves
  DiscountRules
  WorldStateUnlocks
  ServiceProfiles
  BuybackLimit
  BuybackExpirationPolicy
  LimitedStockSavePolicy

Shop UI needs:

  • buy tab;
  • sell tab;
  • buyback tab if supported;
  • compare tooltip;
  • item lock/favorite respect;
  • affordability state;
  • confirmation policy;
  • stock sold-out/restock state;
  • price explanation if modified by world state;
  • controller navigation;
  • accessibility text sizing.

Buyback should be short-window and bounded. A permanent buyback list becomes a stash and can explode save size.

Vendor transaction policy:

PDA_VendorTransactionPolicy
  BuyMarkupCurve
  SellValueCurve
  BuybackMarkupCurve
  RestockInterval
  LimitedStockPolicy
  DiscountRules
  ReputationOrWorldStateModifiers
  ConfirmationThresholds

Stash policy:

PDA_StashProfile
  ProfileTag
  SlotPolicy
  GridSize
  StackPolicy
  ExpansionCosts
  SharedBetweenCharacters
  SavePolicy
  SortFilterPolicy

Vendor, stash, and buyback all use item instance IDs. They should not duplicate items by copying loose item stacks without preserving instance identity and mutation state.

#18.11 Inventory, compare, filters, and player decision support

The inventory should help decisions without solving builds automatically.

Features:

  • compare equipped item;
  • stat delta preview;
  • affix roll range display;
  • item source/lore display;
  • quality, slot, category, and tag filters;
  • sort by recent, quality, slot, value, weight, item power, name;
  • lock/favorite;
  • mark junk;
  • bulk sell/salvage with confirmation;
  • tooltip verbosity setting;
  • controller-friendly grid navigation.

Tooltip model:

FAscentiaItemTooltipView
  Header
  Quality
  CategorySlot
  ItemLevel
  Requirements
  BaseStats
  AffixLines
  UniquePowerLines
  UpgradeState
  SocketLines
  CompareDelta
  LoreText
  SourceText
  VendorValue
  Warnings

The tooltip should be a view model built from resolved item data, not ad hoc UI string assembly.

#18.12 Loot sources, reward pacing, and world integration

Loot tables need context:

FAscentiaLootContext
  SourceType
  SourceTag
  RegionTag
  BiomeTag
  WorldTier
  PlayerLevel
  EncounterDifficulty
  BossTag
  POITag
  CompletionState
  LuckModifiers
  Seed

Loot source profile:

PDA_LootSourceProfile
  SourceTag
  SourceType
  LootTables
  GuaranteedRewards
  WorldTierPolicy
  FirstTimeRewardPolicy
  RepeatRewardPolicy
  AntiFarmPolicy
  PresentationProfile

Reward pacing profile:

PDA_RewardPacingProfile
  ProfileTag
  WorldTier
  RegionTag
  ExpectedCombatRewardsPerHour
  ExpectedDiscoveryRewardsPerHour
  ExpectedBossRewards
  QualityTargetsBySource
  CurrencyTargetsBySource
  MaterialTargetsBySource
  DeadDropThreshold
  DuplicateThreshold
  MilestoneRewardChecks

Loot distribution report:

FAscentiaLootDistributionReport
  SourceProfile
  Runs
  Seeds
  ItemsByQuality
  ItemsByCategory
  AffixFrequency
  PowerFrequency
  CurrencyTotals
  MaterialTotals
  DeadDropRate
  DuplicateRate
  ExpectedVendorValue
  ExpectedSalvageReturn
  OutlierDrops

Reward pacing report:

FAscentiaRewardPacingReport
  ProfileTag
  SimulatedHours
  SourceCoverage
  RewardsPerHour
  QualityPacing
  CurrencyPacing
  MaterialPacing
  BossRewardCoverage
  POIRewardCoverage
  DeadDropFindings
  DuplicateFindings
  SuggestedTuningDeltas

Reward pacing:

  • common enemies give currency/material texture;
  • elites give higher quality chance and focused pools;
  • bosses give authored rewards and unlocks;
  • chests give region identity and discovery value;
  • POIs give completion rewards plus map/world state;
  • The Below can be the experiment space for higher variance rewards;
  • vendors/crafting turn surplus into intentional progress.

No loot source should exist without a reason in the item lifecycle.

Tool outputs:

EUW_AscentiaItemBrowser
  resolved item definition
  instance preview
  source/provenance

EUW_AscentiaAffixMatrix
  affix pool reachability
  modifier tag coverage
  exclusion group conflicts
  tooltip/comparison group preview

EUW_AscentiaLootTablePreview
  loot roll plan
  distribution report
  deterministic seed replay

EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab
  economy simulation report
  faucet/sink balance
  hoarding and dead-drop warnings

EUW_AscentiaCraftingRecipePreview
  crafting preview
  missing costs
  possible result ranges

EUW_AscentiaUpgradeTrackPreview
  upgrade tier deltas
  cost curve
  comparison clarity check

EUW_AscentiaItemTooltipPreview
  tooltip view model
  localization keys
  controller/mobile text stress

Validation examples:

  • "Affix Ascentia.Affix.FireDamage has modifier tag but no SetByCaller mapping."
  • "Rare quality allows 5 affixes, but UI tooltip profile displays only 4."
  • "Loot source RuinsChest can roll no valid weapon for WorldTier 2."
  • "Crafting recipe AshenBlade consumes item instances but has no preview policy."
  • "Upgrade track SwordIron raises damage but item compare does not show the

delta."

  • "BossReward CairnHeart grants unique power but duplicate protection is None."
  • "Economy resource Shard has faucet but no sink before level 20."

Remote proof names:

item_instance_save
affix_modifier_application
loot_distribution_report
unique_power_behavior
boss_reward_grant
crafting_transaction
upgrade_transaction
salvage_transaction
vendor_buy_sell_buyback
inventory_compare_filters
economy_simulation_report

#18.15 P16 proof gates

P16 is not complete until:

  1. Item registry resolves DataTables and PrimaryDataAssets into one inspected

read model.

  1. Affixes use modifier tags and GameplayEffects/SetByCaller magnitudes, not

only the legacy enum.

  1. Loot tables can be simulated by source, world tier, region, and seed.
  2. Quality rules determine affix counts, beams, salvage tier, and UI treatment.
  3. At least one unique or legendary power changes gameplay behavior and is

validated.

  1. At least one boss reward profile grants a memorable authored reward.
  2. Crafting consumes inventory/materials transactionally and previews results.
  3. Upgrading or tempering provides a meaningful sink without invalidating loot.
  4. Selling, buyback, salvage, and vendor stock have UI and save persistence.
  5. Item compare/sort/filter/favorite/junk flows work on mouse and controller.
  6. Economy simulator reports faucets, sinks, expected items/hour, expected

Stones/hour, material pressure, and hoarding risks.

  1. Packaged smoke proves loot, equip, vendor, craft/upgrade/salvage, save/load,

and tooltip presentation without native fallback-only content.

#18.16 P16 starter implementation package

The first P16 package should make item truth inspectable and transactions replayable before the loot surface expands. The immediate goal is not endgame crafting depth. It is stable item identity, a resolver, a transaction ledger, deterministic loot simulation, tooltip views, and economy evidence.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Items/AscentiaItemInstanceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Items/AscentiaItemResolverTypes.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Affixes/AscentiaAffixValidation.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaLootDistributionCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaEconomySimulationCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaItemTransactionReplayCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaItemResolverTest.cpp

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaLootDeterminismTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaItemSaveRecordTest.cpp

Subsystem boundaries:

UAscentiaItemResolverSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  ResolveItemDefinition(ItemTag, ContextTags)
  ResolveItemInstance(ItemInstance, ResolveContext)
  BuildTooltipView(ItemInstance, TooltipContext)
  BuildComparisonView(LeftInstance, RightInstance, CompareContext)
  WriteItemizationReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaItemTransactionSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  PreviewTransaction(Request)
  CommitTransaction(Request)
  RollLoot(LootContext)
  ApplyItemMutation(MutationRequest)
  WriteTransactionLedger(OutputPath)
  ReplayTransactionLedger(LedgerPath)

UAscentiaLootSubsystem
  keeps current deterministic/weighted roll path
  delegates quality, affix, power, and source resolution to P16 data

UAscentiaInventoryComponent
  owns replicated containers and equipment state
  stores item instances and container placement, not tooltip strings

UAscentiaCraftingSubsystem
  becomes a client of item transactions for craft/upgrade/salvage/socket actions

Starter content folders:

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Items/Definitions/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Items/Quality/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Items/Tooltips/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Items/Powers/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Affixes/Definitions/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Affixes/Pools/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Loot/Tables/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Loot/Sources/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Loot/Presentation/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Crafting/Recipes/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Crafting/UpgradeTracks/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Crafting/Salvage/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Economy/Profiles/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Economy/Simulations/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Vendors/Transactions/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Stash/

Starter assets:

PDA_ItemQualityProfile_MVP
PDA_ItemTooltipProfile_MVP
PDA_ItemTransactionPolicy_MVP
PDA_AffixPoolProfile_MVP_Weapons
PDA_AffixPoolProfile_MVP_Armor
PDA_ItemPower_AshenEcho_Prototype
PDA_ItemAspectProfile_Prototype
PDA_LootSourceProfile_MVPArenaEnemy
PDA_LootSourceProfile_MVPArenaChest
PDA_LootSourceProfile_MVPBossReward
PDA_LootPresentationProfile_MVP
PDA_CraftingRecipe_RustedSwordUpgrade_MVP
PDA_UpgradeTrack_Weapon_MVP
PDA_SalvageProfile_MVP
PDA_VendorTransactionPolicy_MVP

PDA_MaterialProfile_IronShard_MVP
PDA_EconomyProfile_MVP
PDA_EconomySimulationProfile_MVPArena_OneHour
PDA_RewardPacingProfile_MVPArena

Primary asset policy:

PrimaryAssetType:
  AscentiaItem
  AscentiaAffix
  AscentiaItemPower
  AscentiaLootTable
  AscentiaLootSource
  AscentiaCraftingRecipe
  AscentiaUpgradeTrack
  AscentiaEconomyProfile

Rules:
  item definitions and powers are PrimaryDataAssets;
  bulk numeric quality/affix/economy curves may be DataTables or CurveTables;
  save records store stable tags, GUIDs, seeds, versions, and compact rolls;
  runtime never saves DataAsset pointers, widget text, actor refs, or transient
  tooltip output.

Item resolver signatures:

FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition ResolveItemDefinition(
  FGameplayTag ItemTag,
  const FAscentiaItemResolveContext& Context);

FAscentiaResolvedItemInstance ResolveItemInstance(
  const FAscentiaItemInstance& Instance,
  const FAscentiaItemResolveContext& Context);

FAscentiaItemTooltipView BuildTooltipView(
  const FAscentiaResolvedItemInstance& Instance,
  const FAscentiaItemTooltipContext& Context);

FAscentiaItemComparisonView BuildComparisonView(
  const FAscentiaResolvedItemInstance& Left,
  const FAscentiaResolvedItemInstance& Right,
  const FAscentiaItemTooltipContext& Context);

Transaction signatures:

FAscentiaItemTransactionResult PreviewTransaction(
  const FAscentiaItemTransactionRequest& Request);

FAscentiaItemTransactionResult CommitTransaction(
  const FAscentiaItemTransactionRequest& Request);

FAscentiaLootRollResult RollLoot(
  const FAscentiaLootContext& Context,
  const FAscentiaLootSourceProfile& SourceProfile);

FAscentiaItemMutationPreview PreviewMutation(
  const FAscentiaItemMutationRequest& Request);

FAscentiaItemTransactionReport ReplayLedger(
  const FAscentiaItemTransactionLedger& Ledger);

Transaction types:

Item.Transaction.LootGrant
Item.Transaction.ContainerMove
Item.Transaction.Equip
Item.Transaction.Unequip

Item.Transaction.Sell
Item.Transaction.Buyback
Item.Transaction.Craft
Item.Transaction.Upgrade
Item.Transaction.Salvage
Item.Transaction.Socket
Item.Transaction.Imprint
Item.Transaction.StashDeposit
Item.Transaction.StashWithdraw

ItemInstance_SaveLoad_RustedSword
ItemResolver_DefinitionSourceTrace
ItemResolver_InstanceTooltipCompare

AffixRoll_RareThreeToFive
