Editor surfaces:

EUW_AscentiaItemBrowser
  inspect item definitions, resolved instances, source trace, save record, and
  fallback debt

EUW_AscentiaAffixMatrix
  show affix reachability by item category, quality, slot, world tier, source,
  exclusion group, modifier tag, tooltip group, and GameplayEffect mapping

EUW_AscentiaLootTablePreview
  run deterministic seed preview and distribution batches by source profile

EUW_AscentiaLootEconomyLab
  simulate reward loops, faucets, sinks, p10/p50/p90 outcomes, hoarding risk,
  dead-drop rate, and upgrade/crafting affordability

EUW_AscentiaItemTransactionReplay
  load ledger entries and show before/after containers, item instance changes,
  failure reasons, save dirty events, and evidence ids

EUW_AscentiaItemTooltipPreview
  preview tooltip/compare output under localization, controller, and long-text
  stress fixtures

EUW_AscentiaCraftingRecipePreview
  preview required costs, missing costs, result ranges, irreversible warnings,
  and transaction output

Commandlet contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaItemizationAudit
  -ItemRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Items
  -AffixRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Affixes
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaLootDistribution
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Economy/Simulations/PDA_EconomySimulationProfile_MVPArena_OneHour
  -Runs=10000
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaEconomySimulation
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Economy/Profiles/PDA_EconomyProfile_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaItemTransactionReplay
  -Ledger=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/Transactions/Latest/ledger.json
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16

Evidence outputs:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/Itemization/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/Resolver/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/LootDistribution/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/EconomySimulation/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/Transactions/{RunId}/ledger.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/Transactions/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/Tooltips/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/Rewards/{RunId}/report.json

Implementation tickets:

P16-001 Item instance identity and save records
  Introduce `FAscentiaItemInstance`, `FAscentiaItemInstanceSaveRecord`,
  instance GUIDs, deterministic seeds, source tags, versioning, and save/load
  fixture tests.

P16-002 Item resolver and tooltip views
  Implement `UAscentiaItemResolverSubsystem`,
  `FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition`, `FAscentiaResolvedItemInstance`,
  `FAscentiaItemTooltipView`, source trace, and compare view.

P16-003 Transaction request/result/ledger path
  Implement `UAscentiaItemTransactionSubsystem`,
  `FAscentiaItemTransactionRequest`, `FAscentiaItemTransactionResult`, and
  `FAscentiaItemTransactionLedgerEntry` for loot, equip, buy, sell, and move.

P16-004 Quality and affix roll grammar
  Add `PDA_ItemQualityProfile_MVP`, `FAscentiaQualityRollRequest`,
  `FAscentiaQualityRollResult`, `FAscentiaAffixRollRequest`, and
  `FAscentiaAffixRollResult`.

P16-005 Modifier tag and GAS application bridge
  Move affix behavior toward `FAscentiaResolvedItemModifier`, Gameplay Tags,
  GameplayEffects, and SetByCaller magnitude mappings with validation reports.

P16-006 Loot source profiles and distribution reports
  Add `PDA_LootSourceProfile_*`, `FAscentiaLootContext`,
  `FAscentiaLootRollResult`, deterministic seed replay, and 10,000-run reports.

P16-007 Craft/upgrade/salvage transaction previews
  Add `PDA_CraftingRecipe_*`, `PDA_UpgradeTrack_*`, `PDA_SalvageProfile_*`,
  `FAscentiaItemMutationPreview`, and preview/commit proof.

P16-008 Vendor, buyback, stash, and transaction policy
  Add `PDA_VendorTransactionPolicy_MVP`, `PDA_StashProfile_MVP`, buy/sell/
  buyback transaction rows, favorite/locked item protection, and save proof.

P16-009 Economy simulation and reward pacing
  Add `PDA_EconomyProfile_MVP`, `PDA_EconomySimulationProfile_MVPArena_OneHour`,
  `FAscentiaEconomySimulationReport`, and `FAscentiaRewardPacingReport`.

P16-010 P16 packaged proof suite
  Run item save, resolver, affix application, loot distribution, transaction,
  crafting/upgrade/salvage, vendor, tooltip, economy, and reward pacing proofs
  in PIE and packaged Development.

Validation gates:

  • any item definition without stable item tag, icon, display text, category,

tooltip policy, and resolver source trace fails validation;

  • any item instance save record with actor refs, asset pointers, widget text, or

unversioned payload fails validation;

  • any affix without modifier tags, GameplayEffect/SetByCaller mapping, tooltip

group, roll range, and exclusion policy fails validation;

  • any loot source without deterministic replay and distribution report fails

validation;

  • any economy resource with faucet but no meaningful sink in the simulation

horizon is reported as hoarding risk;

  • any crafting/upgrade/salvage action that bypasses the transaction path fails

validation;

  • any sell/salvage path that ignores favorite/locked state fails validation;
  • any tooltip view that overflows long localized text fails UI stress proof.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Add FAscentiaItemInstance and save records while adapting current stacks.
  2. Build the resolver and tooltip views over existing item definitions.
  3. Move loot corpse, equip, vendor buy, sell, and container move into the

transaction subsystem with ledger evidence.

  1. Add quality/affix roll data that reproduces current Magic/Rare behavior.
  2. Add loot distribution and economy simulation commandlets before expanding

crafting depth.

  1. Add craft/upgrade/salvage previews only after transaction replay is reliable.

#18.17 The Below endgame run, modular dungeon, reward, and reset package

The MVP already proves a Below entry, authored pocket arena, guardian, return, and cache reward. Production needs a real run architecture. The Below should be the replayable, high-variance experiment space for rewards, hazards, bosses, modifiers, and world-state pressure, but it must stay deterministic, inspectable, save-safe, and authored enough that it does not feel like random rooms glued together.

Engine posture:

  • Level Instances are the right authoring unit for reusable room chunks,

corridors, encounter arenas, treasure rooms, shrine rooms, and exit rooms.

  • Packed Level Actors can be used for static repeated room dressing when runtime

variation is not needed.

  • PCG can propose decoration, clutter, hazards, and optional branches inside a

room, but run-critical doors, exits, bosses, rewards, and checkpoints remain authored and validated.

  • World Partition/Data Layers still own streaming and runtime activation for

Below entrances, return points, and any large Below pocket connected to the overworld.

  • The run generator is data-driven and deterministic by seed. A run can be

replayed from its profile, seed, chosen room sequence, modifier list, and reward seed without relying on live actor order.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRunTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRunSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRoomTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowModifierTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRewardTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowSaveTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Endgame/...

Editor module layout:

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRunValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRoomKitAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowRewardAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Endgame/AscentiaBelowPacingAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaBelowRunAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaBelowRunSimulationCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaBelowRunDeterminismTest.cpp

Starter content folders:

/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Profiles/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Rooms/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/RoomKits/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/LevelInstances/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Encounters/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Modifiers/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Hazards/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Rewards/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Presentation/
/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Simulation/

Starter assets:

PDA_BelowRunProfile_MVP
PDA_BelowRunTierProfile_TierTwo_MVP
PDA_BelowRoomSet_MVP
PDA_BelowRoom_Start_MVP
PDA_BelowRoom_CombatSmall_MVP
PDA_BelowRoom_ShrineChoice_MVP
PDA_BelowRoom_TreasureCache_MVP
PDA_BelowRoom_GuardianArena_MVP
PDA_BelowRoom_Exit_MVP
PDA_BelowModifierSet_MVP
PDA_BelowModifier_AshFog_MVP
PDA_BelowModifier_RotBloom_MVP
PDA_BelowRewardProfile_MVP
PDA_BelowCacheRewardProfile_MVP
PDA_BelowPacingProfile_MVP
PDA_BelowTransitionProfile_MVP

Core data structures:

FAscentiaBelowRunProfile
  RunProfileTag
  RequiredWorldState
  TierProfileTag
  RoomSetTag
  ModifierSetTag
  RewardProfileTag
  PacingProfileTag
  TransitionProfileTag
  SaveResumePolicyTag
  FailurePolicyTag
  MaxRooms
  MinCombatRooms
  MaxOptionalBranches

FAscentiaBelowRunState
  RunGuid
  RunProfileTag
  RunSeed
  RewardSeed
  TierTag
  ActiveRoomIndex
  ChosenRooms
  ChosenModifiers
  CompletedRoomIds
  ClaimedRewardIds
  EntryWorldAnchorTag
  ReturnWorldAnchorTag
  bCompleted
  bFailed
  StartedAtUtc

FAscentiaBelowRoomDefinition
  RoomTag
  RoomTypeTag
  LevelInstanceAsset
  PackedLevelActorAsset
  AllowedDoorSockets
  RequiredDoorSockets
  EncounterProfileTag
  HazardProfileTag
  RewardNodeTags
  PCGDecorationProfileTag
  DataLayerPolicyTag
  StreamingBudgetTag
  NavValidationTag
  CameraReadabilityTag

FAscentiaBelowConnection
  FromRoomTag
  FromSocketTag
  ToRoomTag
  ToSocketTag
  DoorPolicyTag
  LockPolicyTag
  TraversalRequirementTags
  BacktrackPolicyTag

FAscentiaBelowModifier
  ModifierTag
  DisplayTextKey
  DifficultyBudgetDelta
  RewardBudgetDelta
  GameplayTagGrants
  EnemyAffixTags
  HazardTags
  PresentationTags
  AccessibilityRiskTags

FAscentiaBelowRewardNode
  RewardNodeTag
  RewardSourceProfile
  CompletionRequirementTags
  GuaranteedRewards
  WeightedRewardTables
  CurrencyGrant
  UniqueRewardPolicy
  ClaimPolicyTag

Subsystem boundary:

UAscentiaBelowRunSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  CanStartRun(ProfileTag, Context)
  StartRun(ProfileTag, OptionalSeed, Context)
  BuildRunLayout(RunState)
  LoadRunRoom(RoomIndex)
  CompleteRoom(RoomTag, CompletionContext)
  ClaimReward(RewardNodeTag)
  FailRun(FailureReasonTag)
  ReturnAbove(ReturnReasonTag)
  BuildRunSaveFragment()
  RestoreRunFromSave(Fragment)
  WriteBelowRunEvidence(OutputPath)

Rules:

  • A generated Below run is a deterministic plan first and a streamed/playable

space second. The plan writes chosen rooms, sockets, modifiers, encounters, reward nodes, and seeds before gameplay begins.

  • Room kits are authored assets. PCG may decorate, vary clutter, and fill

optional dressing sockets, but it cannot invent critical doors, exits, reward nodes, bosses, or save anchors.

  • Every room definition declares entrance/exit sockets, nav build status,

camera readability, encounter budget, reward node policy, and performance budget.

  • Difficulty modifiers alter declared systems only: enemy profile tags, hazard

tags, reward budget, presentation tags, and accessibility risk tags. No modifier may patch arbitrary Blueprint logic at runtime.

  • Rewards go through P16 loot source profiles and item transactions. Below cache

rewards are not special-case item grants.

  • Encounters go through P18 encounter definitions. A guardian room is an

encounter profile, not a hard-coded enemy spawn.

  • Entry, return, death, disconnect, save/load, and abandon all have explicit

transition policies. No run can strand the player in an unloaded or invalid space.

  • Run save data stores the run plan and meaningful room/reward completion state,

not live actor pointers or generated component state.

  • Accessibility profiles can reduce visual intensity, fog, flashing hazards,

camera shake, and audio density without changing reward seeds or completion authority.

Designer-facing tools:

EUW_AscentiaBelowRunLab
  choose profile, seed, tier, room set, modifiers, reward profile, and preview
  generated layout before launching a run

EUW_AscentiaBelowRoomKitBoard
  inspect room definitions, Level Instance assets, door sockets, nav, camera,
  encounter, hazard, reward, PCG, Data Layer, and performance validation

EUW_AscentiaBelowPacingSimulator
  simulate room count, combat/rest/treasure cadence, modifiers, expected time,
  reward value, failure rate, and resource pressure

EUW_AscentiaBelowRewardLab
  preview cache rewards, unique policies, currency grants, reward-node claims,
  and transaction ledger rows

EUW_AscentiaBelowTransitionProof
  test enter, room travel, return, death, abandon, save/load, and packaged
  streaming readiness

Evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowRuns/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowRooms/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowPacing/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowRewards/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowTransitions/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowDeterminism/{RunId}/report.json

Commandlet contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaBelowRunAudit
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Profiles/PDA_BelowRunProfile_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowRuns

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaBelowRunSimulation
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Endgame/Below/Profiles/PDA_BelowRunProfile_MVP
  -Seeds=1000
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P16/BelowPacing

Starter proof matrix:

BelowRun_MVP_DeterministicLayout
  same profile and seed produce identical rooms, sockets, modifiers, rewards,
  and encounter tags

BelowRoomKit_DoorSocketConnectivity
  every selected room connects entrance/exit sockets without overlaps, missing
  doors, invalid backtracking, or unreachable exits

BelowRoomKit_LevelInstanceValidation
  each room Level Instance has nav, collision, lighting, Data Layer policy,
  camera readability, encounter anchors, and reward sockets

BelowRun_ModifierBudget
  modifiers alter declared tags and budgets only, with accessibility risk rows

BelowReward_TransactionLedger
  cache and room rewards grant through P16 transaction requests with replayable
  ledger output

BelowEncounter_GuardianProfile
  guardian room resolves through P18 encounter/enemy profiles and persists
  defeat/completion according to run policy

BelowTransition_EnterReturnDeathAbandon
  enter, return, death, abandon, save/load, and packaged streaming readiness
  restore the player to valid anchors

BelowRun_SaveLoadResume
  run plan, active room, claimed rewards, and completed rooms survive save/load
  without saving actor pointers

BelowPacing_OneHundredSeeds
  simulation reports p10/p50/p90 room count, time, rewards, resource pressure,
  failure risk, and dead-run conditions

Implementation tickets:

P16B-001 Below run profile, room, modifier, reward, and save structs
  Add `FAscentiaBelowRunProfile`, `FAscentiaBelowRunState`,
  `FAscentiaBelowRoomDefinition`, `FAscentiaBelowModifier`,
  `FAscentiaBelowRewardNode`, and profile DataAssets.

P16B-002 Deterministic run builder
  Add `UAscentiaBelowRunSubsystem`, seed selection, room sequence generation,
  connection validation, modifier selection, and run evidence output.

P16B-003 Room kit and Level Instance validation
  Add room-kit audits for sockets, nav, collision, camera, encounter anchors,
  rewards, PCG decoration policy, Data Layers, and performance budget.

P16B-004 Reward and transaction bridge
  Route Below rewards through P16 loot source profiles and item transactions,
  with cache reward ledger replay and reward pacing reports.

P16B-005 Encounter, hazard, and modifier bridge
  Route guardian/combat rooms through P18 encounter definitions, hazards through
  declared profiles, and modifiers through allowed tags and budget deltas.

P16B-006 Transition, save/load, and failure proof
  Add enter/return/death/abandon/save/load policies, valid anchor checks,
  streaming readiness proof, and run save fragments.

P16B-007 Designer Below labs and dashboard cards
  Add run lab, room kit board, pacing simulator, reward lab, transition proof,
  evidence cards, and package gates for invalid runs.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build P16B-001 and P16B-002 around the current MVP Below run facts.
  2. Add P16B-003 before authoring more room kits or importing modular dungeon

packs.

  1. Add P16B-004 before expanding Below rewards beyond the cache shard.
  2. Add P16B-005 before guardian variants, hazards, or difficulty modifiers.
  3. Add P16B-006 before any run can be saved mid-Below or used in packaged

milestone proof.

#19. Damage Model, Build Math, Status, Criticals, and Defensive Identity

#19.1 Player-facing goal

P13 is where Ascentia becomes a build game, not merely an action prototype with health bars.

The player-facing promise:

  • attacks have readable damage types;
  • armor, resistances, poise, block, parry, dodge, and status effects create

distinct defensive identities;

  • stats matter because they change resources, scaling, crits, equipment burden,

spell economy, and passive unlocks;

  • items matter because their affixes speak the same language as attributes,

abilities, status, and movement;

  • UI explains enough that a player can make a build decision without opening a

spreadsheet;

  • Designers can tune curves, thresholds, payloads, and profiles in the Editor;
  • proof tools show the exact formula path for any combat scenario.

The target feel is not "numbers everywhere." It is Soulslike commitment and readability joined to ARPG build expressiveness. The player should understand why a heavy armor build survives, why a glass spellblade bursts, why poison is pressure rather than instant damage, why frost creates a punish window, and why equip load changes dodge identity.

#19.2 Current Ascentia state

The native MVP is already useful:

  • FAscentiaDamagePacket carries magnitude, damage school, poise damage,

optional status buildup tag/amount/threshold, source tags, and instigator.

  • EAscentiaDamageSchool currently includes Physical, Slash, Strike, Pierce,

Fire, Frost, Lightning, Poison, Rot, Arcane, and TrueDamage.

  • UAscentiaCombatComponent::ApplyDamagePacket is server-authoritative and

handles parry, dodge iframes, block, guard break, armor/resistance formula mitigation, health/poise SetByCaller magnitudes, status buildup, cue request telemetry, and replicated last-damage facts.

  • Physical-family damage uses armor mitigation. The current MVP formula is:
DamageAfterArmor = RawDamage * (100 / (100 + Armor))
  • Per-school resistance is clamped from -95% to +95% and applied after armor:
FinalDamage = DamageAfterArmor * (1 - ResistancePercent / 100)
  • TrueDamage bypasses armor and resistance.
  • Status buildup accumulates per status tag, preserves overflow after threshold,

and emits OnStatusBuildupChanged.

  • Status payloads currently support native Poison/Rot-style damage-over-time and

Frost burst damage/poise, with replicated payload telemetry and OnStatusPayloadTriggered.

  • Critical execution already scales off replicated CriticalChancePercent,

CriticalDamageMultiplier, AGI-derived effective chance, and INT-derived effective multiplier, then applies true-damage execution output.

  • UAscentiaAttributeSet exposes replicated primary stats, resources, armor,

crit, poise, movement, spectrum stats, equip load, and meta incoming damage.

  • Current derived defaults:
MaxHealth    = 40 + 6.0 * CON
MaxBreath    = 70 + 3.0 * END
BreathRegen  = 10 + 0.5 * END
MaxEth       = 20 + 4.0 * FOC
MaxEquipLoad = 30 + 1.0 * CON
  • UAscentiaProgressionComponent handles Stones, level cost, unspent stat

points, invest/refund/reset, active threshold passive tags, and tier unlocks beginning at 20 and every 10 points thereafter.

  • Equip load already drives roll tier, dodge Breath cost, impulse strength,

iframe duration, and overloaded rejection.

The production gaps are also clear:

  • status payload choice is string-matched from the status tag;
  • formulas are component constants instead of named data profiles and curves;
  • armor/resistance/crit/stat scaling has no Designer-facing formula lab yet;
  • status decay, immunity, refresh, stacking, cleanse, boss resistance, and UI

rules are not authored as data;

  • random crit, backstab, riposte, stance-break execution, and weapon-specific

crit curves are not separated enough;

  • stat curves and soft caps are hard-coded linear relationships;
  • threshold passives unlock tags, but the actual passive effects/icons/callouts

still need authored data;

  • build UI does not yet preview effective survivability, damage, status buildup,

roll tier, or passive changes.

#19.3 Production damage pipeline

Damage should move through one authoritative pipeline no matter whether the source is a light attack, spell, throwable, bow shot, trap, status tick, boss move, environmental hazard, or future co-op/invasion actor.

Pipeline:

Ability / weapon / AI / hazard
  -> builds FAscentiaDamagePacket or FAscentiaDamageEvent
  -> validates contact, target, source tags, authority, and friendly-fire policy
  -> resolves source profile: ability, weapon, item affixes, buffs, stat scaling
  -> resolves target profile: attributes, armor, resistances, guard, poise, tags
  -> applies defensive state: parry, iframe, block, guard break, absorption
  -> applies conditional offense: backstab, riposte, crit, weak point, counter hit
  -> applies formula mitigation: armor, resistance, vulnerability, caps
  -> applies status buildup: resistance, threshold, decay, overflow, immunity
  -> writes GAS SetByCaller magnitudes for health, poise, status, and cues
  -> Attribute Set clamps resources and health
  -> emits telemetry, GameplayCues, UI events, audio/VFX, camera, rumble

Every stage must be observable. A Designer should be able to click a damage report and see:

RawDamage
SourceAbility
WeaponProfile
DamageSchool
SourceTags
TargetTags
Armor
ArmorMitigation
ResistancePercent
ConditionalMultipliers
FinalHealthDamage
FinalPoiseDamage
StatusBuildupAdded
StatusBuildupTotal
StatusPayloadTriggered
CueTags

The current ApplyDamagePacket function is the MVP spine. Production should move calculation complexity into a named GAS execution or native calculation object so it can capture source/target attributes and tags consistently. GameplayEffects remain the unit of application; DataAssets and curves author the policy; native code owns the final invariant.

#19.3.1 GAS execution and formula trace contract

Epic's GAS model fits this phase well: Gameplay Abilities own activation, Gameplay Effects own attribute mutation, Attribute Sets own replicated values, and Gameplay Effect Calculations can concentrate complex source/target math in one reusable place. Ascentia should use that shape without hiding the numbers.

Recommended route:

UAscentiaDamageExecutionCalculation
  captures source attributes, target attributes, source tags, target tags
  calls UAscentiaDamageFormulaService
  writes SetByCaller health, poise, status, and cue magnitudes

UAscentiaDamageFormulaService
  pure/native deterministic formula resolver
  usable by runtime execution, editor formula lab, tests, and reports

FAscentiaDamageFormulaInput
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  SourceAbilityTag
  SourceItemId
  DamageProfile
  DamagePackets[]
  SourceAttributeSnapshot
  TargetAttributeSnapshot
  SourceTags
  TargetTags
  DefensiveStateSnapshot
  ConditionalContext
  RandomSeed
  PredictionKeyOrTraceId

FAscentiaDamageFormulaOutput
  PacketResults[]
  TotalHealthDamage
  TotalPoiseDamage
  TotalStatusBuildupByTag
  TriggeredStatusPayloads[]
  CritResult
  GuardResult
  StanceResult
  CueRequests[]
  RejectionReason
  bApplied

FAscentiaDamageFormulaTrace
  TraceId
  SourceSummary
  TargetSummary
  FormulaProfileId
  StepResults[]
  CapturedAttributes[]
  CapturedTags
  FinalOutput
  DesignerNotes

Why keep a formula service separate from the GAS execution? The execution is the runtime adapter. The service is the reusable truth. EUW_AscentiaDamageFormulaLab can call the service against scenario snapshots without spawning real combat. Automation can diff trace JSON. Designers can inspect the same steps that the server uses.

Trace rules:

  • each multiplication, clamp, immunity, rejection, and SetByCaller write gets a

named trace step;

  • trace steps carry before/after values and source asset references;
  • random crits record seed, roll, chance, and result;
  • deterministic execution windows record angle, range, target state, and source

state;

  • true damage records the approving profile and source tag;
  • status writes record threshold, resistance, decay state, overflow, and payload;
  • Designer-facing reports must use display names, but machine reports must use

asset paths, tags, and row ids.

#19.4 Damage profile data

Use authored profiles instead of local constants.

PDA_DamageProfile
  DamageProfileId
  DisplayName
  SourceKind: Weapon, Ability, Spell, Status, Hazard, EnemyMove
  BasePackets[]
  ScalingRules[]
  ConditionalRules[]
  StatusBuildupRules[]
  PoiseRules
  CueProfile
  DamageNumberProfile
  TelemetryGroup

FAscentiaDamagePacketRow
  Magnitude
  DamageSchool
  PoiseDamage
  StatusBuildupTag
  StatusBuildupAmount
  StatusTriggerThreshold
  SourceTags

FAscentiaDamageScalingRule
  Attribute
  Curve
  SoftCapProfile
  WeaponScalingGrade
  MinContribution
  MaxContribution
  SourceTagRequirements

FAscentiaConditionalDamageRule
  RuleTag
  RequiredSourceTags
  RequiredTargetTags
  ForbiddenTargetTags
  Multiplier
  AdditiveBonus
  Priority
  TelemetryLabel

Additional production profiles:

PDA_DamageSchoolProfile
  SchoolTag
  DisplayName
  SchoolFamily: Physical, Elemental, Status, Arcane, True
  ResistanceAttribute
  DefaultDamageNumberProfile
  DefaultCueFamily
  ArmorInteractionPolicy
  VulnerabilityPolicy
  ColorPaletteKey
  TooltipTextKey

PDA_DamageFormulaProfile
  FormulaProfileId
  ArmorCurve
  ResistanceClampMin
  ResistanceClampMax
  MinimumDamagePolicy
  RoundingPolicy
  TrueDamageAllowedSourceTags
  FormulaStepOrder
  TraceVerbosity

PDA_DefenseFormulaProfile
  DefenseProfileId
  ArmorAppliesToFamilies
  ArmorMitigationCurve
  BlockBreathCostCurve
  GuardBreakPolicy
  PoiseDamageTakenCurve
  EffectiveSurvivabilityScenarios

PDA_EffectiveSurvivabilityProfile
  ScenarioSetId
  IncomingDamageSamples[]
  SchoolMix
  IncludeBlock
  IncludeDodgeAvoidance
  IncludePotionRecovery
  IncludeStatusPressure
  TooltipPolicy

FAscentiaDefenseBreakdown
  RawIncomingDamage
  ArmorValue
  ArmorMitigationPercent
  ResistancePercent
  ResistanceCappedPercent
  BlockPreventedDamage
  FinalDamage
  EffectiveHealthEquivalent
  Notes

Rows vs assets:

  • DataTables are correct for numeric bulk: school names, base formula constants,

stat curves, resistance curves, status thresholds, damage-number colors.

  • PrimaryDataAssets are correct for composed content: weapon damage profile,

spell profile, boss move profile, status payload profile, unique item power.

  • GameplayTags are the boundary language: never branch on display strings for

status or school behavior.

#19.5 Formula policy

The formula must be stable enough for designers to tune and transparent enough for players to trust.

Recommended order:

1. Base packet magnitude
2. Source stat scaling
3. Weapon / spell / ability profile multiplier
4. Equipment and passive modifiers
5. Conditional source/target rules
6. Defensive state mutation: parry, iframe, block, guard break
7. Armor mitigation
8. School resistance or vulnerability
9. Final clamps and minimum-damage policy
10. Health/poise SetByCaller application
11. Status buildup and payload

Armor:

  • keep the current 100 / (100 + Armor) formula as the MVP physical-family

curve because it has natural diminishing returns and is easy to explain;

  • promote it into PDA_DefenseFormulaProfile or CurveFloat before content

volume grows;

  • show effective health against physical-family damage in UI and formula lab;
  • decide whether Armor eventually mitigates all damage or only physical-family

damage. Diablo IV's recent public defense overhaul is evidence that broad armor/resistance relationships tend to need multiple iterations and strong UI.

Resistance:

  • keep clamping, but move min/max caps into data;
  • support negative resistance as vulnerability, because it creates readable

debuffs and enemy weaknesses;

  • distinguish temporary resistance buffs from permanent item/attribute facts;
  • show capped and uncapped values in debug/UI so players understand wasted stats.

TrueDamage:

  • allow it only for execution, death/reset, and explicitly authored exceptional

effects;

  • never use TrueDamage as an easy way around formula bugs;
  • require telemetry label and source tag;
  • validate that normal weapons, spells, DOTs, and enemy basic attacks do not use

TrueDamage unless approved.

Damage number policy:

PDA_DamageNumberProfile
  SchoolColor
  CritStyle
  WeaknessStyle
  BlockedStyle
  GuardBreakStyle
  DOTAggregationPolicy
  AccessibilityVariant

Damage numbers should not become the combat UI. They are confirmation and learning tools. The player should read enemy reaction, VFX, SFX, camera, and health movement first.

Formula trace steps:

FAscentiaFormulaStep
  StepTag
  DisplayName
  Operation
  SourceAsset
  SourceField
  Priority
  bBlocksApplication

FAscentiaFormulaTraceStep
  StepTag
  InputValue
  OutputValue
  ModifierValue
  ClampMin
  ClampMax
  bClamped
  bRejected
  RejectReason
  SourceAssetPath

Determinism policy:

  • server is authoritative for all health, poise, status, and resource mutation;
  • editor preview uses the same formula service with frozen snapshots;
  • formula steps use stable ordering, not unordered tag iteration;
  • floating-point output is rounded only at defined display/application

boundaries;

  • status/crit random rolls use traceable seeds in automation;
  • UI never re-computes gameplay truth from display text.

Player trust policy:

  • show "why" before showing every coefficient;
  • expose final damage, primary mitigation reason, resistance/vulnerability, and

status result in compact UI;

  • keep full step-by-step traces in editor/debug tools;
  • avoid formula changes that require players to relearn every build unless the

dashboard marks the rebalance as an explicit design event.

#19.6 Status lifecycle

Status should be a complete lifecycle, not a tag that happens to trigger damage.

Lifecycle:

Incoming hit applies buildup
  -> target status resistance modifies buildup
  -> current buildup increases
  -> optional decay pauses/resumes
  -> threshold reached
  -> overflow retained or cleared by policy
  -> payload starts
  -> immunity/resistance window may begin
  -> cue/UI/audio state activates
  -> payload ticks, bursts, debuffs, or modifies attributes
  -> refresh/stack/extend policy applies on repeat triggers
  -> cleanse/cure/death/save rules resolve

Data:

PDA_StatusPayload
  StatusTag
  DisplayName
  DamageSchool
  BuildupThresholdCurve
  BuildupDecayDelay
  BuildupDecayRate
  TargetResistanceAttribute
  TriggerPolicy: Burst, DOT, Debuff, Stagger, Control, Hybrid
  OverflowPolicy: Retain, ClampToZero, CarryPercent
  RefreshPolicy: Extend, Restart, Stack, IgnoreWhileActive
  StackLimit
  ImmunityAfterTriggerSeconds
  CleanseTags
  SavePolicy
  GameplayCueOnBuildup
  GameplayCueOnTrigger
  GameplayCueWhileActive
  UIIcon
  ColorblindPaletteKey

Runtime state:

FAscentiaStatusState
  StatusTag
  CurrentBuildup
  TriggerThreshold
  DecayDelayRemaining
  ActiveStackCount
  ActiveDurationRemaining
  ImmunityRemaining
  LastSourceActor
  LastSourceProfile
  LastAppliedWorldTime
  bActive
  bImmune

FAscentiaStatusApplicationRequest
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  StatusTag
  BuildupAmount
  DamageSchool
  SourceTags
  ApplicationProfile
  TraceId

FAscentiaStatusApplicationResult
  StatusTag
  AddedBuildup
  EffectiveBuildupAfterResistance
  NewBuildup
  Threshold
  bTriggered
  bPayloadStarted
  bImmuneRejected
  bOverflowRetained
  PayloadTag
  CueRequests[]

Status resistance:

PDA_StatusResistanceProfile
  TargetArchetypeTag
  StatusTag
  BuildupMultiplier
  ThresholdMultiplier
  DecayMultiplier
  PayloadDamageMultiplier
  ImmunityAfterTriggerSeconds
  BossPhaseOverrides[]

PDA_StatusUIProfile
  StatusTag
  BuildupBarStyle
  ActiveIcon
  TriggerCallout
  TooltipTextKey
  ColorblindPaletteKey
  NumberAggregationPolicy

Initial Ascentia statuses:

  • Poison: sustained health pressure; low poise impact; clear DOT number cadence.
  • Rot: heavier long pressure, possibly slower cure and more world/biome identity.
  • Frost: buildup into burst plus poise/slow/stagger opportunity.
  • Bleed: future burst on threshold, likely physical-family and weapon dependent.
  • Burn: future DOT plus panic/readability risk in VFX-heavy encounters.
  • Arcane: future resource disruption, vulnerability, or spectral interaction.

Rules:

  • payload behavior must come from PDA_StatusPayload, not string matching;
  • status UI must show buildup separately from active debuff state;
  • bosses can have different thresholds, decay, immunity, and payload reduction;
  • DOTs should support aggregation display so poison does not spam unreadable

numbers;

  • every status requires color, icon, audio state, cue, accessibility variant, and

tooltip copy before it is production-ready.

  • save/load rules must distinguish transient combat buildup from long-lived

active conditions. As a default, normal buildup should not persist through a reload, while authored long-term curses or world-state afflictions need their own save fragment and UI explanation.

#19.7 Criticals, backstabs, ripostes, and executions

Critical systems should be split by intent:

RandomCrit
  probability-based damage spike, mostly item/stat/build expression

Backstab
  positioning reward, deterministic if angle/range/state rules pass

Riposte
  defense reward after parry or guard break

StanceBreakExecution
  poise/stance reward after pressure

WeakPoint
  target anatomy or boss-mechanic reward

Do not treat these as one "crit" flag.

Data:

PDA_CriticalProfile
  RandomCritEnabled
  ChanceCurve
  DamageMultiplierCurve
  BackstabAngleDegrees
  BackstabRange
  BackstabAllowedWeaponFamilies
  RiposteWindowSeconds
  ExecutionDamageProfile
  VictimAlignmentPolicy
  CameraProfile
  CueProfile
  PromptProfile

Execution decision data:

FAscentiaCriticalCheckInput
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  AttackProfile
  WeaponFamily
  SourceStats
  TargetState
  SourceTags
  TargetTags
  RelativeAngle
  Distance
  RandomSeed

FAscentiaCriticalCheckResult
  CriticalMode: None, RandomCrit, Backstab, Riposte, StanceBreakExecution, WeakPoint
  bAllowed
  Chance
  Roll
  Multiplier
  ExecutionProfile
  PromptProfile
  RejectReason
  TraceSteps[]

PDA_ExecutionProfile
  ExecutionTag
  AllowedWeaponFamilies
  RequiredTargetStateTags
  RequiredSourceStateTags
  AlignmentPolicy
  InvulnerabilityPolicy
  DamageProfile
  MontageSet
  CameraProfile
  CueProfile
  RewardPolicy

Current AGI/INT scaling is useful MVP proof:

EffectiveCritChance = CriticalChancePercent + max(0, AGI - 10) * 0.5
EffectiveCritDamage = CriticalDamageMultiplier + max(0, INT - 10) * 0.02

Production changes:

  • move these constants into crit/stat curves;
  • let weapon families opt into different crit identities;
  • keep random crit modest in Soulslike melee so animation commitment remains

readable;

  • use backstab/riposte/stance-break execution for large deterministic payoff;
  • avoid true damage for all crits. True damage should be execution-specific, not

the generic answer to "crit ignores defense."

  • weak points should belong to target anatomy or boss mechanics, not generic

damage math. A skeleton socket, hit region, phase tag, or exposed weak-point component should provide the fact; the P13 formula should only consume it.

#19.8 Poise, stance, guard, and defensive identity

Ascentia has health and poise damage already. P13 should turn that into a defensive grammar.

Concepts:

Health
  death pressure

Poise
  interruption/stagger pressure

Guard
  Breath conversion and angle/state defense

GuardBreak
  failed resource defense, punish window

StanceBreak
  poise threshold state, execution opportunity

Armor
  physical-family mitigation

Resistance
  school-specific mitigation/vulnerability

Toughness / Effective Survivability
  UI/report view of how much raw damage a build can absorb by school

Data:

PDA_PoiseProfile
  MaxPoiseCurve
  PoiseDamageTakenMultiplierBySchool
  PoiseRecoveryDelay
  PoiseRecoveryRate
  StanceBreakThreshold
  StanceBreakDuration
  BossStanceBreakPolicy

PDA_BlockProfile
  BlockAngle
  BreathCostCurve
  HealthDamageMultiplier
  PoiseDamageMultiplier
  GuardBreakDamageMultiplier
  GuardCounterWindow

Runtime defense state:

FAscentiaDefenseStateSnapshot
  bHasIframes
  bParryWindowOpen
  bBlocking
  bGuardBroken
  bStanceBroken
  BlockFacingDegrees
  CurrentBreath
  CurrentPoise
  MaxPoise
  Armor
  ResistanceBySchool
  EquipLoadTier
  ActiveDefenseTags

FAscentiaGuardResult
  bBlocked
  bPerfectBlock
  bParried
  bGuardBroken
  BreathCost
  HealthDamagePrevented
  PoiseDamagePrevented
  GuardCounterWindowOpened
  RejectReason

PDA_StanceBreakProfile
  TargetArchetypeTag
  PoiseThreshold
  RecoveryDelay
  PunishWindowSeconds
  ExecutionProfile
  BossPhaseInteraction
  CueProfile

PDA_GuardProfile
  GuardTag
  BlockAngle
  BreathCostCurve
  ChipDamagePolicy
  PerfectBlockWindow
  GuardBreakPolicy
  CounterWindowPolicy

Build identities:

  • Heavy guard: high armor, high poise, strong block, slower roll, Breath pressure.
  • Light evasive: low armor, fast roll, longer iframe, less poise, better crit or

status access.

  • Spellblade: moderate defense, Eth scaling, crit/status burst, weaker guard.
  • Rot/poison build: lower immediate burst, high uptime pressure and status

threshold manipulation.

  • Execution build: poise pressure, riposte/backstab windows, high deterministic

payoff.

Designer proof should compare these identities side by side. If two builds use different stats/items but survive and kill in the same way, the build system is not deep yet.

#19.9 Stats, scaling, soft caps, and threshold passives

Stats should answer three questions:

  1. What can I do more often?
  2. What can I survive?
  3. What new build identity opens up?

Current stat roles:

CON -> MaxHealth, MaxEquipLoad
END -> MaxBreath, BreathRegen
FOC -> MaxEth
STR -> weapon/build identity
AGI -> effective crit chance
INT -> effective crit damage
Conviction / Entropy / Nature -> spectrum identity, future spell/world/build axis

Production stat curve:

PDA_StatCurveProfile
  PrimaryStat
  DisplayName
  DerivedAttributeRules[]
  ScalingSoftCaps[]
  TooltipRows[]
  ThresholdPassiveRows[]
  PreviewScenarioSet

FAscentiaDerivedAttributeRule
  SourceStat
  TargetAttribute
  Curve
  MinValue
  MaxValue
  RoundingPolicy

Build preview data:

FAscentiaBuildSnapshot
  CharacterLevel
  PrimaryStats
  DerivedAttributes
  EquippedItems
  ActivePassives
  ActiveBuffs
  DamageProfiles
  DefenseProfiles
  EquipLoadTier
  SchoolResistances
  StatusResistances

FAscentiaDerivedAttributePreview
  Attribute
  CurrentValue
  PreviewValue
  Delta
  SourceStat
  Curve
  SoftCapBand
  TooltipRows[]

PDA_BuildArchetypeProfile
  ArchetypeTag
  DisplayName
  StatPriorityHints
  WeaponFamilyHints
  DefenseIdentity
  StatusIdentity
  ExampleItemTags
  PreviewScenarioSet

Soft caps:

  • avoid endless linear gains for core survivability and damage;
  • expose breakpoints in tooltips;
  • let early points feel meaningful;
  • prevent one stat from being mandatory for every build;
  • verify with level-range scenarios, not one default character.

Threshold passives:

PDA_ThresholdPassiveProfile
  PassiveTag
  PrimaryStat
  RequiredValue
  Tier
  DisplayName
  Icon
  GameplayEffect
  GrantedTags
  UnlockCalloutProfile
  RespecPolicy

The native component can keep evaluating tags. The authored profile should decide what each tag does, how it is explained, and what presentation appears when it unlocks.

The build planner should answer these questions before a point is committed:

What changes immediately?
What threshold passive is closer?
Does roll tier change?
Does effective survivability change by school?
Does this stat affect my equipped weapon/spell scaling?
Does this stat unlock an item, spell, or passive requirement?

#19.10 Equip load and roll tier

Equip load is build math and combat feel at the same time. It must be tuned with motion, animation, Breath economy, and collision proof.

Current tier shape:

Light  -> ratio < 0.30
Medium -> ratio <= 0.70
Heavy  -> ratio <= 1.00
Overloaded -> ratio > 1.00

Production data:

PDA_EquipLoadProfile
  LightMaxRatio
  MediumMaxRatio
  HeavyMaxRatio
  BreathCostByTier
  ImpulseByTier
  IframeByTier
  RecoveryByTier
  AnimationSetByTier
  UIWarningProfile

Rules:

  • roll tier must be visible in stat/equipment UI;
  • changing gear should preview roll tier before confirm/equip;
  • overloaded must explain exactly why dodge is blocked;
  • roll-tier tuning must be tested with the combat motion solver on flat ground,

stairs, ledges, slopes, and wall collisions;

  • Designer tools should show animation length, root-motion distance, capsule

path, intended direction, accepted cancel window, and actual movement result.

Motion proof output:

FAscentiaEquipLoadMotionReport
  BuildSnapshot
  Tier
  DodgeMontage
  RootMotionDistance
  CapsuleDistance
  FloorProjectionResult
  LedgeResult
  StairResult
  WallSlideResult
  BreathCost
  IframeWindow
  RecoveryWindow
  DesignerVerdict

Blueprints and editor assets should make P13 visible.

Designer lab minimum:

Scenario:
  Source build
  Source weapon/spell/ability
  Target archetype
  Target armor/resistance/poise/block state
  Status settings
  Crit/backstab/riposte settings

Output:
  formula trace
  final damage
  poise result
  status buildup/result
  effective survivability by school
  UI preview
  cue preview
  saved evidence JSON

#19.12 P13 evidence, formula lab, and reports

P13 needs evidence that can be reviewed by Designers and consumed by the dashboard.

Formula lab scenarios:

FAscentiaDamageFormulaScenario
  ScenarioId
  SourceBuildSnapshot
  TargetBuildSnapshot
  SourceActionProfile
  TargetDefenseState
  ExpectedBands
  RequiredTraceSteps
  Notes

FAscentiaDamageFormulaReport
  ReportId
  FormulaProfile
  ScenarioResults[]
  ArmorCurveFindings
  ResistanceCurveFindings
  TrueDamageFindings
  CritFindings
  StatusFindings
  FailedExpectations[]
  SuggestedTuningActions[]

FAscentiaBuildMathReport
  ReportId
  LevelRange
  ArchetypeResults[]
  SoftCapFindings
  ThresholdPassiveFindings
  EffectiveSurvivabilityFindings
  EquipLoadFindings
  DeadStatWarnings[]

FAscentiaStatusPayloadReport
  ReportId
  StatusPayloads[]
  ThresholdScenarios[]
  DecayScenarios[]
  RefreshStackScenarios[]
  BossResistanceScenarios[]
  UIAndCueCoverage
  AccessibilityCoverage

Required proof scenarios:

Tools/remote_playtest.py damage_formula_matrix
Tools/remote_playtest.py damage_school_resistance
Tools/remote_playtest.py status_decay_refresh
Tools/remote_playtest.py status_payload
Tools/remote_playtest.py critical_stats
Tools/remote_playtest.py poise_guard_break
Tools/remote_playtest.py equip_load_roll_tiers
Tools/remote_playtest.py threshold_passives
Tools/remote_playtest.py build_summary_preview

Editor evidence outputs:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/DamageFormula/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/DamageFormula/{RunId}/trace_steps.csv
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/BuildMath/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/StatusPayloads/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/EquipLoadMotion/{RunId}/report.json

Acceptance shape:

  • no formula profile can pass validation without at least one scenario;
  • no status payload can pass validation without UI, cue, decay, and resistance

coverage;

  • no threshold passive can pass validation without an effect, icon, tooltip,

unlock callout, and respec behavior;

  • no build archetype can pass validation if all recommended stats produce the

same effective survivability/damage identity;

  • no damage school can pass validation if it lacks icon/color/cue/resistance

policy and display text;

  • every formula change should regenerate old scenario traces so balance drift is

explicit.

#19.13 Interconnects

P13 touches nearly every later system:

  • P10/P11 combat: damage and poise determine stagger, guard break, riposte,

cancel reward, cue intensity, and boss pressure.

  • P14 weapons: weapon families need damage profiles, scaling grades, crit rules,

status access, poise profiles, and equip weight.

  • P15 magic: spells need school, Eth cost, scaling, resist interactions, status

payloads, and resource/status UI.

  • P16 itemization: affixes must grant attributes, modifiers, status changes,

resistance changes, skill modifications, or unique powers through the same tag vocabulary.

  • P17 world: biomes and zones can bias enemy resistances, status hazards, weather

readability, and loot/status identity.

  • P18 AI: enemies need defensive profiles, status vulnerability, poise/stance

behavior, and readable elite affixes.

  • P19 save/death: stats, passives, respec state, active long-term effects, and

character identity must serialize safely.

  • P20 presentation: damage schools, status, crit, block, guard break, and poise

need cue coverage, audio, VFX, damage-number styles, and accessibility settings.

  • P21 shell/settings: remapping, large text, damage-number visibility, colorblind

status palettes, and stat tooltip verbosity belong in settings.

  • P22 multiplayer: source/target attribute capture, status payload authority,

damage prediction, and inventory/affix validation must be network-safe.

#19.14 Validation and proof gates

P13 is not complete until:

  1. Damage formulas are data-backed and can be inspected in

EUW_AscentiaDamageFormulaLab.

  1. Every damage school has a row/profile, display color, resistance policy, cue

family, and damage-number style.

  1. Armor/resistance/true-damage paths are separately verified, including negative

resistance and cap behavior.

  1. Status payloads are data-driven, not string-matched, and support threshold,

overflow, decay, refresh/stack policy, immunity, cleanse, UI, cues, and accessibility palette.

  1. Poison, Rot, Frost, and at least one future burst status such as Bleed have

authored payload profiles and playtest scenarios.

  1. Crit, backstab, riposte, and stance-break execution are separate policies with

separate telemetry.

  1. Stat curves and soft caps are visible in a build planner and validated across

level ranges.

  1. Threshold passives unlock authored effects/icons/callouts and survive

save/load/respec.

  1. Equip load previews roll tier in UI and motion proof confirms flat/stair/ledge

behavior for each tier.

  1. Damage/status/crit/passive/roll settings candidates are registered for P21 UI.
  2. Remote proof covers:
  • damage_model
  • status_payload
  • critical_stats
  • stat_investment
  • threshold_passive
  • roll_type
  • damage_formula_matrix
  • damage_school_resistance
  • status_decay_refresh
  • poise_guard_break
  • equip_load_roll_tiers
  • effective_survivability
  • build_summary_preview
  1. Dashboard evidence links to the latest formula lab report and status payload

report.

#19.15 P13 starter implementation package

The first P13 package should make Ascentia's damage/build math reproducible before it becomes broad. Damage should remain server-authoritative, GAS-backed, and data-authored, while the exact formula path is available to Designers in the Editor.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Damage/AscentiaDamageTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Damage/AscentiaDamageFormulaTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Damage/AscentiaDamageFormulaService.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Damage/AscentiaDamageExecutionCalculation.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Damage/AscentiaDamageProfile.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Status/AscentiaStatusTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Status/AscentiaStatusSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Critical/AscentiaCriticalTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Defense/AscentiaDefenseTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Builds/AscentiaBuildMathTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Builds/AscentiaBuildMathSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Movement/AscentiaEquipLoadTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Damage/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Status/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Critical/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Defense/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Builds/...

Editor module layout:

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Damage/AscentiaDamageFormulaValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Status/AscentiaStatusPayloadValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Builds/AscentiaBuildMathValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Defense/AscentiaDefenseProfileValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaDamageFormulaAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaBuildMathAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaDamageFormulaServiceTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaStatusLifecycleTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaBuildMathSoftCapTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaEquipLoadTierTest.cpp

Subsystem and class boundaries:

UAscentiaDamageExecutionCalculation : UGameplayEffectExecutionCalculation
  GAS adapter
  captures source/target attributes and tags
  calls `UAscentiaDamageFormulaService`
  writes SetByCaller magnitudes and GameplayCue requests

UAscentiaDamageFormulaService : UObject or UGameInstanceSubsystem
  reusable deterministic formula resolver
  ResolveDamageFormula(Input) -> Output
  BuildFormulaTrace(Input, Output) -> Trace
  WriteFormulaReport(Scenarios, OutputPath)

UAscentiaStatusSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  owns status buildup, decay, payload, immunity, and active state
  ApplyStatusBuildup(Request) -> Result
  TickStatusState(Target, DeltaSeconds)
  WriteStatusPayloadReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaBuildMathSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  resolves stat curves, soft caps, threshold passives, and preview snapshots
  BuildSnapshot(Character, Equipment)
  PreviewStatInvestment(Snapshot, Stat, Delta)
  WriteBuildMathReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaCombatComponent
  remains the authoritative gameplay entry point for actual damage application
  delegates formula work instead of owning all constants locally

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Damage/PDA_DamageFormulaProfile_MVP

Formula service signatures:

FAscentiaDamageFormulaOutput ResolveDamageFormula(
  const FAscentiaDamageFormulaInput& Input,
  FAscentiaDamageFormulaTrace* OptionalTrace);

FAscentiaDamageFormulaScenarioResult RunFormulaScenario(
  const FAscentiaDamageFormulaScenario& Scenario);

FAscentiaDefenseBreakdown ResolveDefenseBreakdown(
  const FAscentiaDefenseBreakdownRequest& Request);

FAscentiaCriticalCheckResult ResolveCritical(
  const FAscentiaCriticalCheckInput& Input);

Trace step names should be stable because dashboard diffs and balance reports will compare them:

Damage.BaseMagnitude

Editor surfaces:

EUW_AscentiaDamageFormulaLab
  select source build, target build, damage profile, formula profile
  run scenario
  show formula trace, final health damage, poise, status, cues, expected band

EUW_AscentiaDamageFormulaScenarioMatrix
  run all P13 scenario rows
  compare against expected bands
  export JSON and CSV traces

EUW_AscentiaStatusPayloadPreview
  scrub buildup, resistance, decay, refresh, stack, immunity, cleanse, and DOT

EUW_AscentiaBuildPlanner
  preview stat investment, soft caps, threshold passives, equip load, damage,
  survivability, weapon/spell requirements, and respec result

EUW_AscentiaEffectiveSurvivabilityPreview
  compare archetypes by school, block state, status pressure, and recovery

EUW_AscentiaEquipLoadRollTierPreview
  preview weight changes, tier, Breath cost, iframe window, root-motion policy,
  and required motion proof

EUW_AscentiaCueMatrix
  verify every school/status/crit/guard/poise event has cue coverage

Commandlet contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaDamageFormulaAudit
  -ScenarioTable=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Damage/DT_DamageFormulaScenarios_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaBuildMathAudit
  -ScenarioTable=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Builds/DT_BuildArchetypeScenarios_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13

Tools/run_p13_damage_matrix.ps1
  -Scenario DamageFormulaMatrix
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13

Evidence outputs:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/DamageFormula/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/DamageFormula/{RunId}/trace_steps.csv
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/DamageFormula/{RunId}/scenario_diffs.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/StatusPayloads/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/BuildMath/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/EffectiveSurvivability/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/EquipLoadMotion/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P13/CueCoverage/{RunId}/report.json

Implementation tickets:

P13-001 Damage formula service extraction
  Move armor, resistance, true-damage, crit, poise, and status calculation from
  local component constants into `UAscentiaDamageFormulaService` with trace
  output and unit tests.

P13-002 GAS execution adapter
  Add `UAscentiaDamageExecutionCalculation` as the GAS-facing adapter that
  captures source/target attributes and delegates to the formula service.

P13-003 Damage school and formula profiles
  Add `PDA_DamageSchoolProfile_*`, `PDA_DamageFormulaProfile_MVP`, and
  `PDA_DefenseFormulaProfile_MVP`; validate display text, resistance policy,
  cue family, and true-damage permissions.

P13-004 Status payload data path
  Replace tag-string-matched payload behavior with `PDA_StatusPayload_*`,
  `FAscentiaStatusState`, and `FAscentiaStatusApplicationResult`.

P13-005 Critical, backstab, riposte, and execution split
  Implement `FAscentiaCriticalCheckResult`, `PDA_CriticalProfile_MVP`, and
  separate telemetry for random crit, backstab, riposte, and stance execution.

P13-006 Defense identity profiles
  Add `FAscentiaDefenseStateSnapshot`, `FAscentiaGuardResult`,
  `PDA_GuardProfile_MVP`, and `PDA_StanceBreakProfile_MVP`.

P13-007 Stat curve and threshold passive assets
  Move derived stat constants into `PDA_StatCurveProfile_MVP` and author the
  first threshold passive profiles with icon, effect, tooltip, and respec proof.

P13-008 Build planner and effective survivability preview
  Implement `FAscentiaBuildSnapshot`, `FAscentiaDerivedAttributePreview`,
  `EUW_AscentiaBuildPlanner`, and `EUW_AscentiaEffectiveSurvivabilityPreview`.

P13-009 Equip load profile and motion report bridge
  Add `PDA_EquipLoadProfile_MVP` and make roll tier preview emit
  `FAscentiaEquipLoadMotionReport` references from the combat motion solver.

P13-010 P13 packaged proof suite
  Run formula matrix, status lifecycle, crit/execution, build planner, threshold
  passive, and equip-load proof in PIE and packaged Development, then publish
  dashboard-linked reports.

Validation gates:

  • any PDA_DamageProfile without a formula scenario fails validation;
  • any damage school without icon/color/resistance/cue policy fails validation;
  • any true-damage source without an approved source tag fails validation;
  • any status payload without buildup, decay, refresh, immunity, UI, cue, and

accessibility policy fails validation;

  • any threshold passive without icon, GameplayEffect or granted tags, tooltip,

unlock callout, and respec behavior fails validation;

  • any formula profile change must regenerate scenario traces and list drift;
  • any equip-load profile change must reference a motion report fixture.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Extract formula math into UAscentiaDamageFormulaService without changing

gameplay behavior.

  1. Add trace output and scenario rows for the current MVP formulas.
  2. Add DataAssets that reproduce current values, then point runtime at them.
  3. Replace status string matching with payload assets.
  4. Add formula lab and scenario matrix before adding more schools/statuses.
  5. Only then deepen soft caps, build archetypes, executions, and survivability

previews.

#20. Moveset and Weapon Breadth

#20.1 Player-facing goal

P14 is where weapons stop being stat sticks and become verbs.

The player-facing promise:

  • a dagger, sword, greatsword, shield, bow, staff, throwable, and future exotic

weapon all ask different decisions from the player;

  • a weapon's identity is visible in stance, locomotion overlays, attack timing,

range, damage shape, poise pressure, recovery, cancel windows, sound, trail, hitbox, and UI;

  • two-handing, dual wield, power stance, shield pairing, ammo, quick items, and

weapon arts are authored systems, not special cases;

  • Designers can add a new weapon family or weapon type by authoring data,

montages, notifies, projectiles, cues, and validation assets without spelunking through fallback C++ branches;

  • every weapon action has proof: input accepted, cost consumed, montage played,

notifies fired, hit window matched, damage packet emitted, cue/audio/VFX/UI appeared, and cancel policy behaved.

The production target is not "lots of animations." It is a grammar where every weapon exposes a compact, readable kit and every kit plugs into the same combat, damage, inventory, animation, VFX, audio, UI, settings, and validation systems.

#20.2 Current Ascentia state

P14 already has more native proof than the shallow phase plan implies:

  • light attack combo chains advance through a native three-step sequence,

preserve step-1 base damage, scale step-2/step-3 damage and poise, expire via a combo window, and emit OnLightComboStepStarted;

  • movement attacks exist for running, rolling, and backstep variants, reuse the

light-attack GAS/contact-sweep path, keep separate telemetry, and do not advance standing combo progression;

  • guard counters open after successful block, consume their window, apply boosted

damage/poise, and emit telemetry;

  • jump attacks require the owner to be airborne, apply boosted damage/poise, and

stay isolated from normal combo progression;

  • UAscentiaAbility_WeaponSkill exists as a Breath/cooldown-gated weapon-skill

ability with a default weapon-art tag, source item tag, scaled damage/poise, and OnWeaponSkillStarted;

  • UAscentiaCombatComponent resolves equipped right-hand item tags into

replicated weapon family/type/moveset snapshots;

  • native fallback mappings currently cover Unarmed/Fists, Sword/OneHand,

Dagger/Quick, Greatsword/Heavy, Shield/Buckler, and Bow/Short;

  • off-hand equipment resolves into ShieldPair, DualWield, or PowerStance with

paired moveset tags, two-hand suppression, and conservative multipliers;

  • Ash Shortbow plus Bone Arrow is the first ranged/ammo proof: bow family

required, ammo consumed from the Ammo slot, ranged telemetry emitted, missing bow/ammo rejected safely;

  • Ruin Throwing Knife is the first throwable proof: quick item slot required,

stack consumed, throwable telemetry emitted, empty/non-knife rejected safely;

  • GASP character Blueprints already expose some weapon animation state, while the

native combat component exposes BlueprintImplementableEvents for presentation.

The production gaps:

  • weapon type resolution is hard-coded fallback logic instead of authored

PDA_WeaponType or DT_WeaponTypes;

  • combo/movement/counter/jump/weapon-skill actions need distinct montages,

sections, slots, root-motion policy, notify windows, and cue/audio/VFX timing;

  • weapon skills need per-weapon assignment, UI, cooldown/resource presentation,

montage selection, and balance;

  • ranged needs draw/aim/release flow, reticle/lock-on policy, projectile/trace

choice, ammo HUD, projectile trails, and hit readability;

  • throwables need projectile actor/arc policy, target assist, quick-item HUD,

trajectory preview if desired, and stack/balance rules;

  • dual wield and power stance need authored pairing rows, restrictions,

off-hand visuals, paired montage sets, and conflict messaging;

  • sockets, trail origins, hand offsets, sheathe locations, and collision profiles

must be Designer-adjustable in the Editor;

  • validation must prove each weapon action has animation, notifies, hit windows,

cue coverage, input policy, cost, and damage profile.

#20.3 Production weapon identity model

Weapon identity should be resolved in layers:

Equipped item instance
  -> weapon type row / asset
  -> weapon family
  -> moveset tags
  -> action map
  -> montage set
  -> damage profile
  -> motion profile
  -> cue/audio/VFX profiles
  -> UI prompt and settings keys

The runtime snapshot should remain compact and replicated:

FAscentiaWeaponSnapshot
  ItemTag
  ItemInstanceId
  WeaponFamily
  WeaponTypeTag
  MovesetTags
  DamageProfile
  ActionMap
  MontageSet
  WeaponSkill
  Weight
  SocketProfile

Off-hand snapshot:

FAscentiaOffHandSnapshot
  ItemTag
  WeaponFamily
  WeaponTypeTag
  OffHandMode: None, ShieldPair, DualWield, PowerStance
  OffHandMovesetTags
  PairedMovesetTags
  SuppressedByTwoHanding
  DamageMultiplier
  PoiseMultiplier
  GuardProfile

Resolver contract:

UAscentiaWeaponResolverSubsystem
  ResolveWeaponSnapshot(Inventory, Slot, WorldContext)
  ResolveOffHandSnapshot(Inventory, MainHandSnapshot, OffHandSlot)
  ResolveActionDef(WeaponSnapshot, ActionTag, ContextTags)
  ResolveWeaponSkill(WeaponSnapshot, ItemInstance)
  ResolveAmmoSnapshot(Inventory, RangedProfile)
  ResolveThrowableSnapshot(Inventory, QuickSlot)

FAscentiaWeaponResolutionReport
  SourceItem
  SourceInstance
  WeaponTypeSource
  ActionMapSource
  DamageProfileSource
  MontageSetSource
  SocketProfileSource
  FallbacksUsed[]
  MissingRequirements[]
  ValidationWarnings[]

Rules:

  • runtime code may keep a native fallback for missing content, but the fallback

must be visible in EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser;

  • every snapshot records whether it came from authored item data, generated pack

data, fallback defaults, or temporary Blueprint overrides;

  • weapon snapshots should update when equipment changes, weapon skill changes,

ammo changes, quick item changes, two-hand state changes, or save data restores;

  • UI reads the snapshot, not item rows directly;
  • combat actions read the snapshot, then resolve action data through tags and

Primary Asset IDs;

  • Blueprint presentation reads events and view models, not private C++ defaults.

#20.4 Weapon type and action data

Core data:

PDA_WeaponType
  WeaponTypeTag
  DisplayName
  WeaponFamily
  RequiredEquipmentSlot
  MovesetTags
  DefaultActionMap
  DefaultDamageProfile
  DefaultMotionProfile
  DefaultMontageSet
  DefaultCueProfile
  DefaultSocketProfile
  StatRequirements
  Weight
  TwoHandPolicy
  OffHandPolicy
  RangedPolicy
  ThrowablePolicy
  UIIcon

Action map:

PDA_WeaponActionMap
  WeaponTypeTag
  LightChain
  HeavyAction
  ChargedAction
  RunningAttack
  RollingAttack
  BackstepAttack
  JumpAttack
  GuardCounter
  Backstab
  Riposte
  WeaponSkill
  RangedDraw
  RangedRelease
  ThrowableThrow
  InterruptionRules
  InputBufferPolicy

Action definition:

FAscentiaWeaponActionDef
  ActionTag
  AbilityClass
  Montage
  MontageSection
  SlotName
  RootMotionPolicy
  MotionWarpingPolicy
  DamageProfile
  CostProfile
  TargetingProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  CancelWindows
  DamageWindows
  IframeWindows
  AudioNotifyRequirements
  VFXNotifyRequirements
  CueProfile
  CameraProfile
  RumbleProfile
  UIHintProfile

Action state:

FAscentiaWeaponActionRequest
  Instigator
  ActionTag
  InputTag
  WeaponSnapshot
  OffHandSnapshot
  AmmoSnapshot
  TargetingContext
  IntentVector
  bFromBufferedInput
  ClientRequestTime

FAscentiaWeaponActionResult
  ActionTag
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  AbilitySpecHandle
  Montage
  MontageSection
  ConsumedResources[]
  ConsumedItems[]
  OpenedWindows[]
  TraceId

FAscentiaActiveWeaponActionState
  ActionTag
  WeaponTypeTag
  Montage
  Section
  StartTime
  CurrentPhase: Startup, Active, Recovery, Cancel, Interrupted, Complete
  OpenDamageWindows[]
  OpenCancelWindows[]
  OpenIFrameWindows[]
  PendingProjectileRelease
  bRootMotionOwned
  bCanBufferNextAction

This lets a sword, dagger, and greatsword share the same native "light attack" verb while using different action definitions. It also lets a bow use the same ability/action system without pretending a shot is a melee sweep.

#20.5 Montage, slot, notify, and root-motion contract

Unreal's correct authoring primitives for P14:

  • Animation Montages for one-shot or sectioned action playback;
  • Montage Sections for combo branching, charge/release, looping draw, and

conditional follow-ups;

  • Animation Slots for full-body committed actions and upper-body actions such as

aiming or quick throws;

  • Animation Notifies and Notify States for damage windows, trail windows, swing

audio, projectile release, cancel windows, iframe windows, footstep emphasis, lock-on/warp markers, and cue markers;

  • Root Motion for authored grounded displacement;
  • Motion Warping for target-aligned actions such as ripostes, backstabs,

executions, grabs, mantles, and selected boss moves.

Action timing should live on the animation asset where Designers can see it, but native code must validate it.

Required notify vocabulary:

ANS_Ascentia_DamageWindow
ANS_Ascentia_CancelWindow
ANS_Ascentia_IFrameWindow
ANS_Ascentia_TrailWindow
ANS_Ascentia_AimWindow
ANS_Ascentia_ChargeWindow
AN_Ascentia_SwingAudio
AN_Ascentia_ProjectileRelease
AN_Ascentia_FootstepEmphasis
AN_Ascentia_GameplayCueMarker
AN_Ascentia_MotionWarpTarget

Notify manifest:

FAscentiaAnimNotifyWindowManifest
  Montage
  SlotName
  SectionName
  DamageWindows[]
  CancelWindows[]
  IFrameWindows[]
  TrailWindows[]
  AimWindows[]
  ChargeWindows[]
  ProjectileReleaseFrames[]
  SwingAudioFrames[]
  GameplayCueMarkers[]
  MotionWarpTargets[]
  FootstepMarkers[]

FAscentiaNotifyWindow
  WindowTag
  StartTime
  EndTime
  SourceNotifyName
  RequiredByActionTag
  GameplayMeaning
  bDesignerApproved

FAscentiaMontageActionContract
  ActionTag
  Montage
  ExpectedSlot
  RequiredSections[]
  RequiredNotifyWindows[]
  OptionalNotifyWindows[]
  RootMotionPolicy
  MotionWarpingPolicy
  DedicatedServerNotifyPolicy

Validation examples:

  • every melee action has at least one damage window;
  • every weapon with a trail profile has a trail window and valid sockets;
  • every ranged action has one projectile release notify or explicit trace timing;
  • every cancelable action has cancel windows named by policy;
  • every root-motion montage declares whether ledge/stair/fall behavior uses

combat motion solver rules;

  • every Motion Warping window has a named warp target provider;
  • every montage section named in the action map exists in the montage.
  • every notify required by gameplay authority is configured to fire where

authority needs it. Cosmetic-only notifies may be presentation-only, but damage windows, projectile releases, and resource-relevant windows need an explicit server/runtime policy.

  • every montage used by a weapon action has a manifest generated by

EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor and a compact report attached to the action definition.

Root-motion stance:

FAscentiaWeaponRootMotionPolicy
  ActionTag
  bUsesRootMotion
  bAllowsFloorProjection
  bAllowsLedgeCommit
  bAllowsStairDescent
  bAllowsWallSlide
  FallConversionPolicy
  InputIntentOverridePolicy
  MotionSolverProfile

For ordinary melee, root motion expresses animation commitment. For skills whose distance is a gameplay variable, use combat motion requests or root motion sources instead. For target-aligned executions, use Motion Warping windows with named targets and validation.

#20.6 Combo and chain grammar

Combos should be authored per weapon family/type rather than as global step multipliers.

PDA_ComboChainProfile
  ChainTag
  WeaponTypeTags
  Steps[]
  ResetWindowSeconds
  InputBufferWindowSeconds
  BranchRules
  RecoveryRules
  UIChainPolicy

FAscentiaComboStep
  StepIndex
  ActionTag
  MontageSection
  DamageMultiplier
  PoiseMultiplier
  MotionProfile
  DamageWindowName
  CancelWindowName
  NextInputWindows

Combo state:

FAscentiaComboRuntimeState
  ChainTag
  CurrentStepIndex
  LastAcceptedInputTag
  LastActionTag
  ResetDeadline
  BufferedNextInput
  bCanBranch
  bWasInterrupted

FAscentiaComboBranchRule
  FromStep
  InputTag
  RequiredStateTags
  ForbiddenStateTags
  NextActionTag
  BufferWindowName
  Priority

Rules:

  • combo progression must be driven by accepted inputs, not by montage completion

alone;

  • standing combo, movement attack, rolling attack, guard counter, and jump attack

must remain separate action states unless a chain explicitly branches between them;

  • Designers should be able to preview a chain as a timeline: input, startup,

active, recovery, cancel, cue, and next accepted input;

  • action buffering must preserve player intent without making the character feel

autonomous.

  • chain rules should support failed-input telemetry. "Input ignored because

recovery window was not cancelable" is useful; silence is not.

#20.7 Two-handing, dual wield, power stance, and shields

Pairing is content, not a pile of if statements.

PDA_PairedWeaponProfile
  MainHandFamilies
  OffHandFamilies
  RequiredTypeTags
  ForbiddenTypeTags
  Mode: ShieldPair, DualWield, PowerStance
  PairedMovesetTags
  MontageSet
  DamageMultiplier
  PoiseMultiplier
  GuardProfile
  EquipLoadAdjustment
  UIIndicator
  ConflictMessage

Pairing result:

FAscentiaWeaponPairingQuery
  MainHandSnapshot
  OffHandSnapshot
  bTwoHandRequested
  CharacterStats
  RequiredTags
  ForbiddenTags

FAscentiaWeaponPairingResult
  Mode
  PairedProfile
  bAccepted
  bSuppressedByTwoHand
  bOffHandVisualVisible
  DamageMultiplier
  PoiseMultiplier
  GuardProfile
  RejectionReasonTextKey
  UIIndicatorTag

Rules:

  • two-handing should suppress off-hand behavior through a visible state and UI

prompt, not silent side effects;

  • shield pairing should preserve guard/block/counter identity;
  • dual wield should emphasize speed, pressure, or alternating-hit identity;
  • power stance should require meaningful weapon similarity or profile match and

reward commitment with distinct paired actions;

  • every pairing rule needs a Designer-facing reason string for rejection or

suppression.

  • weapon pair validation should run both ways. A sword plus dagger and dagger

plus sword may intentionally produce different output, but if not, the rule should be symmetric and validated as such.

#20.8 Weapon skills and per-weapon abilities

Weapon skills are not merely "special attack." They are a per-weapon identity slot.

PDA_WeaponSkill
  SkillTag
  DisplayName
  RequiredWeaponTypes
  AbilityClass
  CostProfile
  CooldownProfile
  DamageProfile
  MontageSet
  TargetingProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  CueProfile
  CameraProfile
  UIIcon
  TooltipText

Skill assignment:

FAscentiaWeaponSkillSlot
  SourceItemInstance
  DefaultSkill
  OverrideSkill
  ImprintedSkill
  bLocked
  UnlockCondition

FAscentiaWeaponSkillView
  SkillTag
  DisplayName
  Cost
  Cooldown
  InputGlyph
  RequiredWeaponText
  bUsableNow
  RejectReasonText

Policy:

  • item definition chooses the default weapon skill;
  • equipment, upgrades, or special items may override or augment it later;
  • Skills tab displays equipped weapon skill, cost, cooldown, input, and preview;
  • native ability owns authority, cost, cooldown, and damage application;
  • Blueprint owns montage/cue/camera/audio presentation;
  • no weapon skill is production-ready without a proof scenario and cue coverage.
  • weapon skill replacement is an itemization decision. If Ascentia later supports

imprinting, extraction, or skill gems, the mutation must use the P16 item transaction path and write provenance.

#20.9 Ranged, ammo, target, and projectile policy

Ranged actions need a separate delivery contract.

PDA_RangedWeaponProfile
  WeaponTypeTag
  DrawAction
  AimAction
  ReleaseAction
  RequiredAmmoFamilies
  DefaultAmmoProfile
  ReticleProfile
  LockOnPolicy
  CameraProfile
  ProjectileOrTracePolicy

PDA_AmmoProfile
  AmmoItemTag
  AmmoFamilyTag
  DamageProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  StackConsumption
  TrailCue
  ImpactCue

Projectile/trace:

PDA_ProjectileProfile
  DeliveryType: Projectile, HitscanTrace, ArcTrace, Area
  ProjectileClass
  Speed
  GravityScale
  Range
  Radius
  CollisionChannel
  TargetingPreset
  HomingPolicy
  ClientPredictionPolicy
  ImpactDamageProfile
  ImpactCueProfile

Ammo and delivery transaction:

FAscentiaAmmoSnapshot
  AmmoItemTag
  AmmoInstanceId
  AmmoFamilyTag
  StackCount
  DamageProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  bValidForWeapon

FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryRequest
  SourceActor
  WeaponSnapshot
  AmmoSnapshot
  ActionTag
  AimOrigin
  AimDirection
  TargetingResult
  ProjectileProfile
  ClientRequestTime

FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  ConsumedAmmo
  SpawnedProjectile
  TraceHitResults[]
  AppliedDamageEvents[]
  CueRequests[]
  RemainingAmmo

Rules:

  • ammo consumption is server-authoritative and transactional;
  • missing bow, missing ammo, invalid ammo, and blocked fire states must be

rejected without consuming inventory;

  • reticle and lock-on behavior belong in data/settings;
  • projectile visuals must be readable at combat camera distance;
  • the first production bow should support draw, hold, release, cancel, and

no-ammo feedback before more bow content is authored.

The Gameplay Targeting System should be considered for the next step because it already frames targeting as reusable data assets composed of selection, filter, and sort tasks. Even if Ascentia begins with simple traces/projectile actors, the data shape should not paint us into a corner.

Targeting evidence:

FAscentiaTargetingProof
  ActionTag
  TargetingProfile
  SourceLocation
  AimVector
  CandidateTargets[]
  FilteredTargets[]
  ChosenTarget
  RejectionReasons[]
  DebugScreenshotPath

#20.10 Throwables and quick-item attacks

Throwables live between inventory and combat.

PDA_ThrowableProfile
  ItemTag
  SkillTag
  RequiredSlot
  ConsumeCount
  ThrowAction
  ProjectileProfile
  DamageProfile
  AimAssistPolicy
  TrajectoryPreviewPolicy
  QuickItemHUDPolicy
  OnEmptyPrompt

Throwable transaction:

FAscentiaThrowableUseRequest
  QuickSlot
  ItemInstance
  ThrowableProfile
  AimContext
  bFromBufferedInput

FAscentiaThrowableUseResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  ConsumedCount
  RemainingCount
  ProjectileDeliveryResult
  CooldownApplied
  HUDUpdate

Rules:

  • quick-item stack consumption must be transactional;
  • throw animation and projectile release notify must agree;
  • arc preview is optional, but if enabled it must use the same projectile

parameters as the real throw;

  • lock-on and free-aim throws should be separate policies;
  • quick-item HUD should show count, cooldown/lockout if any, and no-item state.

#20.11 Weapon mounts, sockets, trails, and visuals

P14 must expose physical weapon fit to Designers. This is not a minor polish task; wrong sockets break readability, trails, hitboxes, and character identity.

PDA_WeaponSocketProfile
  HandSocket
  OffHandSocket
  SheatheSocket
  TrailStartSocket
  TrailEndSocket
  GripOffset
  GripRotationOffset
  SheatheOffset
  CollisionProfile
  PreviewCharacter
  PreviewMontages

Fit proof:

FAscentiaWeaponMountReport
  WeaponItem
  WeaponMesh
  SocketProfile
  PreviewCharacter
  PreviewBodyPreset
  PreviewMontages[]
  HandFitFrames[]
  SheatheFitFrames[]
  TrailSocketFrames[]
  CollisionFindings[]
  ClippingFindings[]
  DesignerVerdict

FAscentiaWeaponHitboxReplay
  ActionTag
  Montage
  DamageWindow
  TraceSockets
  TraceSamples[]
  HitTargets[]
  MissedExpectedTargets[]
  DebugOverlayPath

Tools:

  • EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab previews hand fit, sheathe fit, trail sockets, and

montage frames;

  • EUW_AscentiaHitboxReplay overlays actual trace/sweep path against weapon

sockets and target capsules;

  • EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor shows trail/audio/damage/cancel windows on the

same timeline;

  • EUW_AscentiaMotionInspector compares root-motion path, capsule path, and

intended direction.

Validation:

  • every visible weapon has hand and sheathe socket profile;
  • trail profile has valid start/end sockets;
  • damage windows use sockets that exist on the equipped mesh;
  • two-hand/off-hand sockets do not collide with body mesh in preview poses;
  • generated Asset Factory meshes are audited before entering weapon rows.
  • weapon mount reports should be stored as evidence before a generated or

marketplace weapon leaves staging.

#20.12 Designer surfaces

PDA_WeaponType_*

Editor Utility Widgets:

EUW_AscentiaWeaponAuthoring

EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor

EUW_AscentiaProjectileDeliveryReport

Runtime widgets:

WBP_AscentiaWeaponFamilyIndicator
WBP_AscentiaComboStepIndicator
WBP_AscentiaWeaponSkillSlot
WBP_AscentiaAmmoCounter
WBP_AscentiaReticle
WBP_AscentiaQuickItemHUD

Blueprint surfaces:

  • BP_AscentiaWeaponVisual;
  • BP_AscentiaProjectileBase;
  • BP_AscentiaThrowableProjectile;
  • BP_AscentiaWeaponTrailCue;
  • BP_AscentiaReticleController;
  • BP_AscentiaWeaponSkillPresentation.

#20.13 P14 evidence, matrices, and reports

P14 should be proven through matrices, not by trying one weapon in PIE.

Reports:

FAscentiaWeaponActionMapReport
  ReportId
  WeaponTypes[]
  MissingActions[]
  FallbackActions[]
  MontageCoverage
  DamageProfileCoverage
  CueCoverage
  InputCoverage
  ValidationErrors[]

FAscentiaMovesetMatrixReport
  ReportId
  WeaponFamilies[]
  ActionRows[]
  ComboRows[]
  MovementAttackRows[]
  PairingRows[]
  SkillRows[]
  DesignerNotes[]

FAscentiaNotifyAuditReport
  ReportId
  MontageContracts[]
  MissingRequiredNotifies[]
  ExtraUnknownNotifies[]
  WindowTimingWarnings[]
  DedicatedServerPolicyWarnings[]

FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryReport
  ReportId
  RangedProfiles[]
  ThrowableProfiles[]
  AmmoProfiles[]
  TargetingProofs[]
  ProjectileResults[]
  InventoryTransactionResults[]
  ReadabilityFindings[]

FAscentiaWeaponMountReportSet
  ReportId
  WeaponMountReports[]
  HitboxReplays[]
  TrailFindings[]
  SocketWarnings[]
