Evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/WeaponActionMaps/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/NotifyAudit/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/ProjectileDelivery/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/WeaponMounts/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/MovesetMatrix/{RunId}/report.json

Required proof scenarios:

Tools/remote_playtest.py weapon_action_map
Tools/remote_playtest.py weapon_notify_audit
Tools/remote_playtest.py weapon_mount_profile
Tools/remote_playtest.py projectile_delivery
Tools/remote_playtest.py ranged_ammo
Tools/remote_playtest.py throwable_item
Tools/remote_playtest.py paired_weapon_rules
Tools/remote_playtest.py combo_chain
Tools/remote_playtest.py movement_attack
Tools/remote_playtest.py weapon_skill

Acceptance shape:

  • every shippable weapon type has an action map, montage set, socket profile,

damage profile, cue profile, and report row;

  • every shippable montage referenced by an action has a notify manifest;
  • every ranged/throwable profile has a transaction proof and a targeting proof;
  • every generated weapon mesh has a mount/fit report;
  • every fallback action is visible in the matrix and blocks phase completion

unless the fallback is explicitly phase-approved;

  • every Designer verdict is preserved next to machine output so feel decisions

do not vanish into logs.

#20.14 Interconnects

P14 touches:

  • P10/P11 combat motion: action requests, root motion, cancel windows, dodge

cancel, parry/riposte/guard counter, and hitbox proof;

  • P13 damage/build math: damage profiles, scaling, poise, crit/backstab, status,

equip load, and stat requirements;

  • P15 magic: staff/focus weapons, target profiles, projectile profiles, casts,

spell slots, and Eth cost UI;

  • P16 itemization: weapon item instances, affixes, unique powers, upgrade tracks,

socket profiles, and generated names/tooltips;

  • P17 world: placed weapon rewards, biome/region ammo, traversal tools, and

ranged encounter spaces;

  • P18 AI: enemy weapons, ranged/caster moves, shield behavior, elite affixes, and

boss weapon grammar;

  • P19 save/death: equipped weapons, ammo stacks, quick items, weapon skills,

unlocked movesets, and respec/equipment constraints;

  • P20 presentation: trails, impact cues, swing audio, reticles, camera, rumble,

damage numbers, and accessibility variants;

  • P21 settings: input mode, reticle visibility, ammo HUD, quick-item HUD,

trajectory preview, aim assist, feedback intensity, and input buffering readability;

  • P22 multiplayer: ammo consumption, projectile authority, target prediction,

replicated montages/root motion, and anti-cheat-sensitive hit validation.

#20.15 P14 proof gates

P14 is not complete until:

  1. Weapon type resolution is data-backed and no shippable weapon relies only on

hard-coded fallback item-tag branches.

  1. Every weapon family has a default action map, montage set, damage profile,

cue profile, socket profile, and validation report.

  1. Combo chains are authored per weapon type/family and visible in an editor

timeline.

  1. Movement attacks, jump attacks, guard counters, and weapon skills have

distinct montages, not only multipliers on the light-attack path.

  1. Damage, cancel, iframe, trail, swing-audio, projectile-release, and cue notify

requirements are machine-validated.

  1. Two-hand, shield-pair, dual-wield, and power-stance rules are data-authored

with Designer-readable rejection/suppression messages.

  1. At least one weapon skill is item-assigned, appears in UI, consumes resources,

respects cooldown, plays presentation, and emits damage through the P13 damage pipeline.

  1. Bow/ranged proof includes draw, aim, release, cancel, ammo consumption,

no-ammo rejection, reticle, projectile/trace policy, trail, impact cue, and ammo HUD.

  1. Throwable proof includes stack consumption, projectile/arc policy, release

notify, quick-item HUD, empty rejection, and impact cue.

  1. Weapon mount proof shows hand socket, off-hand socket, sheathe socket, trail

sockets, and hitbox overlay for at least sword, dagger, greatsword, bow, and throwing knife.

  1. Remote proof covers:
  • combo_chain
  • movement_attack
  • guard_counter
  • jump_attack
  • weapon_skill
  • weapon_moveset
  • dual_wield
  • ranged_ammo
  • throwable_item
  • weapon_notify_audit
  • paired_weapon_rules
  • weapon_action_map
  • projectile_delivery
  • weapon_mount_profile
  1. Dashboard evidence links the latest moveset matrix, notify audit, projectile

delivery report, and weapon mount report.

#20.16 P14 starter implementation package

The first P14 package should convert the current weapon proof into a weapon authoring pipeline. The immediate goal is not dozens of weapon families. It is one trustworthy grammar where a Designer can see what an equipped weapon is, which action it will play, which montage/section/slot it uses, which windows fire, which sockets drive fit/trails/hitboxes, and which report proves it.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Weapons/AscentiaWeaponTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Weapons/AscentiaWeaponResolverSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Weapons/AscentiaWeaponActionTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Weapons/AscentiaWeaponActionMap.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Weapons/AscentiaWeaponType.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Weapons/AscentiaWeaponSkillTypes.h

Source/Ascentia/Public/Ranged/AscentiaRangedTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Ranged/AscentiaProjectileTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Items/AscentiaThrowableTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Weapons/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Animation/...

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Weapons/AscentiaWeaponActionValidation.h

Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaWeaponMountAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaWeaponResolverTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaPairedWeaponRulesTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaProjectileDeliveryTest.cpp

Subsystem boundaries:

UAscentiaWeaponResolverSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  ResolveWeaponSnapshot(Inventory, Slot, Context)
  ResolveOffHandSnapshot(Inventory, MainHand, OffHandSlot)
  ResolveActionDef(WeaponSnapshot, ActionTag, ContextTags)
  ResolveWeaponSkill(WeaponSnapshot, ItemInstance)
  ResolveAmmoSnapshot(Inventory, RangedProfile)
  ResolveThrowableSnapshot(Inventory, QuickSlot)
  WriteWeaponResolutionReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaCombatComponent
  consumes resolved weapon snapshots and action defs
  no longer owns weapon family/type fallback as invisible private truth

UAscentiaAvatarComponent
  consumes socket profile, trail profile, and notify windows for presentation
  does not decide gameplay legality

UAscentiaProjectileDeliverySubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  validates ammo/throwable requests
  spawns projectile or executes trace policy
  writes projectile delivery evidence

Starter content folders:

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Weapons/Types/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Weapons/ActionMaps/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Weapons/Combos/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Weapons/Pairing/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Weapons/Skills/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Weapons/Sockets/

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Throwables/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Targeting/
/Game/Ascentia/Animation/WeaponContracts/

Starter assets:

PDA_WeaponType_Sword_OneHand_MVP
PDA_WeaponType_Greatsword_Heavy_MVP

Weapon action request signatures:

FAscentiaWeaponActionResult TryStartWeaponAction(
  const FAscentiaWeaponActionRequest& Request);

FAscentiaWeaponActionDef ResolveWeaponAction(
  const FAscentiaWeaponSnapshot& Weapon,
  const FAscentiaOffHandSnapshot& OffHand,
  FGameplayTag ActionTag,
  const FGameplayTagContainer& ContextTags);

FAscentiaAnimNotifyWindowManifest BuildNotifyManifest(
  const UAnimMontage* Montage,
  FName SectionName);

FAscentiaWeaponMountReport PreviewWeaponMount(
  const UAscentiaItemDefinition* Item,
  const UAscentiaWeaponSocketProfile* SocketProfile,
  const USkeletalMesh* PreviewCharacter);

Action tags should be stable and shared by weapons, abilities, UI, and reports:

Weapon.Action.Light
Weapon.Action.Heavy
Weapon.Action.Charged
Weapon.Action.Running
Weapon.Action.Rolling
Weapon.Action.Backstep
Weapon.Action.Jump
Weapon.Action.GuardCounter
Weapon.Action.Backstab
Weapon.Action.Riposte
Weapon.Action.Skill
Weapon.Action.Ranged.Draw
Weapon.Action.Ranged.Release
Weapon.Action.Throw

Notify authority policy:

Damage windows:
  authoritative or server-reconstructed; never cosmetic only

Projectile release:
  authoritative request plus client presentation; no inventory consumption from
  cosmetic notify alone

Trail/audio/camera/rumble:
  cosmetic, but validated for coverage against action contract

Cancel/iframe windows:
  gameplay relevant; exported to manifest and checked by runtime policy

Starter proof matrix:

Sword_OneHand_LightCombo3
Greatsword_Heavy_LightCombo3
SpearShield_LightAttack_ShieldPair
SwordShield_GuardCounter
Bow_DrawHoldRelease_BoneArrow
Bow_NoAmmo_Rejected
ThrowingKnife_QuickItem_Consumed
ThrowingKnife_Empty_Rejected
WeaponSkill_AshOfRuin_CostCooldownDamage
WeaponSwap_Greatsword_To_SwordShield_Attack
NotifyAudit_LightAttack_WhooshTrailDamageCancel
WeaponMount_AssassinGirl_Sword
WeaponMount_AssassinGirl_Greatsword
HitboxReplay_Greatsword_Light1
ProjectileDelivery_BoneArrow_TargetedAndFreeAim

Editor surfaces:

EUW_AscentiaWeaponAuthoring
  create/edit weapon type, action map, montage set, damage profile references

EUW_AscentiaMovesetMatrix
  show all weapon types against required action tags and fallback state

EUW_AscentiaNotifyWindowDoctor
  inspect montage sections, slot names, damage/cancel/iframe/trail/audio windows

EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab
  preview hand socket, off-hand socket, sheathe socket, trail sockets, offsets,
  generated mesh fit, and clipping frames

EUW_AscentiaHitboxReplay
  replay damage windows and trace sockets against target capsules

EUW_AscentiaProjectileRangeLab
  compare projectile speed, gravity, range, arc, homing, reticle, and impact cue

EUW_AscentiaPairingRulePreview
  test main-hand/off-hand/two-hand states and show rejection/suppression text

EUW_AscentiaWeaponActionValidationReport
  run action map, montage, notify, damage, cue, cost, and input validation

Commandlet contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaWeaponActionAudit
  -WeaponRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Weapons
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaNotifyWindowAudit
  -ContractRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Animation/WeaponContracts
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaWeaponMountAudit
  -SocketRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Weapons/Sockets
  -PreviewCharacter=/Game/Characters/AssassinGirl/...
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14

Tools/run_p14_weapon_matrix.ps1
  -Scenario WeaponActionMap
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/WeaponActionMaps/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/MovesetMatrix/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/NotifyAudit/{RunId}/report.json

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P14/PairingRules/{RunId}/report.json

Implementation tickets:

P14-001 Weapon resolver extraction
  Move hard-coded weapon family/type resolution behind
  `UAscentiaWeaponResolverSubsystem`, preserving current fallback results but
  reporting each fallback.

P14-002 Weapon type and action map assets
  Add starter `PDA_WeaponType_*` and `PDA_WeaponActionMap_*` assets for sword,
  greatsword, dagger, shield, bow, and throwing knife.

P14-003 Weapon action request/result path
  Introduce `FAscentiaWeaponActionRequest`, `FAscentiaWeaponActionResult`, and
  `FAscentiaActiveWeaponActionState` so light, movement, guard counter, jump,
  skill, ranged, and throwable actions share one accepted/rejected path.

P14-004 Montage contract and notify manifest
  Generate `FAscentiaAnimNotifyWindowManifest` and
  `FAscentiaMontageActionContract` for current attack, roll attack, bow, and
  throwable montages.

P14-005 Weapon mount lab first slice
  Add `PDA_WeaponSocketProfile_*` assets and `EUW_AscentiaWeaponMountLab`
  preview for AssassinGirl sword, greatsword, shield, bow, and throwing knife.

P14-006 Hitbox replay and trail proof
  Emit `FAscentiaWeaponHitboxReplay` and validate trace sockets, trail sockets,
  damage windows, swing whoosh, and Niagara trail windows together.

P14-007 Paired weapon profile path
  Replace implicit shield/dual/power-stance behavior with
  `PDA_PairedWeaponProfile_*`, pairing reports, and Designer-readable rejection
  text.

P14-008 Ranged/ammo projectile delivery
  Add `FAscentiaAmmoSnapshot`, `FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryRequest`,
  `FAscentiaProjectileDeliveryResult`, `PDA_RangedWeaponProfile_*`,
  `PDA_AmmoProfile_*`, and `PDA_ProjectileProfile_*`.

P14-009 Throwable profile path
  Add `PDA_ThrowableProfile_*`, `FAscentiaThrowableUseResult`, release notify
  validation, quick-item HUD evidence, and empty-slot rejection proof.

P14-010 P14 packaged proof suite
  Run action map, notify audit, weapon mount, hitbox replay, projectile delivery,
  paired weapon, weapon-swap, ranged ammo, throwable, and weapon skill proofs in
  PIE and packaged Development.

Validation gates:

  • any shippable weapon type without an action map fails validation;
  • any action map entry without montage, damage profile, cost policy, cue policy,

and notify contract fails validation;

  • any montage referenced by gameplay without required notify windows fails

validation;

  • any weapon with trail profile but missing valid start/end sockets fails

validation;

  • any ranged/throwable profile without server-authoritative inventory

transaction proof fails validation;

  • any generated or marketplace weapon without a mount report remains in staging;
  • any fallback action must be shown in the matrix and explicitly phase-approved.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Extract the resolver while preserving current weapon behavior.
  2. Add assets that reproduce current sword, greatsword, shield, bow, and

throwing-knife results.

  1. Add notify and socket reports for the attacks already being tested in PIE.
  2. Add weapon-swap and shield-pair proof before expanding movesets.
  3. Add projectile/throwable delivery reports before authoring more ranged

weapons.

  1. Only then grow combo chains, weapon skills, power stance, and exotic weapon

families.

#21. Magic, Eth, Spellbook, Flasks, Summons, and Spell Delivery

#21.1 Player-facing goal

P15 is where magic becomes a second ability economy instead of a debug button that spends blue resource.

The player-facing promise:

  • Eth supports a build identity distinct from Breath: spells, rituals, weapon

imbuements, buffs, debuffs, summons, and utility;

  • spell decisions are visible in the Spellbook, HUD, reticle, cast animation,

projectile or target behavior, impact feedback, status buildup, and recovery;

  • the player can understand why a spell cannot fire: insufficient Eth, cooldown,

missing focus, invalid target, blocked state, no flask charge, or silenced status;

  • Designers can author a spell as data plus Blueprint presentation without

editing native combat code;

  • every spell has proof: input accepted, Eth spent, cooldown applied, montage

played, cast/release notify fired, targeting resolved, delivery applied damage or status, cues appeared, UI updated, and save/load preserved the spellbook.

The production target is not simply "sorceries exist." It is a coherent magic pipeline that shares the same authority, targeting, damage, presentation, settings, validation, save, and future network rules as weapons.

#21.2 Current Ascentia state

P15 already has three real native slices:

  • UAscentiaGameplayAbility supports both Breath and Eth costs through

CheckCost, CommitAbility, and SetByCaller Gameplay Effects;

  • UAscentiaEthCostEffect subtracts Eth and emits

GameplayCue.Ascentia.SpellCast;

  • UAscentiaAbility_SpellCast is a native GAS ability with a 16 Eth fallback

cost, Ascentia.Cooldown.Spell, and Ascentia.Ability.SpellCast;

  • UAscentiaSpellbookComponent replicates known spell tags, equipped spell tag,

learn/equip counts, rejection telemetry, and Blueprint-readable spell views;

  • UAscentiaSpellDefinition : UPrimaryDataAsset registers the AscentiaSpell

primary asset type and exposes skill definition, description, damage, poise, and cue identity;

  • a native Ether Spark fallback keeps the game playable when no authored spell

assets exist;

  • UAscentiaCombatComponent::ExecuteSpellCast records spell tag, damage, poise,

range, Eth cost, server time, action count, and fires OnSpellCastStarted;

  • UAscentiaAbility_UseEthPotion restores Eth through GAS, consumes replicated

EthPotionCharges, rejects full-Eth/no-charge use, and emits OnEthPotionUsed;

  • Eth and Eth potion charges refill through campsites, save restore, and respawn

hooks;

  • HUD/debug readouts expose Eth and Eth potion counts;
  • remote proofs already exist for spell_eth, spellbook_surface,

eth_potion, and baseline regressions.

The production gaps:

  • spell casting currently records telemetry and presentation events, but does

not yet own a cast montage lifecycle, cast window, release notify, projectile, trace, targeting preset, damage application, status application, or impact cue;

  • Spellbook UI is still a placeholder tab rather than a learn/equip/loadout

screen;

  • StartingSpellAssets is a useful Blueprint surface, but there is not yet a

full asset registry, authoring widget, validation report, or content folder convention for production spell catalogs;

  • Eth recovery policy is not designed beyond flask refill/restoration and max

Eth scaling;

  • Eth flask charge allocation/upgrades are not authored;
  • spell cooldown/resource HUD and low-resource prompts are not production UI;
  • buff/debuff spells, summons, utility spells, and spell status payloads are not

implemented;

  • input binding, reticle, cast montage, VFX, audio, camera, and settings are

still pending.

#21.3 Resource model

Eth should not be "Breath with another color." Breath governs physical tempo: attack, block, dodge, sprint, and immediate body commitment. Eth governs arcane tempo: spell access, ranged pressure, buffs, debuffs, summons, and build investment.

Current formula:

MaxEth = 20 + 4.0 * FOC

Resource policy:

FAscentiaEthResourceProfile
  MaxEthFormula
  RegenPolicy: None, Delayed, SlowAlwaysOn, EncounterOnly
  RegenDelaySeconds
  RegenPerSecondCurve
  LowEthThreshold
  LowEthPromptPolicy
  SpellCostScaleByStat
  CooldownScaleByStat
  FlaskRestoreProfile

Runtime resource state:

FAscentiaEthRegenState
  CurrentEth
  MaxEth
  RegenPolicy
  RegenDelayRemaining
  RegenPerSecond
  LastEthSpendTime
  LastDamageTakenTime
  bInCombat
  bRegenSuppressed

FAscentiaSpellCostRequest
  SpellTag
  AbilitySpec
  BaseEthCost
  SourceStats
  CostScaleTags
  CooldownProfile
  bPreviewOnly

FAscentiaSpellCostResult
  FinalEthCost
  CooldownSeconds
  bCanPay
  RejectReason
  CostGameplayEffect
  CooldownGameplayEffect
  SetByCallerMagnitudes

Decisions:

  • if Eth regenerates, it must not erase flask pressure or make low-cost spells

trivial spam;

  • if Eth does not regenerate, the game needs strong flask allocation, camp, item,

and world-restoration language;

  • delayed slow regen can support exploration magic while keeping boss pressure

meaningful;

  • high-cost spells should remain emotionally legible: the player should feel the

cast, cost, risk, and payoff.

Ascentia should keep Health, Breath, and Eth as first-class AttributeSet values so GAS can own costs, cooldowns, buffs, debuffs, UI queries, and future replication discipline. Any Designer-facing cost curve should resolve into the native attribute path, not bypass it.

#21.4 Spell identity model

Spell identity should resolve in layers:

Spellbook equipped spell
  -> spell definition primary asset
  -> school profile
  -> resource profile
  -> cast profile
  -> targeting profile
  -> delivery profile
  -> damage/status profile
  -> cue/audio/VFX/camera profile
  -> UI icon and tooltip

Core asset:

PDA_Spell
  SpellTag
  DisplayName
  Description
  SchoolTag
  AbilityClass
  EthCost
  CooldownSeconds
  RequiredFocus
  RequiredItemTags
  CastProfile
  TargetingProfile
  DeliveryProfile
  DamageProfile
  StatusPayloads
  BuffDebuffProfile
  SummonDefinition
  MagicCueProfile
  UIIcon
  TooltipText

Spell resolution:

UAscentiaSpellResolverSubsystem
  ResolveSpellDefinition(SpellTag)
  ResolveEquippedSpell(Spellbook, Slot)
  ResolveSpellCastProfile(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  ResolveSpellDeliveryProfile(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  ResolveSpellCost(SpellDefinition, SourceAttributes)
  ResolveSpellView(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)

FAscentiaResolvedSpell
  SpellTag
  PrimaryAssetId
  SourceAsset
  SchoolProfile
  CastProfile
  DeliveryProfile
  TargetingProfile
  DamageProfile
  StatusPayloads[]
  BuffDebuffProfile
  SummonDefinition
  CostResult
  CooldownResult
  CueProfile
  FallbacksUsed[]
  ValidationWarnings[]

FAscentiaSpellView
  SpellTag
  DisplayName
  SchoolName
  Icon
  EthCost
  CooldownSeconds
  Range
  DamagePreview
  StatusPreview
  RequirementText
  RejectReasonText
  TooltipRows[]
  bFallback

The existing UAscentiaSpellDefinition is the right seed. Its next expansion should add explicit cast, targeting, delivery, status, school, UI, and asset bundle references. Since it is already a UPrimaryDataAsset, it can use Unreal's Asset Manager path for discovery, audit, chunking, and soft-reference loading.

Spell schools should be data, not enum-only:

PDA_SpellSchool
  SchoolTag
  DisplayName
  DamageSchool
  ScalingStats
  StatusFamilies
  ResistanceProfile
  CueFamily
  UITheme
  LoreText

Example school families:

  • Ember: fire, burn, area denial, panic pressure;
  • Frost: slow, brittle, stamina/Breath pressure, spacing control;
  • Storm: lightning, stagger, fast projectile pressure;
  • Venom: poison, damage over time, attrition;
  • Grave: rot, curse, death-loop pressure, summon synergy;
  • Ether: neutral arcane, projectile fundamentals, utility;
  • Radiance: healing, barrier, conviction-style buffs;
  • Thorn: nature, bleed, rooting, trap-like terrain control.

The school list is a design call, but the architecture should allow schools to map into the P13 damage/status pipeline rather than creating a separate magic damage universe.

#21.5 Spellbook lifecycle

The Spellbook should own four concepts:

Known spells
Equipped spells
Loadout slots
Last selection/rejection telemetry

Current native state:

KnownSpellTags
EquippedSpellTag
SpellLearnCount
SpellEquipCount
bLastSpellbookEquipRejected
LastSpellbookSpellTag
LastSpellbookSpellName
LastSpellbookEthCost
LastSpellbookCooldownSeconds
LastSpellbookRange

Next production state:

FAscentiaSpellbookSaveData
  KnownSpellTags
  LoadoutSlots[]
  EquippedSlotIndex
  FavoriteSpellTags
  RecentlyLearnedSpellTags
  SpellUpgradeState

FAscentiaSpellLoadoutSlot
  SlotTag
  SpellTag
  InputTag
  bLocked
  UnlockSourceTag

Spellbook view model:

FAscentiaSpellbookView
  KnownSpells[]
  LoadoutSlots[]
  EquippedSlotIndex
  SelectedSpell
  FilteredSchoolTags
  NewSpellTags
  FallbackSpellWarnings[]
  SaveStateSummary

FAscentiaSpellbookEquipRequest
  SpellTag
  TargetSlot
  SourceActor
  bPreviewOnly

FAscentiaSpellbookEquipResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReasonTextKey
  PreviousSpell
  NewSpell
  UpdatedView

Rules:

  • learn/equip must be server-authoritative and save-persistent;
  • UI should read a Spellbook view model rather than reconstructing spell rules;
  • invalid equip should give a Designer-authored reason string;
  • fallback Ether Spark should be visibly marked as fallback in editor tools and

validation reports;

  • future character creation/origin systems can grant starting spells by adding

spell tags or spell assets, not by editing player class defaults.

Spellbook UI should support:

  • list/grid of known spells;
  • school filter;
  • equipped slot strip;
  • cost/cooldown/range/damage/status preview;
  • required focus/item messaging;
  • hotbar or single-slot policy depending on design decision;
  • unsaved/new spell callouts;
  • keyboard/gamepad navigation;
  • settings-driven detail density.

#21.6 Casting pipeline

The production casting pipeline:

Input action
  -> ability activation by input tag
  -> CanActivateAbility: Eth, cooldown, state, silence, equipment, target policy
  -> CommitAbility: Eth cost and cooldown
  -> cast montage / cast stance
  -> cast window notify
  -> targeting request or aim snapshot
  -> release notify
  -> delivery actor, trace, area, buff, or summon
  -> damage/status/buff/debuff GameplayEffect application
  -> GameplayCue, Niagara, MetaSound, camera, HUD
  -> recovery/cancel/end ability

The current native ActivateAbility path commits cost, plays a generic action animation, calls ExecuteSpellCast, and ends immediately. That is correct for a small proof slice, but production spells need asynchronous ability execution: cast tasks, montage wait tasks, release delegates, cancel handling, and cleanup. Epic's GAS documentation explicitly frames Gameplay Abilities as multi-stage asynchronous flows with costs, cooldowns, input, animation, replication policy, and Ability Tasks. Ascentia should lean into that rather than building a parallel spell executor outside GAS.

Cast profile:

PDA_SpellCastProfile
  CastType: Instant, TimedCast, Charged, Channeled, HeldAim, Ritual
  Montage
  SlotName
  StartSection
  LoopSection
  ReleaseSection
  CancelSection
  RootMotionPolicy
  MovementLockPolicy
  RotationPolicy
  CancelWindows
  InputBufferPolicy
  ReleaseNotifyName
  CastCue
  LoopCue
  ReleaseCue

Cast runtime contract:

FAscentiaSpellCastRequest
  SourceActor
  InputTag
  SpellTag
  LoadoutSlot
  ResolvedSpell
  AimContext
  TargetingContext
  bFromBufferedInput
  ClientRequestTime

FAscentiaSpellCastResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  ResolvedSpell
  CostResult
  AbilitySpecHandle
  Montage
  StartSection
  OpenedCastWindows[]
  TraceId

FAscentiaActiveSpellCastState
  SpellTag
  CastType
  StartTime
  CurrentPhase: Startup, Holding, Channeling, Release, Recovery, Cancelled, Complete
  Montage
  Section
  PendingTargetingRequest
  PendingDeliveryRequest
  EthCommitted
  CooldownCommitted
  bCanCancel
  bCanRelease
  bInterrupted

FAscentiaSpellCastWindowManifest
  Montage
  SlotName
  CastWindow
  ChargeWindow
  ChannelWindow
  ReleaseNotify
  CancelWindows[]
  CueMarkers[]
  MotionLockWindows[]

Rules:

  • the release of a projectile/trace/area should come from an animation notify or

explicit instant policy, not arbitrary code timing;

  • a failed target check should not spend Eth unless the spell is intentionally

self-committing before target validation;

  • cancel policy must be readable: can the player dodge out, block out, swap

spells, or get interrupted;

  • channeled spells need start, tick, cost-over-time, interrupt, and end rules;
  • movement-locked spells must use the same combat motion/ledge/stair policy as

attacks and dodges.

  • ability tasks should own asynchronous wait points: montage blend-out,

gameplay-event release, target data arrival, channel tick, cancel, and cleanup. Native code should not fake an asynchronous spell by firing all effects in ActivateAbility unless the cast profile is explicitly Instant.

#21.7 Targeting and delivery types

Spell delivery should be data-authored by profile:

PDA_SpellDeliveryProfile
  DeliveryType
  TargetingProfile
  ProjectileProfile
  TraceProfile
  AreaProfile
  BuffDebuffProfile
  SummonDefinition
  ImpactDamageProfile
  ImpactStatusPayloads
  GameplayCueProfile
  bRequiresValidTargetBeforeCommit

Delivery transaction:

FAscentiaSpellTargetingRequest
  SourceActor
  SpellTag
  TargetingProfile
  AimOrigin
  AimDirection
  LockOnTarget
  GroundPoint
  ClientRequestTime

FAscentiaSpellTargetingResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  CandidateTargets[]
  ChosenTargets[]
  ChosenLocation
  ChosenNormal
  TargetDataHandle
  DebugTrace[]

FAscentiaSpellDeliveryRequest
  SourceActor
  ResolvedSpell
  TargetingResult
  DeliveryProfile
  ReleaseTransform
  ChargeAlpha
  ChannelTickIndex
  TraceId

FAscentiaSpellDeliveryResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  SpawnedActors[]
  TraceHits[]
  AppliedDamageEvents[]
  AppliedStatusResults[]
  AppliedGameplayEffects[]
  SummonResults[]
  CueRequests[]

Delivery types:

  • Self: buffs, heals, cleanses, barriers, stance changes;
  • Touch/melee imbue: weapon-coated hits, close-range palms, spellblade bursts;
  • Projectile: slow/fast missiles, homing shards, arcing bolts;
  • Hitscan/trace: lightning, beam tick, instant line spell;
  • Ground target: rune, trap, area pool, delayed eruption;
  • Area around caster: nova, pulse, fear, cleanse;
  • Aura: sustained buff/debuff radius;
  • Summon: temporary actor or companion;
  • Utility/world: reveal, unlock, activate, cleanse hazard, bridge/traversal

spell.

Targeting profile:

PDA_MagicTargetingProfile
  TargetingMode: None, ForwardAim, LockOnTarget, GroundPoint, AreaQuery, Cone
  TargetingPreset
  MaxRange
  Radius
  Angle
  RequiredTags
  BlockedTags
  SortPolicy
  AimAssistPolicy
  ReticlePolicy

The Gameplay Targeting System should be the preferred shape for reusable target selection/filter/sort rules. Simple projectiles and traces are still valid for MVP, but profile names should match the future Targeting preset route. That keeps bow, throwable, spell, enemy caster, summon, weak point, and lock-on systems from each inventing their own acquisition grammar.

Commit timing policy:

  • if bRequiresValidTargetBeforeCommit is true, target validation happens

before Eth and cooldown are committed;

  • if a spell is intentionally risky, it may commit before final delivery, but

the UI and tooltip must say so;

  • channeled spells may commit an upfront cost plus periodic tick costs;
  • cancelled casts must define whether cooldown applies, partial cost applies,

or nothing is spent.

#21.8 Damage, status, buffs, and debuffs

Magic must plug into P13:

Spell delivery impact
  -> FAscentiaDamagePacket
  -> damage school formula
  -> resistance/defense profile
  -> status payload application
  -> GameplayEffects
  -> GameplayCues

Buffs and debuffs should be Gameplay Effects:

PDA_BuffDebuffProfile
  EffectTags
  GameplayEffectClass
  DurationPolicy
  DurationSeconds
  StackPolicy
  MaxStacks
  RefreshPolicy
  AttributeModifiers
  GrantedTags
  RemovedTags
  PeriodicTickPolicy
  CueProfile
  UIIcon
  TooltipText

Buff/debuff application result:

FAscentiaBuffDebuffApplicationRequest
  SourceActor
  TargetActor
  Profile
  SourceSpell
  StackContext
  TraceId

FAscentiaBuffDebuffApplicationResult
  bApplied
  RejectReason
  ActiveEffectHandle
  StackCountAfter
  DurationRemaining
  GrantedTags[]
  RemovedTags[]
  CueRequests[]
  UIStateUpdate

Rules:

  • duration, stacking, refresh, overflow, immunity, and cleanse behavior must be

authored and validated;

  • buff/debuff UI should read active effect tags and view models, not parse cue

names;

  • spell status buildup must share enemy/boss resistance and immunity windows;
  • spell damage numbers need profile control so magic readability does not drown

melee feedback;

  • every buff/debuff spell needs an icon, tooltip, active-state cue, removal cue,

and validation report.

#21.9 Summons and companion spells

Summons are deceptively large. Even a "simple" temporary ally touches AI, encounters, save, networking, UI, performance, and world readability.

Summon definition:

PDA_SummonDefinition
  SummonTag
  DisplayName
  ActorClass
  AbilitySet
  AIBehaviorProfile
  LifetimePolicy
  MaxConcurrent
  SpawnPolicy
  OwnershipPolicy
  AggroPolicy
  ScalingProfile
  DeathBehavior
  DespawnCue
  UIFramePolicy

Summon runtime contract:

FAscentiaSummonRequest
  SourceActor
  SourceSpell
  SummonDefinition
  SpawnTransform
  TargetingResult
  OwnerTeam
  ScalingSnapshot

FAscentiaSummonResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  SpawnedActor
  OwnershipTags
  LifetimeSeconds
  CleanupTriggers[]
  UIFrame

FAscentiaSummonRuntimeState
  SummonTag
  OwnerActor
  SpawnedActor
  SpawnTime
  LifetimeRemaining
  CurrentTarget
  DamageCreditOwner
  bPersistent
  bCleanupPending

Rules:

  • MVP summons should be temporary and cleanup-safe unless persistent companions

are explicitly chosen;

  • summons should have owner attribution for damage, XP, loot credit, and aggro;
  • summon actors must despawn on owner death, camp rest, map transition, or save

unload unless a profile says otherwise;

  • summon UI should show presence, health if relevant, and expiration only when

useful;

  • enemy targeting should distinguish player, summon, and neutral actors through

tags rather than ad hoc class checks.

  • summon behavior should reuse P18 enemy/AI profiles where possible. A summon is

not a special AI universe; it is an owned combatant with different faction, cleanup, scaling, and credit rules.

#21.10 Flask and resource recovery

Current native Eth flask behavior is the right authority base:

  • Eth flask charge count lives on the AttributeSet;
  • use requires missing Eth and at least one charge;
  • restore and charge consumption apply through a Gameplay Effect;
  • campsite/save/respawn refill restores charges.

Production flask profile:

PDA_FlaskProfile
  FlaskTag
  ResourceType: Health, Eth, Hybrid
  RestoreAmount
  RestorePercent
  ChargeAttribute
  MaxChargeAttribute
  UseAbility
  UseMontage
  UseCue
  CooldownSeconds
  FullResourceRejectText
  EmptyRejectText
  HUDIcon

Upgrade/allocation:

PDA_FlaskUpgrade
  UpgradeTag
  DisplayName
  RequiredItemTag
  Cost
  AddsCharges
  AddsRestoreAmount
  AffectedFlaskTags
  AllocationPolicy

Allocation/save state:

FAscentiaFlaskAllocationState
  HealthCharges
  EthCharges
  SharedCharges
  MaxSharedCharges
  RestoreStrengthByFlask
  AppliedUpgrades[]
  LastCampAllocationTime

FAscentiaFlaskUseRequest
  SourceActor
  FlaskTag
  bPreviewOnly

FAscentiaFlaskUseResult
  bAccepted
  RejectReason
  ResourceBefore
  ResourceAfter
  ChargesBefore
  ChargesAfter
  AppliedGameplayEffect
  CueRequests[]

FAscentiaFlaskUpgradePreview
  UpgradeTag
  Cost
  BeforeState
  AfterState
  RequirementText
  bCanApply

Policy options:

  • fixed separate Health and Eth flask pools;
  • shared charge pool allocated at camp;
  • upgrade tree where items choose charge count vs restore strength;
  • hybrid system where rare upgrades add special secondary effects.

The Soulslike-readable route is shared allocation at camp, but Ascentia does not need to copy it blindly. The important architecture point is that allocation should be authored, visible in UI, saved, validated, and replayable in tests.

Editor Utility Widgets:

EUW_AscentiaSpellbookAuthoring

EUW_AscentiaProjectileRangeLab

EUW_AscentiaGameplayCueCoverage

Runtime widgets:

WBP_AscentiaSpellbook
WBP_AscentiaSpellSlot
WBP_AscentiaSpellTooltip
WBP_AscentiaEthBar
WBP_AscentiaEthFlask
WBP_AscentiaCastReticle
WBP_AscentiaSummonFrame
WBP_AscentiaBuffDebuffTray
WBP_AscentiaCooldownSweep

Blueprint surfaces:

  • BP_AscentiaSpellProjectileBase;
  • BP_AscentiaSpellAreaBase;
  • BP_AscentiaSpellReticleController;
  • BP_AscentiaSpellCue_*;
  • BP_AscentiaSummonBase;
  • BP_AscentiaMagicImpactDecal;
  • BP_AscentiaFlaskPresentation.

#21.12 Tooling opportunities

P15 deserves tools because spell content multiplies quickly.

EUW_AscentiaSpellbookAuthoring:

  • list all AscentiaSpell primary assets;
  • show school, cost, cooldown, delivery, damage, status, cue, icon, and loadout

readiness;

  • flag fallback-only spells;
  • create spell asset templates by school/delivery type;
  • open Spellbook UI preview with sample player stats.

EUW_AscentiaSpellDeliveryPreview:

  • simulate projectile arc, range, lock-on target, ground target, cone, area, and

hit radius;

  • show expected release frame from montage notify;
  • compare targeting preset results against projectile path;
  • preview impact cue and damage/status payload.

EUW_AscentiaMagicBalanceLab:

  • sweep Focus, spell level, enemy resistance, cooldown, cast time, and Eth pool;
  • report damage per Eth, damage per second, poise per Eth, status buildup per

cast, casts per flask, and boss time-to-kill estimates;

  • compare spell against same-tier melee and ranged actions.

EUW_AscentiaFlaskPlanner:

  • preview health/Eth charge allocation;
  • show upgrade costs, restore amounts, camp refill result, and save data;
  • run no-charge/full-resource rejection proofs.

EUW_AscentiaSummonPreview:

  • spawn a summon in a test cell;
  • show owner tags, aggro targets, lifetime, cleanup events, and performance

footprint;

  • verify death/camp/map-transition despawn behavior.

#21.13 P15 evidence, spell reports, and validation output

P15 needs spell evidence that covers authoring, runtime delivery, balance, flasks, and summons.

Reports:

FAscentiaSpellbookAuthoringReport
  ReportId
  SpellAssets[]
  MissingIcons[]
  MissingText[]
  MissingProfiles[]
  FallbackSpells[]
  LoadoutWarnings[]
  SaveSchemaWarnings[]

FAscentiaSpellDeliveryReport
  ReportId
  SpellTag
  CastProfile
  DeliveryProfile
  TargetingResults[]
  DeliveryResults[]
  DamageResults[]
  StatusResults[]
  CueResults[]
  CommitTimingFindings[]
  FailedExpectations[]

FAscentiaMagicBalanceReport
  ReportId
  SpellScenarios[]
  DamagePerEth
  PoisePerEth
  StatusPerEth
  CastsPerFlask
  CooldownPressure
  MeleeRangedComparison
  SuggestedTuningActions[]

FAscentiaFlaskPlannerReport
  ReportId
  AllocationScenarios[]
  UpgradePreviews[]
  UseResults[]
  RefillResults[]
  SaveLoadResults[]
  RejectionProofs[]

FAscentiaSummonPreviewReport
  ReportId
  SummonDefinition
  SpawnResults[]
  AggroResults[]
  CleanupResults[]
  DamageCreditResults[]
  PerformanceFindings[]

Evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Spellbook/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/SpellDelivery/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/MagicBalance/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Flasks/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Summons/{RunId}/report.json

Required proof scenarios:

Tools/remote_playtest.py spell_eth
Tools/remote_playtest.py spellbook_surface
Tools/remote_playtest.py spell_delivery
Tools/remote_playtest.py spellbook_save
Tools/remote_playtest.py spell_targeting
Tools/remote_playtest.py buff_debuff_spell
Tools/remote_playtest.py eth_potion
Tools/remote_playtest.py flask_upgrade
Tools/remote_playtest.py flask_allocation
Tools/remote_playtest.py summon_spell

Acceptance shape:

  • every shippable spell has a primary asset id, icon, text, school, cast profile,

delivery profile, targeting policy, cost/cooldown profile, cue profile, and proof row;

  • every non-instant cast has a cast window/release manifest;
  • every projectile/area/trace spell has targeting and delivery evidence;
  • every buff/debuff has effect, stack, refresh, UI, cue, removal, and save rules;
  • every flask upgrade/allocation change has preview, transaction, save/load, and

camp-refill evidence;

  • every summon profile proves ownership, aggro, cleanup, credit, and UI;
  • Ether Spark fallback remains visible and blocks shipping if any shippable

loadout still depends on it.

#21.14 Interconnects

P15 touches:

  • P10/P11 combat motion: cast movement locks, dodge cancel, root motion, stairs,

ledges, interruption, rotation, and input buffering;

  • P13 damage/build math: magic schools, resistances, status payloads, critical

rules, poise, Focus scaling, and threshold passives;

  • P14 weapons: staff/focus weapons, spellblade imbuements, projectile profiles,

targeting profiles, reticles, montage notifies, and weapon skill overlap;

  • P16 itemization: spell-granting items, talismans, affixes, flask upgrades,

crafting materials, unique powers, and economy sinks;

  • P17 world: spell rewards, locked traversal, magic interactions, hazards,

region school identity, and discovery;

  • P18 AI: caster enemies, summon AI, magic-resistant enemies, boss spells, and

anti-cheese targeting;

  • P19 save/death: known spells, equipped loadout, flask allocation, spell

upgrades, summon cleanup, and death-state resource resets;

  • P20 presentation: GameplayCues, Niagara, MetaSounds, camera, damage numbers,

accessibility, and performance budgets;

  • P21 shell/settings: spell HUD visibility, cast input mode, reticle behavior,

low-Eth prompts, buff/debuff notification density, VFX intensity, and input remapping;

  • P22 multiplayer: predicted casts, server-authoritative projectiles, target

validation, summon ownership, cooldown/resource replication, and anti-cheat.

#21.15 P15 proof gates

P15 is not complete until:

  1. Spell assets are discoverable as AscentiaSpell primary assets and no

shippable spell relies only on native Ether Spark fallback.

  1. Spellbook UI can learn, equip, show cost/cooldown/range/status, reject invalid

choices with reason text, and preserve state through save/load.

  1. Spell input activates through Enhanced Input/GAS binding and updates HUD

cooldown/resource feedback.

  1. At least one projectile spell uses a cast montage, release notify, targeting

or aim snapshot, projectile/trace/area delivery, impact cue, and real damage application through the P13 damage path.

  1. At least one buff or debuff spell applies a duration Gameplay Effect, shows UI

state, refreshes/stacks according to profile, and removes cleanly.

  1. Eth flask UI shows charges, rejects full/no-charge use, plays use montage/cue,

restores exact Eth through GAS, saves/restores allocation, and refills at campsites.

  1. Flask upgrade/allocation data exists and can be previewed in an editor tool.
  2. If summons enter MVP scope, at least one summon proves ownership, aggro,

lifetime, damage credit, UI, cleanup, and save/map/death behavior.

  1. Settings include spell reticle visibility, cast input mode, spell HUD/resource

visibility, Eth flask HUD visibility, low-Eth prompt behavior, spell/flask VFX intensity, summon visibility/readability, buff/debuff notification density, and flask-use feedback intensity where applicable.

  1. Validation covers missing icons, missing cues, invalid tags, invalid

GameplayEffects, absent release notifies, invalid projectile classes, missing target presets, missing spellbook text, invalid flask charges, and fallback-only content.

  1. Remote proof covers:
  • spell_eth
  • spellbook_surface
  • eth_potion
  • spell_delivery
  • spellbook_save
  • spell_targeting
  • flask_upgrade
  • flask_allocation
  • buff_debuff_spell
  • summon_spell
  1. Dashboard evidence links the latest Spellbook authoring report, spell

delivery preview report, magic balance report, and flask planner report.

#21.16 P15 starter implementation package

The first P15 package should turn the current Ether Spark, Spellbook, Eth cost, and Eth potion proofs into a spell production lane. The immediate goal is one fully authored spell path with visible cast, targeting, delivery, damage/status, cue, UI, save, and proof contracts.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellTypes.h

Source/Ascentia/Public/Summons/AscentiaSummonTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Magic/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Spellbook/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Flasks/...
Source/Ascentia/Private/Summons/...

Editor module layout:

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Magic/AscentiaSpellDeliveryValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Magic/AscentiaMagicBalanceValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Flasks/AscentiaFlaskValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Summons/AscentiaSummonValidation.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaSpellbookAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaSpellDeliveryAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaMagicBalanceAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaSpellResolverTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaSpellCostCommitTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaFlaskUseTest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Tests/AscentiaSummonLifecycleTest.cpp

Subsystem and class boundaries:

UAscentiaSpellResolverSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  ResolveSpellDefinition(SpellTag)
  ResolveEquippedSpell(Spellbook, Slot)
  ResolveSpellCastProfile(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  ResolveSpellDeliveryProfile(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  ResolveSpellCost(SpellDefinition, SourceAttributes)
  ResolveSpellView(SpellDefinition, SourceBuild)
  WriteSpellbookAuthoringReport(OutputPath)

UAscentiaAbility_SpellCast : UGameplayAbility
  GAS activation, cost/cooldown, ability tasks, montage/cast/release lifecycle
  delegates spell lookup to resolver and delivery to the delivery path

UAscentiaSpellbookComponent
  owns known spells, loadout slots, equipped selection, save-facing state, and
  Spellbook view model events

UAscentiaSpellDeliverySubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  resolves targeting result into projectile, trace, area, buff/debuff, summon, or
  utility delivery

UAscentiaFlaskSubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem or UWorldSubsystem
  previews flask allocation/upgrades and owns non-ability helper queries; actual
  flask use still applies through GAS effects

UAscentiaSummonSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  owns summon spawn, ownership tags, damage credit, cleanup, and reports

Starter content folders:

/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Spells/

Primary asset policy:

PrimaryAssetType:
  AscentiaSpell
  AscentiaSpellSchool
  AscentiaSpellCastProfile
  AscentiaSpellDeliveryProfile
  AscentiaMagicTargetingProfile
  AscentiaFlaskProfile
  AscentiaSummonDefinition

Rules:
  spell catalogs scan `/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Spells`;
  cooked builds load spell bundles through Asset Manager;
  fallback Ether Spark is allowed in editor/dev reports but blocks shipping
  unless it is represented by `PDA_Spell_EtherSpark_MVP`.

Spell resolver signatures:

FAscentiaResolvedSpell ResolveSpell(
  FGameplayTag SpellTag,
  const FAscentiaSpellResolveContext& Context);

FAscentiaSpellView BuildSpellView(
  const FAscentiaResolvedSpell& Spell,
  const FAscentiaBuildSnapshot& SourceBuild);

FAscentiaSpellCostResult ResolveSpellCost(
  const FAscentiaResolvedSpell& Spell,
  const FAscentiaSpellCostRequest& Request);

FAscentiaSpellbookView BuildSpellbookView(
  const UAscentiaSpellbookComponent& Spellbook,
  const FAscentiaSpellbookViewContext& Context);

Cast and commit policy:

Instant:
  validate activation, commit Eth/cooldown, deliver immediately, end ability

Timed projectile:
  validate activation, optionally validate target before commit, commit Eth at
  cast start, play montage, release on notify, apply cooldown on release or cast
  start according to profile

Held aim:
  validate activation, enter hold state, update targeting preview, commit on
  release, cancel without spend if profile permits

Channeled:
  commit entry cost, tick periodic Eth/cooldown/payload, cleanup on interrupt

Ritual:
  movement-locked, long cast, world-state/service integration, explicit
  interruption and refund policy

Starter proof matrix:

EtherSpark_Fallback_AuthoredAsset
EtherSpark_Instant_EthCostCooldownCue
FrostShard_TimedProjectile_ReleaseNotify
FrostShard_TargetBeforeCommit
PoisonWisp_StatusPayload_P13
SelfWard_BuffDuration_UIRefreshRemove
Spellbook_LearnEquipSaveLoad
Spellbook_InvalidEquip_ReasonText
EthFlask_FullRejected
EthFlask_NoChargeRejected
EthFlask_UseRestoresExactEth
FlaskUpgrade_EthChargePreviewSave
SummonEcho_SpawnCreditCleanup
LowEthPrompt_SettingsVisibility

Editor surfaces:

EUW_AscentiaSpellbookAuthoring
  browse spell primary assets, school/cost/cooldown/delivery/readiness, fallback
  warnings, and Spellbook UI preview

EUW_AscentiaSpellDeliveryPreview
  preview cast montage release frame, targeting result, projectile/trace/area,
  impact, damage/status, and cues

EUW_AscentiaSpellTargetingDebugger
  run Targeting preset or simple trace fixtures and show candidate/filter/sort
  decisions

EUW_AscentiaBuffDebuffPreview
  preview duration, stack, refresh, removal, active UI, and cue lifecycle

EUW_AscentiaMagicBalanceLab
  compare damage per Eth, poise per Eth, status per Eth, casts per flask, and
  melee/ranged parity

EUW_AscentiaFlaskPlanner
  preview charge pools, upgrades, camp refill, use rejection, and save state

EUW_AscentiaSummonPreview
  spawn test summon, show owner, aggro, lifetime, cleanup, damage credit, and UI

Commandlet contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaSpellbookAudit
  -SpellRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Spells
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaSpellDeliveryAudit
  -SpellRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Spells
  -ScenarioTable=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Balance/DT_SpellDeliveryScenarios_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaMagicBalanceAudit
  -ScenarioTable=/Game/Ascentia/Design/Magic/Balance/DT_MagicBalanceScenarios_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15

Tools/run_p15_spell_delivery.ps1
  -Scenario SpellDelivery
  -ReportRoot Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15

Evidence outputs:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Spellbook/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/SpellDelivery/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/SpellTargeting/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/MagicBalance/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/BuffDebuff/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Flasks/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P15/Summons/{RunId}/report.json

Implementation tickets:

P15-001 Spell primary asset expansion
  Expand `UAscentiaSpellDefinition` into the full `PDA_Spell` contract with
  school, cast, delivery, targeting, damage/status, cue, UI, and bundle refs.

P15-002 Spell resolver and view model
  Implement `UAscentiaSpellResolverSubsystem`, `FAscentiaResolvedSpell`,
  `FAscentiaSpellView`, and fallback reporting.

P15-003 Spellbook save/load and UI bridge
  Implement `FAscentiaSpellbookView`, `FAscentiaSpellbookSaveData`, loadout slot
  state, learn/equip requests, and invalid-equip reason text.

P15-004 Cast lifecycle and window manifest
  Implement `FAscentiaSpellCastRequest`, `FAscentiaSpellCastResult`,
  `FAscentiaActiveSpellCastState`, `FAscentiaSpellCastWindowManifest`, and the
  first timed projectile release-notify path.

P15-005 Targeting and delivery contracts
  Implement `FAscentiaSpellTargetingRequest`, `FAscentiaSpellTargetingResult`,
  `FAscentiaSpellDeliveryRequest`, `FAscentiaSpellDeliveryResult`, and the first
  projectile/trace delivery report.

P15-006 Buff/debuff spell profile
  Implement `PDA_BuffDebuffProfile`, duration/stack/refresh/remove behavior,
  active UI view, cue lifecycle, and report rows.

P15-007 Eth resource and flask profiles
  Implement `PDA_EthResourceProfile`, `FAscentiaEthRegenState`,
  `PDA_FlaskProfile`, `FAscentiaFlaskUseResult`, and full/no-charge rejection
  proof.

P15-008 Flask upgrade and allocation planner
  Implement `PDA_FlaskUpgrade`, `PDA_FlaskAllocationProfile`,
  `FAscentiaFlaskAllocationState`, `FAscentiaFlaskUpgradePreview`, save/load,
  and camp-refill reports.

P15-009 Summon prototype contract
  Implement `PDA_SummonDefinition`, `FAscentiaSummonRequest`,
  `FAscentiaSummonResult`, `FAscentiaSummonRuntimeState`, owner credit, aggro,
  and cleanup proof.

P15-010 P15 packaged proof suite
  Run Spellbook, spell delivery, targeting, buff/debuff, Eth flask, flask
  upgrade/allocation, summon, and magic balance proofs in PIE and packaged
  Development.

Validation gates:

  • any shippable spell without a primary asset id, icon, text, school, cast

profile, delivery profile, targeting policy, cost/cooldown, cue profile, and proof row fails validation;

  • any non-instant spell without a release notify or explicit release policy

fails validation;

  • any projectile/trace/area spell without targeting and delivery evidence fails

validation;

  • any buff/debuff without GameplayEffect, stack/refresh policy, UI, cue, and

removal behavior fails validation;

  • any flask profile without full/no-charge rejection text and exact GAS restore

proof fails validation;

  • any summon without ownership, cleanup, damage credit, and UI policy remains

prototype-only;

  • Ether Spark fallback must be represented as an authored asset or block

shipping.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Convert Ether Spark into a real PDA_Spell_EtherSpark_MVP.
  2. Implement the spell resolver and Spellbook view model while preserving

current behavior.

  1. Add a timed projectile spell with montage release notify and delivery report.
  2. Add Eth flask profile data and exact use/rejection proof.
  3. Add one buff/debuff spell before adding more projectile variants.
  4. Treat summons as prototype-only until ownership, cleanup, and AI credit are

proven.

#22. Enemies, AI, Encounters, Bosses, and Population Production

#22.1 Player-facing goal

P18 is where Ascentia stops having "an enemy" and starts having an enemy production system.

The player-facing promise:

  • trash enemies teach timing, spacing, status, terrain, and resource discipline;
  • elites recombine familiar rules into pressure tests;
  • bosses define zones through spectacle, readable moves, phase changes, music,

arena rules, reward identity, and memory;

  • groups create decision pressure without becoming unfair noise;
  • enemy behavior respects terrain, leashes, perception, world state, summons,

stealth, ranged pressure, and multiplayer future-proofing;

  • Designers can make enemy families, encounters, boss moves, patrols, and

behavior variants without rewriting the pawn.

The production target is an authoring pipeline where a new enemy is mostly: definition asset, stat rows, ability set, behavior asset, EQS/positioning policy, animations, cues, loot, and validation.

#22.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

  • AAscentiaEnemyCharacter exists as the native enemy embodiment;
  • AAscentiaEnemyAIController exists with Behavior Tree hooks;
  • boss phase, target, health-fraction, movement request, and selected ability

sync have native BT-facing footholds;

  • UAscentiaBossDefinition exists with display name, intro presentation, phase

rows, optional phase ability sets, music state/cue, behavior tag, and fog-wall hook;

  • persistent BB_DraugrBoss_P11 / BT_DraugrBoss_P11 assets run a first

service-to-move-to-boss-melee loop;

  • UAscentiaAbility_BossMelee activates through GAS, respects enemy Breath

affordability, and delegates the strike to the enemy;

  • enemy attack resource gating and telemetry have proof through

enemy_resource;

  • fog wall, boss intro/name card, phase music state, defeat presentation, and

stagger/poise punish-window telemetry exist;

  • AAscentiaEncounterZoneActor.ManagedSpawns is already an editor-visible MVP

spawn surface;

  • remote proof scenarios already touch bt_asset_ai, boss_phase,

boss_presentation, fog_wall, stagger, enemy_resource, and MVP baseline combat loops.

Production gaps:

  • enemy variety is essentially one authored enemy family;
  • enemy stats, resistances, loot, moves, movement, perception, and encounter

budgets are not resolved through production PDA_EnemyDefinition assets;

  • Behavior Tree work is still a foothold, not a complete branch policy for

patrol, idle, investigate, leash, retreat, ranged, caster, group, and boss phase behavior;

  • no EQS spacing/positioning model exists for melee slots, ranged line of sight,

flanking, retreat, or arena anchors;

  • AI perception is shallow: sight exists conceptually, but hearing, damage,

memory, investigation, target switching, and faction/team policy are not production systems;

  • group coordination is not authored, so future packs can easily dogpile, stall,

or attack all at once;

  • current AI still carries player-0 assumptions in places and needs target

acquisition through perception/aggro policy;

  • boss move grammar is not a data-authored matrix with range bands, cooldowns,

telegraphs, notifies, root-motion policy, and counterplay notes;

  • encounter zones need data assets, leash/reset/save policy, budget validation,

and world-state integration.

#22.3 Production AI stack

Recommended default stack:

Enemy pawn/controller
  -> ASC, AttributeSet, movement, animation, perception, telemetry

Enemy definition
  -> identity, stats, resistances, ability set, loot, budget, presentation

Behavior Tree + Blackboard
  -> readable combat flow and current facts

EQS
  -> spatial decisions and target positions

GameplayAbilities
  -> enemy attacks, spells, blocks, dodges, skills, boss moves

Encounter definition
  -> spawn plan, group rules, leash/reset/save, rewards, world state

Use Behavior Trees for core combat because Ascentia already has BT footholds and BTs are readable for Designers. Unreal's Behavior Tree model pairs execution branches with Blackboard keys, which is exactly the shape needed for target, phase, distance, cooldown, health, and group-role state.

Use EQS for "where should I be?" It should not be hard-coded into every enemy: melee approach points, circle points, ranged line-of-sight points, retreat points, flank points, boss anchors, summon points, and return-to-post points should be query profiles.

Use AI Perception for awareness. Sight, hearing, damage, prediction, team, and touch senses should feed aggro/investigation state rather than direct player lookups.

Use StateTree for ambient or structured non-combat behavior: patrol/rest/work, investigate loops, Smart Object usage, simple NPC schedules, and future distant simulation.

Use Smart Objects for world-authored activities and reservations: guard posts, ambush points, ritual sites, ranged perches, campfires, levers, ladders, and boss arena anchors.

Use Mass only for distant non-combat populations, wildlife, crowds, or settlement life. Do not move core boss combat into Mass; bosses require bespoke animation, GAS, root motion, camera, cue, and presentation control.

#22.4 Enemy definition model

Enemy definition:

PDA_EnemyDefinition
  EnemyTag
  DisplayName
  EnemyFamilyTags
  WorldTier
  AttributeProfile
  ResistanceProfile
  StatusVulnerabilityProfile
  PoiseProfile
  ResourceProfile
  PerceptionProfile
  MovementProfile
  AbilitySet
  BehaviorTree
  StateTree
  BlackboardAsset
  EQSProfiles
  LootTable
  CorpseLootPolicy
  EncounterBudgetCost
  SpawnTags
  FactionTag
  VoiceCueProfile
  HitReactionProfile
  DeathPresentationProfile
  SmartObjectUsageTags
  NavAgentType
  ValidationNotes

Enemy family row:

FAscentiaEnemyFamilyRow
  EnemyFamilyTag
  DisplayName
  DefaultBehaviorTree
  DefaultAbilitySet
  DefaultPerceptionProfile
  DefaultMovementProfile
  DefaultLootTable
  AllowedBiomeTags
  AllowedEncounterTags
  DefaultBudgetCost
  DefaultFactionTag

Enemy stat row:

FAscentiaEnemyStatRow
  EnemyTag
  WorldTier
  Level
  MaxHealth
  MaxBreath
  BreathRegen
  MaxPoise
  Armor
  Resistances
  AttackPowerScalar
  StatusBuildupScalar
  RewardStones
  ProgressionValue
  EncounterBudgetCost

Rules:

  • DataTables own bulk numeric tuning and tier sweeps.
  • PrimaryDataAssets own object references, ability sets, behavior assets, cues,

movement profiles, and validation notes.

  • Runtime enemy snapshots should resolve once on spawn, then expose what was

resolved to debug/UI/automation.

  • Designers must be able to see whether a value came from family default, enemy

override, encounter override, world-tier row, or native fallback.

Resolution service:

UAscentiaEnemyDefinitionResolver
  ResolveEnemyDefinition(EnemyTag, WorldTier, EncounterContext)
  BuildSpawnRequest(EncounterSpawnGroup, SpawnAnchor, RuntimeTags)
  BuildEnemyRuntimeSnapshot(EnemyActor)
  WriteEnemyDefinitionReport(OutputPath)

Resolved enemy definition:

FAscentiaResolvedEnemyDefinition
  EnemyTag
  DisplayName
  FamilyTags
  WorldTier
  Level
  AttributeSnapshot
  ResistanceSnapshot
  StatusVulnerabilitySnapshot
  PoiseProfile
  ResourceProfile
  PerceptionProfile
  MovementProfile
  AbilitySet
  BehaviorTree
  BlackboardAsset
  StateTree
  EQSProfiles
  LootTable
  FactionTag
  EncounterBudgetCost
  PresentationProfiles
  SourceTrace[]
  ValidationSeverity

Spawn request/result:

FAscentiaEnemySpawnRequest
  EnemyTag
  EncounterTag
  SpawnGroupTag
  SpawnAnchor
  WorldTier
  InitialFactionTag
  InitialGroupRoleTag
  InitialBehaviorTag
  RuntimeTags

FAscentiaEnemySpawnResult
  SpawnedActor
  AIController
  ResolvedDefinition
  GrantedAbilities
  BlackboardInitialization
  SpawnCueResult
  Warnings[]
  Errors[]

Runtime snapshot:

FAscentiaEnemyRuntimeSnapshot
  EnemyActor
  EnemyTag
  EncounterTag
  CurrentTarget
  CurrentHealthFraction
  CurrentBreathFraction
  CurrentPoiseFraction
  CurrentBehaviorTag
  CurrentPhaseTag
  ActiveMoveTag
  ActiveGameplayEffects
  ThreatEntries
  GroupRoleTag
  ReservedSlotTag
  LeashState
  LastStimulusRecord
  LastEQSResult

Definition report:

FAscentiaEnemyDefinitionReport
  RunId
  EnemyCount
  FamilyCount
  MissingProfileRefs[]
  MissingBehaviorRefs[]
  MissingPresentationRefs[]
  FallbackSources[]
  ResolvedEnemies[]
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

The resolver should be callable from commandlets, validation, Editor Utility Widgets, PIE debug tools, and encounter spawning. That keeps enemy truth out of one-off spawn Blueprints while still making the resolved result visible in the Editor.

#22.5 Perception, aggro, factions, and investigation

Perception profile:

PDA_AIPerceptionProfile
  SightRadius
  LoseSightRadius
  PeripheralVisionAngle
  HearingRange
  DamageMemorySeconds
  InvestigationMemorySeconds
  DominantSense
  TeamPolicy
  TargetSwitchPolicy
  AlertPropagationRadius
  StimulusTagFilters
  DebugColor

Aggro profile:

PDA_AggroProfile
  InitialAggroRules
  DamageAggroWeight
  ProximityAggroWeight
  SpellAggroWeight
  SummonAggroWeight
  HealingAggroWeight
  ThreatDecaySeconds
  TargetSwitchHysteresis
  AlertAlliesPolicy
  LeashBreakPolicy

Faction profile:

PDA_FactionProfile
  FactionTag
  HostileFactionTags
  FriendlyFactionTags
  NeutralFactionTags
  CanDamageSameFaction
  AssistPolicy
  SummonOwnershipPolicy

Investigation states:

  • saw target;
  • lost sight;
  • heard noise;
  • took damage;
  • found corpse;
  • found player trace;
  • returned to post;
  • alerted group;
  • de-escalated.

Rules:

  • target selection should never assume player 0;
  • summons, companions, co-op players, neutral NPCs, and hostile factions need

targetable identities;

  • hearing/noise should support stealth, thrown objects, projectiles, footsteps,

combat impacts, and spell cues later;

  • perception memory must be visible in AI debug output and proof reports.

Perception bridge:

UAscentiaAIPerceptionBridge
  OnStimulusReceived(AIActor, Stimulus)
  RecordStimulus(StimulusRecord)
  UpdateThreatTable(ThreatEvent)
  SelectTarget(SelectionContext)
  ExportPerceptionSnapshot(AIActor)

Stimulus record:

FAscentiaPerceptionStimulusRecord
  StimulusId
  SourceActor
  SourceTeamOrFactionTag
  SenseTag
  StimulusLocation
  ReceiverLocation
  Strength
  AgeSeconds
  WasSuccessfullySensed
  SourceGameplayTags
  EvidenceTags

Threat entry:

FAscentiaThreatEntry
  TargetActor
  TargetIdentityTag
  FactionRelationship
  DamageThreat
  ProximityThreat
  HealingThreat
  SpellThreat
  SummonThreat
  ObjectiveThreat
  RecencyBonus
  CurrentTotalThreat
  LastStimulusTime
  LastKnownLocation
  HasLineOfSight

Target selection:

FAscentiaTargetSelectionResult
  SelectedTarget
  PreviousTarget
  SwitchReasonTag
  ThreatEntries
  HysteresisApplied
  PerceptionMemoryApplied
  BlackboardWrites
  RejectedTargets[]

Investigation state:

FAscentiaInvestigationState
  InvestigationTag
  SourceStimulusId
  InvestigationLocation
  SearchRadius
  StartedAtTime
  ExpiresAtTime
  AlertedAllies[]
  ReturnBehaviorTag

EUW_AscentiaThreatTableDebugger should show the table as a sorted list with source columns. A Designer should be able to answer "why did the boss attack my summon instead of me?" without reading C++ or stepping through perception code.

#22.6 Blackboard and behavior contract

Core Blackboard keys:

TargetActor
HomeLocation
LeashAnchor
CurrentEnemyTag
CurrentBehaviorTag
CurrentPhaseTag
CurrentPhaseIndex
HealthFraction
BreathFraction
PoiseFraction
TargetDistance
TargetAngle
HasLineOfSight
LastKnownTargetLocation
InvestigationLocation
DesiredCombatRange
SelectedAbilityTag
SelectedMoveTag
SelectedEQSLocation
EncounterTag
GroupRoleTag
IsAlerted
IsLeashing
IsReturningHome

Boss keys:

BossPhaseId
BossPhaseBehaviorTag
ArenaTag
FogWallSealed
LastMoveTag
MoveCooldownGroup
EnrageState
AddsAliveCount

Contract:

  • every BT task/service documents which keys it reads and writes;
  • validators fail when a BT references keys missing from its Blackboard;
  • boss phase sync is a service, not duplicated inside every move branch;
  • target acquisition writes facts into Blackboard, while ability execution reads

those facts through a narrow request interface;

  • fallback native tick AI should become an emergency fallback only and should be

visible in validation when still active.

Behavior contract artifacts:

FAscentiaBlackboardKeyContract
  KeyName
  KeyType
  OwnerSystemTag
  RequiredForBehaviorTags
  WrittenByTasks[]
  ReadByTasks[]
  DefaultValuePolicy
  DebugDescription

FAscentiaBlackboardContract
  BlackboardAsset
  RequiredKeys[]
  OptionalKeys[]
  ForbiddenKeys[]
  BehaviorTags
  BossPhaseKeys

AI state snapshot:

FAscentiaAIStateSnapshot
  AIActor
  Controller
  BehaviorTree
  BlackboardAsset
  CurrentBTNode
  CurrentBehaviorTag
  BlackboardValues
  CurrentTarget
  CurrentMoveRequest
  CurrentEQSRequest
  CurrentGameplayAbility
  NativeFallbackActive

BT validation report:

FAscentiaBehaviorTreeContractReport
  BehaviorTree
  BlackboardAsset
  MissingKeys[]
  UnusedKeys[]
  TypeMismatches[]
  NativeFallbackPaths[]
  TasksWithoutReadWriteDocs[]
  ServicesWithoutTickBudget[]
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

EUW_AscentiaAIValidationPanel should open a BT/Blackboard pair and show the contract as a table. Key ownership matters because otherwise Blackboard state becomes another invisible global variable pile.

#22.7 Movement, spacing, and EQS

Enemy movement should be profiled:

PDA_AIMovementProfile
  WalkSpeed
  RunSpeed
  StrafeSpeed
  TurnRate
  Acceleration
  Braking
  PreferredRange
  MinRange
  MaxRange
  CirclePolicy
  RetreatPolicy
  FlankPolicy
  NavAgentType
  RootMotionPolicy
  MotionWarpPolicy
  StuckRecoveryPolicy

EQS profiles:

PDA_EQSProfile
  QueryAsset
  Contexts
  RequiredTags
  BlockedTags
  ScorePolicy
  MinScore
  DebugColor
  FailureFallback

Required EQS query families:

  • melee attack slot;
  • strafing/circle slot;
  • ranged line-of-sight;
  • retreat/recover;
  • flank;
  • ambush reveal;
  • boss arena anchor;
  • summon/add spawn;
  • return-to-post;
  • Smart Object selection.

Rules:

  • multiple melee enemies should reserve different attack slots;
  • ranged enemies should choose line-of-sight positions, not run into melee by

accident;

  • retreat should respect arena boundaries and not flee through fog walls;
  • boss anchor movement should be authored per arena;
  • stuck recovery should be telemetry-backed, not invisible teleporting.

EQS request/result:

FAscentiaEQSRequest
  RequestingActor
  TargetActor
  QueryTag
  QueryAsset
  ContextTags
  RequiredLocationTags
  BlockedLocationTags
  MinScore
  FailureFallback
  DebugReason

FAscentiaEQSResult
  QueryTag
  ChosenLocation
  Score
  CandidateCount
  RejectedCandidates[]
  FailureReasonTag
  BlackboardWrites

Spacing reservation:

FAscentiaSpacingReservation
  ReservationId
  EncounterTag
  OwnerActor
  TargetActor
  SlotTag
  Location
  Radius
  ExpiresAtTime
  ReleaseReasonTag

EQS proof report:

FAscentiaEQSProofReport
  RunId
  QueryTag
  ScenarioTag
  CandidateCount
  SelectedScore
  ReservationConflicts
  FailedQueries[]
  Screenshots[]
  DebugTracePath

EUW_AscentiaEQSPreview should be able to run a profile from a selected enemy or encounter marker and draw chosen, rejected, and reserved points. This is the Editor-facing place where "ranged enemy stands somewhere sensible" becomes inspectable instead of vibes.

#22.8 Enemy moves and ability sets

Enemy moves are data plus GameplayAbilities:

PDA_EnemyMove
  MoveTag
  AbilityClass
  RequiredEnemyFamilyTags
  RangeMin
  RangeMax
  AngleMin
  AngleMax
  CooldownTag
  CooldownSeconds
  BreathCost
  EthCost
  Weight
  PhaseTags
  TargetStateRequirements
  SelfStateRequirements
  MotionProfile
  MontageSet
  TelegraphCue
  ActiveWindowNames
  RecoveryWindowNames
  CounterplayNotes

Ability set:

PDA_EnemyAbilitySet
  EnemyTagOrFamilyTag
  GrantedAbilities[]
  PassiveEffects[]
  StartupEffects[]
  InputOrAICommandTags[]

Selection score:

Score =
  range fit
  + angle fit
  + phase weight
  + cooldown availability
  + target-state bonus
  + self-state bonus
  + recent-move penalty
  + group-role adjustment
  + Designer-authored weight

Rules:

  • Behavior Trees choose when to request a move; GameplayAbilities own authority,

costs, cooldowns, montages, notifies, damage, and cues;

  • Designers should be able to inspect why a move was selected or rejected;
  • every attack montage needs damage windows, cue markers, root-motion policy,

and counterplay notes;

  • enemy Breath/Eth economies should use the same GAS resource path as player

abilities.

Move selection query:

FAscentiaEnemyMoveQuery
  EnemyActor
  TargetActor
  EnemyTag
  EnemyFamilyTags
  BehaviorTag
  PhaseTag
  RangeToTarget
  AngleToTarget
  LineOfSight
  GroupRoleTag
  CurrentResources
  RecentMoveTags
  TargetStateTags
  SelfStateTags

Move score:

FAscentiaEnemyMoveScoreBreakdown
  MoveTag
  IsLegal
  FinalScore
  RangeScore
  AngleScore
  PhaseScore
  CooldownScore
  ResourceScore
  TargetStateScore
  SelfStateScore
  GroupRoleScore
  RepeatPenalty
  DesignerWeight
  RejectionReasons[]

Move selection/execution:

FAscentiaEnemyMoveSelection
  SelectedMoveTag
  SelectedAbilityClass
  ScoreBreakdown
  CandidateScores[]
  BlackboardWrites
  GameplayAbilityRequest

FAscentiaEnemyMoveExecutionResult
  MoveTag
  AbilitySpecHandle
  CommitResult
  MontageResult
  DamageWindowsObserved
  CueEventsObserved
  CancelWindowObserved
  FailureReasonTag

The move selector should be deterministic enough to debug and varied enough to avoid robotic repetition. The report should preserve both the winner and the interesting losers, because rejected moves often explain tuning problems faster than the selected move.

#22.9 Encounter definitions and reset policy

Encounter definition:

PDA_EncounterDefinition
  EncounterTag
  ZoneTag
  POITag
  WorldTier
  EncounterType
  Budget
  SpawnGroups
  PatrolRoutes
  SmartObjectSets
  LeashPolicy
  ResetPolicy
  SavePolicy
  AggroPolicy
  RewardPolicy
  MusicState
  CompletionWorldStateTags
  FailureWorldStateTags
  RuntimeDataLayers
  DebugNotes

Spawn group:

FAscentiaEncounterSpawnGroup
  GroupTag
  EnemyDefinitions
  SpawnTransformsOrAnchors
  PatrolRouteTag
  GroupRoleTags
  SpawnConditionTags
  RespawnPolicy
  MaxAlive
  AlertPropagationRadius

Leash profile:

PDA_EncounterLeashProfile
  LeashRadius
  HardLeashRadius
  VerticalTolerance
  ReturnBehavior
  ResetHealthPolicy
  ResetResourcePolicy
  ResetPositionPolicy
  ResetAggroPolicy

Save/reset policy:

  • trash enemies respawn on rest;
  • patrols reset to route;
  • ambushers reset hidden state unless discovered state says otherwise;
  • unique elites can stay dead until world reset;
  • story bosses stay dead after defeat;
  • summoned adds despawn with encounter owner;
  • repeatable POIs separate discovered state from resettable reward attempts.

Encounter runtime state:

FAscentiaEncounterRuntimeState
  EncounterTag
  ZoneTag
  ActiveSpawnGroups
  AliveActors
  DefeatedActors
  AlertedGroups
  CurrentBudgetUsed
  CompletionStateTag
  LeashState
  RewardState
  RuntimeDataLayers
  GroupCoordinationState

Reset request/result:

FAscentiaEncounterResetRequest
  EncounterTag
  ResetReasonTag
  RestPointTag
  WorldStateTags
  SaveSlotId
  PreserveDiscoveredState
  PreserveDefeatedUniqueActors

FAscentiaEncounterResetResult
  EncounterTag
  ResetActors[]
  DespawnedActors[]
  RespawnedGroups[]
  PreservedStateTags[]
  ClearedStateTags[]
  RewardResetResult
  Warnings[]

Save fragment:

FAscentiaEncounterSaveFragment
  EncounterTag
  Version
  CompletionStateTag
  DefeatedUniqueEnemyTags
  OpenedRewardTags
  DiscoveredAmbushTags
  SpawnGroupOverrides
  LastResetReasonTag

Budget report:

FAscentiaEncounterBudgetReport
  EncounterTag
  Budget
  BudgetUsed
  SpawnGroups
  EnemyCosts
  RoleCounts
  RewardValue
  DifficultyBand
  OverBudgetReasons[]
  MissingPolicyRefs[]

EUW_AscentiaEncounterResetPolicyPreview should show what will reset on rest, death, reload, boss defeat, and world-state change. This is one of the places where the Soulslike loop must be authored rather than inferred.

#22.10 Group coordination and roles

Group roles:

Ascentia.AI.Role.Frontliner
Ascentia.AI.Role.Flanker
Ascentia.AI.Role.Ranged
Ascentia.AI.Role.Caster
Ascentia.AI.Role.Support
Ascentia.AI.Role.SummonedAdd

Group coordination profile:

PDA_GroupCoordinationProfile
  MaxSimultaneousAttackers
  AttackSlotCount
  RangedSlotCount
  FlankSlotCount
  AlertPropagationPolicy
  RoleAssignmentPolicy
  SlotReservationTimeout
  FriendlyFirePolicy
  RetreatWhenOutnumberedPolicy

Coordinator responsibilities:

  • assign roles on spawn;
  • reserve attack, flank, and ranged slots;
  • throttle simultaneous committed attacks;
  • broadcast alert state;
  • prevent body-block piles;
  • release reservations on death, leash, reset, stun, or target switch;
  • expose telemetry for selected role, slot, and attack permission.

Start this as UAscentiaEncounterGroupComponent on the encounter zone. Promote later only if cross-zone populations need it.

Group reservation artifacts:

FAscentiaGroupSlotReservation
  ReservationId
  EncounterTag
  GroupTag
  OwnerActor
  TargetActor
  RoleTag
  SlotTag
  SlotLocation
  StartedAtTime
  ExpiresAtTime
  ReleaseReasonTag

FAscentiaGroupCoordinationState
  EncounterTag
  TargetActor
  ActiveRoles
  ActiveReservations
  SimultaneousAttackers
  RangedPressureCount
  FlankPressureCount
  AlertedActors
  WaitingActors

Coordination report:

FAscentiaGroupCoordinationReport
  RunId
  EncounterTag
  MaxSimultaneousAttackers
  ObservedSimultaneousAttackers
  ReservationConflicts
  SlotStarvationEvents
  BodyBlockEvents
  FriendlyFireEvents
  RoleAssignmentTrace
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

This report is the antidote to "five enemies all swing through each other at once." Designers should be able to loosen or tighten pressure through the group profile and immediately see how it changes reservations and attack permission.

#22.11 Boss production grammar

Bosses are encounter systems, not larger trash enemies.

Boss assets:

PDA_BossDefinition
  BossTag
  DisplayName
  Subtitle
  ArenaTag
  PhaseDefinitions
  DefaultBehaviorTree
  DefaultBlackboard
  BossAbilitySet
  FogWallPolicy
  IntroPresentation
  DefeatPresentation
  MusicProfile
  LootTable
  RematchPolicy
  PersistencePolicy

Boss phase:

PDA_BossPhase
  PhaseTag
  DisplayName
  HealthThreshold
  BehaviorTag
  AbilitySetOverride
  MoveWeightOverrides
  ArenaStateTags
  MusicState
  IntroCue
  PhaseTransitionMontage
  AddSpawnPolicy

Boss move:

PDA_BossMove
  MoveTag
  AbilityClass
  Montage
  RangeBand
  AngleBand
  MinCooldown
  MaxCooldown
  RepeatPenalty
  PhaseTags
  TelegraphCue
  ActiveDamageWindows
  RecoveryWindows
  CancelOrInterruptRules
  RootMotionPolicy
  MotionWarpPolicy
  ArenaConstraints
  CounterplayNote
  CameraProfile
  RumbleProfile
  MusicStinger

Each boss phase should change at least one real system:

  • move weights;
  • new move unlocks;
  • arena state;
  • add pressure;
  • music state;
  • resource economy;
  • counterplay rhythm;
  • traversal hazard.

If a phase only changes text or music, it is presentation, not a boss design phase.

Boss move query and selection:

FAscentiaBossMoveQuery
  BossActor
  TargetActor
  BossTag
  PhaseTag
  ArenaTag
  RangeToTarget
  AngleToTarget
  BossHealthFraction
  BossResourceState
  ArenaStateTags
  RecentMoveTags
  PlayerPressureState
  AddsAliveCount

FAscentiaBossMoveScoreBreakdown
  MoveTag
  IsLegal
  FinalScore
  RangeScore
  AngleScore
  PhaseWeight
  CooldownScore
  RepeatPenalty
  ArenaConstraintScore
  CounterplaySpacingScore
  PresentationReadabilityScore
  RejectionReasons[]

FAscentiaBossMoveSelection
  SelectedMoveTag
  SelectedAbilityClass
  CandidateScores[]
  PhaseContext
  BlackboardWrites
  GameplayAbilityRequest

Boss phase runtime:

FAscentiaBossPhaseRuntimeState
  BossTag
  CurrentPhaseTag
  CurrentPhaseIndex
  EnteredAtHealthFraction
  PhaseEntryTime
  ActiveArenaStateTags
  ActiveMoveWeightOverrides
  ActiveAddGroups
  MusicState
  TransitionLockedUntilWindow

Boss quality reports:

FAscentiaBossQualityReport
  RunId
  BossTag
  ArenaTag

  MoveCoverage
  PunishWindowCoverage
  TelegraphCoverage
  DamageWindowCoverage
  RootMotionArenaCoverage
  CameraReadabilityIssues
  CueCoverageIssues
  PersistenceIssues
  DeathLoopIssues

  Warnings[]

FAscentiaBossArenaProofReport
  RunId
  BossTag
  ArenaTag
  TestedMoves
  TestedPhaseTransitions
  WallCollisionIssues
  LedgeOrFallIssues
  FogWallIssues
  NavIssues
  ScreenshotPaths[]

EUW_AscentiaBossQualityReport should sit beside the boss move matrix. The move matrix tells Designers what the boss can do; the quality report tells the team whether the boss is readable, fair, persistent, and arena-safe enough to enter a milestone.

  • BP_AscentiaEnemyBase;
  • BP_AscentiaBossBase;
  • BP_AscentiaEncounterZone;
  • BP_AscentiaPatrolRoute;
