#22.13 Tooling opportunities

EUW_AscentiaEnemyDefinitionBrowser:

  • list enemy definitions, families, stats, loot, behavior assets, ability sets,

and budget cost;

  • show resolved family defaults vs enemy overrides;
  • highlight missing animations, cues, BT/BB keys, and loot tables.

EUW_AscentiaEnemyMoveMatrix:

  • display moves by enemy family, range band, angle band, phase, cooldown, cost,

montage, damage window, cue, and counterplay note;

  • show repeat-penalty and utility-scoring weights;
  • preview legal moves at a sample target distance/angle/phase.

EUW_AscentiaEncounterPlaytestLab:

  • spawn encounter definitions in a sandbox cell;
  • simulate rest/reset/save policies;
  • show live budget, role assignment, leash state, and kill/reward outcomes.

EUW_AscentiaPerceptionDebugger:

  • visualize sight cones, hearing radius, last known target, investigation point,

threat score, target switch score, and alert propagation.

EUW_AscentiaBossMoveMatrix:

  • show boss moves per phase with range, angle, cooldown, repeat penalty,

telegraph, active, recovery, punish, root-motion, and cue coverage.

EUW_AscentiaThreatTableDebugger:

  • sort threat entries by target and contribution source;
  • show target switch hysteresis, faction relationship, line of sight, last known

location, and stimulus age;

  • replay one target-selection decision from a proof report.

EUW_AscentiaEncounterResetPolicyPreview:

  • simulate rest, death, reload, boss defeat, and world-state reset for an

encounter definition;

  • show spawned, preserved, despawned, and reward-reset outcomes;
  • flag policies that conflict with POI completion, unique elites, or story boss

persistence.

EUW_AscentiaBossQualityReport:

  • collect move-matrix coverage, arena-motion proofs, phase-transition proofs,

presentation coverage, persistence checks, and death-loop checks into one milestone-facing view;

  • highlight whether a boss is prototype-only, playtest-ready, milestone-ready,

or blocked.

#22.14 P18 evidence, AI reports, and encounter proof

Evidence folders:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/EnemyDefinitions/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/EnemyMoves/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/Encounters/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/Bosses/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/PerceptionEQS/{RunId}/report.json

FAscentiaEnemyDefinitionReport
FAscentiaEnemyMoveMatrixReport

FAscentiaEncounterBudgetReport
FAscentiaEncounterPlaytestReport

bt_asset_ai

enemy_resource
enemy_perception

enemy_eqs_spacing
enemy_move_matrix

Acceptance shape:

P18ProofRun
  RunId
  Map
  BuildConfiguration
  EnemyDefinitionReport
  AIBehaviorReport
  EnemyMoveMatrixReport
  EncounterPlaytestReport
  PerceptionEQSReport
  BossQualityReport
  Proofs[]
  BlockingFailures[]

The Dashboard card should link these reports directly. Designers should be able to open the latest failed proof, click through to the offending DataAsset or Behavior Tree, and see the fix path in plain language.

#22.15 Interconnects

P18 touches:

  • P10/P11 combat feel: poise, stagger, punish windows, boss presentation, fog

walls, combat motion, and BT replacement of tick AI;

  • P12 world scripting: world-state tags, objective triggers, NPC reactions,

vendors, tutorials, and encounter-completion state;

  • P13 damage/build math: enemy resistances, status vulnerabilities, poise,

critical windows, defense formulas, and player build counterplay;

  • P14 weapons: enemy weapon families, ranged enemies, shield behavior, dual-wield

enemies, weapon skills, trails, and hit windows;

  • P15 magic: caster enemies, buff/debuff enemies, summons, magic resistances,

spell targeting, and status payloads;

  • P16 itemization: enemy loot tables, boss drops, elite affixes, reward budgets,

crafting materials, and economy sinks;

  • P17 world: zone budgets, patrol routes, POI encounters, World Partition,

Smart Object collections, nav build, HLOD/streaming behavior, and Data Layers;

  • P19 save/death: boss persistence, enemy respawn, encounter reset, corpse-run

interaction, and death-state cleanup;

  • P20 presentation: telegraphs, impact cues, music state, VO/barks, camera,

damage numbers, corpse presentation, and performance budgets;

  • P21 shell/settings: combat assist presets, enemy aggression/resource pressure

settings if supported, boss intro skip/intensity, HUD readability, and debug proof surfaces;

  • P22 multiplayer: target selection across players/summons, replicated aggro,

authority, prediction, boss phase sync, and anti-cheat-sensitive damage.

#22.16 P18 proof gates

P18 is not complete until:

  1. At least three enemy families resolve from PDA_EnemyDefinition plus stat,

movement, perception, ability, loot, and presentation profiles.

  1. Behavior Tree/Blackboard assets are the primary combat control path for core

enemies; native tick AI fallback is disabled or explicitly reported.

  1. AI Perception drives target acquisition, investigation, damage response, and

target memory without player-0 assumptions.

  1. EQS chooses at least melee attack slots, ranged line-of-sight positions, and

retreat/return points.

  1. Encounter definitions own spawn groups, budget, group roles, leash/reset/save

policy, and reward policy.

  1. Group coordination throttles simultaneous attacks and reserves positions for

at least one multi-enemy encounter.

  1. Enemy moves are data-authored, ability-backed, cost/cooldown-gated, and

validated for montage, damage window, cue, range, and counterplay.

  1. At least one boss uses phase definitions, move matrix rules, music state,

fog-wall policy, boss presentation, and persistent defeat policy.

  1. Smart Objects are validated if used by patrol, ambush, guard, arena, or world

interaction behavior.

  1. Designer tools show resolved enemy definitions, boss move matrices, encounter

budgets, EQS selections, perception state, and validation failures.

  1. Remote proof covers:
  • bt_asset_ai
  • enemy_spawn_resolution
  • enemy_resource
  • enemy_perception
  • perception_threat_table
  • enemy_leash
  • enemy_eqs_spacing
  • enemy_move_matrix
  • encounter_budget
  • encounter_reset_policy
  • encounter_playtest
  • encounter_reset_save
  • group_coordination
  • boss_move_grammar
  • boss_move_matrix
  • boss_quality
  • boss_persistence
  1. Dashboard evidence links the latest enemy definition report, encounter

budget report, encounter playtest report, EQS spacing proof, threat-table proof, boss move matrix, boss quality report, and AI validation panel.

#22.17 P18 starter implementation package

P18 should begin with one complete enemy/encounter/boss vertical slice instead of separate partial systems. The first slice should prove that an enemy definition can resolve into stats, perception, behavior, movement, moves, loot, encounter budget, group coordination, and boss-quality evidence without relying on hidden Blueprint defaults or native-only assumptions.

Reference posture:

  • Behavior Trees should remain the visible combat behavior graph for ordinary

enemies, with Blackboard keys treated as a versioned contract rather than informal variable names.

  • AI Perception should own sensory input and stimulus memory. Player targeting

should come from perceived actors, damage stimuli, threat entries, factions, and target-switch policy, never from "player 0."

  • EQS should answer spatial questions such as attack slots, flank points, ranged

line of sight, retreat points, and leash-return points. C++ can request and validate queries, but the questions should remain Designer-visible as EQS assets.

  • StateTree and Smart Objects are appropriate for ambient, patrol, guard,

investigation, and world-interaction behavior. Combat commitment remains in the BT/GAS/move stack until there is a strong reason to split it.

  • GAS should launch enemy moves for the same reason it launches player moves:

costs, cooldowns, tags, effects, cues, cancellation, authority, and audit traces belong in one ability framework.

  • AI Debugging, EQS Testing Pawn usage, and Ascentia evidence reports should be

part of the normal proof path, not emergency-only tools.

Source/AscentiaTests/Private/AI/AscentiaThreatTableTests.cpp

Tools/remote_playtest.py scenarios: enemy_definition_resolve,
  enemy_perception_threat, enemy_eqs_spacing, encounter_reset_save,
  group_coordination, boss_quality

The first native ownership split should be:

UAscentiaEnemyDefinitionResolver : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Resolves DataAssets/DataTables/family defaults into immutable enemy snapshots.

UAscentiaAIPerceptionBridge : UActorComponent
  Lives on enemy AI controllers or enemy pawns; converts UAIPerceptionComponent
  stimuli, damage memory, faction rules, and authored aggro profiles into a
  threat table and target-selection result.

UAscentiaEncounterSubsystem : UWorldSubsystem
  Owns encounter runtime state, spawn requests, reset requests, save fragments,
  budget reports, and evidence collection.

UAscentiaEncounterGroupComponent : UActorComponent
  Lives on BP_AscentiaEncounterZone; owns role assignment, slot reservation,
  simultaneous attacker caps, group alert, and group proof telemetry.

UAscentiaBossMoveResolver : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Scores boss move candidates from phase, range, angle, cooldown, repeat
  penalty, arena state, and counterplay/readability weights.

Starter content folders:

/Game/Ascentia/Design/AI/Enemies/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/AI/Families/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/AI/Perception/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/AI/EQS/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/AI/Moves/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Encounters/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/Bosses/

/Game/Ascentia/AI/Blackboards/
/Game/Ascentia/AI/EQS/

/Game/Ascentia/Blueprints/AI/

/Game/Ascentia/Blueprints/Bosses/
/Game/Ascentia/Editor/AI/

PDA_EnemyMove_DraugrHeavyAttack_MVP
PDA_EnemyMove_DraugrRetreat_MVP

BP_AscentiaEnemyBase
BP_AscentiaEnemy_Draugr_MVP
BP_AscentiaEnemy_Archer_Prototype
BP_AscentiaEnemy_Caster_Prototype
BP_AscentiaEnemyAIController
BP_AscentiaEncounterZone
BP_AscentiaEnemySpawnMarker
BP_AscentiaBoss_DraugrWarden_MVP
BP_AscentiaBossArenaVolume
BT_Enemy_Combat_MVP

EQS_MeleeAttackSlot_MVP
EQS_RangedLOS_MVP
EQS_ReturnToLeash_MVP
ST_EnemyAmbient_PatrolGuard_MVP

BB_Enemy_Combat_MVP should include a small, explicit contract first:

TargetActor
HomeLocation
EncounterTag
GroupRoleTag
CurrentThreatScore
LastKnownTargetLocation
InvestigationLocation
SelectedMoveTag
SelectedEQSLocation
LeashState
CanCommitAttack
CurrentPhaseTag

FAscentiaBlackboardContract should validate key names, key types, required writers, required readers, and branch ownership. BT_Enemy_Combat_MVP should fail the audit if it reads a key that is not in the contract, if a required key has no writer, or if a branch writes target state directly instead of through the perception/threat bridge.

The first resolver report should be intentionally boring:

FAscentiaEnemyDefinitionReport
  RunId
  EnemyDefinition
  FamilyDefaultsApplied
  StatProfile
  MovementProfile
  PerceptionProfile
  AggroProfile
  FactionProfile
  BehaviorTree
  Blackboard
  AbilitySet
  MoveList
  LootTable
  BudgetCost
  PresentationRefs
  MissingRefs[]
  OverrideTrace[]
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

The first perception/threat proof should include:

FAscentiaThreatEntry
  TargetActor
  TargetTeamTag
  TargetTypeTag
  LastKnownLocation
  LastVisibleTime
  LastDamageTime
  SightScore
  HearingScore
  DamageScore
  ProximityScore
  ScriptedPriorityScore
  FactionScore
  CurrentThreatScore
  DecayRate

FAscentiaTargetSelectionResult
  SelectedTarget
  PreviousTarget
  SwitchReasonTag
  HysteresisApplied
  CandidateEntries
  BlackboardWrites
  Warnings[]

This is the point where multi-summon, future co-op, charmed enemies, decoys, stealth, and scripted aggro stop being scary. They become extra threat entries and target-selection policy, not special cases wired into a Behavior Tree.

The first EQS request/result contract:

FAscentiaEQSRequest
  RequestId
  Querier
  TargetActor
  EncounterTag
  QueryTag
  EQSAsset
  ContextTags
  DesiredRoleTag
  MinDistance
  MaxDistance
  RequireLineOfSight
  ReservationPolicyTag

FAscentiaEQSResult
  RequestId
  QueryTag
  SelectedLocation
  SelectedActor
  Score
  CandidateCount
  RejectionReasons[]
  BlackboardWrites
  Reservation
  DebugSnapshotPath

EQS profiles should define Designer-facing question types, not hidden C++ algorithms:

Ascentia.AI.EQS.MeleeAttackSlot
Ascentia.AI.EQS.FlankSlot
Ascentia.AI.EQS.RangedLineOfSight
Ascentia.AI.EQS.RetreatPoint
Ascentia.AI.EQS.LeashReturn
Ascentia.AI.EQS.AmbushReveal
Ascentia.AI.EQS.BossArenaSafePoint

Encounter runtime should start with one spawn definition and one placed-map definition. This preserves the current preference that the MVP arena should not force-spawn a boss every time PIE begins.

PDA_EncounterDefinition_MVPArena_MVP
  EncounterTag = Ascentia.Encounter.MVP.Arena
  SpawnMode = PlacedActorsPlusOptionalSpawnGroups
  AutoStartPolicy = DisabledInPIEUnlessRequested
  SpawnGroups = DraugrTrashOptional, DraugrWardenPlaced
  Budget = MVP_Arena
  GroupCoordinationProfile = PDA_GroupCoordinationProfile_MVP
  LeashProfile = PDA_EncounterLeashProfile_MVP
  ResetPolicy = RestOrReload
  RewardPolicy = ManualPrototype

Boss quality should be treated as a production report, not just a playtest opinion:

FAscentiaBossQualityReport
  RunId
  BossTag
  ArenaTag
  PhaseCoverage
  MoveCoverage
  TelegraphCoverage
  ActiveDamageWindowCoverage
  RecoveryPunishWindowCoverage
  RootMotionArenaCoverage
  CameraReadabilityIssues
  CueCoverageIssues
  EQSSafePointCoverage
  GroupCoordinationIssues
  PersistenceIssues
  DeathLoopIssues
  ScreenshotPaths[]
  Errors[]
  Warnings[]

Commandlets:

AscentiaEnemyDefinitionAudit
  Inputs: enemy definitions, families, stats, behavior assets, moves, loot.
  Output: Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/EnemyDefinitions/{RunId}/report.json

AscentiaAIBehaviorAudit
  Inputs: Behavior Trees, Blackboards, StateTrees, Smart Objects, EQS assets.
  Output: Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/BehaviorTrees/{RunId}/report.json

AscentiaEncounterAudit
  Inputs: encounter definitions, spawn groups, leash profiles, budgets,
    placed actors, reward policies.
  Output: Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/Encounters/{RunId}/report.json

AscentiaBossQualityAudit
  Inputs: boss definitions, boss move matrices, arenas, EQS profiles, cue
    manifests, montage contracts.
  Output: Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/BossQuality/{RunId}/report.json

Editor Utility Widgets:

EUW_AscentiaEnemyDefinitionBrowser
  Opens a definition and shows resolved family defaults, overrides, behavior
  assets, move list, loot, budget cost, and latest resolver report.

EUW_AscentiaThreatTableDebugger
  Replays one target selection with sight/hearing/damage/faction/scripted
  scores, hysteresis, and Blackboard writes.

EUW_AscentiaEQSPreview
  Runs the selected EQS profile from a chosen enemy/target pair and visualizes
  candidate locations, scores, rejected candidates, and slot reservations.

EUW_AscentiaEncounterResetPolicyPreview
  Simulates rest, death, reload, boss defeat, and world-state reset outcomes
  without entering PIE.

EUW_AscentiaBossQualityReport
  Shows phase/move coverage, telegraph/cue coverage, arena/root-motion issues,
  screenshots, and milestone status.

Proof matrix:

EnemyDefinition_Draugr_Resolve
EnemyDefinition_ThreeFamilies_Resolve
BlackboardContract_MVP_NoMissingKeys
BT_Enemy_Combat_MVP_NoNativeTickFallback
Perception_SightDamageHearing
ThreatTable_NoPlayerZeroAssumption
ThreatTable_TargetSwitchHysteresis
EQS_MeleeSlot
EQS_RangedLOS
EQS_ReturnToLeash
EnemyMove_Draugr_LightAttack
EnemyMove_MissingMontageFailsAudit
EnemySpawn_MVPArena_Group
EncounterBudget_MVPArena
EncounterReset_SaveLoad
Encounter_AutoStartDisabledInPIE
GroupCoordination_ThreeEnemies
BossMoveMatrix_DraugrWarden_Phase1
BossQuality_DraugrWarden_Arena

P18 starter tickets:

  • P18-001: Create enemy definition resolver structs, subsystem, first three

DataAssets, resolver report, and EnemyDefinition_Draugr_Resolve proof.

  • P18-002: Create BB_Enemy_Combat_MVP, BT_Enemy_Combat_MVP,

FAscentiaBlackboardContract, BT/BB validation, and no-native-tick fallback report.

  • P18-003: Add UAscentiaAIPerceptionBridge, threat entries, target-selection

result, sight/hearing/damage stimuli proof, and no-player-0 proof.

  • P18-004: Add EQS profile/request/result structs, three starter EQS assets,

EQS preview widget, and melee/ranged/leash spatial proof.

  • P18-005: Convert enemy moves into data-authored GAS requests with montage,

damage window, cue, cost, cooldown, range, and counterplay validation.

  • P18-006: Add encounter definition, runtime state, spawn request/result, budget

report, and optional-spawn policy for the MVP arena.

  • P18-007: Add leash/reset/save fragments and prove rest, reload, death, and

placed-boss persistence behavior.

  • P18-008: Add group coordination component, attack-slot reservation, role

assignment, simultaneous attacker cap, and three-enemy pressure proof.

  • P18-009: Add boss move resolver, phase runtime state, boss quality profile,

and first Draugr Warden quality report.

  • P18-010: Wire P18 commandlets, dashboard evidence links, packaged smoke, and

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P18/... output folders.

Recommended implementation order:

  1. P18-001 and P18-002 first. The team needs a resolved enemy snapshot and a

visible BT/BB contract before perception, moves, and encounters have a stable target.

  1. P18-003 and P18-004 next. These remove the riskiest AI assumptions:

player-0 targeting and hand-authored spacing.

  1. P18-005 only after the first enemy can perceive and choose where to stand.

A move matrix is less valuable if the target and position inputs are wrong.

  1. P18-006 through P18-008 turn individual enemies into authored encounters.
  2. P18-009 should reuse the same resolver, threat, EQS, move, encounter, and

group contracts. Bosses are a stronger grammar over the same foundations, not a second AI architecture.

  1. P18-010 closes the loop with commandlets, dashboard evidence, and packaged

proof.

#23. Content Data Pipeline, Registries, Import, Validation, and Fallback Debt

#23.1 Player-facing goal

The player never sees the content pipeline directly, but they feel its quality in every item, spell, enemy, boss, quest, reward, and UI tooltip.

The production promise:

  • Designers can create and tune content in spreadsheets, DataTables,

PrimaryDataAssets, Blueprint assets, and Editor tools without C++ changes once the native contract exists;

  • runtime systems consume resolved data, not half-merged fallbacks scattered

through gameplay classes;

  • every row, asset, tag, soft reference, cue, montage, loot entry, and generated

item can be validated before playtest;

  • fallback content keeps the game playable, but fallback debt is visible and

cannot silently ship as production content;

  • data changes can be traced from source sheet/CSV to generated DataTable to

resolved registry record to gameplay/UI proof.

This chapter is the spine under P13-P18. Damage, weapons, spells, itemization, world zones, enemies, bosses, vendors, and rewards all rely on the same data truth.

#23.2 Current Ascentia state

Working pieces:

  • AscentiaTypes.h already defines rich FTableRowBase rows such as

FAscentiaItemDefinitionRow, FAscentiaWeaponTypeRow, FAscentiaAffixRow, FAscentiaSkillDefinitionRow, FAscentiaLootTableRow, FAscentiaCraftingRecipeRow, FAscentiaItemNamePatternRow, and FAscentiaAttributeThresholdRow;

  • UAscentiaItemDefinition, UAscentiaAffixDefinition, and

UAscentiaLootTableAsset are PrimaryDataAssets;

  • UAscentiaSpellDefinition, UAscentiaAbilitySet, and

UAscentiaBossDefinition continue the PrimaryDataAsset pattern for later systems;

  • UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem registers native fallback items/affixes/loot

and scans Asset Manager primary assets;

  • UAscentiaLootSubsystem, UAscentiaInventoryComponent, character visuals,

UI, and combat already ask the registry or resolved item definitions for parts of their truth;

  • the affix-GE engine has a first slice through UAscentiaAffixEffect,

UAscentiaAffixEngineLibrary, and modifier tags;

  • architecture docs already identify Ascentia Data.xlsx, CSV exports, and

generated DataTables as the intended scale path.

Production gaps:

  • design spreadsheet/CSV data still does not fully drive the game;
  • generated DataTables are not yet the active runtime source for the registry;
  • row structs and PrimaryDataAssets are not reconciled into field-level resolved

records;

  • native fallback registry carries too much content authority;
  • source-layer provenance is not exposed to Designers;
  • no tracked Tools/data/ sync command owns xlsx/CSV/DataTable import as one

repeatable pipeline;

  • no content registry report proves fallback, row, asset overlay, soft-reference

health, loot distribution, or cook readiness;

  • validation is described, but not yet formalized as a unified machine-readable

and Designer-readable gate.

#23.3 Production source-of-truth model

Use four layers:

source workbook / sheet
  -> tracked CSV
  -> generated DataTable or CurveTable
  -> resolved registry read model
  -> runtime instance state

Rules:

  • .xlsx is an authoring convenience, not the only source of truth;
  • CSV is the diffable, reviewable source for bulk tabular content;
  • generated DataTables are editor/cook artifacts and must be reproducible;
  • PrimaryDataAssets own object references, soft references, bundles,

presentation, notes, and handcrafted overrides;

  • runtime instances own rolled, saved, player-specific state;
  • gameplay code reads resolved records and runtime instances, not raw CSV or

sheet files.

Unreal supports this shape well: DataTables import CSV into FTableRowBase structs, Asset Manager discovers Primary Assets such as UPrimaryDataAsset, Data Registries formalize ordered source/fallback behavior, and Data Validation provides editor/commandlet gates. Ascentia does not have to adopt every engine plugin immediately, but its local architecture should behave like those systems.

#23.4 Source types and ownership

Source responsibilities:

Tracked CSV
  bulk numbers, tags, row IDs, curve points, loot weights, stat curves

Generated DataTable / CurveTable
  Unreal editor/cook representation of CSV truth

PrimaryDataAsset
  object references, Blueprint classes, meshes, icons, montages, cues,
  Niagara/MetaSound refs, designer notes, asset bundles

Native fallback
  minimum playable smoke-test content only

Runtime instance
  rolled affixes, seed, durability, sockets, save ID, player state

Programmers own:

  • row structs;
  • registry merge code;
  • validation rules;
  • provenance metadata;
  • runtime read models;
  • save-compatible IDs;
  • commandlets/reports.

Technical Designers own:

  • sheet layout;
  • CSV export;
  • GameplayTag taxonomy;
  • import manifests;
  • Editor Utility Widgets;
  • validation triage.

Designers own:

  • row values;
  • PrimaryDataAsset references;
  • Blueprint ability/cue/presentation subclasses;
  • balance curves;
  • content notes;
  • approval state.

#23.5 Registry and merge policy

The local UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem should become the first production resolved registry. Later systems can copy the pattern or share a generalized UAscentiaContentRegistrySubsystem.

Precedence:

native fallback
  < DataTable row
  < PrimaryDataAsset object refs
  < explicit PrimaryDataAsset stat override
  < runtime instance state

Do not let DataAssets silently override numeric rows. Every override must be field-marked or profile-marked.

Resolved record:

FAscentiaResolvedRecordHeader
  ContentTag
  RowName
  PrimaryAssetId
  SourceSetTag
  ContentVersion
  bUsesNativeFallback
  ValidationSeverity
  FieldSources

FAscentiaFieldSource
  FieldName
  SourceLayer: NativeFallback, DataTable, PrimaryAsset, AssetOverride, Runtime
  SourcePath
  SourceRow
  SourceAssetId
  Notes

Item resolved record:

FAscentiaResolvedItemDefinition
  Header
  DisplayName
  Category
  QualityFloor
  EquipmentSlot
  StackLimit
  Weight
  VendorValue
  StatRequirements
  WeaponFamily
  WeaponTypeTag
  MovesetTags
  BaseDamagePackets
  DefenseProfile
  EquipEffects
  GrantedAbilitySet
  Icon
  WeaponMesh
  WornMesh
  GripTransform
  SocketProfile
  SwingTrailSystem
  CueProfile

This pattern should be repeated for spells, enemies, bosses, world zones, POIs, vendors, objectives, and settings.

#23.6 Data import scripts and settings

Required local structure:

Tools/data/
  export_xlsx_to_csv.py
  import_csv_to_datatables.py
  sync_content_data.ps1
  validate_content_sources.py
  report_content_registry.py
  manifests/
    content_import_manifest.json

Unreal project settings:

UAscentiaContentDataSettings
  SourceWorkbookPath
  CsvSourceRoot
  GeneratedDataTableRoot
  ImportManifest
  PrimaryAssetScanRoots
  ValidationProfiles
  EvidenceOutputRoot
  bAllowPrototypeFallbacks
  bFailCookOnFallbackDebt

Import manifest:

FAscentiaContentImportEntry
  SourceName
  CsvPath
  DataTablePath
  RowStruct
  KeyField
  RequiredColumns
  OptionalColumns
  OwningSystemTag
  ValidationProfile

Script workflow:

sync_content_data.ps1
  -> export_xlsx_to_csv.py
  -> validate_content_sources.py
  -> launch UnrealEditor-Cmd import_csv_to_datatables.py
  -> run content registry report
  -> run validation commandlet
  -> write evidence summary

Important rule: when Python touches Unreal assets, use Unreal's Python API and editor asset tools, not raw filesystem moves. Unreal asset files contain object references that are easy to break from outside the editor.

#23.7 Folder and naming contract

Canonical content root:

/Game/Ascentia/Design/
  DataTables/
    DT_Items_Melee
    DT_Items_Armor
    DT_WeaponTypes
    DT_Affixes
    DT_LootTables
    DT_Skills
    DT_Spells
    DT_EnemyStats
    DT_BossMoves
    DT_WorldZones
    DT_Vendors
    DT_Settings
  Items/
    PDA_Item_*
  Affixes/
    PDA_Affix_* only if asset-bearing affixes appear later
  AbilitySets/
    PDA_AbilitySet_*
  Spells/
    PDA_Spell_*
  Enemies/
    PDA_EnemyDefinition_*
  Bosses/
    PDA_BossDefinition_*
  World/
    PDA_WorldZone_*
    PDA_POI_*
  UI/
    PDA_SettingsDefinition_*
  Tools/
    EUW_AscentiaContentSync
    EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser

Naming:

  • DataTables: DT_<System>_<Slice> when a table is broad, e.g.

DT_Items_Melee;

  • PrimaryDataAssets: PDA_<Type>_<Name>;
  • GameplayTags are the stable gameplay IDs;
  • row names are stable import keys and should not be presentation strings;
  • redirects must be tracked when IDs change.

#23.8 Validation layers

Validation must run at four layers.

Structural:

  • CSV headers match manifest;
  • row struct exists;
  • required columns exist;
  • row names are unique and stable;
  • enum/text values parse;
  • numeric values are finite;
  • arrays/tag containers parse.

Reference:

  • GameplayTags exist;
  • PrimaryAsset IDs are registered;
  • soft object paths resolve;
  • Blueprint classes inherit from required bases;
  • DataTable row handles point to existing rows;
  • GameplayCue tags begin with GameplayCue;
  • montage/notify names exist where referenced.

Semantic:

  • item category matches equipment slot;
  • weapon family has moveset and socket profile;
  • spell delivery has targeting and cue profile;
  • enemy move range matches behavior selection bands;
  • boss phase thresholds are sorted;
  • loot tables have nonzero reachable weights;
  • affix pools are reachable by at least one item type;
  • fallback-only content is allowed only in prototype scope.

Runtime proof:

  • registry initializes without missing packages;
  • resolved records expose field provenance;
  • loot roll is deterministic by seed;
  • sample item/spell/enemy/boss definitions resolve through row plus asset;
  • UI displays resolved data;
  • cook/package validation sees no unresolved production references.

#23.9 Reports and evidence

Every content sync writes:

Saved/Automation/Evidence/content_data/<timestamp>/
  report.json
  import_summary.json
  registry_summary.json
  fallback_debt.json
  validation_errors.json
  loot_distribution_samples.json
  resolved_record_samples.json

Report schema:

FAscentiaContentDataReport
  Timestamp
  GitCommit
  ImportManifestVersion
  SourceWorkbookHash
  CsvHashes
  DataTablesImported
  PrimaryAssetsScanned
  ResolvedRecordCountsByType
  FallbackOnlyRecords
  PrototypeAllowedFallbacks
  ValidationErrors
  ValidationWarnings
  SoftReferenceFailures
  CookRiskSummary
  SampleProofs

Fallback debt:

FAscentiaFallbackDebtEntry
  ContentTag
  System
  NativeFallbackSource
  MissingDataTableRow
  MissingPrimaryAsset
  UsedByScenarios
  Severity
  Owner
  TargetPhase

Fallback debt is not a shame list. It is how the team keeps the game playable while seeing exactly what still needs authoring.

#23.10 Designer tools

EUW_AscentiaContentSync:

  • run export/import/validate;
  • show manifest entries;
  • show latest report;
  • open broken rows/assets;
  • compare current report with previous report.

EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser:

  • search by GameplayTag, row, asset, or display name;
  • show resolved field values and provenance;
  • show fallback-only fields;
  • preview item, spell, enemy, boss, vendor, and loot definitions;
  • run sample loot rolls;
  • open the source row or DataAsset.

EUW_AscentiaRegistryDiffViewer:

  • compare two reports;
  • show changed rows, removed rows, changed soft references, changed loot

distributions, changed budget costs, and newly introduced fallback debt.

EUW_AscentiaDataTableSchemaDoctor:

  • check row structs vs CSV headers;
  • show added/removed/renamed fields;
  • produce migration notes for Designers.

EUW_AscentiaFallbackDebtReporter:

  • group fallback debt by phase, system, owner, and severity;
  • separate prototype-allowed fallback from production-blocking fallback.

#23.11 Save compatibility and migrations

Save files should store stable gameplay IDs and runtime instance state, not raw row copies.

Save example:

FAscentiaSavedItemInstance
  InstanceId
  ItemTag
  ItemSeed
  Quality
  PrefixAffixTags
  SuffixAffixTags
  AffixMagnitudes
  SocketedItemTags
  Durability
  ContentVersionSeen

Rules:

  • row values can change between patches;
  • saved instances resolve through current content unless a migration says to

freeze or translate values;

  • removed tags require redirects or migration entries;
  • production content sync should produce a save-migration risk summary;
  • no save field should depend on display text.

Migration manifest:

FAscentiaContentMigrationEntry
  FromTag
  ToTag
  FromVersion
  ToVersion
  MigrationPolicy
  Notes

#23.12 Interconnects

This pipeline touches:

  • P13 damage/build math: damage schools, status payloads, stat curves, passive

thresholds, resistance curves;

  • P14 weapons: weapon type rows, action maps, combo chains, socket profiles,

montage/cue references;

  • P15 magic: spells, schools, cast/delivery profiles, summons, flask upgrades;
  • P16 itemization: items, affixes, loot, crafting, vendors, upgrades, economy;
  • P17 world: zones, POIs, Data Layers, discovery, map markers, world tier;
  • P18 AI: enemy stats, perception profiles, movement profiles, boss moves,

encounter budgets;

  • P19 save/death: stable IDs, content migrations, character identity, death

echo references;

  • P20 presentation: cues, icons, audio, VFX, surface profiles, readability;
  • P21 shell/settings: settings rows, localization text, input glyph rows,

package validation;

  • P22 multiplayer: replicated content IDs, anti-cheat-sensitive data, server

authority, client prediction.

#23.13 Proof gates

The content pipeline is not production-ready until:

  1. Tools/data/sync_content_data.ps1 can run from a clean checkout and produce

CSV/DataTable/import/registry reports.

  1. UAscentiaContentDataSettings owns data source paths and is visible in

Project Settings or an equivalent Editor-facing settings asset.

  1. UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem ingests DataTables between native fallback

and scanned PrimaryDataAssets.

  1. Resolved records expose source-layer provenance per field.
  2. EUW_AscentiaContentSync and EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser expose the

pipeline to Designers.

  1. Data validation catches malformed rows, missing tags, unresolved soft refs,

invalid loot entries, invalid category/slot pairs, missing cue/montage refs, and production fallback debt.

  1. Reports are written under Saved/Automation/Evidence/content_data/....
  2. A remote proof verifies fallback, row, asset overlay, deterministic loot,

inventory UI display, equip effect application, and no missing package warnings.

  1. Save migration risk is reported when tags or row IDs are removed/renamed.
  2. Dashboard links the latest content data report and fallback debt report.

Remote proof names:

content_data_sync
content_registry_resolution
content_fallback_debt
content_validation_gate
loot_distribution_report
save_content_migration

#23.14 P23 starter implementation package

This is not a numbered gameplay phase in the same way as P12-P22, but it should be treated like a production phase because it is the spine underneath all content-heavy systems. The starter package should prove one repeatable path: source workbook or CSV -> generated DataTable -> scanned PrimaryDataAsset -> resolved record -> runtime proof -> Designer-visible evidence.

Reference posture:

  • Use Unreal DataTables and CurveTables for bulk, diffable numeric and row data.

Rows should be FTableRowBase structs with stable row names and stable gameplay identifiers.

  • Use Asset Manager and Primary Assets for object-heavy content, soft-reference

discovery, bundle loading, cook awareness, and eventual chunking rules.

  • Shape local registry behavior around Data Registry principles: ordered

sources, explicit fallback/override policy, sync and async access where needed, and source-order evidence.

  • Use Data Validation for asset and row correctness gates. Validation should be

callable from Editor workflows, commandlets, and package profiles.

  • Use Asset Registry scans for editor-side reports and unloaded asset inspection

instead of loading every asset just to count or classify it.

  • Keep native fallback content small, visible, and phase-approved. Fallback

should keep smoke tests alive, not become the hidden content pipeline.

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentDataSettings.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentSourceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaResolvedRecordTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentRegistrySubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentRegistryReportTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaFallbackDebtTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentMigrationTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Data/AscentiaContentValidationTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/Data/AscentiaContentDataSettings.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Data/AscentiaContentRegistrySubsystem.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Data/AscentiaResolvedRecordTypes.cpp
Source/Ascentia/Private/Data/AscentiaContentRegistryReports.cpp

Editor module layout:

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaContentImportManifest.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaContentSourceValidator.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaDataTableSchemaValidator.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaPrimaryAssetReferenceValidator.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Data/AscentiaFallbackDebtValidator.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaContentImportManifest.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaContentSourceValidator.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaDataTableSchemaValidator.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaPrimaryAssetReferenceValidator.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Data/AscentiaFallbackDebtValidator.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaContentDataSyncCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaContentRegistryAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaFallbackDebtAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaSaveContentMigrationAuditCommandlet.cpp

Tooling layout:

Tools/data/export_xlsx_to_csv.py
Tools/data/validate_content_sources.py
Tools/data/import_csv_to_datatables.py
Tools/data/report_content_registry.py
Tools/data/report_fallback_debt.py
Tools/data/sync_content_data.ps1
Tools/data/manifests/content_import_manifest.json
Tools/data/manifests/content_source_manifest.json
Tools/data/manifests/content_migration_manifest.json
Tools/data/samples/Ascentia_Data_Sample.xlsx
Tools/data/samples/csv/items_mvp.csv
Tools/data/samples/csv/weapon_types_mvp.csv
Tools/data/samples/csv/loot_tables_mvp.csv

Tests and proof helpers:

Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaContentDataSettingsTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaResolvedRecordTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaContentRegistryTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaFallbackDebtTests.cpp
Source/AscentiaTests/Private/Data/AscentiaContentMigrationTests.cpp
Tools/remote_playtest.py scenarios: content_data_sync,
  content_registry_resolution, content_fallback_debt, content_validation_gate,
  loot_distribution_report, save_content_migration

The first runtime ownership split:

UAscentiaContentDataSettings : UDeveloperSettings
  Stores source roots, generated DataTable roots, scan roots, manifests,
  validation profiles, evidence roots, and fallback policy.

UAscentiaContentRegistrySubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  Owns resolved read models for shared content families and exposes typed lookup
  by GameplayTag, row name, and PrimaryAssetId. It can delegate item-specific
  behavior to the existing item registry until the shared registry is proven.

UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem
  Keeps gameplay-facing item APIs but changes its source order to native
  fallback -> DataTable rows -> PrimaryDataAsset refs/marked overrides ->
  runtime instance. It writes item registry and fallback debt reports.

UAscentiaContentValidationLibrary : UBlueprintFunctionLibrary
  Exposes read-only validation helpers for EUWs and commandlets.

Starter Project Settings:

UAscentiaContentDataSettings
  SourceWorkbookPath = SourceData/Ascentia Data.xlsx
  CsvSourceRoot = SourceData/CSV
  GeneratedDataTableRoot = /Game/Ascentia/Design/DataTables
  ImportManifest = Tools/data/manifests/content_import_manifest.json
  SourceManifest = Tools/data/manifests/content_source_manifest.json
  MigrationManifest = Tools/data/manifests/content_migration_manifest.json
  PrimaryAssetScanRoots =
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/Items
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/Spells
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/AI
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/Encounters
    /Game/Ascentia/Design/Bosses
  EvidenceOutputRoot = Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/ContentData
  bAllowPrototypeFallbacks = true
  bFailCookOnFallbackDebt = false for Editor, true for Shipping profiles

Starter manifest entries:

items_mvp
  CsvPath = SourceData/CSV/items_mvp.csv

AscentiaContentDataSync
  -Manifest=Tools/data/manifests/content_import_manifest.json

Editor Utility Widgets:

EUW_AscentiaContentSync
  Buttons: Dry Run, Import CSV, Rebuild Registry Preview, Run Validation,
  Open Latest Report. Shows manifest rows, changed sources, generated assets,
  and failures with click-through links.

EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser
  Search by tag, row, asset, display name, or owning phase. Shows field values,
  source layer, fallback state, soft refs, preview icon/mesh/cue, and sample
  runtime lookup.

EUW_AscentiaRegistryDiffViewer
  Compares two registry reports. Shows changed resolved values, changed source
  layers, new fallback debt, removed records, and loot distribution deltas.

EUW_AscentiaDataTableSchemaDoctor
  Compares CSV headers, manifest columns, row structs, and DataTable assets.
  Emits migration notes and blocks silent column drift.

EUW_AscentiaFallbackDebtReporter
  Groups fallback by system, phase, owner, severity, and proof dependency.
  Separates prototype-approved debt from package-blocking debt.

Validation profiles:

PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Items_MVP
  RequiredTags: ItemTag, CategoryTag
  RequiredRefs: icon optional in prototype, mesh required for equipment
  SemanticRules: category/slot compatibility, stack limit, value, weight
  FallbackPolicy: prototype fallback allowed only for MVP smoke items

PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Loot_MVP
  RequiredTags: LootTableTag
  SemanticRules: reachable weights, no empty production table, deterministic
    sample rolls by seed, reward value envelope

PDA_ContentValidationProfile_Weapons_MVP
  RequiredTags: WeaponTypeTag, WeaponFamilyTag
  RequiredRefs: action map, socket profile, cue profile, visible weapon mesh

Proof matrix:

ContentData_Settings_Load
ContentData_Manifest_Parse
ContentData_CSV_Header_Validation
ContentData_Import_DryRun
ContentRegistry_Item_Row_Resolve
ContentRegistry_Item_AssetOverlay_Resolve
ContentRegistry_FieldSource_Trace
ContentRegistry_Fallback_Debt_Report
ContentRegistry_NoMissingPrimaryAssets
ContentRegistry_NoMissingGameplayTags
LootDistribution_MVP_SeededSample
DataTableSchema_Items_MVP
RegistryDiff_RowChange_Detected
SaveContentMigration_RemovedTag_Risk
PackageProfile_FailOnProductionFallbackDebt

P23 starter tickets:

  • P23-001: Add UAscentiaContentDataSettings, config defaults, Project

Settings visibility, and settings snapshot report.

  • P23-002: Add content import/source/migration manifests and Tools/data

dry-run validation scripts.

  • P23-003: Add shared resolved-record header, field-source trace, lookup

request/result, and registry report structs.

  • P23-004: Teach UAscentiaItemRegistrySubsystem to ingest MVP DataTables

between native fallbacks and PrimaryDataAssets while preserving current playable behavior.

  • P23-005: Add fallback debt structs, report writer, fallback policy asset, and

AscentiaFallbackDebtAudit.

  • P23-006: Add AscentiaContentRegistryAudit with field provenance samples,

soft-reference checks, GameplayTag checks, and cook-risk summary.

  • P23-007: Add EUW_AscentiaContentSync and EUW_AscentiaResolvedContentBrowser

with read-only preview first, mutation buttons disabled until dry-run reports are trusted.

  • P23-008: Add DataTable schema validator and registry diff viewer.
  • P23-009: Add save-content migration risk audit using P19 save fixtures and

content redirect rows.

  • P23-010: Wire content data, registry, fallback debt, and migration reports into

dashboard evidence and package profiles.

Recommended implementation order:

  1. P23-001 through P23-003 first. Settings, manifests, and resolved-record

traces are the contract that every content family will reuse.

  1. P23-004 next, using items because Ascentia already has native fallback items,

PrimaryDataAssets, loot, inventory UI, and package proofs.

  1. P23-005 and P23-006 then make fallback debt and registry health visible.
  2. P23-007 and P23-008 expose the system to Designers without granting unsafe

write buttons too early.

  1. P23-009 should run before broad save/death-loop work because content ID

removal and redirects are save compatibility problems.

  1. P23-010 closes the loop with dashboard/package evidence.

#23.15 Art asset acceptance, material, LOD, collision, and kit production package

P23 also needs a gate for non-tabular content. A sword mesh, armor piece, ruined-wall kit, foliage cluster, landscape stamp, VFX prop, or marketplace pack can look useful while still being unsafe for production: wrong folder, unknown license, bad pivot, no collision, wrong socket, unbounded material cost, missing physical surface, broken LOD/Nanite policy, no Data Layer/HLOD assignment, or no proof that it cooks.

This package defines how art assets graduate:

external source / generated output / hand-authored DCC export
  -> staging package
  -> provenance record
  -> normalization pass
  -> visual/material/collision/LOD validation
  -> Designer acceptance
  -> production folder promotion
  -> registry/cook/evidence proof

Engine-facing posture:

  • use Nanite for appropriate high-detail static geometry, but still record

fallback, material, collision, foliage, and platform policies;

  • use explicit Static Mesh LODs or UE automatic LOD generation where Nanite is

not appropriate;

  • use custom UCX collision for meshes with gameplay-important openings, ledges,

traversal, or concavity;

  • use sockets and pivots as authored gameplay data, not incidental DCC leftovers;
  • use parameterized parent materials and Material Instances for art-direction

variation without recompiling or forking parent shaders;

  • keep material/weather/physical-surface response connected to P17 world, P20

presentation, P14 weapons, P16 itemization, and P18 enemy/world interaction.

Runtime/editor files:

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/Art/
  AscentiaArtAssetTypes.h
  AscentiaArtAcceptanceProfile.h
  AscentiaMeshPolicyTypes.h
  AscentiaMaterialPolicyTypes.h
  AscentiaKitPolicyTypes.h
  AscentiaArtAcceptanceValidator.h
  AscentiaArtAcceptanceReportTypes.h

  PrototypeApprovedCount

Promotion stages:

Staging
  raw imported/generated/marketplace content, no gameplay references allowed

Normalized
  renamed, moved under staging taxonomy, source/provenance attached, redirectors
  fixed, obvious import errors resolved

ValidatedPrototype
  usable in prototype maps and smoke tests; warnings allowed when owner approved

ProductionCandidate
  passes acceptance profile except final human art direction verdict

ProductionAccepted
  promoted to production folder, registry/provenance report written, cook proof
  exists, and dashboard card links latest evidence

Policy rules:

  • gameplay systems may not hard-reference Art/Staging assets;
  • generated or marketplace assets must carry a source record before promotion;
  • every accepted Static Mesh has an explicit Nanite/LOD decision, not an

inherited default;

  • traversal, camera-blocking, projectile-blocking, weapon-hit, and pickup assets

must use named collision policy;

  • modular kit pieces must declare grid, pivot, connector, snap, and collision

continuity policy;

  • weapons and armor must declare socket/mount/fit preview policy before they can

be used by item definitions;

  • parent materials live in approved parent folders; production variants should

usually be Material Instances;

  • physical material/surface response must be declared for walkable, hittable, or

impactable surfaces;

  • UDS/weather-responsive assets must declare wetness/snow/dust response or opt

out with an explicit reason;

  • every promotion writes a dry-run plan before moving or mutating assets.

Designer-facing tools:

EUW_AscentiaArtAcceptanceBoard
  Lists staging assets, source records, acceptance stage, owner system, blocking
  findings, human verdict, and promotion eligibility.

EUW_AscentiaPackIngestor
  Imports or registers external packs into staging, attaches provenance, scans
  redirectors/dependencies, and refuses production references.

EUW_AscentiaMeshPolicyDoctor
  Shows Nanite/LOD decision, material slots, collision policy, sockets, pivot,
  bounds, scale, Data Layer/HLOD readiness, and cook warnings.

EUW_AscentiaMaterialSurfaceLab
  Previews material instances under clear, dusk, rain, snow, torch, hit impact,
  footstep, and blood/dust contexts.

EUW_AscentiaKitAssemblyPreview
  Snaps modular pieces on the kit grid, checks connectors, collision continuity,
  nav continuity, camera passage, and silhouette repetition.

EUW_AscentiaArtPromotionPlanner
  Builds a transaction-aware dry-run move/rename/fixup plan from staging to
  production folders with source-control and redirector warnings.

Commandlet and script contracts:

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaArtAcceptanceAudit
  -SourceRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Art/Staging
  -Profile=/Game/Ascentia/Art/Validation/PDA_ArtAcceptanceProfile_MVPWorld
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/ArtAcceptance

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaMeshCollisionLODAudit
  -AssetRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Art/Meshes
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/MeshPolicy

UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe Ascentia.uproject -run=AscentiaMaterialSurfaceAudit
  -MaterialRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Art/Materials
  -WeatherProfiles=Clear,Rain,Snow,Night
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/MaterialSurface

Tools/art/ascentia_art_acceptance.ps1
  -SourceRoot /Game/Ascentia/Art/Staging
  -Profile MVPWorld
  -DryRun

Implementation tickets:

P23A-001 Art source/provenance record
  Add `FAscentiaArtSourceRecord`, staging manifest, source hash, license/vendor
  note, and source record validation.

P23A-002 Art acceptance profiles and stage model
  Add `PDA_ArtAcceptanceProfile_*`, asset stage enum, owner system tags,
  promotion blockers, and human verdict fields.

P23A-003 Mesh policy audit
  Add Nanite/LOD decision checks, material slot limits, socket/pivot checks,
  collision policy checks, Data Layer/HLOD readiness, and mesh report output.

P23A-004 Material surface audit
  Add parent material/Material Instance policy, required parameter checks,
  physical material coverage, weather response preview, and surface report.

P23A-005 Modular kit assembly preview
  Add kit policy, grid/snap/pivot/connector validation, nav/collision continuity
  proof, and screenshot matrix.

P23A-006 Weapon/armor fit acceptance bridge
  Connect accepted weapon/armor meshes to P14 weapon mount lab and P19 character
  creator fit/physics preview before item definitions can use them.

P23A-007 Promotion planner
  Add dry-run move/rename/fixup plans, source-control warnings, redirector
  checks, and transaction-aware approved promotion.

P23A-008 Dashboard and package gate
  Add art acceptance cards, staging-reference blockers, production-blocking
  finding counts, and package-profile failure on unsafe production references.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build P23A-001 and P23A-002 before importing more external packs.
  2. Add P23A-003 next because collision/LOD/Nanite mistakes become gameplay and

performance bugs quickly.

  1. Add P23A-004 before UDS/weather adoption spreads material variants.
  2. Add P23A-005 when the first modular zone kit starts replacing placeholder

arena geometry.

  1. Add P23A-006 before Asset Factory armor/weapon output becomes default gear.
  2. Add P23A-007 and P23A-008 once the dry-run reports are trusted.

#23.16 Surface response, weather material, footstep, impact, and hazard package

This package turns the P23 material-surface audit into a runtime production contract. The art gate can prove that an imported asset has an acceptable Physical Material and Material Instance policy, but the shipped game also needs the consequences of that surface to be consistent: footsteps, weapon impacts, projectile puffs, decals, snow, wetness, dust, elemental status, kill volumes, rot pools, accessibility variants, and PCG/Landscry surface provenance should all resolve through the same authored profile layer.

Primary references:

  • Unreal Physical Materials and Surface Types:

<https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/physical-materials-user-guide-for-unreal-engine>

  • Material Parameter Collections for global wetness/snow/destruction-style

parameters: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/using-material-parameter-collections-in-unreal-engine>

  • Decal Materials for projected impact/blood/scorch responses:

<https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/decal-materials-in-unreal-engine>

  • Niagara as the VFX authoring/runtime layer for footsteps, impacts, dust, rain,

snow, and hazard bursts: <https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/overview-of-niagara-effects-for-unreal-engine>

The rule: gameplay never asks "what sound/decal/particle should stone play?" directly. Gameplay asks the surface response layer with a context payload. The surface response layer resolves profile data, sends presentation requests to P20, sends hazard/status damage through P13, updates global weather material state through the P17/P20 atmosphere bridge, and writes evidence.

Runtime code:

Source/Ascentia/Public/Surfaces/AscentiaSurfaceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Surfaces/AscentiaSurfaceResponseSubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Surfaces/AscentiaFootstepTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Surfaces/AscentiaImpactResponseTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/Surfaces/AscentiaEnvironmentalHazardTypes.h

FAscentiaSurfaceId

  BiomeTags

  AccessibilityIntensityGroup

FAscentiaImpactResponse
  SurfaceTag
  ImpactKind
  WeaponFamilyTag
  DamageSchoolTag

  DecalMaterial
  DecalLifetime
  HapticProfile
  bCanSpawnBlood
  bCanLeavePersistentMark

FAscentiaWeatherMaterialResponse
  BiomeTag
  SurfaceTag
  WeatherStateTag
  MaterialParameterCollection
  ScalarParameters
  VectorParameters
  RequiredMaterialParameters
  TransitionSeconds
  ScreenshotFixture

FAscentiaEnvironmentalHazardProfile
  HazardTag
  SurfaceTag
  VolumeClass
  DamageSchoolTag
  StatusPayloadTag
  TickInterval
  EntryCue
  PersistentCue
  ExitCue
  SavePolicy
  AIPathingPolicy
  AccessibilityPolicy

FAscentiaHazardDamageRequest
  Instigator
  Victim
  HazardTag
  DamageSchoolTag
  StatusPayloadTag
  Magnitude
  SurfaceTag
  SourceActor
  AuthorityContext

FAscentiaSurfaceResponseReport
  SurfacesExpected
  SurfacesFound
  MissingPhysicalMaterials
  MissingFootstepProfiles
  MissingImpactProfiles
  MissingWeatherResponses
  MissingHazardDamageRoutes
  MissingAccessibilityProfiles
  PCGDefaultSurfaceLeaks
  BlockingFindings

Runtime policy:

  • UAscentiaSurfaceResponseSubsystem is the facade. Animation notifies,

collision hits, projectile hits, traversal probes, footstep events, and hazard volumes call it with a context struct.

  • SurfaceType_Default is never valid for production world geometry unless the

profile explicitly marks the asset as silent/decorative.

  • Physical Materials map to Gameplay Tags in PDA_PhysicalSurfaceMap_MVP; code

should not switch directly on raw enum values outside the resolver.

  • Footstep and impact requests return presentation requests, not raw Niagara or

audio spawns. P20 then applies concurrency, settings, accessibility intensity, and budget policy.

  • Hazard damage goes through the P13 damage packet/status pipeline. Hazard

volumes cannot mutate health directly.

  • Weather and biome changes set authored parameters on Material Parameter

Collections or approved per-instance overrides; freeform material parameter mutation is rejected by the audit.

  • PCG/Landscry imported surfaces must retain provenance and surface coverage so

generated terrain does not silently fall back to generic stone.

  • AI pathing can read hazard policy as cost/blocked/ignored, but damage remains

authority-owned gameplay.

  • Accessibility variants can suppress decals, reduce particle burst size, lower

flash intensity, and provide clearer audio/visual substitutes without changing combat math.

Designer surfaces:

EUW_AscentiaSurfaceResponseLab
  Shows Physical Material -> SurfaceTag -> response profile mapping, missing
  coverage, preview fixtures, and blocking package findings.

EUW_AscentiaFootstepAndImpactMatrix
  Previews walk/run/roll/land footsteps and weapon/projectile impacts across
  Stone, Dirt, Mud, Wood, Metal, Water, Flesh, Rot, and Snow surfaces.

EUW_AscentiaWeatherMaterialResponseLab
  Previews rain, snow, ash, and interior/dry transitions through Material
  Parameter Collections, screenshot fixtures, and required material parameters.

EUW_AscentiaHazardScenarioLab
  Runs rot pool, fall kill volume, fire patch, poison cloud, and Below hazard
  fixtures through P13 damage/status and P20 presentation requests.

Evidence:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Surfaces/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Footsteps/{RunId}/matrix.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Impacts/{RunId}/matrix.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/WeatherMaterials/{RunId}/report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Hazards/{RunId}/scenario_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Surfaces/{RunId}/screenshots/
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Surfaces/{RunId}/assets.csv

Proof gates:

Surface_PhysicalMaterial_ToGameplayTagCoverage
Footstep_StoneMudWetSnow_AudioVFX
Impact_SlashStone_DecalNiagaraAudio
Impact_SlashFlesh_BloodPolicy
Weather_RainSnow_MaterialParameterCoverage
Hazard_RotPool_DamagePacketAndStatus
Hazard_FallKillVolume_DeathLoopAnchor
PCG_SurfaceCoverage_NoDefaultPhysicalMaterial
Accessibility_LowFlashSurfaceEffects

Implementation tickets:

P23S-001 Surface id/profile structs and physical material map
  Add `FAscentiaSurfaceId`, physical surface map asset, Gameplay Tag mapping,
  and validation that production assets do not leak default surfaces.

P23S-002 Surface response subsystem facade
  Add `UAscentiaSurfaceResponseSubsystem`, footstep/impact/hazard request and
  result structs, Blueprint-callable preview functions, and P20/P13 adapters.

P23S-003 Footstep and impact matrix first slice
  Add Stone, Dirt, Mud, Wood, Metal, Flesh, Water, Rot, and Snow coverage for
  walk/run/roll/land footsteps plus slash/projectile impact fixtures.

P23S-004 Weather material response bridge
  Add Material Parameter Collection profile routing for rain, snow, ash, and dry
  transitions, plus screenshot/evidence fixtures for UDS/weather profiles.

P23S-005 Environmental hazard profile and damage bridge
  Add rot pool and fall kill volume profiles, P13 damage/status routing,
  AI pathing policy, save/load policy, and death-loop anchor checks.

P23S-006 Designer labs and audit commandlets
  Add Surface Response Lab, Footstep And Impact Matrix, Weather Material
  Response Lab, Hazard Scenario Lab, and commandlet reports.

P23S-007 Dashboard and package gates
  Add dashboard cards and package blockers for missing response profiles,
  default surface leaks, invalid hazard damage routes, and missing accessibility
  variants.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build P23S-001 before expanding Landscry/PCG terrain output.
  2. Build P23S-002 and P23S-003 before adding more weapon families or footstep

notifies.

  1. Build P23S-004 before UDS/weather presets become zone defaults.
  2. Build P23S-005 before The Below or open-world hazards become progression

blockers.

  1. Build P23S-006 and P23S-007 once the first matrix can be trusted by artists,

designers, and QA.

#23.17 Third-party asset, plugin, license, provenance, and upgrade governance package

Ascentia will use third-party assets and tools. That is not a weakness; it is normal production leverage. The risk is letting those packs become invisible architecture. Game Animation Sample, Essential Bow, AssassinGirl, Ultra Dynamic Sky, marketplace animation packs, sound libraries, generated meshes, UI skins, Fab art kits, plugins, and future mocap/audio/vendor content need explicit intake, license notes, technical risk, replacement plans, and upgrade evidence.

This section is production governance, not legal advice. The binding license is always the actual vendor/license text and purchase record. The Ascentia rule is that no external asset or plugin reaches production without a visible source record, approved use case, technical audit, license summary, and owner.

Primary references:

  • Fab's Standard License summary says assets may be used commercially or

privately, modified into projects, commercially distributed as incorporated project content, used with compatible tools, and shared with collaborators on the project; it also says assets may not be redistributed standalone. https://www.fab.com/eula

  • Fab license documentation lists Creative Commons Attribution and Standard

license types, explains Personal/Professional tiers, and calls out the NoAI meta tag conflict with CC-BY distribution. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/fab/licenses-and-pricing-in-fab

  • Unreal plugins are discovered from .uplugin descriptor files, and plugins

with Source folders are compiled by UnrealBuildTool with the game project. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/plugins-in-unreal-engine

  • Unreal modules are the basic code organization unit for editor tools,

runtime features, libraries, and other functionality. Plugin and module boundaries should therefore be treated as production architecture, not just install state. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/unreal-engine-modules

  • FPluginDescriptor contains plugin metadata such as version, engine version,

modules, marketplace URL, docs URL, support URL, target platforms, content support, and dependency plugin references. Ascentia can audit these fields. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/API/Runtime/Projects/FPluginDescriptor

  • FModuleDescriptor exposes module compatibility, build/load checks, loading

phases, and module read/write/update behavior. Module drift is a real package and editor-startup risk. https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/unreal-engine/API/Runtime/Projects/FModuleDescriptor

Runtime module layout:

Source/Ascentia/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaExternalContentTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaVendorLicenseTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaPluginRiskTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaContentProvenanceTypes.h
Source/Ascentia/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaExternalContentRegistrySubsystem.h
Source/Ascentia/Private/ContentGovernance/AscentiaExternalContentRegistrySubsystem.cpp

Editor module layout:

Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaExternalContentAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaLicenseComplianceAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaPluginDescriptorAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaExternalUpgradeAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Public/ContentGovernance/AscentiaAttributionAudit.h
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/ContentGovernance/AscentiaExternalContentAudit.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaExternalContentAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaLicenseComplianceAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaPluginDescriptorAuditCommandlet.cpp
Source/AscentiaEditor/Private/Commandlets/AscentiaExternalUpgradeAuditCommandlet.cpp

Starter folders:

/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/Sources/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/Licenses/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/Plugins/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/Approvals/
/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/UpgradePlans/
/Game/Ascentia/Editor/ContentGovernance/
/Content_Staging/External/
/Content_Staging/Generated/
/Content_Staging/Quarantine/

Starter assets:

PDA_ExternalContentRegistry_MVP
PDA_LicensePolicy_FabStandard_MVP
PDA_LicensePolicy_CC_BY_MVP
PDA_LicensePolicy_InternalGenerated_MVP
PDA_ThirdPartyPluginProfile_GASP
PDA_ThirdPartyPluginProfile_EssentialBow
PDA_ThirdPartyPluginProfile_UltraDynamicSky
PDA_ThirdPartyPluginProfile_CommonUI
PDA_ExternalContentApprovalPolicy_MVP
PDA_ExternalUpgradePolicy_MVP
PDA_AttributionCreditPolicy_MVP
PDA_NoAIAndGenAIUsePolicy_MVP

Core data:

FAscentiaExternalContentSource
  SourceId
  SourceDisplayName
  SourceKind: Fab, Marketplace, EpicSample, VendorPlugin, OpenSource,
    Contractor, InternalGenerated, Mocap, AudioLibrary, ReferenceOnly
  MarketplaceUrl
  VendorUrl
  PurchaseAccountOrSeat
  PurchaseDate
  VersionOrDownloadDate
  OriginalRootPath
  StagingRootPath
  ProductionRootPath
  OwningDiscipline
  OwnerUser
  LicensePolicy
  ApprovalState
  bAllowedInShipping
  bAllowedInSourceControl
  bAllowedInPublicScreenshots
  bAllowedForGenerativeAITraining
  Notes

FAscentiaLicensePolicy
  LicenseTag
  LicenseDisplayName
  LicenseSourceUrl
  LicenseSummary
  RequiredProofFiles
  RequiresAttribution
  RequiresCreditLine
  AllowsCommercialProjectUse
  AllowsModification
  AllowsCollaboratorSharing
  AllowsSourceRepositoryStorage
  AllowsStandaloneRedistribution
  AllowsGeneratedDerivativeUse
  NoAIRestriction
  ReferenceOnlyRestriction
  ReviewRequiredBeforeShipping

FAscentiaExternalAssetProvenance
  AssetPath
  SourceId
  SourcePackagePath
  ImportedAtUtc
  ImportedBy
  TransformPipelineTag
  DerivedFromAssetPaths
  LicenseTag
  AcceptanceState
  ReplacementPlanTag
  bModified
  bGeneratedDerivative
  bContainsHumanLikenessOrVoice
  bContainsTrademarkOrLogo
  bContainsMusicOrLicensedAudio
  bRequiresCredit
  EvidencePath

FAscentiaThirdPartyPluginProfile
  PluginName
  PluginDescriptorPath
  PluginKind: Engine, Project, Marketplace, Fab, EpicSample, Internal,
    ExperimentalEngine, ExternalSource
  RuntimeRisk: None, Low, Medium, High, Blocker
  EditorRisk: None, Low, Medium, High, Blocker
  ShippingPolicy
  ExpectedEngineVersion
  ExpectedPluginVersion
  ExpectedModules
  ExpectedDependencies
  ExpectedTargetPlatforms
  MarketplaceUrl
  DocsUrl
  SupportUrl
  AdapterClass
  ReplacementPlan
  bCanBeDisabledWithoutBreakingCook
  bRawProviderUsageAllowed

FAscentiaExternalContentApproval
  ApprovalId
  SourceId
  RequestedUse
  SystemTags
  ProductionAssetRoots
  Reviewer
  ApprovedAtUtc
  ApprovalState
  BlockingReasons
  RequiredFollowUpTickets
  ExpirationPolicy

FAscentiaExternalUpgradePlan
  SourceId
  CurrentVersion
  TargetVersion
  UpgradeReason
  ExpectedChangedAssets
  ExpectedChangedModules
  RequiredRegressionScenarios
  RequiredRollbackPlan
  bRequiresEngineRestart
  bRequiresFullRecook
  bRequiresSaveMigration
  bRequiresLicenseRecheck

Runtime boundary:

UAscentiaExternalContentRegistrySubsystem : UGameInstanceSubsystem
  ResolveExternalContentSource(SourceId)
  QueryAssetProvenance(AssetPath)
  QueryPluginRisk(PluginName)
  IsExternalContentAllowedInBuild(SourceId, BuildProfile)
  EmitExternalContentEvidence(Result)

Rules:

  • External content starts in staging or quarantine, not /Game/Ascentia

production folders.

  • Every promoted asset has a source record. "Downloaded from somewhere" is a

blocker.

  • The tool records a license summary for production visibility, but the source

license text/purchase record remains the authority.

  • Fab Standard assets can be incorporated into the game under the license, but

Ascentia must never expose raw/source assets as standalone redistributable content.

  • CC-BY content requires attribution planning. Do not mix CC-BY, NoAI, human

likeness, voice, logo, or music assets into production without explicit policy rows.

  • Reference-only assets cannot be treated as source-format art, retargeting

source, mesh kit input, or generated derivative source unless the license row says the actual source format is available.

  • Plugin descriptors and module descriptors are scanned on every package gate.

Engine-version drift, missing modules, unsupported target platforms, and unexpected runtime dependencies are blockers or explicit waivers.

  • Raw third-party actors should be wrapped by Ascentia profiles/adapters before

gameplay systems depend on them. UDS is a weather provider, not the weather architecture; GASP is an animation provider, not the combat architecture.

  • Upgrades are scenario-driven. A plugin or asset pack update needs expected

changed files, regression scenarios, rollback stance, and owner sign-off.

  • Contractor or generated content needs the same provenance records as Fab

content, plus human-likeness/voice/trademark/music fields when relevant.

  • Credits and attribution are not a last-week task. The policy writes credit

line requirements as soon as content is accepted.

Designer tools:

EUW_AscentiaExternalContentRegistry
EUW_AscentiaLicenseComplianceBoard
EUW_AscentiaPluginDescriptorDoctor
EUW_AscentiaExternalAssetQuarantine
EUW_AscentiaExternalUpgradePlanner
EUW_AscentiaAttributionAndCreditsBoard
EUW_AscentiaNoAIAndGeneratedContentAudit
EUW_AscentiaExternalContentPromotionGate

EUW_AscentiaExternalContentRegistry shows source rows, license policy, purchase/version date, staging roots, production roots, owner, approval state, and current shipped usage.

EUW_AscentiaLicenseComplianceBoard compares license policy to asset usage: commercial project use, modification, collaborator sharing, source repository storage, standalone redistribution risk, attribution, NoAI, reference-only restriction, and shipping review state.

EUW_AscentiaPluginDescriptorDoctor reads .uplugin descriptors and expected plugin profiles, then reports version drift, missing modules, unsupported target platforms, unexpected dependencies, docs/support URLs, raw provider use, and adapter coverage.

EUW_AscentiaExternalAssetQuarantine previews newly imported folders before promotion: naming, folder layout, asset class, skeleton/material/LOD/collision policy, license state, duplicate source, and dangerous references.

EUW_AscentiaExternalUpgradePlanner compares current and candidate external versions and links required regression scenarios: GASP locomotion, Essential Bow roll direction, UDS weather profiles, CommonUI shell, PCG/Landscry import, Asset Factory mesh fit, and packaging.

Commandlets:

AscentiaExternalContentAudit
  -Registry=/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/PDA_ExternalContentRegistry_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/ExternalContent

AscentiaLicenseComplianceAudit
  -Registry=/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/PDA_ExternalContentRegistry_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/LicenseCompliance

AscentiaPluginDescriptorAudit
  -ProfileRoot=/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/Plugins
  -PluginRoot=Plugins
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/PluginDescriptors

AscentiaExternalUpgradeAudit
  -UpgradePolicy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/PDA_ExternalUpgradePolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/ExternalUpgrades

AscentiaAttributionAudit
  -Policy=/Game/Ascentia/Design/ContentGovernance/PDA_AttributionCreditPolicy_MVP
  -ReportRoot=Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Attribution

Evidence:

Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/ExternalContent/{RunId}/source_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/LicenseCompliance/{RunId}/license_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/PluginDescriptors/{RunId}/plugin_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/ExternalQuarantine/{RunId}/quarantine_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/ExternalUpgrades/{RunId}/upgrade_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/Attribution/{RunId}/credits_report.json
Saved/AscentiaEvidence/P23/GeneratedContent/{RunId}/generated_content_report.json

Proof matrix:

ExternalContent_AllProductionAssetsHaveSource
ExternalContent_StagingOnlyBeforeApproval
License_FabStandard_NoStandaloneRedistributionRisk
License_CCBY_AttributionRowsGenerated
License_ReferenceOnly_NotUsedAsSourceFormat
PluginDescriptor_GASP_ProfileMatchesDescriptor
PluginDescriptor_UDS_ProfileMatchesDescriptor
PluginDescriptor_TargetPlatformsSupported
PluginDescriptor_RuntimeDependencyWaivers
Upgrade_EssentialBow_RollRegressionRequired
Upgrade_GASP_LocomotionRegressionRequired
Upgrade_UDS_WeatherScenarioRequired
Attribution_CreditsGeneratedFromPolicy
NoAI_GeneratedContentPolicyReviewed

P23V starter tickets:

  • P23V-001: Add external content source, license policy, provenance, plugin

profile, approval, and upgrade-plan structs.

  • P23V-002: Add External Content Registry and License Compliance Board with

Fab Standard, CC-BY, internal-generated, and reference-only starter policies.

  • P23V-003: Add Plugin Descriptor Doctor using .uplugin and module descriptor

metadata for GASP, Essential Bow, UDS, CommonUI, PCG, Python/MCP, Mover, and NetworkPrediction.

  • P23V-004: Add External Asset Quarantine and promotion gate before staged

folders can become production assets.

  • P23V-005: Add External Upgrade Planner with regression scenario links for

animation, atmosphere, UI, PCG, mesh generation, and package gates.

  • P23V-006: Add Attribution/Credits Board and NoAI/generated-content audit.
  • P23V-007: Add dashboard and package blockers for missing source records,

missing license policies, unapproved production usage, plugin drift, and missing attribution.

Recommended sequencing:

  1. Build P23V-001 through P23V-003 before importing or upgrading any more

content packs.

  1. Add P23V-004 before the next Fab/Marketplace kit is promoted into /Game.
  2. Add P23V-005 before updating GASP, Essential Bow, UDS, or CommonUI.
  3. Add P23V-006 before public screenshots, store pages, credits, soundtrack,

voice, or contractor content broaden.

  1. Add P23V-007 once the registry can block packaging safely.

#24. Phase-by-Phase Writing Plan

This section is the first pass at "how each step of the dev plan will be written." Each phase should eventually receive its own expanded chapter.

#P11: Boss quality bar and combat-feel kernel

Write this phase as a combat-feel production sprint.

Native:

  • defensive FSM;
  • combat motion solver v1;
  • ability/cancel/input-buffer rules;
  • boss encounter component;
  • boss move selection query/result structs;
  • boss phase and attack rule contracts;
  • damage/contact windows;
  • fog-wall and defeat persistence handoff;
  • telemetry.

Data:

  • boss encounter profile;
  • boss definition;
  • boss arena profile;
  • boss fog-wall profile;
  • boss stagger/punish profile;
  • boss move definitions;
  • boss phase definitions;
  • boss reward/persistence profile;
  • cue profile rows;
  • camera/rumble profiles;
  • montage sets;
  • combat state tags.

Designer surface:

  • boss Blueprint defaults;
  • Behavior Tree;
  • Blackboard keys;
  • GameplayAbility boss attacks;
  • boss move matrix;
  • phase preview;
  • arena anchor/fog-wall profile;
  • GameplayCue family;
  • montage notifies;
  • boss quality dashboard;
  • fog wall actor;
  • UMG boss bar/name card.

Proof:

  • boss intro scenario;
  • boss move matrix scenario;
  • boss phase scenario;
  • fog wall scenario;
  • boss contact-window scenario;
  • impact cue scenario;
  • critical/stagger scenario;
  • boss arena motion scenario;
  • boss defeat persistence scenario;
  • combat motion ledge/stair scenario;
  • PIE human feel pass.

#P12: World scripting, NPCs, objectives, vendors, tutorials

Write this phase as a world-state authoring sprint.

Native:

  • world-state tag registry;
  • objective evaluation;
  • dialogue line filtering;
  • vendor transaction authority;
  • guidance/objective hint resolver;
  • tutorial prompt observed-action state;
  • social consequence subsystem;
  • faction/reputation facade;
  • crime/witness server validation;
  • NPC memory/disposition bridge;
  • service trust resolver;
  • save/load persistence.

Data:

  • objective assets;
  • dialogue tables;
  • NPC definitions;
  • vendor stock assets;
  • tutorial prompt assets;
  • objective hint sets;
  • map clue profiles;
  • discovery memory rows.
  • faction registry;

#P13: Damage model and build depth

Write this phase as the build-math foundation.

  • stat investment and threshold passives;
  • equip-load roll tiers.

#P14: Moveset and weapon breadth

#P17: First world zone
